# Home

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/bbd826dc-e76c-40a2-8f31-9c916f29e14e/numi-Confluence.jpg)![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/be897cda-20aa-4c84-9365-6c4e4e0160e7/logo%402x.png)
### NUMI
Numi is a platform originally conceived to offer a solution for a White Label Money Distribution Solution. Also named Numi Baseline, the platform handles and orchestrates several services, processes, integration to achieve transaction execution and transaction information handling, user’s identification and security processes, amongst others.
Numi Application is a white label mobile application intended to expose all the services provided by Numi Platform. The Application provides a configurable Branding Feature that allows to fast produce a Branded Mobile Application, with the basic features of the Numi Platform.

**Numi as a Product**
The Numi Platform and Application are offered as the basic product for building a custom tailored money distribution solution. The product can be modified and enhanced to add modules, features and specific functionalities according to the needs for the implementation.
The Basic Premise is that the Numi Platform allows to move money from one end to another.
Find Depicted in this space s all the capabilities for the Numi Baseline

## In this Space
In this space you will find the information related to the Baseline Functionalities for the NUMI Platform,
*   KYC and CIP
*   Money Transfer Mechanisms
*   Payment of other services such as Top UP
*   Wallet Handling
*   Fees Handling
*   Platform Architecture Details

The Platform culture are the baseline concepts that are handled throought the platform and that should be considered in the implemenation of features or enhancements, integrations with third parties or new implementations that stand over the Numi Platform.

# Numi Wallet

## General Concepts
*   The Wallet is conceived as a digital solution to provide means to “move” money
*   The Wallet is an abstraction handled by the Platform that “contains” Products.
*   The Wallet needs to be connected with different third parties to handle the diferent product
*   The Wallet _main_ product is a bank account,
    *   The bank account / account needs to have a type and the control of rules associated to the type
        *   personal account
            *   credit account
            *   investments account
            *   salary account
            *   social aid account
        *   merchant account
            *   different types of merchants
    *   The type will hold the details related to rules and compliance per each type
*   The user (phone number) needs to be able to handle all the accounts he posesses with the same phone
*   The bank account is handled by either a Sponsor Bank or The Card Processor (e.g. Galileo)
    *   In Mexico the System of Record needs to be certified by CNBV, so it always is a Bank
    *   In USA the System of Record can be the Card Processor
*   The Wallet concept should be separated form the Cards concepts
    *   The Wallet contains cards
    *   The cards need to be handled by the Card Processor (e.g. Galileo)
*   The virtual or physical **cards** (either or both) should be added as secondary products to the account
    *   The Account may or may not have a card associated
    *   The Account may only have “one associated Active Card of each type (e.g. Virtual or Physical or Bullk)” each time
        *   The only way to have “several Active Cards” is to have a main card and the “Additionals”
        *   Right now the Additionals are “not in scope”
            *   To Review with UX [Valeria Ruiz](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/5cf14e2ae68f3d0fab740870?ref=confluence)
        *   For the time being June 2021 the handling of multiple request of same Virtual / Physical / Bulk Card Type: the platform should return an error (Exception) reporting that the type of card already exists.
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)
Handle request of an existing type of Card in the Platform - Tzune
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1001)[](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1001)*   [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1001)
    [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1001)*   [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1001)
        [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1001)*   [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1001)[TZN-1001](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1001) In QA
        *   Handle request of an existing type of Card in the Platform - SACBE

## The Creation of the Account and the Creation of the Card
*   When the user reaches the appropiate CIP Level, the Creation of the Account and the Creation of the card needs to be handled appliying a specific configuration **per country**
*   **Automatically Setting per Account Creation**
    *   The Account Creation process is triggered automatically when the user reaches the level
    *   Once the Account is Created Successfully:
        *   The Card can be created
            *   _Automatically_ created after the account is ready
            *   _Semi-Automatically_ offerered to be created when the account is ready
                *   this will be recurrent, and/or fixed in the UI, if the user does not have a card
*   **Semi Automatically Setting of Account Creation**
    *   The UI starts “offering” the Account Creation when the user reaches the level
        *   this will be recurrent, and/or fixed in the UI, if the user does not have an account
    *   When the user selects the option in the UI to Create its Account, the Creation process is started
    *   Once the Account is Created Successfully:
        *   The Card can be created
            *   _Automatically_ created after the account is ready
            *   _Semi-Automatically_ offerered to be created when the account is ready
                *   this will be recurrent, and/or fixed in the UI, if the user does not have a card
## Stablishing the Ways the Money Flows
_NOTE: MISSING GRAPHIC_
*   Transactions that are started from the Card and the first to know is the Card Processor (e.g. Card Swipe, Online Shopping):
    *   The events first, and the RDF File are the main source of information
    *   Our Platform needs to update the Account System of Record (if applicable, when different from the card processor, e.g.: Tzune)
*   Transaction that are started from the Account and the first to know is the Account System of Record (either the Bank of the Account Processor) (e.g. SPEI Transactions in Mexico)
    *   Our Platform needs to Update the Card Processor if applies (e.g. the balance needs to be mantained by Galileo in the case of SACBE)
## Implementation Specialization
### **SACBE**
*   SACBE implementation does not comply with the General Concepts : the wallet “is born” with a virtual card associated to it, and the account number “is” the internal number (PRN) of the Card
    *   The Virtual Product account is the “main” Account
    *   All other Products (Physical) are “secondary” Accounts, “associated to the main
    *   The Balance is shared: the main account and the secondary accounts display the same balance, and the Transactions performed by either that affect the shared balance
    *   The information of the Virtual Product needs to be Handled as the Main Account and maintain all the information throhough the life cycle of the User Experience when the main account is required.
### **TZUNE**
*   Tzune implementation needs to comply with the General concepts of having an account first, and the cards later
    *   Because of the “Hybrid” solution between Galileo and ABC Capital due to the rules of CNBV
    *   The account need to be created in ABC Capital
        *   The Process is complicated righ now, the documentaion (CIP) needs to happen in the side of the bank (“Expediente del Cliente”). They’re like 10 services
        *   ABC needs to allow the creation o the Account
            *   this process
    *   Once the Account is Created the CLABE is provided
    *   Once the Account is Created in ABC,
        *   Galileo DOES NOT to be informed _until_ the Card is requested by the user
            *   No Card, no Balance update from Galileo
    *   The Account Statement comes from ABC Capital
    *   When the User creates the Card
        *   Galileo Requires the CLABE as external Identifier of the Account
        *   Galileo Requires that ABC holds record of the Card associated PRN in the account informtion
        *   Due to the way Galileo implements the accounts, it has been offered as a solution to create the main Account is created as a physical product but not set to emboss and not activated
        *   This implies that the account is created as a physical product and NOT a Virtual

## User Exeprience Notes
To be REviewed with [Valeria Ruiz](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/5cf14e2ae68f3d0fab740870?ref=confluence)
*   To be defined the mechanisms how the user is notified
    *   That the Account is Created and he is able to Create a Card
    *   That the User is Able to Request either an Account, a Card or Both
*   The Sequence and Validations the UI needs to perform to Provide the Cresation of the Account, Card, etc
*   Error/Message Display enabled to allow the user to “decide” what to do if the Sequence and Validations are not fullfilled by him

# Transaction Handling Definitions

*   The Transaction should have a well defined type
*   The Transaction Limits should be defined by Type of Transaction
*   The transactions should be pushed throught their lifecicle to end in the success completion, with mechanisms to ensure the completion, when the conditions to its completion are met, some of the conditions might be
    *   The payment methods are in shape
    *   the limits breached either by the sender or the receiber have been re-set due to a new period
*   The User Experience should be able to provide the information of the reason when a Transaction Fails

Numi Plan tasks for Transaction Handling Technical Debt

## Considerations for Improvements on Transaction Handling

**Meeting 17-09-2021**
\--- Control de Limites por Tipo de Transaccion
\--- Mecanismo para asegurar la ejecución de la transaccion cuando las condiciones esten dadas (ej, se resetea el contador de limites)
\--- Mecanismo para dilucidar cuando una transaccion es sujeta de ser cancelada y que implica la cancelación (condiciones/cuando/quien) (\*)
\--- Mecanismo para identificar la razon de la falla o suspención/espera de la transaccion
Se identifican en experiencia de usuario las siguientes mejoras:
\--- Comunicación clara de las razones de la falla o suspensión/espera de una transacción
\--- Si la transaccion es sujet de ser cancelada por el usuario
\--- Ofrecer al usuario enviador la posiblidad de cancelar una transaccion si correspondiera de acuerdo a lo definido en los mecanismos en el backend (ver(\*))

# Notification for Customer Support on Platform Errors

If a backend error is encountered for a user operation or transaction and a ticket needs to be submitted the format of the ticket should always be:
Subject: Error message + User's mobile phone
Body: User's full name + more detailed information on the error (e.g. elastic request+response)
If the error is pertaining a transaction then the transaction id and correlation id will need to be included as well.
When submitting tickets the instance where we are submiting will determine the ticket state (Open/Closed), for all QA/PreProduction/Dev submissions the status of the ticket should always be Closed.
A field will also be provided where the instance will be provided (QA/PreProduction/Dev/Production)
The field names for each Wolkvox module are custom so for each brand we are submitting tickets Carolina will need to provide the field name's and possible values to be implemented.

# Data and Validations Required for Creating a User in NUMI

In certain implementations, there is the need to create a user in the Numi Platform “outside” of the regular “numi app” approach, and outside the validations that the platform performs when the users of the regular app move forward in the customer identification process (cip)
For this specific case, when an external service needs to create a user in NUMI platform, and the creation needs to be asynchornouse -outside the Platform validation- the following check-list of data and validations need to be considered, prior to send the information to the Numi Platform to actually create the user
Failing to provide this data or the pre-validations of the data, will cause the user to not be properly created in the Platform
**Clarification**
The validations required listed in this check-list, are specific to the data to be used in the creation of the user in the platform and furhter the account in the account processor (e.g Galileo for HWAY). This check-list does not include the existing, formatc, or data types specific validations _that need to be taken care of also_, by the corresponding implementation.
## Basic User Data
### Data
*   To create a basic user the following data is necessary
    *   User Name = Name, Middle Name(\*) and Last Name
    *   Phone Number = Mobile Phone number
    *   Email = optional
    *   PIN = Authentication Code for user, 6 digits, only 2 on 2 digits can be repeated
### Validations
*   To Validate the information of the Basic User Data the following needs to happen
    *   _TBD if any validation is needed specifically_
(\*) verify if the middle name is already handled in the platform

## Personal User Data
### Data
*   To complete the basic profile of the user the following data is necessary
    *   Birth Date
    *   Gender
### Validations
*   The birth date should be legal age (over 18)
## Form of Identification User Data
### Data
*   To continue with the data for the user , the identification information is to be provided
    *   Form of ID = the idtype provided
        *   [Vanesa V. Vilas](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/5cd5c60bf348990dbc3c2063?ref=confluence) to confirm the list of id types and the specific code for each one
    *   Number of the ID (can be either provided by the user of obtained from the scanned data)
    *   For “Scanned IDs”
        *   Expiration Date
        *   Name in the ID
        *   Country of the ID
        *   State (when applicable)
            *   [Vanesa V. Vilas](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/5cd5c60bf348990dbc3c2063?ref=confluence) to complete when this data is needed
### Validations
*   The validations of the Identification of the User are as follows
    *   When the ID is an SSN the format should respond to the standard
        *   Digits in the corresponding format
        *   Can accept also ITIN format
        *   see Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-32037](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-32037))
    *   The ID must not be expired (when applicable)
    *   The ID must be from the approved countries (_TBD if this requirement will change_)

## Proof of Life for Users
### Data
*   when the user provided a “non photo” form of id (e.g. SSN) the proof of life is required
    *   Provide proof of life with Iproov User Experience
    *   Image of the proof of life
### Validations
*   the proof of life from the provider service (Iproov) should have responded succesfully
*   the last frame of the proof of life must be stored

## Additional Information for User Identification Validation
### Data
*   The additional information for User Identification Validation in USA is specifically related to the addresss of the user, the data for a valid adress should cointain the following eleements
    *   Address 1
    *   Address 2
    *   City
    *   State
    *   ZipCode
### Validations
*   The validation for the Address should cointain the following considerations
    *   It should be a USA Address
    *   When possible , it should be validated with Google Address validation
        *   If the address is not found by google it can still exists, so all the elements of the address must be present
        *

# How a Send Funds Work

Considering the corridor configuration, the send funds from one origin payment method (a.k.a funding method) to a destination payument method (a.k.a delivery method) will internally have a "middle step" that will consist in depositing the money in the user´s account
Either if the account is a mimic of an actual bank account, like the programs implemented with a card processor such as Galileo, or Virtual Accounts from the platform, like the programs that do not have a Processor, the process is always the same:
### Step 1
The funds from the origin payment method are loaded into the user´s account, when the transaction is executed with the partner that provides this type of transaction (e.g. Tabapay, provides Pull funds from a Debit/Credit Card, commonly known in the industry as AFT)
*   in this moment the fees may or may not be included into the funds that need to be debited from the origin payment, depending on the funding method and the type of implementation.
*   If the funding method has fees, the fees must be included in the debit that needs to be performed to the origin payment method.
*   This fee is configured in the corridor as Source Fee
### Step 2
The fees charged needs to be reflected as a separated transaction in the user’s money movement, after the transaction is confirmed to be success by the partner that handles the funding method access (e.g. tabapay).
Depending on the implementation the method to reflect the fees charged may vary
*   for Galileo implementation is an assess Fee
    *   this method has its own verbiage and parameters that need to be represented see: [https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_assessfee](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_assessfee)
*   for non-Galileo implementation is an unload fee
    *   the unload fee is a platform service that allows to represent the movement of the money of the fee out of the users account into other account
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
In this case the method to unload the fees need to contemplate a new feature that needs to be developed: Handled a Standarized Transaction Type
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/NMC-846)[](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/NMC-846)*   [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/NMC-846)[](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/NMC-846)[NMC-846](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/NMC-846) - Handle a Standarized Transaction Type CLOSED
    *   The Standarized Transaction Type will allow to identify the different money movements that need to occur in the sponsor bank accounts (e.g. MCB Accounts) to be able to se the corresponding funds in the different accounts affected:
        *   The FBO Account for the funds to be deposited in the user´s account
        *   The Fees Account for the funds to be deposited in the Fees Account of the Program
        *   The Provider's Account, for the funds to be deposited in the Providers Account of the Program (e.g, Tabapay's Account for Hway)

### Step 3
The funds are sent from the user´s account into the destination payment method, when the transaction is executed with the partner that provides this type of transaction (e.g. Cybersource provides the push of funds to a debit/credit card)
*   The amount of the Transaction will be the one selected by the user to send to the destination payment method
*   in this moment the fees need to be deduced from the user's account,
*   By Business Definition the Fee configured in the corridor as destination fee needs to be always paid by the sender
*   the fees that need to be debited from the user's account will depend on the destination payment method and the type of implementation.
*   If the the destination payment method has fees, the fees must be deducted from the user’s account
*   This fee is configured in the corridor as Destination Fee

# Dashboard / Consola

## General Details
The Numi Console provides information of the Users, their level, CIP Status, Payment Methods, as well as information of the Transactions performed within the Platform.

For the Compliance Team the Numi Console provides information of the level of CIP, the status of that level (e.g. achieved, soft failed, hard failed), the different compliance related validation status (e.g. Name Validation, Phone Validation, OFAC Validation, ID Validation).
In future releases the following features are planned to be incorporated:
*   Allow the Compliance team to enable a user that was blocked
*   Allow the Compliance team to re-launch a user that is not allowed to continue due to validation errors (e.g. false positives in black lists)

## Scope of the Dashboard
The Overal Business Requirement for Dashboard is described in this extraction from the Platform Definitions documentation
**5.4.Herramientas de Soporte**
**5.4.1.Consola de Clientes e histórico de movimientos**
Esta es la principal herramienta dirigida a los consultores del área de atención a clientes. En ella se puede consultar toda la información “no” sensible del usuario y datos enmascarados de los medios de pago/fondeo que hasta la fecha haya definido el usuario.
Provee mecanismos para la búsqueda de usuarios y consulta de operaciones y servicios registrados en la plataforma, éstas búsquedas solo presentan información básica para proteger la integridad de los usuarios. Solo después que se ha aplicado satisfactoriamente un cuestionario de autenticación, el consultor puede tener acceso a un nivel mayor de información del usuario y sus transacciones. Dependiendo del nivel del consultor que accede a la información, es la cantidad de datos del usuario y sus transacciones que se presentan.
**5.4.2.Consola de Operaciones del Procesador**
Cuando existe una tarjeta configurada en la plataforma de SACBE, es posible habilitar acceso al consultor a la consola de monitoreo de transacciones que para este fin expone el Procesador que integra la plataforma. Acceder a esta consola también depende de los privilegios asignados al consultor.
**5.4.3.Registro de Bitácoras**
Todas las acciones que el consultor ejecuta en las consolas se registran en las bitácoras. Identificando:
*   Fecha y hora
*   Consultor que la ejecuta
*   Tiempo que permaneció en las pantallas
*   Usuario autenticado
*   Nivel de acceso del consultor
**5.5.Políticas de seguridad en el servicio al cliente**
Desde el punto de vista de seguridad, la plataforma de SACBE permite:
*   Definir niveles de acceso para consultores.
*   Activar y desactivar consultores.
*   Filtrar la información a desplegar en función del nivel de acceso que el consultor que accede a la información tiene.
*   Asignar un nivel de acceso para consultar información sensible, no se incluye en este rubro la información de los medios de pago/fondeo.
*   No se presenta en ningún mecanismo información relacionada con los factores de autenticación del usuario.
A nivel operativo del área de atención a clientes y fuera del contexto del uso de las consolas, tenemos implementado:
*   Sistemas físicos para el control de acceso al área de atención a clientes.
*   El acceso a las consolas esta restringido a los equipos que están dentro del área de atención a clientes.
*   Se han implementado sistemas para la grabación de llamadas.
*   No se permite el acceso de dispositivos móviles y computadoras personales al área de atención a clientes.
**5.6.bloqueo de cuentas del usuario**
La plataforma de SACBE provee mecanismos para bloquear cuentas de usuario en los siguientes casos:
*   Después de 3 intentos fallidos de acceder a la cuenta. Antes que el usuario ejecute el tercer intento, las aplicaciones especializadas informan al usuario del riesgo de bloquear la cuenta si falla de nuevo en su autenticación.
*   Bloqueo por ausencia prolongada. Es factible configurar en la plataforma el tiempo de inactividad que debe ser evaluado para proceder con el bloqueo de una cuenta que no ha si utilizada.
*   Después de 3 intentos de crear una sesión de trabajo cuando ya existe una activa. Antes del tercer intento, las aplicaciones especializadas informan al usuario del riesgo de bloquear la cuenta si intenta nuevamente mientras existe otra sesión activa.
Para desbloquear una cuenta, el usuario puede contactar al área de atención a clientes.

# Unlock User in Console

## General Details:
SACBE Compliance Requirement June 2021
##   

## Requerimiento: Botón Amnistia
_Escenario: Si el usuario se bloquea en nivel 1 por OFAC y es un falso positivo_
*   se debe brindar la posiblidad en la Consola de Desbloquear el usuario -[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-2294](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-2294)
*   Generar la UI en la Consola con el botón
*   El botón dispara el desbloqueo del usuario y la restauracion del nivel que estaba cursando
*   Se debe registrar que el usuario se desbloqueó por falso positivo
*   El desbloqueo tendrá un periodo de expiración de N dias (a confirmar con Compliance, default = 2)
*   La información de "desbloqueado por falso positivo" y "fecha de expiración" se debe mostrar en la consola junto con los datos de CIP
*   se debe enviar una notificación al usuario para que continúe lo que estaba haciendo - Generate Notification for User that has been unlocked[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-2295](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-2295)

*   Asegurar que la aplicación reacciona al evento de backend que ahora tiene el nivel 1 y permite al usuario continuar operando

# Baseline Documentation

In this section you will find reference to all the baseline documentation to be managed as a starting point for any **_Implementation_** of Numi Platform

# Platform Feature Details

In the following pages you will find the details and considerations regarding Numi Baseline Platform Interaction and Specifications, as described in the legacy documentation site (Mingle).
The objective of this section is to provide context of existing features that were not particularly developed for a later implementation (such as SACBE). This features are instanced from the original Numi Platform as they are, or maybe with a little enhancement or _twist_ to accomplish the implementations business specific needs.

# Beneficiaries in the Platform

The beneficiaries are a specific portion of information that belongs to the Platform User.
For a user, a Beneficiary is the destination of a transaction, that is not meant to be for the user himself, either money or mobile-time.
**A Beneficiary will have the following information**
*   Basic Information, the one currently used in the application: phone (from which is extracted the country code), name, last name, and email
*   Address Information, the information will contain the same fields used in the rest of the Platform and will be handled the same way: Address 1, address 2, City, State and Zip Code. As a side note when entering the information this should be a valid location provided by Google Services
*   The List of Delivery Methods the User has selected for the Beneficiary, this is, all the means (bank accounts, credit cards, debit cards)  the User has saved into his Beneficiary information to send money to.
*   This information needs to be saved to be handled in the future to allow the user to modify or delete.
**In all cases the SACBE Platform (for any app, on any business model) needs to incorporate the concept of "Beneficiary" as a business entity..**
The Beneficiary as a Business Entity will be handled in the future to allow the user to have a "Beneficiaries Book [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1334)[SAC-1334](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1334) Backlog " to be able to review the data of each beneficiary, modify or delete
**The information of the Location of the Beneficiary should be configured**
*   **by country:** Despite the use of beneficiaries as a business entity, and the need to save the data for the beneficiaries, the use or not of the saved accounts / methods, and the corresponding display in the UI needs to be configurable by country. This is, even though the data needs to be saved and handled as a business entity , its use in the UI will respond to a configuration depending on the country.
*   **by operation type,** cross border, domestic.
**The context of application for the Beneficiary Entity as per April 2020**
*   **Applies for Cross border**
*   **Applies for p2p for Mexico**
    *   For SPEI Transactions when adding a new account to send money to or transfer out
    *   There´s a waiting time to be configured for the notification of the creation of the beneficiary, setted in 30 minutes for the Mexican Market 

Pending Definitions as per June 2020
*   Beneficiaries Book UI
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)

](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1092)*   [SAC-1092](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1092) - Beneficiaries as a Business Object in the Platform CLOSED

# Top Up : Buying/Requesting Mobile Air Time

IMPORTANT _The following specs are pasted from Legacy Mingle Document Site_
## Narrative
As a numi user, on certain eligible regions, I will be able to buy, or request, _mobile airtime_ for a destination mobile phone line (including my own), from a range of supported countries and carriers, by funding the operation with one of the supported funding methods available in my country.

## Specifications
### Overview

Mobile airtime is a particular operation type, initiated by a numi user, that essentially moves funds from payment method X of user A, to release them to user B, with payment method _mobile-airtime_.
As such, the operation can either be a _Buy_ (the initiator is the user buying) or a ‘_Request_’ (the initiator is the recipient), similar to any other numi operation.

### Initiating a mobile airtime operation
The mobile airtime operation can be initiated in a number of ways, including:
*   By tapping on the home action _Buy_ or _Request_ (see next sections for flow descriptions).
*   By following an attribution link, outside of the app, received thru any supported channel (e.g. SMS, email, Facebook campaign, affiliate partner, etc).

### Operation flow for buying mobile airtime

The operation flow for buying mobile airtime will consist primarily of the typical numi send flow, aside from the following particularities:
*   _Phone to reload_, the initial screen, will allow the user to either choose a contact from their address book (_For someone else_) or an additional option (_For myself_), the latter which when tapped, will skip the _To Whom_ screen (see next).
*   _To Whom_ screen, will behave as usual: requesting the user to enter _recipient first and last name_, _mobile phone_ with _country code_, and _email address_.
    *   By querying a service, the app will:
        *   Firstly, determine if the recipient phone line is of is of type mobile, displaying a warning screen, if it’s not, as shown in ☛ [#5](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/5).
        *   Next, determine if the recipient phone supports receiving mobile airtime, displaying a warning screen if it doesn’t, as shown in ☛ [#5](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/5).
        *   Finally, obtain the list of possible mobile airtime amounts to choose from, depending on the destination country and mobile number (all parameters sent to the query service).
*   _How Much_ _screen_ will present a slightly customized behavior: (refer to the look and feel as shown on ☛ [#5](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/5))
    *   The app will display a list of options with the available amounts to buy, expecting the user to select one.
    *   If the options returned by the service, have the same source and destination currency, only the amount plus currency code is displayed for each.
    *   If options have different source and destination currency, the app will display the destination amount and currency first, then a separator, and the source amount and currency code afterward.  
    *   There won’t be any reference to _exchange rates_ (the concept is not applicable to mobile airtime).
*   _Payment method screen_ will behave identically to the _Source of payment_ numi screen, allowing to either choose from an existing payment method on file, or add a new method on the fly, and use it.
    *   _Adding a new payment_ _method_ will behave just like it does on the standard flows. The newly created method will be stored in the user’s profile.
    *   As usual, available funding methods, and their fees will be configurable, and they will be calculated following existing rules (see ☛ [#334](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/334) for methods and ☛ [#324](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/324) for fees).
*   _The confirmation screen_ will present a slightly customized behavior:
    *   There is no option for _who pays the fee_ (fee is always paid by buyer).
    *   The _details popup screen_ will describe: the amount to _Reload_ in destination currency and amount to charge in source currency (or just the amount to charge, if same currency), and the _payment fee_ (if applicable).
*   The mobile airtime operation will be displayed on the _home screen_, in the same fashion as other operations, distinguishable only by a custom icon.
    *   _For myself_ operations, will be displayed with a fixed label _Mobile Airtime_, instead of the recipient’s name.
###   

### Operation flow for requesting mobile airtime

The operation flow for requesting mobile airtime will consist primarily of the typical numi request flow, aside from the following particularities:
*   _To Whom_ screen, will allow the user to tap on _To a contact_, to then choose one from their address book.
    *   By querying a service, the app will:
        *   Firstly, determine if the mobile phone to request to, supports buying mobile airtime, displaying a warning screen if it doesn’t.
*   _How Much_ _screen_ will present a slightly customized behavior: 
    *   The app will allow the user to enter a desired amount to request. (with its currency code indicator).
    *   The app will display a small disclaimer text, indicating that _Amount may vary based on package availability_.  
    *   There won’t be any reference to _exchange rates_ (the concept is not applicable to mobile airtime).
*   _Payment method screen_ will behave identically to the _Source of payment_ numi screen, allowing to either choose from an existing payment method on file, or add a new method on the fly, and use it.
    *   _Adding a new payment_ _method_ will behave just like it does on the standard flows. The newly created method will be stored in the user’s profile.
    *   As usual, available funding methods, and their fees will be configurable, and they will be calculated following existing rules
*   _The confirmation screen_ will not apply to the request flow.
*   The mobile airtime operation will be displayed on the _home screen_, in the same fashion as other request operations, distinguishable only by a custom icon.
    *   Initiated requests will be displayed as _Pending_ until the buyer proceeds to complete them, or if the operation fails, for timeout or any other cause.
###   

### Operation flow for funding a mobile airtime request
The operation flow will be exactly to the buy mobile airtime flow, aside from the following particularities:
*   _How Much_ _screen_ will present a list of possible options to buy (like in a regular buy operation); however, since in the original request, the user entered a specific amount, and the buyer can only select one out of a number of fixed amount choices, the app will pre-select the option with the closest amount, rounding to the higher value (if it exceeds the max option, then that one is pre-selected). The buyer will always be who decides the amount to load, regardless.
*   Pending requests to be funded, will be displayed as _Pending_, with the usual highlight (_call to action_), until the buyer proceeds to complete them, or if the operation fails, for timeout or any other cause.
###   

### Notes on currencies
*   All user interface and communication will be presented on the proper currency for the involved party (buyer, receiver).
    *   For buyer/funder users, all amounts and currency codes will correspond to the _funding currency_ indicated by the service
    *   For recipient/requester users, all amounts and currency codes will correspond to the _destination currency_ indicated by the service
> Note: depending on the destination country and carrier, the destination currency may or may not match the local currency of the recipient user; for this reason, the app must align to whichever the system indicates as ≈should be honored

# Legacy Top Up : Buying/Requesting Mobile Air Time

# PASTED FROM OLD MINGLE
# Buy or request mobile airtime
(**v19** - Latest version, last modified over 2 years ago)
 (no tags set)
###  Dependencies
## Narrative
As a numi user, on certain eligible regions, I will be able to buy, or request, _mobile airtime_ for a destination mobile phone line (including my own), from a range of supported countries and carriers, by funding the operation with one of the supported funding methods available in my country.

## Specifications
### Overview
Mobile airtime is a particular operation type, initiated by a numi user, that essentially moves funds from payment method X of user A, to release them to user B, with payment method _mobile-airtime_.
As such, the operation can either be a _Buy_ (the initiator is the user buying) or a ‘_Request_’ (the initiator is the recipient), similar to any other numi operation.

### Initiating a mobile airtime operation
The mobile airtime operation can be initiated in a number of ways, including:
*   By tapping on the home action _Buy_ or _Request_ (see next sections for flow descriptions).
*   By following an attribution link, outside of the app, received thru any supported channel (e.g. SMS, email, Facebook campaign, affiliate partner, etc).

### Operation flow for buying mobile airtime
The operation flow for buying mobile airtime will consist primarily of the typical numi send flow, aside from the following particularities:
*   _Phone to reload_, the initial screen, will allow the user to either choose a contact from their address book (_For someone else_) or an additional option (_For myself_), the latter which when tapped, will skip the _To Whom_ screen (see next).
*   _To Whom_ screen, will behave as usual: requesting the user to enter _recipient first and last name_, _mobile phone_ with _country code_, and _email address_.
    *   By querying a service, the app will:
        *   Firstly, determine if the recipient phone line is of is of type mobile, displaying a warning screen, if it’s not, as shown in ☛ [#5](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/5).
        *   Next, determine if the recipient phone supports receiving mobile airtime, displaying a warning screen if it doesn’t, as shown in ☛ [#5](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/5).
        *   Finally, obtain the list of possible mobile airtime amounts to choose from, depending on the destination country and mobile number (all parameters sent to the query service).
*   _How Much_ _screen_ will present a slightly customized behavior: (refer to the look and feel as shown on ☛ [#5](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/5))
    *   The app will display a list of options with the available amounts to buy, expecting the user to select one.
    *   If the options returned by the service, have the same source and destination currency, only the amount plus currency code is displayed for each.
    *   If options have different source and destination currency, the app will display the destination amount and currency first, then a separator, and the source amount and currency code afterward.  
    *   There won’t be any reference to _exchange rates_ (the concept is not applicable to mobile airtime).
*   _Payment method screen_ will behave identically to the _Source of payment_ numi screen, allowing to either choose from an existing payment method on file, or add a new method on the fly, and use it.
    *   _Adding a new payment_ _method_ will behave just like it does on the standard flows. The newly created method will be stored in the user’s profile.
    *   As usual, available funding methods, and their fees will be configurable, and they will be calculated following existing rules (see ☛ [#334](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/334) for methods and ☛ [#324](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/324) for fees).
*   _The confirmation screen_ will present a slightly customized behavior:
    *   There is no option for _who pays the fee_ (fee is always paid by buyer).
    *   The _details popup screen_ will describe: the amount to _Reload_ in destination currency and amount to charge in source currency (or just the amount to charge, if same currency), and the _payment fee_ (if applicable).
*   The mobile airtime operation will be displayed on the _home screen_, in the same fashion as other operations, distinguishable only by a custom icon.
    *   _For myself_ operations, will be displayed with a fixed label _Mobile Airtime_, instead of the recipient’s name.
###   

### Operation flow for requesting mobile airtime
The operation flow for requesting mobile airtime will consist primarily of the typical numi request flow, aside from the following particularities:
*   _To Whom_ screen, will allow the user to tap on _To a contact_, to then choose one from their address book.
    *   By querying a service, the app will:
        *   Firstly, determine if the moile phone to request to, supports buying mobile airtime, displaying a warning screen if it doesn’t, as shown in ☛ [#5](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/5).
*   _How Much_ _screen_ will present a slightly customized behavior: (refer to the look and feel as shown on ☛ [#5](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/5))
    *   The app will allow the user to enter a desired amount to request. (with its currency code indicator).
    *   The app will display a small disclaimer text, indicating that _Amount may vary based on package availability_.  
    *   There won’t be any reference to _exchange rates_ (the concept is not applicable to mobile airtime).
*   _Payment method screen_ will behave identically to the _Source of payment_ numi screen, allowing to either choose from an existing payment method on file, or add a new method on the fly, and use it.
    *   _Adding a new payment_ _method_ will behave just like it does on the standard flows. The newly created method will be stored in the user’s profile.
    *   As usual, available funding methods, and their fees will be configurable, and they will be calculated following existing rules (see ☛ [#334](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/334) for methods and ☛ [#324](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/324) for fees).
*   _The confirmation screen_ will not apply to the request flow.
*   The mobile airtime operation will be displayed on the _home screen_, in the same fashion as other request operations, distinguishable only by a custom icon.
    *   Initiated requests will be displayed as _Pending_ until the buyer proceeds to complete them, or if the operation fails, for timeout or any other cause.
###   

### Operation flow for funding a mobile airtime request
The operation flow will be exactly to the buy mobile airtime flow, aside from the following particularities:
*   _How Much_ _screen_ will present a list of possible options to buy (like in a regular buy operation); however, since in the original request, the user entered a specific amount, and the buyer can only select one out of a number of fixed amount choices, the app will pre-select the option with the closest amount, rounding to the higher value (if it exceeds the max option, then that one is pre-selected). The buyer will always be who decides the amount to load, regardless.
*   Pending requests to be funded, will be displayed as _Pending_, with the usual highlight (_call to action_), until the buyer proceeds to complete them, or if the operation fails, for timeout or any other cause.
###   

### Notes on currencies
*   All user interface and communication will be presented on the proper currency for the involved party (buyer, receiver).
    *   For buyer/funder users, all amounts and currency codes will correspond to the _funding currency_ indicated by the service
    *   For recipient/requester users, all amounts and currency codes will correspond to the _destination currency_ indicated by the service
    *   Note: depending on the destination country and carrier, the destination currency may or may not match the local currency of the recipient user; for this reason, the app must align to whichever the system indicates as ≈should be honored

# Corridors in Numi

# Corridors and payment methods
The platform defines a matrix of possible “_corridors_” thru which a money transfer operation can be carried out, by a certain user.
Each entry will define a _corridor_ thru which an operation can be carried out, defined by five variables:
*   Product Type 
    *   Funds(P2P), Wallet/Card, VirtualAgent (Remittances), Airtime
    *   
*   Source country
    *   Is the country of the user that originates the transaction
    *   For the time being source country available will be only  US or MX for the P2P Application
    *   For the Remittances application this list of available source countries depends on the MTO configuration
*   Funding or source payment methods:
    *   Every time a user wishes to load funds into their account or to fund a money transfer operation, they can choose from a number of different funding methods. Depending on the corridor, the user level, and potentially other conditions, the user will be allowed to choose among some, or all, of the payment method types detailed in Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-21977](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-21977))
*   Destination country
    *   Is the country of the user that originates the transaction
    *   For the time being destination country available will be only  US or MX for the P2P Application
    *   For the Remittances application this list of available source countries depends on the MTO configuration
    *   *   
*   Payout or destination payment methods
    *   Every time a user desires to send funds to a recipient, he can choose from a number of different payment methods, so that the recipient can collect the money.  Depending on the corridor involved, the user level, and some other circumstances, the user will be able to select among any of the destination payment method types as described in Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-21977](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-21977))
    *   For the Remittances application this information is not available beforehand. The _end part (destination method) of the corridor is dynamic_ (and hence, not pre-configured in tables) depending on the methods available on the MTO. Because of this, the Corridors are completed dynamically during processing fetching  the information of the MTO involved in the Transaction.
## IMPORTANT _The following is pasted from Legacy Mingle Documentation_
_Last Updated September 2018_
# Corridors and payment methods
(**v17** - Latest version, last modified over 2 years ago)
The platform defines a matrix of possible “_corridors_” thru which a money transfer operation can be carried out, by a certain user.
Each entry will define a_corridor_thru which an operation can be carried out, defined by four variables:
*   [Source country](http://#)
*   Funding or source payment methods
*   [Destination country](http://#)
*   Payout or destination payment methods

## **Technical implementation (front-end)**
The app will determine the enabled corridors, by looking them up on a master table, maintained and broadcasted by, the server.
The table will contain a set of rows, one for eachenabled “_corridor_” (permutation of four variables: source country, destination country, source payment method, destination payment method).
If a particular corridor is not included on the table, the app should not allow to choose such source method, destination method, destination country (for the recipient), or source country (for the sender/funder).
### Remarks
*   Any entry on the table with**destination method value of empty (null)** should be assumed as “**_my wallet_**”, i.e. indicating an enabled **_wallet load method_**.
*   ​Any entry on the table with**source method value of empty (null)** should be assumed as “**_my wallet_**”, i.e. indicating an enabled **_wallet transfer out method_**.

*   Other information that will help understand the status of the implementation

# MTO Dynamic Corridors

The Corridors information is completed dynamically for the Remittances model.
For each Country selected, there should be a list of available Destination Payment Methods available, that will be provided by each MTO that has operation (and contract with our company) in the selected country.
Since the Payment Methods available depend on the Correspondant that serves for that MTO in the selected Country and State, the list may be subject to change periodically, that is why there should be a service that allows to synchronise MTO data with SACBE data.
## Mocked Data Currently in use
In the following _Google Sheet_ you will find the information currently in use for the corridors to be accessed when executing money flow through remittances (virtual agent)

# Legacy Corridor Issues in Mobile

From the functional definition stand point, the payment methods available to “send” money should be also available to “receive” money.
In other words, a user requesting money should be able to define the payment method he wants to get money into, and they should be the same available if a user was sending money to him.
These definitions are configured in the Corridors
When
There is an issue with a legacy logic definition in Mobile layer, that makes mandatory to define the corridors with the “Request” operation type, to allow the requestor user to view and select the available payment methods he wants to receive money into.
Even if these corrirdors are properly set when the transaction is a Send, and the sender user can select the payment methods to send money to, the Mobile layer will check specifically for the “Request” operation type

```auto
public enum OperationType
{
    SendFunds,
    RequestFunds,
    WalletLoad,
    WalletTransferOut,
    TopUp,
    RequestTopUp,
    SendFundsVirtualAgent,
    RequestFundsVirtualAgent,
    StorePayment,
    SubscriptionFee,
    Reward,
    WalletTransfer
}
```

# Home Page and Previous Transactions List

IMPORTANT **_Legacy Documentation from Mingle Site_**

# Home screen and transaction list 
The Design should reflect the detailed in the UX Flow:  Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-32617](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-32617))

**Home screen:**
When there are no existing operations for the current user, the home screen will merely display a full screen, with the numi logo at the centre, and two action buttons below,  (_Pay, Request_) that will initiate a _Pay_, or a _Request_ operation, respectively. The same screen will be displayed if the current user is not logged in yet (i.e. “anonymous”), in addition to a _Sign-Up_ action link at the bottom half, that will initiate that flow.
**Banner area:** 
The upper area of the home screen will be contrasted by an accent background colour, and will be presented differently depending on whether the user has an active wallet or not:With an active wallet, the banner will display:
1.     *   The wallet current balance and currency code, in large text, at the centre.
    *   A text “_My Wallet_” above it.
    *   Two buttons (_Money In, Money Out),_ just beneath the balance, that will serve as shortcuts to the _Wallet – Load_, and _Wallet – Transfer out_, flows, respectively.
    *   Without an active wallet, just the numi logo at the centre.
**New Operation actions:** 
Just beneath the _banner area_, two buttons (_Pay, Request_) will initiate a _Pay_, or a _Request_ operation, respectively.
Upon tapping either button, a sub-menu popup will roll out, prompting the user to select the type of product to pay or request (plus an arrow button, to close the sub-menu).
Possible product types are _Money, TopUp, Bill pa_y. The app will only display the options available for the country of origin for the current user.(\*\*\*\*)
Upon tapping any of the product types, the app will initiate the correspondent flow.
**Operation list**
The home screen will list all of the user operations, one row for each. Whenever more than one operation related to the same contact exists, only the most recent one will be shown. 
Each item will display the operation details:
*       *       *   An icon with the contact name initials.
        *   The full contact name.
        *   The operation date (see (\*\*\*\*\*)).
        *   The operation amount (in the current user’s currency, with the currency code to the right).
The text will be displayed in **full black colour** when the operation is in states: _Complete_, and in **light gray**  when it’s on _In progress, Pending_, _Fail_ or _Expired_.)
*       *   *   The operation status according to the following detail (\*\*\*)
Each item will display, on the left bottom corner, either an icon or an action button, depending on the operation state, as follows:
*   _Pending (no action required):_ {see icon in [_UX Flow_](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-crPWkuXem5m-q-OrqyA8qeyHwIy5vC7/view)} intended for Request operations when shown to the Requester, that are waiting for the payer to act on it.
*   _Pending (action required):_ {action button} intended for operations that require an action from the current user, as follows. 
*   _Also, a coloured left border will highlight the operation row (see_ [_UX Flow_](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-crPWkuXem5m-q-OrqyA8qeyHwIy5vC7/view) _)._
    1. (_RECEIVE_) _Pay_ operations presented on the _Requester_ side, that require them to provide the destination method.
    2. (_PAY_) _Request_ operations presented on the _Payer_ side, that require them to provide the funding source method.
    3. (_ADD CASH_) _Pay_ operations presented on the _Payer_ side, that require them to manually pay by cash at a physical location.
*   _In progress:_ {see icon in [_UX Flow_](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-crPWkuXem5m-q-OrqyA8qeyHwIy5vC7/view)} any other intermediate state not covered above, that represents an operation that is still being processed at any stage.
*   _Complete:_ {see icon in [_UX Flow_](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-crPWkuXem5m-q-OrqyA8qeyHwIy5vC7/view)} operations that have completed successfully.
*   _Fail:_ {see icon in [_UX Flow_](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-crPWkuXem5m-q-OrqyA8qeyHwIy5vC7/view)} operations that have failed permanently for any reason.
*   _Expired:_ {see icon in [_UX Flow_](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-crPWkuXem5m-q-OrqyA8qeyHwIy5vC7/view)} operations that were waiting for a user action (see \*\*\*), and that reached timeout (according to business rules).
**“Conversation” screen:** 
When tapping any operation in the home screen (except for the operation action buttons), the app will navigate to the “conversation” view, where all operations involving that particular contact, will be displayed.
Each operation will be displayed as a “card” in a stacked layout (see [UX Flow](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/250)).
The screen title will display the full name of the involved contact.
The same New Operation buttons (_Pay, Request_) will be available, right beneath the screen title (behaving as explained in the new operations section), only that the current contact will be set as the default _To Whom_, for the created operation).
Each operation card will present the operation details, as follows:
*   An icon with the contact name initials  displaying as the card header:(\*\*)
*   “Payment to “ + _contact-first-name_, if it is a _Pay_ operation (or a _Request_ that has been paid) and the current user is the _payer_, or “Payment from “ + _contact-first-name_, if they are the _receiver_.
*   “Request to “ + _contact-first-name_, if it is a _Request_ operation (not paid yet_)_ and the current user is the _requester_, or “Request from “ + _contact-first-name_, if they are the _payer_.
*   “TopUp to “ + _contact-first-name_, if it is a _TopUp_ operation and the current user is the payer, or “TopUp from “ + _contact-first-name_, if they are the _receiver_.
*    "Load" if it is a _Wallet Load_ operation for the user that is currently using the App.
*   "Transfer Out" if it is a _Wallet Transfer_ (from a Wallet to another payment method) for the user that is currently using the App.
Amount: the operation amount, in the current user’s currency, with the currency code to the right. The colour rule applies (see
*   ) .
*   Status: to the right of the amount, a label indicating current status, one of (_Pending, In progress, Complete, Fail, Expired_) (see  (\*\*\*)).
*   Product type: below the amount, a label indicating the product involved, one of (_Money, TopUp, Bill pa_y_)_ (see (\*\*\*\*)).
*   Icon or Action button: below the status, either a status icon or an action button, will be displayed, when appropriate, just like in the home screen ( see (\*\*\*)).Operation time and date: at the bottom of the card.
*   The time will be displayed normally, and the date will display: text “_Today_”, or “_Yesterday_”, in those circumstances, or the actual date value, for any other case.(\*\*\*\*\*)
*   The date and time will correspond to the date and time of creation of the _Pay_ or _Request_ operation.
*   Exceptionally, when a _Request_ is successfully paid, the date and time displayed will correspond to the moment the operation was paid.
**Operation details**
*   When tapping on a specific operation (card item as described in the corresponding section), the operation details screen will be displayed. 
*   The screen will be comprised of:
    *   The subtitle label, indicating the direction (To) and recipient (From) of the operation (behaving as explained in (\*\*)).
The operation amount, at the top-centre, in the current user’s currency, with the currency code to the right. The colour rule applies (see
*   ).The “_Details_” actionable label, below the amount, that will reveal the details modal. This option will only be visible when the following conditions are met:
    1. For operations in status _Complete_.
    2. When displayed by a user paying the operation, or by a _recipient_ user (_receiver_ or _requester_), that pays part or all of the fees.
*   An icon and label, indicating the operation status, or an action button, whichever applies ( see (\*\*\*)).
*   A full row with the time and date (following the same rules as in (\*\*\*\*\*)).
*   The transaction ID.A label indicating the contact involved in this operation, following these rules:
    1. The contact displayed is always the other party; so if the current user is the payer, the recipient is displayed, and when it’s the recipient, the _payer_ is displayed.
    2. For a _Pay_ operation, the payer user will see the label as “To + {contact}” and the _receiver_ will see “From + {contact}”.For a _Request_ operation (not paid yet), the requester will see “To + {contact}” and the _payer_ will see “From + {contact}”.
    3. For a  _Request_ operation (already paid), it will behave as a standard _Pay_ operation. So, when paid, the behaviour is inverted: the _payer_ will see it as “To + {contact}”, and the _requester_ as “From + {contact}”.
*   Next, a label reading “Category: “ + product type (i.e. _Money, TopUp, Bill pay_).The involved payment method from the current user:
*   If the current user is the _payer_, then the source method is displayed (“From: “ + {src-method-name}).
1. If the current user is the _recipient_, then the destination method is displayed (“To: “ + {dest-method-name}) .
2. From the above, It follows that for operations pending user action, this label will not be displayed (i.e. a _Request_ operation displayed by the _payer_, before a source method is entered, or a _Pay_ operation displayed by the _receiver_, before a destination method is entered).
*   Location Information, this section will only be available for a Transaction Type "Cash Pickup", the Address for the Pickup Location will be displayed
    *   The Address will be displayed as a Link only for direct remittances, and using the link will open the Map for the Location
    *   The Address will NOT be displayed as a Link to a Map for Open Remittances
    *   
*   Repeat action button, at the bottom of the screen, available for eligible operations, that will initiate the _Repeat operation_ flow. 
    *   “_Repeat_” button, for _Pay_ operations under “Complete” status, and only when displayed by the _Payer_.
    *   “_Repeat Request_” button, for _Request_ operations under “Complete” status, and only when displayed by the _Requester_.
        *   _Note: for Remittances the Repeat Request Operation SHOULD NOT be available_ due to the nature of this type of business, which involves the MTO, that does not allow to control the features at  the end of the transaction.

# Limits and Velocity

## General Details
The limits are rules to be achieved by the transaction of the accumulative of transactions, and by the details of the transactions performed by each user.
The velocity are a cumulative of determinate limits, in a specific period of time pre-set. 
In each case, if the rules are not accomplished, the user should be notified and the transaction that broke the rule(s) should be stopped
## Technical Details
The configuration for limits and velocity is a Platfform Configuration that needs to be applied with the following premises:
*   Configuration is per Brand/Country - Also called per program
*   Within each Brand/Country, the specific Payment Method related limits and velocity, might have to be reconfigured per vendor

Review the following for the detail on how the Limits and Velocity configuration is applied in the Platform:
Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-22197](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-22197))

# Relation Limit (original implementation)

## General Detail
This limit considers the relationship between users.
When a user is a sender, it considers the number of users they send to.
When a user is a recipient, it considers the number of users from whom it receives.
##   

## Technical Details

The filter steps are the following:
1. If the user is the sender, it is necessary to list all the corresponding operations where the user’s phonenumber is equals to the operation’s Sender.ContactId.
2. If the user is the recipient, it is necessary to list all the corresponding operations where the user’s phonenumber is equals to the operation’s Recipient.ContactId
3. The operations taken in account for conventional relation limits are the ones which status is “Completed“.
4. The operations taken in account for relation limits by intention are those which status is different from Cancelled, Completed or Failed.
5. Values for this kind of limit are specified for certain providers and certain kind of accounting.
6. To validate this, from now on, operations have two fields referring to provider transactions, one for the sender and the other one for the recipient.
7. Below, there is an example:
8. 9. "SenderLimitProviders": \[
10. {
11. "ProviderName": "GalileoOCTDomestic",
12. "AccountingType": "Debit"
13. }
14. \],
15. "RecipientLimitProviders": \[
16. {
17. "ProviderName": "CyberSourcePayout",
18. "AccountingType": "Credit"
19. }
20. \]
21. Once the operations are listed taking into account their status and the user’s phonenumber, the limit’s provider and the limit’s accounting are added to the filter, using the field corresponding to the user.
22. To avoid taking operations with the same sender and recipient, the ProductType for the operations must be “Fund“ (this is another condition added to the filter)
23. With the operations obtained, they are then grouped by Recipient.ContactId (if the user is the sender) or by Sender.ContactId (if the user is the recipient)

| Transactions which don't count for Relation Limit |
| --- |
| Wallet transfer between own accounts (Personal - Saving & vice versa) |
| WalletLoad with ACH |
| WalletLoad with Digital |
| WalletTransferOut with ACH |
| Subscription |
| Redeem Rewards |
| Rewards Remittances |
| Rewards by Dosh |

## Query samples
### General query from OperationView by 7 rolling days (Conventional limit)

```plain
SenderContactId:+19163015817 AND
Status:Completed AND
CreatedAt:[2024-01-12T06:00:00.0000000Z TO 2024-01-18T16:05:39.3354830Z]
RecipientContactId:+19163015817 AND
Status:Completed AND
CreatedAt:[2024-01-12T06:00:00.0000000Z TO 2024-01-18T16:05:39.3354830Z]
```

### Query from ProviderTransactionView by provider, 7 rolling days(Conventional limit)

```plain
Deleted:false AND
UserId:+19163015817 AND
ProviderName:GalileoOCTCrossBorder AND
AccountingType:Debit AND
CreatedAt:[2024-01-12T06:00:00.0000000Z TO 2024-01-18T16:05:39.3354830Z]
RelatedPaymentData:a246884d-0c36-47b1-aace-95c6ebd96206
```

### General query from OperationView by 7 rolling days and operations in progress (Intention limit)

```plain
SenderContactId:+19163015817 AND
NOT Status:Completed AND
NOT Status:Cancelled AND
NOT Status:Failed AND
CreatedAt:[2024-01-12T06:00:00.0000000Z TO 2024-01-18T16:05:39.3354830Z]
RecipientContactId:+19163015817 AND
NOT Status:Completed AND
NOT Status:Cancelled AND
NOT Status:Failed AND
CreatedAt:[2024-01-12T06:00:00.0000000Z TO 2024-01-18T16:05:39.3354830Z]
```

### Query from IntentionProviderTransactionView by provider, 7 rolling days(Intention limit)

```plain
Deleted:false AND
UserId:+19163015817 AND
ProviderName:GalileoOCTCrossBorder AND
AccountingType:Debit AND
CreatedAt:[2024-01-12T06:00:00.0000000Z TO 2024-01-18T16:05:39.3354830Z]
```

# Limits control for "intentions" or "instructions"

## General Details
There is a oingoing issue that is generating possible frauds risks in some scenarios, due to the fact that the user can have as many as he wants transactions “in transit” / “not completed”.
This causes a lack of control in the limits (both amount and velocity). The limits can be breached due to the fact that the user is not accounted for in the transactions that are not completed
Due to this context, there is a specific type of limit that needs to be controled, outside of the regular cummulative limits for the completed transactions.
The limits of the “not yet completed” transactions, this means, transactions that are not yet completed for whatever reason, also called the “intentions” or “instructions” need to be accumulated separatedly, with their own set of values to control
TBD = current limit intenntions configuration for Live Programs
*   HWAY =
*   RAAS =
Need to be Defined by Business/Product with [Judith Tapia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/61e983f108c1f7006a3a64e1?ref=confluence)

## Raw Requirements
Following the discussion and diagram userd in the raw requierement gathering
*   **Diagram**
    *   [https://app.diagrams.net/#G14xLBKYZrUc-cB7-afVavtCXJ7XmUXSdF](https://app.diagrams.net/#G14xLBKYZrUc-cB7-afVavtCXJ7XmUXSdF)
*   **Video**
    *   
## Technical Details
The platform needs to implement within the Limits Rules a new set of rules for the transactions not yet completed, for each type of money flow available, and each type of payment method available
*   SendFunds Transactions
    *   Send funds trhough p2p (account to account)
    *   Send funds througu card (oct)
*   Transfer out Transactions (TBD if this transactions need to be accounted for)
    *   Transfer out to Bank Account (outgoing ACH)
*   Load Transactions
    *   Load through Card (Digital Wallets as per november 2023)
    *   Load through Bank Account (incoming ACH)
When a transaction is started, as long as it remains in a “not completed/not final” status, this means not one of the following:
*   completed successfully
*   failed
The transaction should be accounted for in the “intention” limits definition, described in this document, for whatever period of time / amount is configured and as long as its status has not reached one of the mentioned above.
When the transaction is completed with any of the above mentioned statuses,
*   The accumulation of sum amount / counted transaction -whatever that applies- will be decreased and the limit reached by the user will be updated with the corresponding decrease, resulting in the user having more available limit for amount or counted transactions and being able to start more transactions.
When the transaction is completed successfully
*   The “actually reached” (current implementation) limits will then be updated, by adding the accumulation of sum amount / counted transaction -whatever that applies -to the corresponding limits counter. This way the limits that the user registers for the completed transactions are updated.

# Relations Limits Handling (2.0)

## General Details
Since the relataion limits was never quite fully implemented in the platform, certain issues were identified and fiexed along the way of the different projects that used this feature (HWAY, RAAS) but it was necessary to address that the different “patches” in place were not enough to cover the the requirement as its full
**The Functional Requirement in abstract is the following**
The Platform must provide a limits and velocity feature to be able to control **how many receivers a sender has,** in a given period of time, and/ or **how many senders a receiver has,** in a given period of time.
If the limit has been reached, the user should be informed properly, and the user will not be able to perform any other transaction with the corresponding relation (either sender or receiver) until the period of time is reached

## Technical Details
*   The relation limits are a specific type of limits control that do not align exactly with the rest of the limits and velocity configuration, hence the relation limits will have to have their own specific configuration, with their specific parameters, separated from the regular configuration of the limits engine. A new configuration for the platform will be created for the relation limits.
*   [Claudia Bolanos](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/557058:4d2023d6-97c0-4108-ba2b-cb62c1b0531c?ref=confluence) / [Christian Galan](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/5e80354d32b0710c1f65caa3?ref=confluence) to add the name and details of the new relation limits configuration. Sample configuration:

```plain
{
    "Nature": "CrossBorder",
    "RuleNature": "ByIntention",
    "Role": "Sender",
    "TimeType": "Rolling",
    "Time": 30,
    "Max": 10
}
```

*   The platform needs to implement the mechanisms to handle this new configuration for relation limits, using the configuration to compare the values
*   The platform will have enhancements to be able to implement the relation limits in the way that the new configuration is used, and the regular limits and velocity configuration details are ignored, specifically when handling the relations limits. For instance, the provider is not an information to be considered in the handling of the relations limits.
*   The important information the relation limits will consider are =
    *   Operation Nature: AccountToAccount, CrossBorder or Domestic
    *   RuleNature: Conventional or ByIntention
    *   User Role: Sender or Recipient
    *   Time lapse
    *   Maximum value

The following list indicates the provider that participate in each of the transaction types in h.way.

| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| P2P Wallet to Wallet | Galileo | Amount | Galileo | Amount |
| Transfer between own accounts (Personal - Saving & vice versa) | Galileo | Amount | Galileo | Amount |

| WalletLoad with DigitalWallet | TabaPay | SenderAmount + FeeAmount | Galileo | SenderAmount + FeeAmount |
| WalletTransferOut with ACH | Galileo | SenderAmount | GalileoACH | SenderAmount |
| SendFunds OCT Domestic | GalileoOCTDomestic | SenderAmount | CyberSourcePayout | Amount |

| Topup | Galileo | SenderAmount | DTOne | SenderAmount |
| Subscription | Galileo | SenderAmount | NA | NA |
| Redeem Rewards | NumiVirtualAccount | SenderAmount | Galileo | SenderAmount |
| Rewards Remittances | NA | NA | NumiVirtualAccount | Amount |
| Rewards by Dosh | NA | NA | NumiVirtualAccount | Amount |

The **sum limit** is calculated by one of the amounts of the operation (SenderAmount, RecipientAmount or Amount). The table indicates which amount is used according Operation type.
Money In
Money deposits, such as external ACH upload, cash load by “Incomm”, roundup to piggy don't count for limits, but are considered for Risk assessment.

# Balance Update Scenarios

The way the Wallet balance is Updated is related to three specific moments:
*   When there´s an external Load/Transfer event
*   In any Send/Request Transaction executed succesfuly
*   When there’s an external event informed By the Card Processor

# Message Screen Interaction (Sub-Flow)

Note: the base information from this page comes from Mingle since August 2018
\[numi/#253\] Attachments in send and request
## Specifications
*   During the operation flows (Send, Request, Buy), a _Message_ screen will be presented, following the _How Much_ screen, consisting of:
    *   The contact icon (with name initials) and full name, at the center
    *   A row of action buttons, one for each type of message to attach
    *   A text field for entering a reason (labeled _For:_) 
*   The user can enter a reason in the text field, and attach a file to the operation, by tapping on any of the attachment type buttons in the screen .
    *   An image, by selecting an existing image on the device. The app will rely on the system built-in image picker (i.e. gallery).
    *   A capture, by activating the device camera, after which the user can snap a photo.
    *   An animated GIF, by selecting one of a list of predefined animations, by using a carousel-type selector \[design team to provide assets\].
*   The app will upload the selected attachment, alongside the operation, so that it can be reachable by an URL, by either user involved in the operation.
*   The system will include the attachment, in email communications \[ALL?\] related to the operation.
*   Within the Operation Details screen, an action button at the bottom will allow the user to open the attached file for that operation. \[Why different icon/s?\]
## UX Flow Detail: 
[UX Flows MX](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SAC/pages/45982000/UX+Flows+MX)

###

# CIP (Customer Identification Process)

## General Details
The customer identification process is the process used to verify that a user's identity is true and perform legal validations that are going to differ according to the Country where the process needs to be held on
The current implementation lays on the exchange of communication with a third party that will provide the verification for the user's phone number,  address, and the given identification form, according to what is needed in this aspects for each country regulatory system. In that sense, the platform will be able to connect to diverse third parties for diverse countries.
The platform implements the means to keep the user's records safe, in external security vaults (provided by a different third party than the ones stated in the previous paragraph) and encripting the information when applies.
The customer identification process contains the following features:
*   **Telephone Verification:**  using the information provided by different third parties the customer’s phone number is analyzed and validated to ensure:
    *   _Validation_: to allow to confirm that the phone number is active, a mobile phone, a valid number, etcetera
    *   _Verification_: to allow to confirm the customer is associated to that phone number
*   **Address Validation:** to ensure the customer provided a valid and legally allowed address, the platform connects to different third parties that provide the validity, longevity, and characteristics of the given address, and performs and analysis of the results
*   **Identity validation:**  executing a set of rules depending on the regulatory mandate of the different countries, accessing different services with the information of the user, the Numi Platforms performs a logic to validate whether the identity data given by the customer,  gathers or not the conditions to be given access to the full capabilities of the Platform.
*   **Document scanning** executing a third party service to allow the document scanning and the identification of the information of the image validating format/ data of the image itself, and then extracting the necessary information to handle and validate the user identification information
*   **Black List Validation** executing a third party service to validate the information of the user against the given country black lists and if available performing analysis of the results score to either deny the service (hard fail) to allow the user to review its data and retry (soft fail). Also the black list validation is stored in the compliance console for further analysis of a compliance officer
*    **Incremental Onboarding** The mobile application has the ability to allow the user to enter data as necessary for the type of action he wishes to do. This can be either enabled or disabled in the platform. For SACBE implementation the incremental onboarding is currently disabled
*    **CURP validation** For Mexico implementations, the platform provides connects to external services providing the specific data of the user to obtain a valid CURP.
