## Legacy Documentation
> CIP For US  
> The following information is pasted from Mingle  
> **The CIP practice, which is enforced since the Patriot Act of 2001, is intended to enable the bank to form a reasonable belief that it knows the true identity of each customer.**  
>   
> The CIP must include account opening procedures that specify the identifying information that will be obtained from each customer. It must also include reasonable and practical risk-based procedures for verifying the identity of each customer. Banks should conduct a risk assessment of their customer base and product offerings, and in determining the risks, consider:  
> The CIP must be incorporated into the bank's BSA/AML compliance program, which is subject to approval by the bank's board of directors.  
> The CIP rule applies to a “customer.” A customer is a “person” (an individual, a corporation, partnership, a trust, an estate, or any other entity recognized as a legal person) who opens a new account, an individual who opens a new account for another individual who lacks legal capacity, and an individual who opens a new account for an entity that is not a legal person (e.g., a civic club). A customer does not include a person who does not receive banking services, such as a person whose loan application is denied. The definition of “customer” also does not include an existing customer as long as the bank has a reasonable belief that it knows the customer’s true identity.  
> At a minimum, the bank must obtain the following identifying information from each customer before opening the account:  
> Name.  
> Date of birth, for individuals.  
> Address:  
> For an individual: a residential or business street address, or if the individual does not have such an address, an Army Post Office (APO) or Fleet Post Office (FPO) box number, the residential or business street address of next of kin or of another contact individual, or a description of the customer’s physical location.  
> Identification number:  
> An identification number for a U.S. person is a taxpayer identification number (TIN) (or evidence of an application for one), and an identification number for a non-U.S. person is one or more of the following: a TIN; a passport number and country of issuance; an alien identification card number; or a number and country of issuance of any other unexpired government-issued document evidencing nationality or residence and bearing a photograph or similar safeguard.  
> Based on its risk assessment, a bank may require identifying information in addition to the items above for certain customers or product lines.  
>   
> Customer Verification  
> The CIP must contain risk-based procedures for verifying the identity of the customer within a reasonable period of time after the account is opened.  
> The verification procedures must use the identifying information obtained by the bank.  
> A bank does not need to establish the accuracy of every element of identifying information obtained, but it must verify enough information to form a reasonable belief that it knows the true identity of the customer. The bank’s procedures must describe when it will use documents, non-documentary methods, or a combination of both.  
>   
> Lack of Verification  
> The CIP must also have procedures for circumstances in which the bank cannot form a reasonable belief that it knows the true identity of the customer.  
> These procedures should describe:  
> Circumstances in which the bank should not open an account.  
> The terms under which a customer may use an account while the bank attempts to verify the customer’s identity.  
> When the bank should close an account, after attempts to verify a customer’s identity have failed.  
> When the bank should file a SAR in accordance with applicable law and regulation.  
>   
> Recordkeeping Requirements and Retention  
> A bank’s CIP must include recordkeeping procedures. At a minimum, the bank must retain the identifying information (name, address, date of birth for an individual, TIN, and any other information required by the CIP) obtained at account opening for a period of five years after the account is closed.  
> The bank must also keep a description of the following for five years after the record was made:  
> Any document that was relied on to verify identity, noting the type of document, the identification number, the place of issuance, and, if any, the date of issuance and expiration date.  
>   
> Source: [https://www.fdic.gov/regulations/examinations/bsa/ffiec\_cip.pdf](https://www.fdic.gov/regulations/examinations/bsa/ffiec_cip.pdf)  
>   
> Common questions  
> **What address should be obtained for customers who live in rural areas who do not have a residential or business address or the residential or business address of next of kin or another contact individual? For example, is a rural route number acceptable?**  
> Yes, the number on the roadside mailbox on a rural route is acceptable as an address. A rural route number, unlike a post office box number, is a description of the approximate area where the customer can be located. _In the absence of such a number, and in the absence of a residential or business address for next of kin or another contact individual, a description of the customer’s physical location will suffice._ (January 2004)   
>   
> **Can a bank use an employee identification card as the sole means to verify a customer’s identity?**  
> A bank using documentary methods to verify a customer’s identity must have procedures that set forth the documents that the bank will use. The CIP rule gives examples of types of documents that have long been considered primary sources of identification and reflects the Agencies’ expectation that banks will obtain government-issued identification from most customers. _However, other forms of identification may be used if they enable the bank to form a reasonable belief that it knows the true identity of the customer. Nonetheless, given the availability of counterfeit and fraudulently obtained documents, a bank is encouraged to obtain more than a single document to ensure that it has a reasonable belief that it knows the customer’s true identity. (January 2004)_   
>   
> **Can a bank use an electronic credential, such as a digital certificate, as a non-documentary means to verify the identity of a customer that opens an account over the Internet or through some other purely electronic channel?**  
> _A bank may obtain an electronic credential_, such as a digital certificate, as one of the methods it uses to verify a customer’s identity. _However, the CIP rule requires the bank to have a reasonable belief that it knows the true identity of the customer_. Therefore, for example, the bank is responsible for ensuring that the third party uses the same level of authentication as the bank itself would use. See also FFIEC guidance titled “Authentication in an Electronic Banking Environment” (July 30, 2001). (January 2004)

# Platform CIP Overal Process

The following steps detail the overall process for the CIP feature in the platform.
Please note per the last edit Date, The steps marked in orange are pending implementation.
_\>>> The magenta color indicates ongoing updates on this page_
## Preconditions:
*   A Person installs the Application and for certain actions it does not need to either have credentials or information 
*   From the moment he person needs to execute certain actions that require credentials or identification information the user will be gaining "levels" of access within the Platform
*   The Levels correspond to the amount of identity information the system has about a user, and for the amount of actions available depending on that information
*   The first time the person signs in the platform the basic information gathered in the user´s account will comply with the first level of access in the platform
*   The base level for a user is depicted in the platform  Level 0 (Zero)
*   The Level 0 does not necessarily comply to any CIP regulation, usually known as KYC, but they do fulfil the first step for the Internal CIP for the System
*   The data included in the Level 0 and the internal CIP are
    *   User Name
    *   Phone Number
        *   _Note: the phone number is subject of a specific validation provided by a third party that will hold a relation with the telephone companies to allow to verify that the phone number is valid, and is currently active_
*   At this point, the Platform will also perform, internally, the binding of the registered user to the device he is using (e.g. SIM card data), providing by this way, the data to enforce, when necessary the [2nd Authentication Factor](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SAC/pages/45981869/2nd+Authentication+Factor%3A+Something+you+have)
## CIP Steps
*   The first step for the _actual_ CIP process is verify the Email and the Address for the user
    *   This will lead the app to verify if the user has the proper Level to include this information to his profile, or to _take_ the user to that level if necessary
    *   The Level of access for the user that _includes_ a valid Email and Address is Level 1
    *   The Application will request the user to enter the Email
    *   The verification of the Email, contains the following criteria
        *   The general format validation of the email
        *   The validation that the email actually exists
    *   The Application will request the user to enter the address
    *   The verification of the Address, contains the following criteria
        *   The general validation of the Format
        *   The validation that the address actually _exists (this is, the geolocation service finds a real location point with this data)_
    *   At this point, with the completed User Data:
        *    The OFAC check will be executed (with the user full Name): The OFAC validation is executed against Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-23237](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-23237))to ensure the user is not providing false information of information that is bound to criminal activities
        *   The Validation with the internal records to ensure that none of the Data belongs to a Blocked User.
    *   If the user completes the data registration and its information is valid through this point of the Process, then the **Level 1** will be achieved.
    *   Then the application **may** require to continue to validate to the next data to achieve Level 2, or allow the user to continue with Level 1
        *   Depending on the action to be performed by the user, the **Level check** will be executed, and if such achieved Level is not enough for the action, then the user will be taken again to the **Level Upgrade** (completion of its data) 
*   The second step is the verification of the user's ID
    *   This specific verification may _vary Private (_[_https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-22117_](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-22117)_)_
        *   _The App will request the information of the user's ID based on the country rules_
            *   _For US Citizens, the SSN Number needs to be provided as an Input Text_
            *   _For Non-US Citizens, the App will request_
                *   _the National ID_
                *   Drivers Licence
                *   Green Card
                *   Other
            *   _If the SSN is invalid the App may request for an extra form of ID for US Citizens_
        *   _The App needs to validate that the document is properly scanned_
        *   _The Data will be extracted of the Scanned ID_
        *   There will be an analysis of the data of the SCANNED ID, 
            *   To Be defined USA
            *   In some implementations Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-26037](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-26037)) for IFE / INE for Incluvida (MX Citizens)
        *   If requested in this process, the Images of the documents provided by the user will be secured in VGS Vault
        *   At this point in the current implementations, if the ID undgergoes the validations successfully, the user will get its **Level 2** created
*   The third instance of the verification, when necessary, will be providing some sort of proof of residency such as a Bill with the address and the name of the user
    *   _Missing Definiotions pending for Compliance for Proof of Residence_
*   _The fourth instance of the verification, when necessary, will be providing some sort of proof of income, such as Sales Balances of a Credit Card_
### Account Creation after CIP
Depending on the configuration of the implementation, the user might get its account created
This configuration is the type of “automatic acount creation” defined, that can be
*   automatic
*   semi-automatic
*   non-automatic
Review = [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/744030209/Numi+Wallet#The-Creation-of-the-Account-and-the-Creation-of-the-Card](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/744030209/Numi+Wallet#The-Creation-of-the-Account-and-the-Creation-of-the-Card)
### CIP Soft Fail Scenarios
In the following list you will find the scenarios where the application needs to provide a "soft fail" this is, the means for the user to provide more information to be able to try again to continue with the CIP process. In each entry of the list, is also detailed, if applicable, the depending scenarios 
1. Invalid Email
2. Invalid Address
3. Invalid Document (Typed or Scanned) _Note: depending on the country rules the document can be typed/scanned or just scanned_
    1. Proof of ID needed
    2. Unreadable ID
    3. Enter New ID (entered another ID before)
4. Proof of Residence (PR) Needed
    1. Unreadable Proof of Residence
    2. Enter New Proof of Residence
5. Proof of Income (PI) Needed
    1. Unreadable Proof of Income
    2. Enter New Proof of Income
    3. 
### CIP Diagrams

**1) Overall CIP Process Flow / User Enrollment Levels involved**

**2) Overall Process Flow to Soft Fail / Hard Fail** 

**3) Backend Flow to Soft Fail / Hard Fail**

**4) CIP on - HOLD**

See the following google folder for the complete list of Numi CIP Diagrams

# Incremental Levels of Signup

## General Details
The Baseline Numi App contains a feature to allow different levels of uses, called incremental levels.
SACBE implementation will use this baseline feature with some enhancements to allow to identify the user as an Affiliated User (see: [Users Considerations (Remittances Model)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SAC/pages/45982003))
The detail of the incremental level process is provided below:
## Specifications
### Note: The information below is pasted from Mingle
*   The platform will define several _user levels_ to which a potential user can apply and belong; from the most basic (initial default), up to the most complete.
    *   For the first release, the following are defined (with their required fields):
        *   **_Level 0 - Light_**: _first name, last name, mobile number, email address_
        *   **_Level 1 - Address_**: _Level 1 +home address, date of birth, gender_
        *   **_Level 2 – Full_**: _Level 2_ + a form of _legal ID_. (either _taxpayer identification number_ or a _scanned id. image_, for US residents; _scanned id. image_ for all other regions)
*   Certain types of operations offered by the platform will only be available for _registered users_ of a certain _user level_.
    *   For new users to be able to carry out such action, the app will first inform them the need to be registered, taking them to the proper _sign-up_ flow (see details below).
    *   For existing users to be able to carry out such action, the app will first inform them the need to provide additional information, taking them to the proper _sign-up_ flow (see details below).
*   The app will implement a _sign-up_ flow, consisting of a sequence of levels, each with a sequence of screens, that collect user information (e.g. a _Level 0_ with 2 screens, a _Level 1_ with 2 screens, a _Level 2_ with 4 screens, and so on).
*   For anonymous users initiating a _sign-up_ (by manually tapping on a _sign-up_ button), the app will make them complete the _entry-level sign-up_ flow (_Level 0_) only.
*   For anonymous users attempting to initiate an operation, the app will make them complete the _sign-up_ flow, up to, and including, the level required for such operation (see details below).
*   For existing users attempting to initiate an operation, that requires a level higher than the one they are on, the app will make them complete the _sign-up_ flow, up to and including such required level (see below).
*   When users must upgrade, or sign-up, targeting a certain _user level_, the _sign-up_ flow will take them over all the screens, from all the necessary _user levels_, sequentially (e.g. if the user must upgrade from _Level 0_ to _Level 2_, the flow will consist of the screens from Level 1, followed by the screens from Level 2, in the right order), and the whole operation will be processed, at the end of the flow, as a single transaction. 
*   
> _?Note: ideally the platform should implement this feature as configurable as possible, so that future products and features, can be set up to require specific levels or personal info. items_
### Graphics
### [MTO User Identification and Sign Up](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SAC/pages/45981964/MTO+User+Identification+and+Sign+Up)
###   

### Basic scenarios
*   For new users requesting sign-up (i.e. tapping on a “_sign-up”_ button), the app will make them complete a “_Level 0_” sign-up.
*   For new users attempting to “_buy mobile airtime_” for any recipient, the app will make them complete a “_Level 0_” sign-up.
*   For new users attempting to “_request mobile airtime_” to any sender, the app will make them complete a “_Level 0_” sign-up.
*   For new users attempting to “_send funds_” to a debit card or mobile wallet, the app will make them apply for a mobile wallet (i.e. numi card) first, by completing a “_Level 0 – 1 – 2_” sign-up.
*   For new users attempting to “_send funds_” or “_request funds_” to be received in a mobile wallet, the app will make them apply for a mobile wallet (i.e. numi card) first, by completing a “_Level 0 – 1 – 2_” sign-up.
*   For new users attempting to “_send funds_” or “_request funds_”, to be received in a debit card, the app will make them complete a “_Level 0 – 1_” sign-up.

*   For existing users attempting to “_buy or request mobile airtime_”, the initial _Level 0_ is sufficient; no upgrade needed.
*   For existing users attempting to “_send funds_” to a debit card or mobile wallet, **if not at least on “****_Level 2_****”,** the app will make them apply for a mobile wallet (i.e. numi card) first, by completing a “_Level 2_” upgrade.
*   For existing users attempting to “_receive funds_” or “_request funds_” to be received in a debit card, **if not at least on “****_Level 1_****”,** the app will make them complete a “_Level 1_” upgrade.
*   For existing users attempting to “_receive funds_” or “_request funds_” to be received in a mobile wallet, **if not at least on “****_Level 2_****”,** the app will make them apply for a mobile wallet (i.e. numi card) first, by completing a “_Level 1 – 2_” upgrade (if not on _Level 1_), or a “_Level 2_” upgrade (if on _Level 1_).

**End of Information Pasted From Mingle**
## **The information below may change due to some negotiations with Compliance Department to avoid adding unnecesary interaction if the data to gather is actually optional (gathered for statistics and not for CIP)**
## User Levels for SACBE
According to specific compliance regulation for the Money Remittances Business, in order to avoid crimes such as money laundry, there is certain information that needs to exists to validate the user to perform a transaction.
Since the information is more than the current information requested by the User Levels handled in the Numi Baseline there is the need to apply new User Levels for the Money Remittances implemented by SACBE
This new levels "add" information to the currently existing levels in Numi Baseline, therefor they are deeper levels (higher number) starting from the existing ones
The information required according to each level is the following;
*   **_Level 3 - NAME - Level 2_** _\+ country of birh, date of birth,_ Nationality_, geolocation of the mobile phone_ 
*   **_Level 4 - NAME - Level 3 +_** _Identification Copy_ **_(Review; this is achieved in level 2 of Numi baseline, Is this level is achieved automatically without user intervention?)_**
*   **_Level 5 - Full File - Level 4 +_** _State/Province of Birth + Own Resources Declaration, must include information such as Business Type and/or Activity /Occupation + Electronic Signature_
    *   _For Mexico Users= need to add  "Clave de Elector" at this level_
    *   _Review = See other countries_

# Sign up for Users created externally

## Note: the following is pasted from Mingle
## Specifications
*   Certain users will be registered in the platform, by certain authorized external systems, that do not interact with the end user directly.
    *   e.g. _users generated by affiliates, during marketing campaigns._
*   Such users will be created in the system, with the least amount of information possible (i.e. mobile phone), and with a system-generated security PIN (undisclosed to the user).
*   Furthermore, the system will flag those users internally, so that at a later time, they can be detected to have been system-generated.
*   At a certain point, the user could decide to install and _Log-in_ to the app by using the mobile phone previously registered by the external system (see above). In that scenario, the _sign-up_ flow will behave in the following manner:
    *   The user will initiate the app. _sign-up_ process, as usual.
    *   After entering their mobile phone number, the system will detect that such mobile phone already exists in the system, showing a screen indicating just that (see [#275](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/275)).
    *   From that screen, the user can either choose to try to _Sign-Up_ (see [#147](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/147)) or else try to regain access by issuing a _Reset PIN_ (see [#268](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/268)). Tapping either will launch the corresponding flows, as already defined.
    *   After successfully completing either flow (_Log-in_ with existing known credentials, or _Reset PIN_) the app will resume whatever flow was being conducted, just when the user attempted to sign-up with their mobile phone.

# Reset Pin

## Note: the following is pasted from Mingle
## Specifications
1. The app will facilitate an action button labeled “_Forgot PIN?_” on the _Login_ screen, that will initiate this flow.
2. First, an initial screen will be displayed, requesting the user to enter their mobile phone number used during sign-up (see [#273](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/cards/273)). If a phone was entered on _Login_ (1), it will be pre-filled on this screen_._
3. Upon tapping _Next_, the system will generate and store, a _temporary unique 6-digit verification code_, that will be sent to the user’s mobile phone via SMS.
4. Next, the app will present a screen, requesting the user to enter the verification code received via SMS. In addition, the user will be allowed to request the code to be re-sent, by tapping on an action label called “_Re-send code_”.
    1. If the user enters an invalid code, an error message screen will be presented, that will also allow the user to request the code to be re-sent, by tapping on an action label called “_Re-send code_”.
5. If the user enters a valid code, the app will present a final screen, requesting the user to choose a definitive PIN and confirm it, after which it will invoke a command on the server, to commit the PIN change against the user account.
6. After a successful PIN change, the user will get automatically logged in, and the app will resume whichever flow initiated the _Reset PIN_ feature.

# Branding and Affiliates

## Historic Information
(the following is pasted from mingle)
> CO BRANDING AND AFFILIATES - Narrative  
> As a monies platform, I will engage with one or more business partners, (i.e. “**_Affiliates_**”), with the goal of boosting sales and user conversions; in exchange for a revenue share.  
> By doing so, the system will enable channels, to be used by these affiliates, so that they can request _new user enrolments_, new operations (_send, request, load, transfer out_), and potentially any other feature that the platform releases.  
> The system will track users and operations brought up by those affiliates, and it will calculate commissions on them via certain defined business rules.  
> Finally, certain special affiliates will benefit from a special ‘branded’ mobile app, that will adapt their look and feel, to reflect the brand associated to the partner.  
>   
> Branded app for special affiliates   
> Narrative  
> As a platform, I will offer the possibility to certain _affiliate partners_, to make the mobile app look, feel and behave, in a way that matches their brand image.  
>  Specifications  
> Each app installation will detect the cases in which it will be opened via a partner/affiliate channel (e.g. marketing campaign, a link sent via SMS or email, etc.).  
> The app will preserve the _affiliate Id_ value from such deep link (representing the affiliate), and it will keep it as a global setting on that installed instance; from then on, the app will include this **affiliate Id** value in a newly onboarded user (i.e. sign-up), and in all new operations, initiated by this user_._ In this way, the system will be able to track all users and operations, associated with that originating affiliate.  
> The app will determine, by querying an _Affiliates_ synced. table, whether the configured _affiliate_ is of type: _Cobranded_.  
> If so, the UI/UX will apply a custom look and feel (“_theme_”), seamless for the user, designed for that specific affiliate (including custom onboarding screens, view layouts, multimedia assets, string resources, copy, external URLs, etc.).  
> These “_themes_” associated to affiliates, will be bundled as part of the app distributable. _Consequently, adding, modifying or removing custom themes will imply publishing a new app version_.  
>    
> Support affiliate partners in the platform   
> Narrative  
> As a platform, I will engage with one or more business partners, (i.e. “_Affiliates_”), with the goal of boosting sales and user conversions; in exchange for a revenue share.  
> By doing so, the system will enable channels, to be used by these affiliates, so that they can request _new user enrolments_, new operations (_send, request, load, transfer out_), and potentially any other feature that the platform delivers.  
>    
> Specification  
> The system will implement a new entity “_Affiliates_”, to be stored in the DB. At a minimum, each entry should define:  
> **Affiliate ID (internal ID, unique across the system)**  
> **Name – text – REQUIRED**  
> **Contract Start – date/time – REQUIRED**  
> **Contract End – date/time – OPTIONAL**  
> **Contract Type – option – REQUIRED**  
> **Standard**  
> **Cobranded**  
> **Card Design Code – text – OPTIONAL**  
> **Card Programs \[ … an array of Card Program … \] – OPTIONAL**  
> **Card Program {**  
> **Type – option – REQUIRED**  
> **Level 1**  
> **Level 2**  
> **Level 3**  
> **Program Id: text – REQUIRED**  
> The structure will be maintained on the server db., and it will be replicated to all client apps. via a synchronized table.  
> The following entities: _Users, Operations_, will include additional fields (optional), that should be carried around, passing thru all of the data flows (app, backend, DB, logs, etc.)  
> _Affiliate Id_ – (optional) pointer to a row on Affiliates  
> _Affiliate Data\[ key, value \]_ – (optional) a dictionary of string key-value pairs, with no semantic, to be used by the affiliate for storing information related to such user/operation.  
>   
> Custom notifications by affiliate   
> Narrative  
> As a platform, I will enable configuring certain affiliates, with a customized set of email templates, so those can closely match their brand image.  
>  Specifications  
> The backend will rely on a baseline set of customer email templates (assets included), to be used when composing outgoing customer emails (both marketing and transactional).  
> The system will allow certain affiliates, defined in the database (☛ see section above), to have custom email templates configured. If such flag is enabled, the backend will load the correspondent custom set of templates, that will be available alongside the rest (assets included).  
>

# Manage Saved Payment Methods (My Accounts)

## Note: the following is pasted from Mingle
Specifications
*   Whenever the user needs to create and enter a new external account, a special flow will be presented. This includes:
    *   Selecting _Add New Account_ option, from the _Source of Funding_ selector screen, and tapping _NEXT_.
    *   Selecting _Add new account_ option, from the _Profile > My Accounts_ screen.
*   The flow will first present a list of _enabled payment methods_ (i.e. account types), for the current user. The list of enabled methods will be configured per country of origin.
    *   For the scope of the _numi top-up_ release, methods will be as follows: _Debit Card, Credit Card, Bank Account_ (US), _Debit Card, Credit Card_ (rest of the world).
*   Each option will be presented in a “card” layout, with the name, at the lower bottom, and the applicable fee, below it (either a fixed fee or a variable fee). If there is no fee applicable, the app should still leave that explicit (i.e. Fee 0.00 USD).
*   After tapping on either option, the app will initiate the proper flow to add an account of such type, as defined.

# New Atribution Links

## General Details
the atrtribution links feature implemented currently in the platform has become obsolete
The handling of the attribution features have come to a point that they need to evolve to be able to provide better and deeper information for the marketing and business usage

Requirement Gathering
## Technical Details
*   The new atrribution links feature is currently under development as per August 2022
*   There’s a third party involved in the attribution link generation called Kochava, see ➡Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-23517](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-23517))
*   The Marketing and Business tools that analyze the data and provide insight to generate business desitions and marketing campaigns, need to be included in the ecosystem of the feature to be able to feed information to those tools.
*   These tools are so far included in a tool called HubSpot
    *   The data stored for the user and its device in the platform needs to be refined to be able to pass this information in Eventso to HubSpot

## Requirement Gathering Meetigs

# Legacy Attribution Links / Deep Linking

## General Description

The Attribution Links are a legacy feature from Sales Factor Baseline that allows to create links with information about attributions,  this is, parameters that can be analyzed to connect to the applications with previously "loaded" information  (e.g. information for Attributes for Affiliates, see Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-21597](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-21597))). This feature is given to be used, for example,  in email campaigns that will allow the recipients of the campaign to access the application with the given link, by downloading it and installing it from the link.
The deep linking feature is a variant from the attribution link, that will contain extra information to be processed mostly into a transaction that needs to be accepted/confirmed/actioned within the application.
The most common example of a deep link is the one generated from within the application when a user starts a transaction. On the other side, the user that receives the link, will be invited to proceed with the download and installation of the application, or to sign in (either one that applies for new/existent user ). After that, the information contained in the link will be processed, to display the data of the transaction to the user and request an acknowledge, a confirmation, or to take action with further steps into the transaction life cycle.

## Configuration
To configure an attribution link an administrator should create the link itself with some basic parameters from the Leap Central Console
<<<<< section to be completed <<<<<<<

# Platform Fees

## General Detail
Numi charges certain fees, for running fund operations thru the platform.
The fees will vary, depending on the corridor (source country, destination country), the funding method and the destination payment method, 
In addition, the fee can be payed entirely by the sender or entirely by the recipient
> _As a Legacy feature the fee was allowed to be split between the two (50/50)._
##   

## Fees Configuration
The fees need to be configured in the Corridors of Each Implementation
See: Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-21437](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-21437))
##   

## Payment Module Fees
When the Fees are to be calculated for the Platform Payment Module (e.g. when the user charges another user for its products or services) **_The Fees will always be payed by the Merchant User, this is, the user that is receiving a Payment_**
### Example Calculations
see the following sheets for the example calculations
## Legacy Details
_Note, the following is Legacy from Mingle Documentation Site_
_Last Updated September 2018_
## Who pays the fee
The user initiating the operation, can choose how the fee will be charged:
*   “**Sender pays**”: the sender will be charged for the sum of original amount, **plus** the total fee amount; the recipient will be payed for the original amount.
    *   e.g. Amount: 20.00; Total fee: 1.99. Sender is charged 21.99, recipient gets 20.00
*   “**Recipient pays**”: the sender will be charged for the original amount; the recipient will be payed the net amount, minus the deducted total fee amount.
    *   e.g. Amount: 20.00; Total fee: 1.99. Sender is charged 20.00, recipient gets 18.01
*   “**Split fee**”: the sender will be charged for the original amount to send, plus half the total fee amount; the recipient will be payed for the original amount, **minus** half the total fee amount.
    *   e.g. Amount: 20.00; Total fee: 1.99. Sender is charged 21.00, recipient gets 19.01 (the fee is rounded up for the sender part)

## **How is the fee comprised**
The total operation fee is comprised of the sum of three components:
*   **Funding fee**: the fee associated with the funding method.
*   **Transaction fee**: the fee charged for operating with the platform (as a whole).
*   **Destination fee**: the fee associated with the destination payment method.

## **Implementation details (app)**
The app will look up the appropriate fee(s), on a master table, maintained and broadcasted by, the server.
The table will contain a set of rows, with the appropriate fees to charge, depending on the corridor (source country, destination country, source payment method, destination payment method).
The app will filter this table by those four variables (empty country catches all), and when more than one matches, the more specific one takes precedence.
### Remarks
*   Any entry with an **empty** **source country value (null)** should match **_any source country_** **(wildcard)**
*   Any entry with an **empty destination country value (null)** should match **_any destination country_** **(wildcard)**
*   Any **empty destination method value (null)** should be assumed as “**_my wallet_**”, i.e. indicating an enabled **_wallet load method_**.
*   Any **empty source method value (null)** should be assumed as “**_my wallet_**”, i.e. indicating an enabled **_wallet transfer out method_**.

## Example
**_Rules:_**

| **Src. method**<br> | **Src. country**<br> | **Dest. method**<br> | **Dest. country**<br> | **Src fee**<br> | **Trx fee**<br> | **Dest fee**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Debit card | US | Wallet | US | 0 | 1.99 | 0 |
| Debit card | US | Debit card | US | 0 | 1.99 | 0 |
| Debit card | US | Debit card | (empty) | 0 | 5.99 | 0 |
| (empty) | US | Bank account | US | 0 | 0 | 0.99 |

To send funds, from US, to US, from debit card to debit card, the correct fee is row [#2](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/2), since that rule is the most specific one.
To send funds, from US to any other country, from debit card to debit card, the correct fee is row [#3](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/3), since that rule is the only one that matches (empty country matches all countries).
To transfer out funds (US), to a bank account, the matching row is [#4](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/4), so the fee to charge is _0.99_.

# Fund With Bank Account

## General Details
To be able to Fund a Wallet with Bank Account the process depends on the Country
*   **_For USA : ACH_**
    *   The ACH (automated clearing house) is a standard banking process that allowsto send or receive money from external bank accounts
*   **_For Mexico :SPEI_**
    *   The SPEI “highway” is a protocol that allow the transfer “inter-banking” using a unique identifier for bank accounts in mexico called CLABE
## Technical Details
### Implementation Details for ACH
IMPORTANT **The following details apply for SACBE per February 2021. Theres on ACH flow for any other implementation**
The Provider to either Debit to a Bank Account from the Wallet or Credit from the Bank Account to the Wallet is the Card Processor Galileo
The Way the Platform handles the ACH with Galileo is decribed as a step by step below:
*   **Load to Wallet**
    *   Call the addAchAccount endpoint
    *   In the Response, use the field achAccount ID to get the New ID for the associated achAccount
    *   Call the createAChTransaction
    *   set the request with
    *   achaccountID = New Id
    *   creditDebitIndicator =D
    *   When the payment is processed Galileo will send an Event = BPMT AC
    *   The Open to Buy is updated by Galileo
    *   The event will provide the accountNo and the transction ID in the field "ext\_trans\_id" and then if it is type AC it also has an "ach\_trans\_id"
    *   The Platform will look for the Transaction ID, and update the Status to Success
    *   The platform will look for the accountNo and update the balance
    *   **When the Transaction created by a createAchTransaction Debit Credit Indicator C- Fails:**
        *   The ACHR event will be triggered (when an external account tries to take money off of Galileo and Galileo denies it).
*   **Transfer Out from Wallet to external Bank Account**
    *   Call the addAchAccount endpoint
    *   In the Response, use the field achAccount ID to get the New ID for the associated achAccount
    *   Call the createAChTransaction
    *   set the request with
    *   achaccountID = New Id
    *   creditDebitIndicator =C
    *   from the response get the “open to buy” updated by Galileo
    *   The Event BADJ is triggered by Galileo
*   **When the Transaction created by a createAchTransaction Debit Credit Indicator C- Fails**
    *   The ACHC, when there is a request to credit the Galileo account and that is denied by Galileo
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/8572428d-a1bb-4c2b-bc87-c8c2a4f737d4/image-20210203-205146.png)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/7c9a640c-6bd8-47e5-a22e-b64bfcdbda22/image-20210318-233234.png)

### Implementation Detals fro SPEI
*   Service for SACBE see: (complete with link)
*   Service for Tzune see: (complete with link)

# Add a bank account as a payment method on the user profile
(**v37** - Latest version, last modified about 3 years ago)
 **Qualified**
###  Dependencies
## **Narrative**
As a monies user, I will be able to add my external bank account to my profile, so I can use it on the platform, including:
*   use it as a source of funding to send or fund requests
*   use it to load funds into my monies card
*   use it to transfer out funds of my monies card
The app will enable the bank account feature, depending on the user’s country of origin (configurable rule via a synced. table maintained by the server, see ☛ [#334](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/334)).
_For this release, and until overriden by a new requirement, only US-based users will have “bank account” available for the scenarios described above (funding source option, load option, transfer out option, payment method type on profile)._
When adding a new payment/funding method to the profile, after selecting “Bank Account” as the account type, the user will be taken to a special flow designed for this purpose.
Such flow will be presented as as a contained web application, hosted by a third party vendor (**Plaid**, pronounced “Plad” – [www.plaid.com](http://www.plaid.com/)).
    Demo: [https://plaid.com/demo/](https://plaid.com/demo/) 

The app will present this custom flow as an _embedded full-screen web view_, in a way that the user experience feels as much contained as possible (see UX definition below for reference ☛ [#319](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/319)).
The custom provider flow will prompt the user to:
1. first, select the bank.
2. enter the online banking credentials.
3. (optionally) complete any secondary authentication method(s) enforced by such bank (e.g. SMS verification, personal questions, token, etc.).
4. (optionally) select an account, if more than one is available.
The app will capture the account selection event, so that, when the user completes the flow, the app will receive back a token that represents the login session to the bank (called **{****_public\_token_****}**).
In the final step, the app will upload such token to monies **_Payment Validation Service_** _(dependency:_ ☛ [_#327_](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/monies/cards/327)_)._
The service will return a data structure, representing the bank account information, that will be stored on the payment method data structure, so it can be carried along to the server.
This includes the account friendly name (to be stored on [**PaymentMethod.Name**](http://PaymentMethod.Name)), and the encrypted data (to be stored in the field **PaymentMethod.TokenData**).
_This logic is comparable to the action of adding a debit or credit card to the user profile._

## **Additional Information**
*   Refer to the provider documentation, guides and sample code to embed the custom flow inside a web view (_note: this code sample is targeted at native Droid/iOS, adaptation should be done to include it on the app_)
    *   [https://plaid.com/docs/api/#webview-integration](https://plaid.com/docs/api/#webview-integration)
*   In order to operate with **Plaid**, some keys must be provided (these are constant for monies, and shared amongst all environments).
    *   Consult with your **technical manager** (master credential spreadsheet)
*   Make sure to configure the app to use the correct provider environment, depending on the app build/version _(i.e. use “sandbox” for the dev/QA builds, use “production” for the production build)_

# Bank Account as Payment Method

_Note:_
_the following information is pasted from Old Legacy Mingle site, should be taken into consideration that many information here might be outdated or not appropiate_

# Process bank account data via the payment validation service
(**v26** - Latest version, last modified about 3 years ago)
 Qualified
###  Dependencies
## Narrative
As a monies **Payment Method Validation service**, I will be adapted so I can support the “bank account” payment method, in addition to debit and credit cards.
> _The purpose of this service, is to support the app, during the process of adding new payment methods to the user profile. Since this involves handling sensitive data, the service helps by ensuring data protection and privacy._
The service will be extended so it can handle requests for an additional payment method type, in this case the type “bank account”.

### Logic for Bank Account requests
*   When a request of type “BankAccount” is received, along with a **{public\_token}**, the service will invoke the bank account authorization provider (**Plaid**, ☛ [www.plaid.com](http://www.plaid.com/)), to exchange such temporary token, for permanent tokens (**{access\_token}** and **{item\_id})**
*   _(see next_ **_section (a)_** _for instructions)._
*   Next, it will invoke another provider api, presenting this **{access\_token}**, plus the proper provider keys, in order to get the actual bank numbers (**routing and checking number**)
*   _(see next_ **_section (b)_** _for instructions)._
*   Next, the service will build the **account friendly name**, following this algorithm:
*   **_abbreviated bank name + “-” + last four digits of the account number (as returned by the provider)._**
    *   _​_To calculate the abbreviated name, the logic is as follows:
    *   if the name does **_not_** begin with: BANK, FEDERAL, NATIONAL, then, take the _first five characters_; otherwise, _take the first letter of each word_.
    *   _Examples:_
    *       _BoA-2234           (_**_B_**_ank_ **_o_**_f_ **_A_**_merica)        (begins with “Bank”)_
    *       _WELLS-0984      (_**_Wells_** _Fargo Bank)      (does not begin with a reserved word)_
    *       _CITIB-0183         (_**_Citib_**_ank)                    (does not begin with a reserved word)_
*   Finally, a structure will be returned to the client (in this case, the app), containing the fields listed below, obtained from the calls to the auth. provider.
    *   {access\_token}
    *   {item\_id}
    *   {account.type}
    *   {account.subtype}
    *   {account friendly name}
    *   {account.numbers.account}    **» ENCRYPTED** 
    *   {account.numbers.routing} .    **» ENCRYPTED** 
> _The response payload must have certain sensitive fields encrypted (marked above) in a reversible manner, using a shared secret key in a way that only this service, and the other backend components (TPE), can reverse it._  
> _The mobile app cannot and should not be able to decrypt those fields. Thus, the secret key won’t be embedded in the app._

## Additional Information
*   ☛ Bank account operations sequence diagram
*   [https://app.diagrams.net/#G17qiYnxhkG7GxT8fzTdQWeBRxTiLbzWwM](https://app.diagrams.net/#G17qiYnxhkG7GxT8fzTdQWeBRxTiLbzWwM)
*   **(a)** For guides and documentation on how to perform this token exchange with the provider, refer to ☛ [https://plaid.com/docs/api/#exchange-token-flow](https://plaid.com/docs/api/#exchange-token-flow)
*   **(b)** For documentation on how to pull account “numbers”, on behalf of a token, refer to ☛ [https://plaid.com/docs/api/#auth](https://plaid.com/docs/api/#auth)
*   In order to operate with Plaid, some keys must be provided (this are constant and remain the same for all environments)
    *   client\_id: **599f48914e95b83d70203baa**
    *   public\_key: **91f3eb6cf8bc62e2b356a298b97167**
    *   secret: **_request from your manager_**

# ACH Load Testing

### Steps to complete a transaction Wallet Load successfully.
1. Crear transacción de WalletLoad ACH
2. Ejecutar postman Payment RL, por el monto total **Total Payment**. (Sumatoria del **Amount** + **Funding fee**)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/3fa94ec8-1fce-45f6-9de1-3655a062242e/totalAmount.jpeg)
1. De la respuesta de Payment RL, tomar el valor **new\_balance**.
2. Esperar a que la operación esté en **OnHold** / **WaitingSenderToBeChargedByAch** y tomar el valor **Reference num** que se muestra en el recibo.
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/b81deb78-3f23-4879-a307-23a6170e4d93/receipt.png)
1. _Acreditación a la Wallet_. Ejecutar postman ACH In - OK para simular la confirmación de la acreditación de fondos a la cuenta personal. Editar los valores **ach\_trans\_id** por **Reference num**, **amount** por **Total Payment,** **open\_to\_buy** por **new\_balance** e indicar el pmt\_ref\_no y timestamp acordes a la prueba que están realizando.
2. La operación se completaría de manera satisfactoria.

### Steps to fail a transaction Wallet Load
1. Crear transacción de WalletLoad ACH
2. Esperar a que la operación esté en **OnHold** / **WaitingSenderToBeChargedByAch** y tomar el valor **Reference num** que se muestra en el recibo.
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/b81deb78-3f23-4879-a307-23a6170e4d93/receipt.png)
1. Consultar el balance de la cuenta del usuario en CST.
2. Ejecutar postman ACH In - Failed para simular la confirmación el rechazo de la transferencia. Editar los valores **ach\_trans\_id** por **Reference num**, **amount** por **Total Payment,** **open\_to\_buy** por **el balance actual consultado** e indicar el pmt\_ref\_no y timestamp acordes a la prueba que están realizando.
3. La operación se finaliza como Fallida.

# ACH Out Testing

### Steps to complete a transaction Wallet Transfer Out successfully.
1. Crear transacción de WalletTransferOut with ACH.
2. Esperar a que la operación esté en **OnHold** / **WaitingSenderToBeChargedByAch** y tomar en consideración los valores **Total Payment**. (Sumatoria del **Amount** + **Funding fee**) y **Reference num** que se muestra en el recibo.
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/62e80f88-ba1d-4705-9b53-ec681924ae0a/receiptOut.jpeg)
1. Consultar el balance de la cuenta del usuario en CST.
2. _Acreditación a BankAcount_. Ejecutar postman ACH Out - OK para simular la confirmación de la acreditación de fondos al Banco. Editar los valores **ach\_trans\_id** por **Reference num**, **amount** por el importe negativo de **Total Payment,** **open\_to\_buy** por **el balance consultado** e indicar el pmt\_ref\_no y timestamp acordes a la prueba que están realizando.
3. La operación se completaría de manera satisfactoria.

# Old Mingle Wiki

n this section you might find all the information that was hold in the old Mingle Site under “Numi Wiki”

# Numi User

A **numi user** is a physical person that enrolls in the numi platform, by means of a numi application.
Each **numi user** is linked to a unique individual, and any given individual can hold a single user on the platform only.
A **numi user** is uniquely identified by its fully-qualified mobile phone number (in international format), e.g., +13034445678 (see standard  ☛ [E.123](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E.123))

# Numi Card User

A **numi card user** is a [numi user](http://mingle.leapfactor.net:8080/projects/numi/wiki/numi_user), which happens to be eligible to receive a numi prepaid debit card. At present, **this applies to all US-based numi users only**.
All US-based monies users apply for a prepaid debit card, during their sign-up process, automatically (they cannot opt-out).
Because of how the use cases are built, if they cannot get an active prepaid debit card, they won’t be able to operate with monies at all.
On all other regions where the monies card is not applicable, the app will not make any mention or reference to it whatsoever.

# Static Content

The content listed here may be included at an implementation depending on the scope, compliance constrains, marketing strategies or user experience designing. The list need to be used as a reference in order to determine the specific items that will be included.

| **Item**<br> | **Description / Comment**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| Terms & conditions of the app | Includes the terms, the rules and the guidelines of acceptable behavior and other useful sections to which users must agree in order to use or access the mobile app. |
| Privacy Policy | Explains how the app gather, store, protect, and use any personal data provided by the users. |
| Terms & conditions of the bank account | Includes the terms, the rules and the guidelines of acceptable behavior and other useful sections to which users must agree in order to issue the bank account. |
| Bank account contract |  |
| Card benefits |  |
| Geo-location agreement | In case the user explicitly needs to agree in order to let the app collect geo-location information. |
| Commercial contract | Commercial contract or agreement the user needs to accept in order to receive services or products, for example acquiring services. |
| Transactions cancelation policy | Terms, rules and/or guidelines users need to follow in order to cancel a transaction (if possible). |
| Limits table |  |
| Fees table |  |
| Communication matrix |  |
| Mail templates |  |
| Marketing materials for app stores |  |
| Onboarding screens |  |
| FAQ microsite |  |
| Support tree |  |
| Loading methods guidelines |  |
| Feature intros/helpers |  |

# Static Content Update Process

1. The PM/TA gets the update from the customer of the implementation (e.g SACBE content team)
    1. The customer of the implementation, that “owns” the content, needs to send the html file
2. A Jira Ticket is created for this content to be uploaded to DEV and QA environments
    1. This task is performed by mobile team
    2. Mobile team needs to verify the content of the html file to be upladed and make sure it complies to be used within the app of the implementation
    3. Mobile team will be responsible for updating DEV and QA environments
    4. Mobile team needs to maintain a version control of the changes
Pending definition: is it Git going to be used for Version Control [Alfredo Vina](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/557058:e0e8fe2e-3f6d-4338-a529-6c028c4a250a?ref=confluence)
3\. When the tasks described in the previous item are completed, the Jira ticket is sent to QA for testing/Validtion
a.The acceptance criteria of the Jira ticekt must contain the content of the file sent by the customer for QA team to be able to validate the correct content
b. When the QA team Aproves the ticket, it should be included in the release content
4\. When the time comes for a production release, the Mobile team needs to include the change of static content in the deployment checklist for the release
5\. The Static Content is moved to Production
a. This task is performed by Technical Operations team, as part of their deployment Tasks.
b. This will happen only if all the previous step are executed properly

# Affiliates

La plataforma posee una funcionalidad para crear links de atribución y deep links con determinadas carácteristicas que permiten definir desde donde y hacia donde se generan los links, y utilizar diferentes mecamismos para registrar información en dichos para ser utilizados por operaciones, marketing, etc

## CONCEPTOS DE LA PLATAFORMA
Affiliated =SOCIO DE NEGOCIOS - que refiere
Referee = Referido, usuario que instala el App desde una liga que "alguien" le envió)
Referal = Referente, usuario "especial" (Ej, promotor) que envia una liga a otro usuario

# Bill Payment

## General Details
The bill payment feature is an abstract pooposal of functionality and user experience.
Depending on the availability of different services for the Bill Payment Service Provider, some of the features proposed may change
Basically the Bill Payment will have the following features
*   Give the ability to the user to filter by different parameters
    *   Type of Service to Pay, State or City.
    *   Provide a Preview of the Services abailable by the user´s registered State or City in the Initial list
*   Provide a Section “My Services” that displays the user previusly selected services
*   Once a Service is selected, provide the flow to pay the service
    *   Display the details of the Bill
    *   Allow the user to select from enabled payment methods
    *   Allow the user to review the payment details
    *   Allow the user to continue with the flow and pay

# Split-Transaction Validation for Credit/Debit Cards

## High Level Business Requirement
Por favor hagamos un spec del tema de card splits para tener account ownership verification en tarjetas. Es un feature nuestro y no quiero que SACBE lo diseñé.
La idea es usarlo solo la primera vez y/o cuando un card load rebase un monto determinado. El UX que te lo arme Leandro y que lo apruebe Vale.
Mientras el usuario no válida el dinero se queda sin aplicar y si el usuario no proveed los datos en xxxx horas la operación de reversa.
Para evitar problemas con el gateway y los emisores pondremos el dinero en hold primero y luego de la verificación haríamos los cargos.
## Technical Discussion Notes (17-09-2021)
*   When the user wants to use a credit/debit card, the first transaction needs to be splitted in two transactions
*   The User Experience needs to give clear alerts and messaging for the user to let the user know that he needs access to his card statement when he executes the first transaction with this payment method
*   The percetage of the split needs to be _random_ this will allow the Transactions to have two amounts that are never the same
**Random Calculation Algoritm applied**
var rand = new Random(Environment.TickCount);
var percentSplit = [rand.Next](http://rand.Next)(20, 80);
decimal amount1 = Math.Floor(totalAmount (percentSplit 1.0m / 100));
decimal amount2 = totalAmount - amount1;
*   The actual load Transaction needs to be put on hold (e.g. in Galileo)
*   The User Experience needs to require as proof the amounts of the transaction split (either both amounts, one of the amounts,
*   The Platform needs to validate and match the amounts with the ones that the user input
*   If the amounts do not match, the transaction is failed
*   In the case the transaction split validation fails, there should be a mechanism to register this transaction failed for this reason, to allow the fraud-risk areas analyze this, and given the case, block the user if the behafiour is identify as fraude
*   When the user did not input the transaction split for a certain amount fo time, and the transaction is failed due to this, there should be a mechanism to block the card as a payment method to any further transactions

# New Numi Onboarding

This page contains the abstract and business information to provide context for the new onboarding flow that aims to provide a better/smoother user experience and less friction to the users

## General Details
After 6+ months of usage in one of the implementations (SACBE) and 3+ months in another (Tzune) it has come to the attentio of User Experience / Support team that the users strugggle with the amount of information they have to enter / valiate / review
Considering that most of the information can be extracted from the ID scan it was evident that the way to go was to “SCAN First”, and automate the data validation process at its most, and then have the user complete the information missing
It is also evident that stopping the user in the process to have him “come back later” is also frustrating, so the strategy need to change to allow the user to “move forward” in the flow, including more “behind de scenes” logic to keep track of what the user is missing to complete its profile to be able to pass all the validations (id, personal data, black lists) and have its bank account/numi account created.

## Technical Details
In order to achieve the goals set in the previous secition, the following changes need to be applied (at both levels, Platform and Mobile App)
*   Change the way and the order the information is captured
*   Keep track of the User Sign In Status to be able to address the missing information at a later stage, withouth stopping the user´s flow
*   Update the mechanism to be able to complete the user information based on the status of the sign in
*   Add Mechanism to capture information such as the Address with less user friction

### New Signup Status Analysis
The following Graphic Depicts the different functional steps the user will be able to perform during the Sign In Process and each possible scenario of Status / Messaging for the User
The idea behind this analysis is provide a list of “states” the Sign Up Process might have, to be able to provide a thorough handling of scnearios where the user can “drop” and “re-engage” the process

## User Experience Details
For each implementation the User Experience Details are registred in the Corresponding Space,
See Tzune: [New Sign In](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BCO/pages/1790017539/New+Sign+In)

# Alerts for Disabled Features

## Raw Requirements from meeting 13-10-2021
Alertas de Operacion
*   Basado en una configuracion de VT de Firebase
    *   TIPO DE ALERTA
    *   TEXTO DE ALERTA
    *   VIGENCIA DE LA ALERTA
*   Se define un protocolo para esas alertas
*   Los tipos pueden ser
    *   Bloquear toda el App
    *   Bloquear una sección de la aplicación
    *   Alertas para cuando el usuario no puede ejecutar ciertas funcionalidades:
        *   medio de pago deshabilitado (ya sea por horario, porque no funciona el proveedor, etc)
        *   funcionalidad deshabilitada (ya sea por servicio caido, por problemas con el proveedor, etc)
    *   En estos casos el app debe controlar especificamente el área definida para revisar en la tabla de alertas y si la misma no está configurada, se continua normalmente, si la alerta esta configurada, se muestra el alerta.

UI Details = the configuration in the VT needs to provide the message to be displayed in each case

# Name Matching Logic

## General Details
The following logic needs to be taken in consideration when the name of the user needs to be matched to be validated under different scenarios
The current scenarios where the name of the user needs to be validated are
*   The Name of the Scanned ID in the CIP Process
*   The Name of the Owner of an Exernal Bank Account when using PLAID to access an External Bank Account to get funds from or transfer funds to
*   For clarity purposes in the rest of this document this is called “Name Provided”

## Original Business Requirement
Se considera un match positivo si:
*   Existe conicidencia cuando excluyendo articulos, preposiciones, caracteres (-, '), letras individuales (L,C), etc. en cualquiera de los sentidos (nuestro nombre contra el de Plaid o al reves) existe todo el nombre.

## The Logic
*   The name of the user needs to be “contained” in the Name Provided it is being compared to
*   Each word of the name needs to be separated and compared individually
    *   The individual comparison needs to apply the levenshtein algorithm
    *   The individual comparison needs to reach an scoring higher than **85%**
*   The special characters, punctuation signs, (semicolon, period, colon,hyphen, etc.). and spaces needs to be ignored
*   The prepositions in spanish names needs to ge ignored
*   Once each word is compared, and each individual score is obtained, an average of the individual score of each word comparison will be obtained
*   Depending on this average, the Name of the User and the Name Provided will be considered as matching or not, if the average results in higher than **80%**

## Current Configuration October 2025
CardOwnerApproximation: 80

ClabeOwnerApproximation: 00

AllowedFullNameApproximation: 80

para clabe estamos con 0% por el tema del evento CEP que no regresa el ownername

**Comparison Example RAAS**
Soledad M Heano Silva vs Marcela Henao: 29.29
Marcela Henao vs Soledad M Heano Silva: 37.14

**Backend Notes**

en mi caso antes de comparar se quitan las palabras de una sóla letra y acentos y caracteres especiales

mejor dicho se remplazan los acentos

public string RemoveDiacritics(string text)
        {
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[àáâäã\]", "a");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[ÀÁÂÄÃ\]", "A");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[èéêëẽ\]", "e");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[ÈÉÊËẼ\]", "E");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[ìíîïĩ\]", "i");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[ÌÍÎÏĨ\]", "I");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[òóôöõ\]", "o");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[ÒÓÔÖÕ\]", "O");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[ùúûüũ\]", "u");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[ÙÚÛÜŨ\]", "U");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[ñ\]", "n");
            text = Regex.Replace(text, "\[Ñ\]", "N");

            return text;
        }

y se quitan estas preposiciones, que son comunes en los nombres latinos
                    "DE", "LOS", "LA", "EL"

y el criterio de aprobacion es 80%

# Markup for Exchange Rates

## General Details
the markup is an attribute of the International/Crossborder Transaction, that consists in applying “Basis Points”(\*) to the given exchange rate, to obtain a modified exchange rate to be applied to the transaction, resulting in a revenue over the final transaction amount.
[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basis\_point](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basis_point)
### Related Raw Requirements
Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-22937](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-22937))
## Technical details
### Calculation Components
1. The **Basis Points** are configured by business, and need to be used in the calculation of the final exchange rate.
    1. There will be two types of basis points, Default Basis Points and Risk Basis Points, The Default Basis Points are the ones default to use to generate the markup. The Risk Basis points, are the ones to use when the specific definition of the scenario involves a risk of loosing money.
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
Task = Add New Platform Configuration for Basis Points
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-846)[](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-846)1. [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-846)[HWAY-846](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-846) - Add New Platform Configuration for Basis Points Blocked
2. There will be one Default Basis Point and one Risk Basis Point will be defined for Each Provider available to define the Cross Brder Transaction (e.g Cybersource, Mastercard\_Send)
2. The **Provider Exchange Rate** needs to be obtained by the provider of the transaction:
*   For Visa = Foreign Exchange Rate API, that is currently implemented in the platform
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
For Mastercard = TBD. Task = Research and Implement Foreign Exchange Rate for Mastercard
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-845)*   [HWAY-845](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-845) - Research and Implement Foreign Exchange Rate for Mastercard Backlog
The **formula** for the final exchange rate should be as follows
**Final Exchange Rate** = Provider Exhange Rate -(Provider Exchange Rate \* VERRR CUAL ES/10000)

Once obtained the Final Exchange Rate is the one to be applied to convert to the destination country currency, the Amount of the Crossborder Transaction, that will be informed to the user performing the transaction
The difference between the Amount obtained with the Provider Exchange Rate and the Amount obtained with the Final Exchange Rate will be the Revenue obtained
TBD = how the Revenue obtained is registered in the Revenue Accounts of the Program
### Different Scenarios
There are different scenarios based on the **definition** of the Transaction, this is, the different elements involved and the information available for those elements, at a certain point of the flow:
*   If the user exists or not, both sender and receiver
*   If the payment method is “already” defined or not
This definitions will lead to different logic on what is the exchange rate to be applied for the transaction, and hence what will be the outcoming revenue
### Pre-Definitions
There are certain basic pre-defined elements that need to be considered for the logic of the scenarios
*   The decision on which Exchange rate is the “less beneficial” , for the scenarios thar require it, needs to be performed online, requesting all the Transaction Rates for the Providers in the moment of the _Transaction Quote_
*   Visa Exchange Rate is considered less beneficial for the user
*   The Risk Basis Points and the Default Basis Points need to be applied depending on the scenarios, for the ones that are considered to may have risk of losing money due to their nature, the Risk Basis Points will be used. Otherwise the Default Basis Points are the ones to use.
*   The operation Sender needs to see the amount he sent that was deducted from its account
*   The operation Receiver needs to see the amount calculated after applying the exchange rate and the basis points in each case, expressed in the destination currency selected
### Scenario 1
An existing user **sends** to an existing receiver
*   The receiver user already exists in the platform
*   The destination payment method is already defined as a default for the user
The Exchange Rate to use will be the one for the provider of the destination payment method.
The Basis Points to use will be de Default Basis Points
Task = Calculate Rate for Crossborder Transactions where Existing User sends to an Existing Receiver [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-847)[HWAY-847](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-847) - Calculate Rate for Crossborder Transactions where Existing User sends to an Existing Receiver In QA

### Scenario 2
An existing user **sends** to a NON existing receiver
*   The receiver user DOES NOT exist in the platform
*   The destination payment method IS UNKNOWN
The Exchange rate to use is the one of the “less beneficial” Provider. (1)
The Basis Points to use will be the Risk Basis Points
Task = Calculate Rate for Crossborder Transaction where Existing User sends to Non-Existing Receiver [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-848)[HWAY-848](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-848) - Calculate Rate for Crossborder Transaction where Existing User sends to Non-Existing Receiver In QA
(1) If from all the providers some of the exchange rates could not be retrieved, the les beneficial for the receiver needs to be selected

### Scenario 3
An existing user **funds the request** from an existing user
*   The receiver user already exists in the platform
*   The destination payment method is already defined as a default for the user
The Exchange Rate to use will be the one for the provider of the destination payment method.
The Basis Points to use will be de Default Basis Points
Task = Calculate Rate for Crossborder Transactions where Existing User REQUEST to an Existing User [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-852)[HWAY-852](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-852) - Calculate Rate for Crossborder Transactions where Existing User REQUEST to an Existing User In QA

### Scenario 4
An existing user **receives money** and defines the payment method
*   The receiver user MAY OR MAY NOT exist in the platform
*   The destination payment method was UNKNOWN
The Exchange Rate to use will be the “less beneficial” Provider. (1)
The Basis Points to use will be the Risk Basis Points
Task = Calculate Rate for Crossborder Transaction where User Receives in New Payment Method [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-849)[HWAY-849](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-849) - Calculate Rate for Crossborder Transaction where User Receives in New Payment Method IN PROGRESS

# Legal Information for a Receipt (Transaction Completed Details)

Due to Credit/Debit Card Payment Industry standards required to operate with these payment methods, the information provided to the user in the Transaction Completed Details / Information (Receipt) the following data that needs to be included:
Merchant logo,
Contact info,
Payment country,
Authorization code,
Transaction Policies.
The following information needs to be included besides the actual transactino information in the Receipt
*   Logo of Merchant & email (contact information) - Logo of the Program
*   Country - Country the transaction was executed
*   Authorization Code (transaction code From Processor if available)
*   Transaction Policies (might be a link)

# Quote Transaction Feature

## General Details
The quote transaction feature is a feature that execute certain calculations and validations before actually executing the transaction, this mechanisms does the following:
*   perform all the validations that need to take place depending on the transaction type, for the transaction to ve “valid”:
    *   Limits and velocity checks
    *   OFAC validation for users (when applies)
    *   Balance Checks
    *   Valid Payment Methdo Checks
*   Calculate the “cost” of the Transaction, obtaining fees to be applied, calculating the exchange rates, with their markup applied (when applicable), and calculating the amount to be ent to the receviing user if necessary
*   Allow the user to review the transaction details, including the calculation provided in the previous step like the fee, the exchange rate (when applies ) and other information
*   Provide a time frame where the Quote provided is “valid”.
*   Not allowing the user to select that quote / execute the transaction with the expired quote
*   Allowing the user to accept the Quote that is valid, and finally executiong the transaction with the details / fees / exchange rates, accepted by the user
## Business Requirement
## Raw Requirements
COTIZACION DE LA TRANSACCION
Situacion Actual
*   el monto que acumula limites se contabiliza ante el evento de carga en Galileo
Problematica del Quote
*   no es efectivizada la carga en Galileo al momento del Quote, no se podria acumular en ese momento
*   Prevenir que pueda hacer N cotizacines que va a fallar cuando se quede sin saldo
*   Posibles Soluciones
*   Acumulado Real / vs Acumulado Estimado(quote)
*   Tiempo de time out del acumulado estimado
*   Warning al usuario
*   No restringir la ejecución de la operacion
*   Observaciones
    *   hoy en dia si se implementa el control de sesion para 1 sesion para 1 usuario
    *   es poco probable que haya mas de una transacción para un usuario
Limite de receptores por usuario
*   cuando se contabiliza: cuando se efectiviza la operación?
Revisar: cada tipo de transaccion cual es el momento en qeu se deben contabilizar los limites.
Ej Para Crossborder
se debe validar con cumplimiento si esta restriccion es cuando se completa la operacion o cuando se inicia.
Aclarar en las stories
los acumulados solamente se contabilizan, calculan y validan al momento de realizar la carga
Las validaciones se mantienen en el flujo normal
Hay consideraciones que deben ser
**Registrar en Backlog:**
Cancelaciones: manejo de problemática para evitar perder revenue por ejemplo en las operaciones de entrada
*   acciones para permitir al usuario decidir (esperar o cancelar)
*   realizar un analisis proyectar en time line "para informar al usuario" cuando una transaccion que superó los limites puede ingresar a su cuenta

# Quote Transaction UI Behaviour

Note, this behaviour is reflected on Hway implementation

User is displayed with a quote in cross border transactions to reflect the exchange rate and the fees that affect the price of the transaction, and how the amount is afffected, this is, what the actual cost of the transaction is.

# Transaction Pricing and Basis Points

The Transaction Price is the cost that Leap Financial has to pay for a Transaction
There are different variables on the calculation of the Transaction Pricing
*   Provider of the Transaction
*   Funding Method
    *   Cash
    *   Debit Card
    *   Credit Card
    *   ACH
*   Exchange Rate Provider
The pricing needs to be configured as basis points to be able to transfer the cost to the end user (the originating user) as part of the exchange rate markup
All these mechanism are meant “to hide” the fee, so the user does not see the actual fee, but he price of the exchange rate we offer “hides” this costs
**Update**
As Per December 9th 2022 the Desiction was made not “to hide” the pricing (the fees) to the user, but inform them in a total amount -instead of the itemization/aggregation ussually informed-
## Gonzalez / NMC / Hway.send Implementatin
*   The promise to the user is that “there is no fee”
    *   this is a marketing tendency amongst the same type of business / competitor
*   The partner implementing the nmc white label will have the ability to “add” their own basis points, on top of the baseline basis points
*   There will be, hence
    *   Fixed Basis Points, for the ones that leap financial will add
    *   Variable Basis Points, for the ones that the partners will add
*   The Basis Fixed and the Variable Basis points are added to obtain the total Fee
*   See the implementation details sections for the calculation details
*   Notes for the Specialization for the Fees Calculation :
## Implementation Details
See Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-22537](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-22537))
