# Transaction Pricing Calculation

## General Notes
*   The calculation is to provide the cost of the transactions for the user
*   Different Calculations based on the funding method
*   The fixed basis pounts is always the same
*   The variable basis points is floating
*   Partner May Add Basis points to the variable basis points
*   The current markup implementation is defines as follows
## The Calculation Elements
*   Amount
*   Base Exchange Rate (public knowledge fee)
*   Basis Points
    *   Total = Fixed + Variable
*   Money Value of Basis Points
    *   Monetary Value of basis points (check this definition)
*   Result Amount (what the destination user gets)
    *   Amount - Calculated Fee
*   To avoid displaying it as a fee it is needed to hide the fees in the new exchange rate
*   New Exchange rate
*   Transaction Price = Amount and + Amount / Calculated Fee converted to Basis Points
## What needs the be done
*   Add or Identify in the platform
    *   What's the fixed fee
    *   What's the variable fee
    *   What's the extra basis points on the variable fee
    *   What's the basis points converted to exchange rate
*   Execute the calculations
*   Evaluate the Possible Solutions for Partner Basis Points
    *   Fixed Configuration value
    *   Only available for short term
*   As a Temporary solution, use Individual Fixed Configurations
    *   Each partner might have a configuration stored in the backend with specific information

## Pending Items
*   As a definitive solution, it is necessary to Apply the multi tenancy at backend level and remove the solution applied at UI level
    *   Each partner will have their own configuration if a Partner is an Affiliate
*   Pending Definitions
*   How's the calculations and the risk including the payment method provider

# Transaction Pricing based Payment Method

The user might be presented with the different alternatives of transation pricing, and how much the receiving user will get over the original amount, based on the payment method
The different transaction pricing will be impacted by the exchange rate (with the hidden fees) and its markup calculated in the different steps according to the elements of the operation
To Achieve this different alternatives, the following needs to happen at platform level

*   **When the user Request**
*   **As of November 2022 the only available destionation method is Card.**
*   First, the transaction pricing needs to identify
    *   if the user exist
    *   if the user has already a defined payment method
*   When the user exist and the destination payment method is defined, the exchange rate to apply is “safe”.
*   When the destionation payment method is NOT defined, the exchange rate to apply needs to be the one that is based on the Risk markup
After this is achieved, it is necesary to re-calculate the the exchange rate when the payment method is defined
*   the calculations will include any markup based on the payment method defined, to “hide” the fees that apply to that payment method
*   **the resulting information needs to be set up as a Fee for the Transaction (see notes below)**
*   the different fees based on the payment method, will be offered to the user, and the UI will perform the calculations to offer the different transaction amounts to the user
*   As a premise, it will always be a default payment method to be used as base

*   **When the user Sends**
*   Remains to be tested because the destination method is not defined
*   The Base Exchange rate is based on the Risk, because the destination method is not knwon
*   After the analysis of the funding method selected to pay, the exxchange rate fee with different options can be presented to the UI

*   **Yet to be Defined**
*   What will happen in scenarios with multiple options of funding metdhos and multiple options of destination method
As per December 2022 there was a change of requirement from business to **avoid hiding the fees**
**Please see notes below from meeting with change requirement communication**
## Cambio de Requerimiento de Business
*   no escondas el fee en el tipo de cambio
*   se debe calcular el variable bsp o variable fee y mostrar el fee resultante solamente al usuario
*   se agrega el markup por tennant al calculo9
Por ahora el UI es quien tiene la información de los datos variables y markup de cada tennant y se lo pasa el backend
*   en los fee que se agregaron a los corredores para permitir "ocultarlo" en el tipo de cambio, se debe mantener on the side
*   se debe generar otro servicio / objeto de calculo que permita calcular las fees totales con el markup y el bsp variable y entregar las fees a mostrar al usuario
*   En la UI en lugar de mostrar el monto que el usuario obtiene por cada medio de pago se debe mostrar el fee
*   Lista que muestra:
*   medio de pago --- fee
*   (expresado en moneda del sender)
*   con el preseleccionado se hace el calculo que se muestra lo que obtiene el usuario receptor
*   se muestra tambien el costo de la operacion
*   en la seccion gris se muestra el exchange rate
*   el funding fee / payment fee es diferente de estas fees que se deben calcular en base al variable y al markup
*   las fees que se deben caldular van en el **transacction fee**

# Fixes to Quote Transaction Dec 2022

It was identfified that due to some legacy code and mixed requirements from previous implementations ssome isues rised in the quote transaction calculations and display of the amounts of the transactions
The following needs to be taken into consideration to perform the fixes: Fixes to Quuote Transaction Legacy Issues [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1293)[HWAY-1293](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1293) CLOSED
*   For the Calculations in the Quote the amount of the Transaction is the Principal Amount
*   The amount the sender user sets up is the Principal Amount
*   The Principal Amount converted to the destination currency is the Amount the receiver user will get
*   The Fees are the defined as ussual:
    *   Funding Fee (funding payment method)
    *   Transaction Fee
    *   Destination Fee (destination method fee)
*   The Fee to be applied is the sum of all the mentioned fees
**The Rule as of 2022**
The one that pays for the transaction is the one who sends the money: the sender user will always pay the fees
## In Conclusion
### The amount to be sent is:
The Amount defined by the Sender -
In the Currency defined by for the sender
(considering using the exchange rates when applicable)
### What the sender pays is:
The amount defined in the item above - Adding the Fees calculated based on the funding method fee / destionation method / transaction fee

# Send Money Cross Border Through OCT

## General Details
This feature is ment to allow a cross border payment method using the “rails” of OCT (Original Credit Transaction) provided by different vendros : Cybersource or Mastercard Send.
Using this industry standard flow, Leapfinancial can provide a “remittance like” flow, allowing to perform a money moevement across borders, but withoth the legal implications, because the OCT flow already takes care of the cross border related items, because the destination payment method is a well-known industry standard.
In Business / Sponsor Bank definitions this flow is also called “Leap Financial Remittances Flow”
Basically, the sender user will select that he wants to send money to a user in another country, setting a debit / credit card as destination payment method. Then the receiver user will receive this money sent notification and provide the details of the debit / credit card, and at this point the OCT transaction using third party endpoints will be executed.
There’s an alternative scenario where a user in the destination country can request money from the origin country user, this scenario is pending definition on the way the destination user requests (either a sibling app in the destionation country: e.g = hway mexico, or another mean like the numi.ask app)
## Flow
The Graphic Below describes at a business / finance level how the process works:

## Countries
As per September 2023 the origin country will be USA and the destination country could be any of the defined in the following sheet, according to the different providers
**Please note as of 09-2023 the only provider in PROD for hway and hway.send / numi.send is** **_Cybersource_**
[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ixgfnqOd8bDExCY68n-JjbQCnANhPXckD5iR-YQYb64/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ixgfnqOd8bDExCY68n-JjbQCnANhPXckD5iR-YQYb64/edit?usp=sharing)
For Numi.Ask and Numi.Send implementations, the the origin country will be USA and the destination countries are yet to be defined
This country definition might be changed in other implementations
Both the origin country and the desttination country will be defined in the Platform Configuration as part of the Corridors Configuration

# Leap Financial Create Program Account API

# Introduction
Leap Financial External Account Creation is Intended to Allow Partners to Create Accounts within a Leap Financial Program.
The account creation process involves gathering and validating the customer’s information, including personal information, address, and identity document. The Leap financial Platform then runs validations, including third-party identity and address validations, and blacklists search to ensure the data provided by the customer is valid and legal, according to compliance requirements in the USA territory, to be a primary account owner.
Once the customer data is proven valid, the primary account is created.
The process also allows creating cards and accounts depending on the main account, if desired. These accounts might be for other customers, such as friends or family. If the accounts are for other customers, these dependents accounts are created under the premise that the main account holder has guaranteed the validity of the data.
**Note on the Address Information:** to avoid fraud, the address information for the secondary accounts and cards, and where the physical cards will be delivered when requested, is mandatory to be the same as the primary account. For this reason, the accounts are linked, and the address information is automatically completed by the Leap Financial Platform for the dependent’s accounts.
# Overview
This API is provided with a set of endpoints to:
●       Validate the data necessary for the Customer to comply with legal requirements for the account creation in leap financial program.
●       Create the primary account and if required, the primary card.
●       If required, create secondary account and cards.
# Authentication
The Authentication will be executed through a header API Key
The API KEY will be provided by the Implementation Manager
# Endpoints and Sequence
Below is the descritpion of the endpoints to be used in the customer validation and creation of the accounts
The endpoints are **to be invoked in a specific sequence** to allow the main account creation, **gathering the necessary customer information first**, **creating the user second**, and **then requesting the account creation.**
After the main account is created, If required, there’s also endpoints to create a secondary account.
Finally the endpoints to create the Physical Cards related to each account, either primary or secondary, are also available
## **1 - Providing Customer CIP Validation information**
## Validate Phone and OTP
Use this APIs to validate the phone and the OTP prior to the creation of the customer
### Validate Phone
**Definition**

```auto
POST /api/Customer HTTP/1.1 
Content-Type: application/json 
Accept: text/plain 
```

**Name**

```auto
Post /api/Customer/ValidatePhone
```

### Request
**Body**

```auto
{ 
  "MobilePhone": "string"
}
```

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| `MobilePhone` | string | true | Customer’s Mobile Phone |

### Response
**Body**

```auto
{
  "success": "bool"
}
```

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| `Success` | bool | true | true if the customer’s phone is valid, false if it is invalid |

### Send OTP
**Definition**

```auto
POST /api/Customer HTTP/1.1 
Content-Type: application/json 
Accept: text/plain 
```

**Name**

```auto
Post /api/Customer/SendOTP
```

### Request
**Body**

```auto
{ 
  "MobilePhone": "string",
  "MessageTemplate": "string"
}
```

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| `MobilePhone` | string | true | Customer’s Mobile Phone |
| `MessageTemplate` | string | true | Message to send to the customer, must contain thr format {{otp}} in the template to replace with the actual value. Example : “Hello your code is {{otp}}” |

### Response
**Body**

```auto
{
  
}
```

### Response Codes

| **Status**<br> | **Meaning**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Detail**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| 200 | [OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1) | Success | if the response is success, an sms will be sent to the user with the MessageTemplate provided |
| 400 | [Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1) | Bad Request | If response is bad request, no sms content will be sent. This error is sent when the phone number provided was not previously validated with `ValidatePhone` |
| 500 | [Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1) | Error | This is the response code when no sms content was sent due to an error |

### Validate OTP
**Definition**

```auto
POST /api/Customer HTTP/1.1 
Content-Type: application/json 
Accept: text/plain 
```

**Name**

```auto
/api/Customer/ValidateOTP
```

### Request
**Body**

```auto
{ 
  "MobilePhone": "string",
  "Otp": "string"
}
```

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| `MobilePhone` | string | true | Customer’s Mobile Phone |
| `Otp` | string | true | OTP that was sent to the customer through sms after executing `SendOTP` |

### Response
**Body**

```auto
{
  "success": "bool"
}
```

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| `Success` | bool | true | true if the customer’s otp is valid, false if it is invalid |

### Send OTP
## Create Customer
Use this API as first step to create a customer which data will be validated through Leap Financial’s CIP Process and will return the result of that process: either the customer was created or not, if not created, will provide also a code for the reason.
**Important**
**There is two preconditions prior to call this API:**
1. The Phone Number and the OTP needs to be Validated with the provided APIs
2. In the necessary scenarios, if the user needs to provide an image id, the data of the image needs to be already uploaded for the user, using the External Identifier field as the same identifier provided in the upload of the images ([see Upload Images](http://#Upload-Images))

**Definition**

```auto
POST /api/Customer HTTP/1.1 
Content-Type: application/json 
Accept: text/plain 
```

**Name**
`POST /api/Customer`
### Request
**Body**

```auto
{
  "externalIdentifier": "string",
  "firstName": "string",
  "middleName": "string",
  "lastName": "string",
  "dateOfBirth": "string",
  "gender": "Male",
  "countryCode": "string",
  "state": "string",
  "city": "string",
  "addressLine1": "string",
  "addresLine2": "string",
  "zipCode": "string",
  "idDocType": "DriversLicense",
  "idDocNumber": "string",
  "idDocExpirationDate": "string",
  "idDocState": "string",
  "idDocCountry": "string"
  "mobilePhone": "string"
}
```

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| externalIdentifier | string | true | A custom unique identifier for this Customer, specified by you. Usually this is the user's identifier in your system. |
| firstName | string | true | Customer's First Name |
| middleName | string¦null | false | Customer's Middle Name |
| lastName | string | true | Customer's Last Name |
| dateOfBirth | string | true | The Customer's Date of Birth |
| gender | string | true | The Customer's Gender. Two possible values are accepted in this field= “Male” of “Female” |
| countryCode | string | true | The Country code of the Customer's Address |
| state | string | true | The State of the Customer's Address |
| city | string | true | The City of the Customer's Address |
| addressLine1 | string | true | Customer's First line of Address |
| addresLine2 | string | true | Customer's Secondary line of Address |
| zipCode | string | true | The Postal Zip Code of Customer's Address |
| idDocType | [IdDocumentType](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/c%3A/Users/Alex/git/leap/programplatform/README.md#schemaiddocumenttype)<br>(see enumeration details below) | true | The Customer's Id Document type, one of the possible values of the enumeration |
| idDocNumber | string | true | The Number of Customer's Id Document |
| idDocExpirationDate | string¦null | false | If applicable for the given the id type, the Expiration date of the id |
| idDocState | string¦null | false | If applicable for the given the id type, the State of id |
| idDocCountry | string¦null | false | If applicable for the given the id type, the Country id |
| mobilePhone: | string |  | Mobile Phone of the user that was validated using [Validate Phone](http://#validate-phone) |

### Response

```auto
{
  "success": true,
  "id": "string",
  "validationErrors": [
    "None"
  ]
}
```

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| success | boolean | false | Indicates if the customer validation succeeded or not. |
| id | string¦null | false | The Customer Identification of the customer created. Needs to be used as reference in other api calls |
| validationErrors | \[[ValidationError](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/c%3A/Users/Alex/git/leap/programplatform/README.md#schemavalidationerror)\]¦null<br>(see enumeration details below) | false | A List of validation errors. The array will contain only “None” when no validation errors are present. |

## Upload Identity Support Documents
Use this api to complete the customer information **when an Image Id needs to be provided**.
Check the id Type enumeration details to verify **wich types require an image to be uploaded**. [See Id-Types](http://#IDType)
Use the ExternalIdentifier parameter to identify the customer the images belong to
**Definition**

```auto
POST /api/Customer HTTP/1.1 
Content-Type: multipart/form-data 
Accept: text/plain
```

**Name**
`POST /api/Customer/Upload`
### Request

```auto
ExternalIdentifier: string
File: string
DocumentType: DriversLicense
scanType: Front


```

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| ExternalIdentifier | string | true | The same external identifer referenced in the customer validation call and the customer create call |
| File | string(binary) | true | A string representation of the image of the Customer's Id Document |
| DocumentType | [IdDocumentType](https://file+.vscode-resource.vscode-cdn.net/c%3A/Users/Alex/git/leap/programplatform/README.md#schemaiddocumenttype)<br>(see enumeration details below) | true | The Customer's Id Document type |
| scanType | string, one of two possible values = “Front” of “Back” | true | a string indicatint which side of the scan is the image |

### Response
### `{ "success": true }`

| **Status**<br> | **Meaning**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Detail**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| 200 | [OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1) | Success | if the response is success a json object indicating that the upload was success will be returned |
| 400 | [Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1) | Bad Request | If response is bad request, a json with the details of the problem that caused the bad request, will be returned. See Json Example Below |

## **2 - Creating Main Account**
Use this API to create the main or principal account for a **customer that has been created succesfully using the Customer Create endpoint. (**[**See Create Customer**](http://#create-customer) )
If the account is created successfully the main account ID will be returned, which is the account number provided by the Processor (Galileo).
If the account cannot be created, the failure reason will be provided.

**Definition**

```auto
POST /api/Customer HTTP/1.1 

Content-Type: application/json 

Accept: text/plain 
```

**Name**
`POST /api/Account`
### Request
**Body**

```auto
  "customerId": "string"
}


```

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| customerId | string | true | id of the customer returned by the Create Customer endpoint |

### Response
**Body**

```auto
{
  "accountNumber": "string"
}
```

| **Status**<br> | **Meaning**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| 200 | [OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1) | Success | If the response is success, and the account is created, the account number will be returned |
| 400 | [Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1) | Bad Request | If response is bad request, account number will be null and a json with the details of the problem that caused the bad request, will be returned. See Json Example Below |

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| `accountNumber` | string | true | account number for the account created, as provided by the card processor. For Galileo this is **the PRN** of the account |

## **3 - Creating secondary Account**
Use this endppint to create a secondary account dependant of a primary or main account already existing. A secondary account will likely be destined to a friend or family member. The primary or main account CIP process already held, guarantees that the secondary account holder is a safe user, so no customer validation is required for secondary accounts.
**Definition**

```auto
POST /api/SecondaryAccount HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json
```

**Name**
`POST /api/SecondaryAccount`
### Request

```auto
{
  "accountNumber": "string",
  "firstName": "string",
  "middleName": "string",
  "lastName": "string",
  "dateOfBirth": "string",
  "mobileNumber": "string",
  "email": "string"
}
```

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| accountNumber | string | true | accounNumber of the primary account to be associated with this secondary account, which was provided as reponse by the account method |
| firstName | string | true | First Name of the Secondary Card Holder |
| middleName | string¦null | false | Middle Name of the Secondary Card Holder |
| lastName | string | true | Last Name of the Secondary Card Holder |
| dateOfBirth | string | true | Date of Birth of the Secondary Card Holder |
| mobileNumber | string | true | Mobile phone of the Secondary Card Holder |
| email | string | true | Email of the Secondary Card Holder |

### Response
**Body**

```auto
{
  "accountNumber": "string"
}
```

| **Status**<br> | **Meaning**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| 200 | [OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1) | Success | If the response is success, the account number of the secondary account created, will be returned |
| 400 | [Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1) | Bad Request | If response is bad request, account number will be null and a json with the details of the problem that caused the bad request, will be returned. See Json Example Below |

### Properties Dictionary

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| `accountNumber` | string | true | account number for the secondary account created, as provided by the card processor |

## **4 - Creating Physical Cards**

| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| accountNumber | string¦null | true | accounNumber of the primary account to be associated with this physical card, which was provided as reponse by the account method |

```auto
  "accountNumber": "string"
}
```

| **Status**<br> | **Meaning**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| 200 | [OK](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.3.1) | Success | If the response is success, the account number of the secondary account created, will be returned |
| 400 | [Bad Request](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6.5.1) | Bad Request | If response is bad request, account number will be null and a json with the details of the problem that caused the bad request, will be returned. See Json Example Below |

# Enumeration Details
### Customer IdDocumentType
#### List of Possible enumerated values for this field

| **Value**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| DriversLicense | Id provided is a Drivers Licence for USA state. Requires to upload the image |
| SocialSecurityNumber | Id provided is a SSN |
| GreenCardNumber | Id provided is a Green Card. Requires to upload the image |
| PassportNumber | Id provided is a Passport. Requires to upload the image |
| MatriculaConsularNumber | Id provided is a Matricula Consular for Mexico. Requires to upload the image |
| StateId | Id provided is a USA state ID. Requires to upload the image. |
| IndividualTax | Id provided is an ITIN |

###   

### Customer ValidationError
#### List of possible enumerated values for this field

| **Value**<br> | **Details**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| None | There has been no validation errors |
| InvalidAddress | One or many of the elements of the address has caused the identity validation to fail |
| UnreadableDoc | The information of the document provided has caused the identity validation to fail |
| InvalidPersonalData | One or many of the elements of the personal data (name, last name, DOB, gender) has caused the identity validation to fail |
| BlackListed | The name of the user has provided a hit in one or many black lists causeing the identity validation to fail. |

## Postman Collection
Attached to this document the postman collection with the definitions of the endpionts is provided

[Leapfinancial Program API.json](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/49994279-bf2c-4407-90a8-86e061bbbcea/Leapfinancial%20Program%20API.json)

# Technical Implementation Details

## CIP Data
To undergo the validations necessary by compliance to accept the data of a person to create its account in a leapfinancial program, it is necessary to ensure that the information provide is sufficient and valid to perform the necesary validations mechanism for the identity of the user whose account will be created in ta later process
The data necessary to perform these validations is the following:
*   First Name = string
*   Middle Name = string
*   Last Name = string
*   Gender = string ( femenino, masculino, otros )
*   Address = string, first line of address
*   Addres 2 = string, sedondary line of address
*   City = string
*   State = string - needs to be translated into a two letter ISO Code for USA states
*   ZIP = string
*   Date of Birth
*   ID Type = specific number accoirding to the table below
*   ID Number = the number of the id
*   ID Exp Date = if applicable, the expiration date of the ID (see table below orange and magenta cells)
*   ID State = if applicable, the State of the ID (see table below, magenta cells)
*   ID Country = if applicable, the country of the ID (see section country below)
## Id type definitions and considerations
Consider the following table for the format and requirements of the ID fields
Note = Where a date is required, it is the expiration date of the credential.

| **ID Type**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Layout of ID Number**<br> | **Scanned Document Required**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| 1 | Driver's License Number | <DL# 1–20 alphanumeric>|<DL State 2 alpha>| <DL expiration mm-dd-yyyy><br>Example: abcd12345|UT|05-02-2020 | YES |
| 2 | Social Security Number | 9 digits, all numeric. See [Validation for ID type 2](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/api-reference-customer-id-types#validation-for-id-type-2).<br>Example:123456789 | NO |
| 3 | Green Card Number | <9-digit green card ID>|<mm-dd-yyyy><br>Example: 123456789|05-02-2020 | YES |
| 4 | Passport Number | <6–9 alphanumeric passport ID>|<mm-dd-yyyy><br>Example: abc1234|05-02-2020 | YES |
| 6 | Matricula Consular Number | <7–9 digit MCN ID>|<mm-dd-yyyy><br>Example: 123456789|05-02-2020 | YES |
| 13 | State ID | <DL# 1–20 alphanumeric>|<DL State 2 alpha>|<DL expiration mm-dd-yyyy><br>Example: abcd12345|UT|05-02-2020 | YES |
| 15 | ITIN (Individual Tax Identification Number) | 9-digit ITIN ID<br>Example: 123456789 | NO |

## Id Country
When the ID type provided is passport, the country of the id is also required

# Handling of The Results based of the Identity Validation

Below is the example of a CURL that the platform needs to execute to perform the identity validation with a third party - Idology- (please note the format of the fields)

```auto
curl --location "https://web.idologylive.com/api/idiq.svc" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "username=REDACTED" ^
--data-urlencode "password=REDACTED" ^
--data-urlencode "firstName=Carolina" ^
--data-urlencode "lastName=Speroni" ^
--data-urlencode "address=8550 Byron Ave" ^
--data-urlencode "address2=2F" ^
--data-urlencode "city=Miami Beach" ^
--data-urlencode "state=FL" ^
--data-urlencode "zip=33141" ^
--data-urlencode "dobYear=1982" ^
--data-urlencode "dobMonth=11" ^
--data-urlencode "dobDay=05" ^
--data-urlencode "ssn=748014743" ^
--data-urlencode "ssnLast4=4743"
```

Based of the data provided, this provider might return different responses
The CIP Api should handle the results of the provider and and return the response accordingly, either by creating and providing the CIP identifer, or failing the CIP and including a describing message
### 1) CIP Identifier failed due to Black List Hit

**If the following elements are present in the result**
**<results>**
        **<key>result.match.restricted</key>**
        **<message>result.match.restricted</message>**
    **</results>**
    <restriction>
        **<key>global.watch.list</key>**
        <message>Patriot Act Alert</message>
        <pa>
            <list>Politically Exposed Persons</list>
            <score>92</score>
        </pa>
    </restriction>
**The CIP identifier cannot be created and the message should be “Black List Match Found”**
Example of Black List Hit Full Reponse

```auto
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<response>
    <id-number>4779097088</id-number>
    <summary-result>
        <key>id.failure</key>
        <message>FAIL</message>
    </summary-result>
    <results>
        <key>result.match.restricted</key>
        <message>result.match.restricted</message>
    </results>
    <restriction>
        <key>global.watch.list</key>
        <message>Patriot Act Alert</message>
        <pa>
            <list>Politically Exposed Persons</list>
            <score>92</score>
        </pa>
    </restriction>
    <qualifiers>
        <qualifier>
            <key>resultcode.pa.dob.not.available</key>
            <message>PA DOB Not Available</message>
        </qualifier>
    </qualifiers>
    <idliveq-error>
        <key>id.not.eligible.for.questions</key>
        <message>Not Eligible For Questions</message>
    </idliveq-error>
</response>
```

### 2) CIP Identifier failed due to incorrect / incomplete Data
For the following elements present in the result
</results>
    **<qualifiers>**
        **<qualifier>**
            **<key>see table below:resultcode</key>**
            <message>message</message>
        </qualifier>

**The CIP identifier cannot be created and the message should be corresponding to the table below**
The cells highlited in yellow might have a behaviour = **if this is the only resultcode** the id is considered pass and the validation from idology, successfull

The cells highlited in red will have different behaviour = **if this is the only resultcode**, the id is considered failed

| **Result Code** | **Message / Error Type** | **Allow Attempts** |
| ---| ---| --- |
| resultcode.coppa.alert | Personal Information is Invalid | **No** |
| resultcode.address.does.not.match | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.street.name.does.not.match | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.street.number.does.not.match | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| [resultcode.input.address.is.po.box](http://resultcode.input.address.is.po.box) | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| [resultcode.located.address.is.po.box](http://resultcode.located.address.is.po.box) | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.warm.address.alert | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.zip.does.not.match | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.input.address.is.not.deliverable | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.located.address.is.not.deliverable | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.yob.does.not.match | Personal Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.yob.within.one.year | Personal Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.mob.does.not.match | Personal Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.no.mob.available | Personal Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.multiple.records.found | Personal Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.newer.record.found | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.high.risk.address.alert | Address Information is Invalid | **No** |
| resultcode.address.velocity.alert | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.address.stability.alert | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.address.longevity.alert | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.address.location.alert | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.alternate.address.alert | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.no.dob.available | Personal Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.ssn.not.available | Document Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.ssn.does.not.match | FAILED | **No** |
| resultcode.ssn.within.one.digit | Document Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.input.ssn.is.itin | NO MESSAGE - PASS | n/a |
| resultcode.located.itin | NO MESSAGE - PASS | n/a |
| resultcode.ssn.tied.to.multiple.names | Document Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.subject.deceased | FAILED | **No** |
| resultcode.state.does.not.match | Address Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.ssn.issued.prior.to.dob | FAILED | **No** |
| resultcode.ssn.not.valid | Document Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.single.address.in.file | NO MESSAGE - PASS | n/a |
| resultcode.data.strength.alert | Personal Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.activation.date.alert | Personal Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.last.name.does.not.match | Personal Information is Invalid | Yes |
| resultcode.thin.file | FAILED | **No** |
| resultcode.bankruptcy | FAILED | **No** |
| resultcode.age.below.minimum | FAILED | **No** |
| resultcode.age.above.maximum | FAILED | **No** |
| resultcode.multiple.records.found | FAILED | **No** |

Example of Incomplete Data Response from provider

```auto
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<response>
    <id-number>4779097886</id-number>
    <summary-result>
        <key>id.success</key>
        <message>PASS</message>
    </summary-result>
    <results>
        <key>result.match</key>
        <message>ID Located</message>
    </results>
    <qualifiers>
        <qualifier>
            <key>resultcode.newer.record.found</key>
            <message>Newer Record Found</message>
        </qualifier>
        <qualifier>
            <key>resultcode.low.risk</key>
            <message>Low Risk Score</message>
        </qualifier>
    </qualifiers>
    <idnotescore>100</idnotescore>
</response>
```

### 2) CIP Identifier created correctly
If none of the above elements is present and the response looks like the succesfull restul displayed above, then
**The CIP identifier can be created**

# Device Identificator

In order to capture ana analyze user behaviour, and take advantage of this information in marketing campaigns, the SDK included in the App Architectore, from the provider Kochava, registers information of the device and register certain events:
*   automatic events are registred such as instalation
*   other events are registered through the backend such as the registration copmlete (when the
Part of the information that kochava needs for either type of event are the device “advertisement” identifiers, interal device identifiers that are a standard in the mobile industry and can be userd with different purposes
In IOS = IDFA
In Android = ADID

**Related Pages**
Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-26437](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-26437))
Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-23517](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-23517))

# Platform Alerts Mechanisms

## General Information
[Vanesa V. Vilas](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/5cd5c60bf348990dbc3c2063?ref=confluence) to complete

## Tecnhical Implementation Details
For implementation details see [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2289)[HWAY-2289](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2289) - Generate a New Type of Alerts for Providers Exceptions - Current Providers CLOSED and all its subtasks

# Testing Procedures for Alerts Mechanism

## Forcing alerts for each provider/service:

**Galileo (WalletsApi)**
1- In GalileoUS.config change “Url“ text (“4.0“ for “3.0“)
2- Create new user and take it to level 2.
3- Test WalletsApi status ([http://qa.h-way.com:440/wallets/status)](http://qa.h-way.com:440/wallets/status))

**Tabapay (Operation.Worker)**
1- In Tabapay.config delete “ClientID“ value.
2- Execute WalletLoad with DigitalWallet.
3- Test Operation.Worker status ([http://qa.h-way.com:440/operationworker/status)](http://qa.h-way.com:440/wallets/status))

**CyberSource (Operation.Worker)**
1- In CyberSourcePayout.config delete “ProxyUrl” value.
2- Execute OCT operation (Wallet → Card)
3- Test Operation.Worker status ([http://qa.h-way.com:440/operationworker/status)](http://qa.h-way.com:440/wallets/status))

**CyberSource (PaymentMethodApi)**
1- In CyberSourcePayout.config delete “ProxyUrl” value.
2- Add card as a new payment method (to execute BinLookUp)
3- Test PaymentMethodApi status ([https://qa.h-way.com/paymentmethod/status)](http://qa.h-way.com:440/wallets/status))

**Twilio (Operation.Worker)**
1- In <ServiceName>.config delete “SMSProviderNumber“ value.
2- Execute an operation that ends succesfully.
3- Test Operation.Worker status ([http://qa.h-way.com:440/operationworker/status)](http://qa.h-way.com:440/wallets/status))

**Twilio (TPEServiceApi)**
1- In <ServiceName>.config delete “SMSProviderNumber“ value.
2- Create new user and ask for the OTP.
3- Test TPEServiceApi status ([https://qa.h-way.com/tpe/api/status)](http://qa.h-way.com:440/wallets/status))

**Twilio (NotificationsApi)**
1- In <ServiceName>.config delete “SMSProviderNumber“ value.
2- Activate SecondaryCheck for a user.
3- Test NotificationsApi status ([http://qa.h-way.com:440/notifications/status)](http://qa.h-way.com:440/wallets/status))

**Idology (TPEServiceApi)**
1- In Idology.config change “ExpectID\_APIUrl“ value (for example, remove “q“ from “idiq”)
2- Create new user and take it to level 2.
3- Test TPEServiceApi status ([https://qa.h-way.com/tpe/api/status)](http://qa.h-way.com:440/wallets/status))

## Turning off alerts for each provider/service (in Postman):

[https://qa.h-way.com/mexbc/json/reply/QuerySubscriberMessageRequest](https://qa.h-way.com/mexbc/json/reply/QuerySubscriberMessageRequest) (POST)

**Galileo (WalletsApi)**

```auto
{
    "messageType": "RemoveAlertsInWalletsApi",
    "payload": "{\"Provider\":\"Galileo\"}"
}
```

**Tabapay (Operation.Worker)**

```auto
{
    "messageType": "RemoveAlertsInOperationWorker",
    "payload": "{\"Provider\":\"TabaPay\"}"
}
```

**CyberSource (Operation.Worker)**

```auto
{
    "messageType": "RemoveAlertsInOperationWorker",
    "payload": "{\"Provider\":\"CyberSourcePayout\"}"
}
```

**CyberSource (PaymentMethodApi)**

```auto
{
    "messageType": "RemoveAlertsInPaymentMethodApi",
    "payload": "{\"Provider\":\"CyberSourcePayout\"}"
}
```

**Twilio (Operation.Worker)**

```auto
{
    "messageType": "RemoveAlertsInOperationWorker",
    "payload": "{\"Provider\":\"Twilio\"}"
}
```

**Twilio (TPEServiceApi)**

```auto
{
    "messageType": "RemoveAlertsInTPEServiceAPI",
    "payload": "{\"Provider\":\"Twilio\"}"
}
```

**Twilio (NotificationsApi)**

```auto
{
    "messageType": "RemoveAlertsInNotificationsApi",
    "payload": "{\"Provider\":\"Twilio\"}"
}
```

**Idology (TPEServiceApi)**

```auto
{
    "messageType": "RemoveAlertsInTPEServiceAPI",
    "payload": "{\"Provider\":\"Idology\"}"
}
```

# Users with International Phone Numbers

## General Details
There is a business requirement that brings the need of **allowing users with “phone numbers outside USA” to be able to create their users and their accounts.**
These users are demographics of persons that might have a phone and a SIM from outside USA, but they have a legal address in USA and the legal right to have an account.
For this reason is correct and compliant to allow them to have accounts in USA
_In further implementations, when other programs in other countries exist, it will be necessary to adjust this requirement to analyze what are the requirements for a user with a foregeinger phone number to have an account in a specific country, but since the only account program now is in USA, this situation will be handled in the future._
For the time being this requirement is suscribed to the USA Programs
## Requirement Details
*   A person that provides an International Phone Number from Any Country( \* ) **will be able to get their use created in the platform**
The user will be created in Level 0 of CIP
*   A person user that provides an International Phone Number from Any Country( \* ) w**ill be able to get their Account created in the platform and in the Sponsor Bank, if he wishes to**
The user will need to undergo the CIP Process for USA: the user will need to provide a USA Address and a valid form of ID to be able to get their account
The user will undergo the Identity Validations currently in place of USA Users

( \* ) Definition Pending = is any country banned ?

## Implementation Details
The Platform needs to implement the changes to remove the mechanisms that currently analyze the country of the phone to allow the user to move forward with the sign up process, and further more, with the request to create its account in USA
The Mobile architecture needs to implement the changes to be able to handle any country phone number and allow the user to continue with its sing in process and further more with the CIP Interaction that will allow him to request create the account in USA if he wishes to
Both the Platform and the Mobile Layer need to guarantee the following
*   The user will provide a phone number from any country and will be able to continue with the process
*   The user will provide their personal information and will be able to continue with the process
*   If the user wishes to continue with the the Account Creation, the country of the account will be USA, so the following needs to happen
    *   The user will provide at this point information pertinent to have their Identity validations in USA
        *   USA Addresss
        *   Allowed form of ID for USA (the ids are the same currently available in the CIP, with no changes)
    *   The user will undergo the identity validations for USA (e.g Idology validation)
    *   The information of the user will be validated with the current logic available in the platform for the CIP for USA users

# USA phone area code issues

It is a problematic for international phone numbers that certain countries share the country code and format from USA
## Countries and territory sharing area code with USA
The following info describes the countries / territories that have this issue
The folllowing area codes added after the +1 allow to identify the countries / territories tha are NOT USA Neither Canada
### ountry / Territory Map

| <br>**NPA** | <br>**COUNTRY/TERRITORY** | <br>**LOCATION** | <br>**CITIES** (\* denotes capital) |

# COUNTRIES HANDLING

Numi platform has the ability to handle multiples countries for the funds movements as well as for the users and accounts creation

To handle a specific Country and all its related mechanisms and features in Numi Platform, the information of the Country must be gathered, agreed upon by the team.

Also, certain tasks that affect the platform components need to be executed to include the information related to the country.

This page intends to centralize all the information related to the handling of countries

1) RAAS Projects - Tasks to Add a New Country / Countries so far in the plaform
[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1EgzNYFlbxX2MDAF5IyIkj0\_RNY6zfyd52y\_\_\_Baldzg/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1EgzNYFlbxX2MDAF5IyIkj0_RNY6zfyd52y___Baldzg/edit?usp=sharing)

2) Agreements on Configuration
[https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/10pyX1xjcWfCVyo-O6F30Nru2d6QHYbCMOIRzCm-n9UU/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/10pyX1xjcWfCVyo-O6F30Nru2d6QHYbCMOIRzCm-n9UU/edit?usp=sharing)

3) Script to integrate a new country in QA
[https://docs.google.com/document/d/1moRETce\_6bAI4blLGZu3gOZh7QkUWsObOlyaTGYSwOA/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1moRETce_6bAI4blLGZu3gOZh7QkUWsObOlyaTGYSwOA/edit?usp=sharing)

# Industry Documentation and Glossaries

Glossary

| Term | Definition |
| ---| --- |
| ACH | In banking, ACH stands for **Automated Clearing House**, which is a network that coordinates electronic payments and automated money transfers. ACH is a way to move money between banks without using paper checks, wire transfers, credit card networks, or cash. |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |
|  |  |

**Pending Translation:**
**Mobile Cloud Services** por sus siglas en ingles (MCS) o servicios móviles en la nube. Son los servicios que los usuarios consumen, ya sea directa o indirectamente, incluidos, entre otros, el envío, la recepción, el reenvío, la transmisión de información, la gestión segura del contenido almacenado temporal o permanentemente y los datos personales y transaccionales, y la gestión de dichos servicios en la nube, que incluye, entre otros, servicios tales como comprobante de recepción, verificación de identidad, seguridad, servicios de directorio avanzados, trazabilidad, autenticación, medición de tráfico y patrones de uso, sincronización de datos de documentos estructurados y no estructurados, comunicaciones, almacenamiento, datos y gestión de usuarios, y muchos otros servicios para el procesamiento de información financiera o comercial.   
**Transaction Processing Engine** (TPE) son los servicios que las aplicaciones especializadas consumen para acceder a los servicios de la plataforma y también a servicios de terceros. incluidos, entre otros: banca, tarjetas de crédito y débito, préstamos, verificación de identidad, mensajes, seguridad, pagos, etc. Este modulo también se conoce como Payment Engine (Motor de Pagos).
**Wallet.** Son aplicaciones web y/o móviles que brindan a los usuarios acceso a ciertas funciones y servicios de la Plataforma. Las aplicaciones se pueden proporcionar como aplicaciones independientes o como componentes o módulos para ser integrados en otras aplicaciones.
**Módulos.** Son las características independientes que, en conjunto, permiten que un usuario ejecute una función o proceso comercial particular, como Gestión de tarjetas, Gestión de cuentas, Atención al cliente, Servicios de pago, etc. Los módulos se pueden implementar por separado o en conjunto.
**KYC360** es un módulo que se puede implementar de forma independiente e incluye un conjunto diverso de funciones que incluyen procesos como: Originación del cliente, registro, activación, recopilación de información, identificación del cliente y gestión de riesgos, entre otras funciones.
**La plataforma SACBE** (Plataforma) incluye MCS, TPE y las aplicaciones proporcionadas a los clientes (Wallet). La Plataforma maneja la identificación del usuario, la administración del usuario en general, los perfiles de usuario, la administración del dispositivo e integra un proceso de identificación del cliente para administrar el riesgo y la seguridad. La Plataforma SACBE también puede proporcionar módulos para manejar ciertos procesos y aspectos comerciales de pagos, servicios bancarios y otros servicios financieros y no financieros.
**Los adaptadores** son las piezas de software que son específicos de un determinado sistema de terceros como un servicio Tokenización un Gateway de pagos o los servicios de Core Bancario o procesamiento. Los adaptadores permiten aislar la integración en un espacio de ejecución contenido mientras mantienen un conjunto estándar de servicios internos.

# Address Input, Capture and Usage

## Address Data General Details
The Address is a Data Element used throghout the Platform Operation

*   The User Address for the Identity Validation
*   The Deliery Addresss for the Physical Card
*   The Billing Address for the AVS of a card to be used in an AFT Transaction
*   Other
##   

## Address Input
#### Mobile Apps:
The Mobile Apps use Google Maps “Location Validation Service” to provide the User Interface of Apps (based on the Numi Platform). This provides the apps with the ability to autocomplete the Address Information throughout the User Experience, providing with the data a specific “place” located by Google

The user cannot enter a full address by hand withouth having it validated / autocompleted with Google.

Today the address input is captured and validated against Google Geolocation Service.
See : Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-30777](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-30777))

#### **Problems Detected:**
*   When the user has some specific Address that Google Cannot recognize the Adress Input is not properly captured
*   Google Allows to identify and autocomplete up to the street/door number level.
    *   Hence the fields used as a standard to enter the specific details of a Postal Address (such as department, section) are lost when the Google Location fields are validated with the input data
    *   Sometimes Google Location services provide an incomplete Address with no sufficient data to be used in certain integrations such as the Sponsor Banks and Money Transactions Providers.

**Work done in the the Projects**
*   Change the User Experience for the Address Input
    *   The Tasks for this in HWAY are from JIRA Import To be included as links here
    *   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4180](https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4180))
*   Change the way the Address data is captured/stored
    *   The Tasks for this in HWAY are from JIRA Import To be included as links here
    *   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4095](https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4095))

## Considerations for the Address Capture

**The User Experience should contain the following features**
*       *   Allow the user to enter manually a full _Postal Address_ with the specifics that are needed for each country, if they are “not found” by the Google Autocomplete Service
        *   Validate that the address has all the elements to be a postal address
        *   Provide the means to Add the Zip Code if it's missing
        *   Provide the means to Add the City if it´s missing
*       *   Allow the user to enter the “aditional fields” for the Postal Address : E.G Piso / Departamento, this means, adding an extra field
    *   The State / Provinces list should always be a drop down if the Field is empty in any case
        *   If it’s autocompleted by Google and the State is not part of the Autocomplete (is empty)
        *   if the user needs to choose from it because the autocomplete did not work

**The changes in the handling of the Address Data handled by the UI and stored in the platform are as follows**
*       *   Add an extra field such as “address 2”
        *   for the extra details of the address, such as department number, suite number, building number, etc
*       *   For Mexico Specifically, change the way the Google Location Object is mapped to the Address Data
        *   Remove the usage of Address 2 for Colonia, and keep it for the details of the Adress as it is intended to
        *   Use Address 3 field for administrative level 1 (e.g Municipio)
        *   Use Address 4 field for Colonia

**Considerations**
*   The **Customer Support** teams needs to have context to be able to help the users in case there is an issue with the address input

*   The **Operations** teams needs to have a process set in place to:
*   Detect when users are _consistently_ adding wrong addresses
*   Add the Exception

# Refresh Address Input, Capture and Usage

## Temas Generales que deben considerarse
*   Es necesario homologar la información que Mobile posee en la captura respecto de la información que backend recibe y procesa
    *   Mobile tiene el numero, departamento, etc por serparado en el onboarding
    *   Falta Validar si está agregado en la captura de direccion de la entrega de la tarjeta
    *   Falta que backend obtenga los datos que Mobile ya tiene
*   Es necesario manejar el Address como una entidad del sistema
*   Address 1 = Calle y Numero cuando Google lo proporciona
*   Identificar cual es el campo en el que el numero se almacena cuando el usuario lo proporciona

[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)Place Details and Place ID handling](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1437)
## [TZN-1437](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1437) TO DO
*   Se debe revisar el uso del parámetro placeID cuando se le permite al usuario editar la dirección que ya se obtuvo a traves de Google, ya que no es válido obtener el mismo place ID
*   Siempre se debe manejar el elemento PlaceDetails de la dirección, para enviarlo como parte de la información de la dirección a la plataforma, con las siguientes Alternativas:
    *   Si el servicio de autocompletar de Google, no resuelve un place Details, el mismo debe ser “construido” por la capa de Mobile, con la información que proveyó el usuario
    *   Si el servicio de autocompletar de google lo resuelve, debe manejarse la posbilidad de que el usuario lo haya modificado, y reflejar esas modificaciones manuales en los datos de Place Details
*   La Plataforma debe validar lo siguiente:
    *   Si el PlaceID existe, significa que el google location service resolvió el place detail y es “tal cual” lo resolvió.
    *   Si el PlaceID no existe, significa que el place details ha sido “modificado”, por la capa Mobile.

# Handle multi language State short name

**Context - Problem to be resolved**

The Address elements are retrieved through the integration from the Mobile Layer with the Google Location Services "Google Places" API, then the Mobile layer sends the information to the Platform, and the platform handles the naming conventions of certain elements of the Address to be exposed to thir parties, is by using "Mappings" containing the specific format required for that Atttirubute in the Third Party

For Example, for the request to the Provider of ID varlidation, Idology, the State needs to be a Two Letter ISO, so a Mapping is created of the states for each countries that the platform handles, specifically for Idology, with the name of the state, and the format the provider needs it (two letter ISO). Then when the mobile layer sends the Address elements, from Google Places, the element is identified in this mapping and sent to the provider with the correct format.

The same happens with all the Providers that require elements from the Address in the Platform integration, the platform creates mappings with the element, and then those maps are used to match the address element, such as the state, with the specific element sent by the mobile layer.

When more countries started to being added, and this mean handling a variety of Address formats from Google Location Services, It was identified that for certain countries, certain Google Places Attributes are returned in the specific locale attributes, and not in English.

This created a problem with the mappings, because the element retrieved by mobile was not matching any of the elements of the mapping.

This problem at this moment is affecting specifically the mapping of the State, since the Platform extracts this information from the State "Short Name" that can be retrieved by google in a variety of languages.

**Implementation Definitions**
It was defined that the accurate way to obtain a correct description of the short name of the state for a given country, would be by using the the official language of the country that the address is being requested from, when processing the Address.
To Achieve this, and to decouple this solution from the different dependencies that this mechanism has (the different systems that invoke the platform) it was defined that the address needed to be identified first using the geolocation elements: latitude and longitude, this is, not relying in the address elements retrieved by any entity that delivered it, but obtainint the address altogheter again.
The mechanism willwork as follows
*   Obtain the latitude and longitude elements from the origina Address Capture
*   with the latitude and longitude invoke the reverse location API from Google
curl --location '[https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/geocode/json?key=AIzaSyDr7nh71YUz3hYHGtxTQIX8-sP\_eiOL6Xo&latlng=21.8446366%2C-102.3042548&language=es'](https://maps.googleapis.com/maps/api/geocode/json?key=AIzaSyDr7nh71YUz3hYHGtxTQIX8-sP_eiOL6Xo&latlng=21.8446366%2C-102.3042548&language=es%27)

[

developers.google.com

https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/geocoding/requests-reverse-geocoding?hl=es-419

](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/geocoding/requests-reverse-geocoding?hl=es-419)

*   from the Reverse Geolocation information (JSON)
    *   Identify the element location type that corresponds with the value GEOMETRIC CENTRE
    *   within the element identify the administrative\_area\_1
    *   that value needs to map the value in the State "Translator" for each Provider
        *   The State translator is the JSON that is stored in the Platform Configuration Files and maps the state short name with the value required for each Provider with the format / length required for each provider

**Note from TA:**
as per September of 2025 the administrative\_area\_1 obtanied from an address provided in the platform, will be the first element obtained independently of the location type (this is, the location type is not any longer relevant

# Handle non standarized Address

Depending on many elements in the coutnries the handling of the Address may or may not have a standarized pattern
In certain countries the structured pattern will have the traditional elements:
*   street name + number
*   Vicinity / Locality / Municipal Division
*   City
*   Zip Code
*   Province / State

But in other countries this could be a mixed pattern
*   no street name, only city or neighbourhood
*   no zip code known to the population

And finally other countries could have traditionally _a different way_ to identify an address
*   a geografic reference = "the house by the lake"
*   a reference to another known place = "the building next to the farmacy"
*   a local reference = "in front of the house that belongs to Maria and Pedro"

The current Address Capture in the Mobile APPS, covers the more structured Address Capture.

With more countries coming into the Numi Solution, it is necessary to be able to incorporate other ways to address Capture to cover the needs of the users that are defining their address in "non structred " ways.

See reference document that explains for Latin America the Advise on how to capture the Address based on the different coverage of structured information that may be available in the culture and the knowledge of the population:

[AddressPercountryGoogle.pdf](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/c7f01c04-64ae-491d-a545-e2741a5fed6f/AddressPercountryGoogle.pdf)

For te time Being, the way to handle non standarized address will be as follows:
*   allow the user to enter a free-text with the most accurate description of the address
*   allow the user to drop the pin in the map

This _different way_ of Address Capture needs to be defined by country configuration, this is, depending on the country, this specialized address capture may or may not be necesary

The task for implementing this feature in RAAs is the following Private ([https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-5125](https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-5125))

# CIP Missing Elements 2 / Otros

Reunión de Puesta en común de CIP ENHACEMENTS NECESARIOS PARA LOS PROBLEMAS ENCONTRADOS EN PROD de SACBE - 22-01-2021
###   

### 1) Escneario: el usuario cargó mal su nombre (no es su nombre legal que se usa en el cálculo del curp)
*   Situación Actual:
*   \---- Cuando está subiendo a nivel 2, el calculo de curp tira error porque el nombre no es les llega, tira Soft Fail, Se le pide al usuario cambiar los datos
*   \---- En al Soft Fail Personal Data actual los datos editables son:
*   \-------- Email
*   \-------- Fecha de Nacimiento
*   \-------- Lugar de Nacimiento
*   \---- Cuando el problema surge por el nombre, al no permitir editar el nombre, actualmente , el usuario queda en un estatus de soft fail indedinido sin poder ser "rescatado"
*   Propuesta de Solución:
*   \---- En el proceso de CIP ante una falla específica de éste tipo debe ofrecerse al usuario la posbilidad de _editar su nombre_ junto con el resto de los datos personals
*   \----- cambio de mobile y backend
*   \----- debe incorporarse la logica para permitir ingresar el nombre y el apellido en este caso (UI)
*   \----- debe recalcularse el curp con los datos ingresados por el suuario (backend)
*   \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* PREMISA: ESTO SOLO ES POSIBLE PORQUE EL USUARIO AUN NO TIENE UENTA BANCARIA \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
### Escenario: El proceso de SCAN no puede obtenerse correctamente el CURP (por falla en el OCR que confunde caracterres, ej B x R=)
*   Situación Actual:
*   Como el CURP escaneado no coincdide con el curp calculado, esto tira hard fail
*   \---- El hard fail es "injusto" si es un problema del SCAN
*   \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\* PREMISA: ESTO SOLO ES POSIBLE PORQUE EL USUARIO AUN NO TIENE UENTA BANCARIA \*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
## ESTOS DOS PUNTOS ANTERIORES DEBEN SER APROBADOS POR CUMPLIMIENTO ----------------------------------

1. SSN / CURP REPETIDOS = HARD FAIL
2. LIMITES DE LOS PROVEEDORES (LIMITES INFERIORES/ GALILEO / GREEN DOT) COMO SE VALIDAN EN LA UI - AGREGAR LO QUE FALTA
## Nuevo Requerimiento:

2. \--- Para mejorar el proceso de CIP de soft fail de matcheo de dirección

# Security Breaches

## Contexto
Reunión a cuenta de los problemas de Loads que en apariencia son “fraudulentos” en SACBE Prod
## Participantes
Lionel-Hector-Oscar-Sergio-Alfredo-Vanesa
## Topicos
*   **Etapas de Idenficación “Lineas de Defensa”**
*   **Identificación**
    *   Controles en el SignUp
    *   Falta mecanismo de identificar el nombre que dio el usuario con el nombre que tiene la “Telco”
        *   Una parte del Nombre hace match (apellido)
        *   Se baja la probabilidad de que el usuario sea fraudulento
    *   **Face match**
        *   deberia incoprporarse la validación de face match contra el ID
    *   **Duplicados**
        *   una persona con el mismo National ID no puede tener 2 cuentas (2 usuarios con el mismo CURP, 2 usuarios con el mismo SSN, etc)
        *   no podría tener 2 identidades en el mismo device (SIM Card)
        *   no puede tener diferentes
    *   **Geolocalizacion**
*   **Account ownership verification**
    *   que metodos de Fondeo estan usando los usuarios
    *   las cuentas deben ser del usuario
    *   **Para Tarjetas**
        *   **Card Validation**
            *   que pasa si en Tabapay se pone un numero de Tarjeta que no hace match con el nombre
        *   **CVV Volatil**
            *   almacenamiento en VGS es volátil
            *   despues de cierto tiempo volver a pedir el cvv
            *   implicancias de UX
        *   **Uso de Tarjetas Robadas**
            *   Para asegurarse que no se estén haciendo loads con tarjetas robadas
                *   Nombre valido (ingresado por el usuario)
                *   Telefono valido para el nombre (el que tiene la telco vs ingresdo por el usuario)
                *   Nombre en la tarjeta valido (el nombre escaneado)
                    *   permitir Editar
*   **Reglas de control de Fraude (comportamiento)**
    *   frecuencias
    *   montos
    *   cantidades
    *   beneficiarios
*   **Big Data Analysis**
    *   Monitor/Robot
    *   Identificación de Patrones de comportamiento fraudulentos
*   **Identificación de fraudulentos**
    *   bloqueo
    *   persecución
    *   **Identificacion de Fraudulentos**
        *   bloquear al usuario
        *   enviar a “hablar con Soporte”
            *   El App bloquea la cuenta
            *   Galileo bloquea el plastico
        *   Enviar un “poison pill” al device
            *   Tener listas negras de la plataforma para:
                *   validar los SIM
                *   validar los SSN
                *   validar los numeros
            *   Puede ser “quitado de la lista” por compliance
**Otros puntos**
*   Finger Print del Usuario
    *   no es factible hacerlo “en casa”

## NOTAS DE HECTOR
*   No duplicados
*   Solo un usuario por Tel
*   Poison Pill (apagar el app)
*   SIM Card management (1 usuario)
*   Verificación de datos con fuentes alternas (Tel)
*   Control para duplicados
*   Face Recognition
*   Solo tarjetas del usuario (User Account Ownership Verification)
*   Micro depósitos (Paypal)
*   Lectura digital de tarjetas
*   CVV Management
*   Monitoreo de transacciones
*   Limits Velocity
*   Geolocalización
*   Validación de tarjetas
*   Big data Analysis
*   AI
*   Bloquear cuentas
*   Listas negras (SIM, Users, tarjetas)

# Exchange Rate, Markup and Fees Handling

Se tiene la siguiente Epica: Exchange Rate, Markup and Exchange Fees Handling [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/epic.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-485)[HWAY-485](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-485) Backlog
Se deben identificar las tareas necesarias para definir tasks y stories, de acuerdo a las siuigentes notas
## Notas Reunión Identificacion de Problemáticas de Excuange Rate y Markup
## Primer Issue

*   hay paises que pueden recibir dinero en mas de una moneda, por ejemplo la moneda local y USD
**En la arquitectura actual**
*   el corredor no define una "moneda" en la implemetnación actual
*   existe un archivo de configuración a nivel mobile, que define la moneda, pero no permite definir mas de una moneda, porque es una configuración para una entidad unica (no permite mas de una entrada)
### Solución
*   se debe implementar una solución para permitir que se utilicen multiples monedas de destino para un pais (cambios en UI)
*   Not MVP
## Segundo Issue
*   existe una fee para OCT cross border, dicho fee es "marginal" porque se transfiere el fee de debit card a el gateway de pagos, Cybersource, directamente, y "no hay ganancia" para Leap
*   el markup es la diferencia entre el cambio oficial y el cambio que se el aplica a la transaccion. Esos centavos de difrencia son la ganancia para Leap.
*   El fee se le paga al gateway y el markup es la ganancia de Leap
*   la ganancia utilizando el exchange rate está en el markup de la diferencia entre USD y la moneda local, si se tuviera una transaccion cross border USD a USD el markup se pierde
*   no hay forma de manejar el markup cuando el pais y origien y el pais de destino tienen la misma moneda. Y ademas de eso hay que pagarle el fee al gateway. En este escenaario Leap está perdiendo dinero.
### Solución
*   se debería cubrir esa perdida de markup con una fee, que nos permita tener la ganancia necesaria sin recurrir al markup
*   Esa fee debe ser comunicada al usuario
Diferente moneda origen que destino: Si tengo manera de manejar el markup; no necesito una fee extra.
Misma moneda origen y destino: No tengo manera de manejar el markup: Debe generarse una fee
## Tercer Issue
*   se debe validar si el Quote Transacion está tomando el tipo de cambio de Visa
*   se debe asegurar que el tipo de cambio del Quote Transacion es "en tiempo real" y que NO lo esté tomando de la tabla de Exchange Rate ya que esta ultima se actualiza con un JOB cuya frecuencia de desconoce
*   el Quote transaction funciona en una transaccion que se completa (es decir tiene el medio de pago destino definido, porque el usuario destinatario existe), con un pequeño margen de riesgo el tiempo en que tarda en hacerse la transaccion
*   Tenemos la problemática de si el usuario receptor NO EXISTE, porque el tipo de cambio en varios dias puede haber cambiado
### Solucion
*   si el usuario no existe o no tiene medio de pago, existe un riesgo de diferencia del tipo de cambio del transacion quote, se debe generar un fee como "colchon" para cubrir esta situación.
## Posibles Implementaciones
**A nivel Corridors**
*   Agregar a nivel corridor la moneda de origen y destino y la fee -si aplica-
*   Un pais de destino que permita recibir en dos monedas va a tener dos corredores
**A nivel Reglas**
*   Mecanismo de Reglas para Agregar al Quote Transaction para definir si va o no a agregar un fee dependiendo de la moneda destino
## Video
## Primer reunión de identificacion de Requerimientos

[exchange rate por pais. markup, exchange fee.mp4](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/a22120bd-088e-4244-8a05-0df10e04db70/exchange%20rate%20por%20pais.%20markup%2C%20exchange%20fee.mp4)

# OTP External Service

## General Details
It is necessary for certain scenarios to provide an OTP Service for external validation of the number with the OTP
*   As Per June 2020 this service is going to be implemented for SACBE

## Technical Details
*   API to send OTP
    *   Receives: a mobile phone number
    *   Returns: OTP
*   APi to Validate OTP
    *   Receives: mobile number and OTP
    *   Returns:
        *   Validates if the OTP belongs to the mobile number
        *   If the Preivious Validation is true sends an SMS with the Attribution Link to Download the App

# RDF File Usage Analysis

**Escenarios de diferencia en el Balance:**
1. Tx en la Plataforma nuestra que Galileo si registró (ej time out, se asume que galileo no lo hizo, se rollbackea en nuestra Plataforma, pero Galileo la ejecutó)
*   La operacion ya está cancelada de nuestro lado
*   Se debería comunicar a Galileo que haga rollback de la Tx a su vez (adjustment?)
2. Tx que ocurrió fuera de la plataforma (Auth) y por algun motivo no llegó a través de los eventos
*   debemos conciliar la diferencia de Tx
3. Tx que ocurrió en la plataforma y no fue registrada en Galileo
*   debemos conciliar la diferencia de Tx

**Estados de Actualizacion del balance (actual)**
*   Cuando la plataforma registra un movimiento hacia Galileo - Galileo devuelve el balance actualizado
*   Cuando Galileo ejecuta un evento externo - Galileo devuelve el balance actualizado

**Notas**
se necesita revisar a nivel implementacion el manejo de timeout con las llamadas a los proveedores
*   Mecanismo para validar que mi saldo se actualizo hasta cierto evento de Galileo
*   Certeza de que el saldo es el correcto
*   Ultimo movimiento de RDF File > ultimo movimiento Plataforma
*   Then tomar el saldo del RDF(puede ser?)

# Deployment Local Environment

# Software
*   Visual Studio community 2017
*   .Net framework 4.6.2
# LeapCental (LeapCentralVNext)
*   Binaries from Source Code:
1. Leap Central vNext code can be obtained by executing the following git command:
2. 3. **git clone**
4. [http://gitlab.leapfactor.net/platform/leapcentralvnext.git](http://gitlab.leapfactor.net/platform/leapcentralvnext.git)
5. 6. If any problem arise with the SSL certificate, the following command will deactivate the verification (execute it and try the previous one again):
7. 8. git config http.sslVerify false
9. 10. It would be ideal to clone it in C:\\LF\\ , where the **LCNumiInitializer** will look for the binaries by default, but in any case the folder can be customized later on.
11. Checkout the branch "Develop2018"
12. Open the LeapcentralNext.sln file with VS and compile it.
13. _Before compiling, please check that you have the LeapFactor NuGet Package Source configured in your Visual Studio_
    1. [http://packages.leapfactor.net:81/nuget/default](http://packages.leapfactor.net:81/nuget/default)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/d9c0ff4e-982c-477c-9c95-0bd789ff04a9/image2019-11-26_7-28-5.png)

1. 

*   Data Stores
The LCNumiInitializer will look for the data folder in: C:\\Data\\LCNext .
Nevertheless, the path can be changed by modifying the key DataBasePath from the App.config of the project .
*   RavenDB:
1. Download RavenDB-Build-30155 from [https://ravendb.net/downloads](https://ravendb.net/downloads) and extract it in C:\\Data\\LCNext\\Raven
    1. Optional Raven zip: [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1utffs7\_QRpt\_L3KOoqQlQ2HFPrJ5QsEi/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1utffs7_QRpt_L3KOoqQlQ2HFPrJ5QsEi/view?usp=sharing)
2. Set the value for the key Raven/Port from the Raven.Server.exe.config file to 8081
*   GES:
3. Download Event Store version 4.0.1 from [https://geteventstore.com/downloads/](https://geteventstore.com/downloads/) and extract it in C:\\Data\\LCNext\\GES
    1. Optional GES rar version v4.0.1 on google drive: [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Jj8MpTi2rRN4lsOsxXuQ2J7QdxQagu79/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Jj8MpTi2rRN4lsOsxXuQ2J7QdxQagu79/view?usp=sharing)
4. Run it with the following configuration:
Db: "C:/data/LCNext/GES/Data"
IntIp: 127.0.0.1
ExtIp: 127.0.0.1
IntTcpHeartbeatTimeout: 20000
ExtTcpHeartbeatTimeout: 20000
IntTcpHeartbeatInterval: 15000
ExtTcpHeartbeatInterval: 15000
RunProjections: "All"
It could be written in a ges.config file and then run it with the config parameter: EventStore.ClusterNode.exe --config=ges.config
*   Cache
1. Download the latest version of NCache (Open Source version) from: [http://www.alachisoft.com/download-ncache.htm](http://www.alachisoft.com/download-ncache.htm) l and install it
    1. [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1\_T6q\_Tf9rWcV5tTngOjhAcsoHoUBYyim/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1_T6q_Tf9rWcV5tTngOjhAcsoHoUBYyim/view?usp=sharing)
2. For a local node (127.0.0.1):
    1. Set the key NCacheServer.BindToIP from the file: **NCache\\bin\\service\\Alachisoft.NCache.Service.exe.config** to **127.0.0.1**
    2. Set every IP from the file: **NCache\\config\\client.ncconf** to **127.0.0.1**
    3. **If you are in a firewalled environment please open the following ports:**
        1. **TCP 9800**
        2. **TCP 8250**
        3. **TCP 8251**
    4. source: [https://www.alachisoft.com/resources/tips/how-to-configure-ncache-in-firewalled-environment.html](https://www.alachisoft.com/resources/tips/how-to-configure-ncache-in-firewalled-environment.html)
    5. 
3. Run in the command line: **Startcache /s localhost myCache**
4. Verify that myCache is in status “Running”: **listcaches /s localhost**
5. Note: LeapCental will create (at the first time it run) and use a new cache (LCNextCache) using as default the myCache configuration

*       *   LCNumiInitializer
    *   
1. Check App.config file:
    1. LCBinariesPath
    2. DataBasePath
    3. ViewsDBUrl
2. Compile & run and follow console app intructions
3. Copy the Api Key exposed on the console app
# Numi
*   Binaries from Source Code:
1. The Numi TPE code can be obtained by executing the following git command. Put it in C:\\LF\\NumiTPE
2. 3. **git clone**
4. [**http://gitlab.leapfactor.net/numi/backend/numitpe.git**](http://gitlab.leapfactor.net/numi/backend/numitpe.git)
5. 6. Replace in the solution the Api Key with the one generated on the previous step
7. Rebuild Solution

*   NumiInitializer
1. Run NumiInitializer
2. Follow steps (on questions type "y" and press Enter)

*   Numi.Migrations
1. Run Numi.Migrations
