# AWS Accounts

# Steps

# AWS
**Account**
1. Configure aws account if necesary
2. Create serverless Deployment user
3. Create serverless Event system deployment bucket
4. Create serverless NumiAws deployment bucket 

**EventSystem**
1. Configure stage if necesary
2. configure endpoints url if necesary
3. Configure logger if necesary
4. Configure deployment user
5. Deploy
    1. Configure issuer and gateway in authorization lambda 
    2. Deploy CommonResources
    3. Copy lambda url and set the NesUrl in Configuration Manager
    4. Deploy Analytics
    5. Deploy all the Adapters

**NumiAWS**
....
# Firebase
1. After creating the firebase Project
    *   Activate Sign-in Method by Email/password (Only Email/Password)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/35f6851f-59c1-4208-822a-5d13ac91a285/image2022-1-28_10-33-28.png)
*       *   Get Web API Key from Project Settings/General and create a file with apiKey and set name firebase-\[projectFirebaseName\]-apiKey.txt
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/2a70dcb5-de72-4b40-b030-0df8f65f7559/image2022-1-28_10-57-13.png) 
\[projectFirebaseName\] equals to Project ID in Firebase
for example:  ![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/02d5d18f-2fbd-42d1-8b9c-30f640a75a43/image2021-5-31_16-56-51.png)
the file name will be: **firebase-tzuneprod-apiKey.txt**

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/b88bed66-90a9-438e-91b7-9822b2fea6ac/image2022-1-28_11-16-50.png)
Go to link Manage service account permissions from Project Settings/Service accounts. In Google Cloud Platform/Service Account create a new key for firebase-adminsdk. Use format json and replace firebase-\[projectFirebaseName\]-cert.json
*   
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/05cb5510-c2ea-4b87-9545-09caf334190e/image2022-1-28_10-42-57.png)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/5cb033f2-6026-420e-8bd5-c9a1740361a8/image2022-1-28_10-40-51.png)

*   Create database cloud firestore (production mode, nam5 (us-central) location)
    1. Define database rules for read, write, delete
    2. Define **public** read only corridor\_virtual
*   Enable Cloud Messaging AP
    1. Go to Project Settings / Cloud Messaging, you can see "Cloud Messaging API (Legacy)" disabled
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/e495a878-fd81-4d4d-a4bd-9e99bac65eb8/image2022-12-22_16-38-56.png)
2\. Then, go to "Manage API in Goggle Cloud Console" to enable Cloud Messaging
3\. After enabling the API go back to firebase and refresh then you will get the FCM _Server key_ and _Sender ID_ for messaging
4\. You will use _Server key_ and _Sender ID_ in **LCMigration** section.
*   Configure firebase in central. In the LCNext Config keys:
    1. **FirestoreProject** use the de firebase Project ID value (From Firebase console, go to Project Settings/General)  
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/02d5d18f-2fbd-42d1-8b9c-30f640a75a43/image2021-5-31_16-56-51.png)

1.     1. 
2. **FirebaseApiKeyFile** set file name firebase-\[projectFirebaseName\]-apiKey.txt (includes Web API Key)
3. **FirebaseServiceAccountFile** set filename firebase-\[projectFirebaseName\]-cert.json
4. **FirestoreServiceAccountFile** set filename firebase-\[projectFirebaseName\]-cert.json
*   Generate plist and give to mobile (bundles)
    1. Create firebase ios app and download GoogleService-Info.plist
    2. Create firebase android app and download google-service.json
*   For jenkins deploy, the files firebase-\[projectFirebaseName\]-cert.json and firebase-\[projectFirebaseName\]-apiKey.txt should be located in directory C:\\tools\\LCNextresources
*   The bundles defined in 3, must be added in LCMigracion **assets** according to application mobile.
    1. Define \[environment\]Bundles.json
    2. fcm folder (firebase cloud messaging) define json file by environment. Set **gcmAuthToken** using _Server key_ value, gcmSenderId using _Sender ID_ from Firebase console/Project Settings/Cloud messaging.
    3. apn folder can be empty
*   more details..

# Server
**Open Ports**
443 - LeapCentral
440 - TPEServiceApi and Event listener
80 - GeoService
If the server is a AWS Virtual Machine, open the ports on the VM configuration on AWS Console.

**SSL Certificate**
Install the certificate and associate this with the 443 port

**Configure Host**
In the host file (C:\\Windows\\System32\\drivers\\etc) set:
<<local ip>> <<environment url>>

**Configuration Manager**
1. Validate with develop team, which environment use to clone the config in order to speed up the process.
2. Fix the url to match the new environment

**Jenkins**
1. Configure the server Agent
2. Generate deployment jobs
    1. Clone an existing one
    2. Validate an change the REVISION parameter default value
    3. Change the Agent with the new one
    4. Adjust the Workspace directory
    5. On Build job trigger change the TARGET parameter with the one configured on the Configuration Manager as Environment
    6. Change the email Subject
3. At the Build job:
    1. Add at TARGET the new environment
    2. Add a new condition to contemplate the new environment in config replace

**NCache**
1. Install NCache
2. Make sure myCache is running

**Proxy**
If the server is for develop, QA and demo add access of this server to the proxy

**LCNumiInitializer (**[**LCNextInitializer-AudienceFixed.zip**](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/download/attachments/45976618/LCNextInitializer-AudienceFixed.zip?api=v2)**)**
Is very important to run this on the server and be sure that the communication to LC is not routed by proxy or similars
1. Check App.config file:
    1. \*\_ServiceUrl (use https & server url ej: [dev.sacbepayment.com](http://dev.sacbepayment.com))
    2. LCBinariesPath ("C:\\Numi\\LCNext\\2\\" the last '\\' is very important)
    3. DataBasePath (this must point to the folder where GES and Raven are located)
    4. ViewsDBUrl (Raven url)
    5. LCAudience ([https://gateway](https://gateway).{domain}.com)
    6. GESExtPortHttp (GES Port HTTP available)
    7. IsDevelopment (when true, cleans raven and cleans GES)
    8. MoneyExchangeBC\_ServiceURL  must be equal to the value in NumiPTE / NumiMoneyExchangeBC\_Main\_REST\_ServiceURL (replace http:// with ss:// or https:// with sss://)
    9. SubscribersBC\_ServiceURL must be equal to the value in NumiPTE / SubscriberBC\_Main\_REST\_ServiceURL (replace http:// with ss:// or https:// with sss://)
    10. WalletBC\_ServiceURL must be equal to the value in NumiPTE / WalletBC\_Main\_REST\_ServiceURL (replace http:// with ss:// or https:// with sss://)
2. Run and follow console app instructions
3. Copy the Api Key exposed on the console app
Note: In case of Unauthorized, edit hosts file, because the initializer runs in localhost.

**NumiInitializer**
1. Check App.config file:
    1. ViewsDBUrl (Raven url)
    2. LCServiceUrl
    3. LCAPIKey
2. Run NumiInitializer
3. Follow steps (on questions type "y". Do not press Enter, the program will close at the if you do that)

**Api Key Configurations**
Update LCApiKey on All configurations that contains a LCApiKey.

**LCMigration**
1. Update config file according environment 
    1. LCServiceUrl
    2. LCApiKey
    3. ViewsDBUrl (Raven url)
    4. Enviroment: Valid values: local | dev | qa | staging | prod
    5. BrandName: for example sacbe | tzune | hway
    6. StartDate, (format yyyy-MM-dd) Default value "2021-05-17"
2. Assets
    1. In brand application directory includes files:
    2. In apn directory should be located certificates according bundles for ios
    3. In fcm (firebase cloud messaging) directory should be located \[environment\]Settings.json file
        1. Set values for gcmAuthToken, gcmSenderId. You can get from firebase Project settings / Cloud Messaging in Project credentials section.
    4. Define \[environment\]Bundles.json, where you indicate storeInstances, appVersion and apn cetificate and password for ios device, which will be searched assets/\[brandApp\]/apn directory
3. ...
**Numi Migrations**
1. Check configuration manager 
    1. AffiliatesBCEndopintUrl must be equal to the value in NumiPTE / AffiliatesBC\_Main\_REST\_ServiceURL (replace http:// with ss:// or https:// with sss://)
    2. WalletsBCEndpointUrl must be equal to the value in NumiPTE / WalletBC\_Main\_REST\_ServiceURL (replace http:// with ss:// or https:// with sss://)
2. Deploy Jenkins job.
3. Run Numi.Migrations manually in the server
**Numi AWS**
     1.Deploy Buckets
     2. configure access key and secret Key
    3. create user with

**NumiTPE (IEP / DeploymentUnit)**
1. Run Jenkins jobs
2. Start the DeploymentUnit manually in the server

**NumiConsoleUI (Support console)**
1.Create a site in IIS for the support console. For example **supportqa**
2.In the deploy job fo jenkins set the site name
> "C:\\Windows\\System32\\inetsrv\\appcmd" set site supportqa -\[path='/'\].\[path='/'\].physicalPath:"%WORKSPACE%\\%BUILD\_NUMBER%"
3.Create a virtual directory in IIS for the user files (images of TPEServiceAPI) and set the URL in Configuration Manager NumiConsoleUI for USERFILES\_URL.

**Run Services**
1. Run all services jobs

# Develop

**git clone**
**https://\[user\]@**[**scm.leapfactor.net**](http://scm.leapfactor.net/)**:8443/r/**[**numi/NumiTPE.git**](https://sergio.polizzi@scm.leapfactor.net:8443/r/numi/NumiTPE.git)

# Event System V2 (SNS)

# Description
Numi event system to route event communications through Numi services. 

# Diagram
#### [https://app.diagrams.net/#G1XXTwDbbGjp5P6mYyfgME\_wOgXhCY9bs9](https://app.diagrams.net/#G1XXTwDbbGjp5P6mYyfgME_wOgXhCY9bs9)

# Feature
### Retries
On event communication failure the system will wait <<WaitingTime>> and retry <<Retries>> then the system will put the event on the dead letter bucket
##### WaitingTime
On the adapter queue set: VisibilityTimeout: 300 (Seconds)
##### Retries
On the adapter queue set: maxReceiveCount: 7
### Communication Execution time
The lambda use to communication has an execution time out of <<ExecutionTimeout>> and the http communication has a time out of <<HttpTimeout>> (default: 20s)
#####  ExecutionTimeout
On the push lambda set: timeout: 30 (Seconds)
##### HttpTimeout
on the push lambda set: TIMEOUT: 20000 (Milliseconds) inside environment config

# Adapter Creation

1.Creation of adapter folder tree. Within “EventSystemV2/Adapters/” folder you must create a folder with the name of the new adapter and inside her you must copy the "handler.ts" and "serverless.yml" files of another existing adapter (this is for take as basis).

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/c3b986c2-1af1-4358-b925-bfa3896d73b1/Imagen%201.png)

[2.In](http://2.In) the "serverless.yml" file of the new adapter you must configure his service name for AWS. This must be done in "name" property within "service" section, for example :

**_service:_**
  **_name: events-system-v2-affiliate-api-adapter_**

It is important that the service name is not equal than another existent module name.

3.Variables for the Queue (SQS), DLQ (dead letter queue) and Dead Letter Bucket. Go to "environment" section within "provider" section. There are 3 variables you must modify (their name and value). These variables serve to name the "Queue", the "DLQ" and the "Dead Letter Bucket" of the new adapter, for example for the "AffiliateApi" adapter:

**_AFFILIATE\_API\_SQS: ${self:provider.environment.APIDYNPREFIX}-affiliate-api-sqs_**
**_AFFILIATE\_API\_DEAD\_LETTER\_SQS: ${self:provider.environment.APIDYNPREFIX}-affiliate-api-dead-letter-sqs_**
**_AFFILIATE\_API\_DEAD\_LETTER\_BUCKET: ${self:provider.environment.APIDYNPREFIX}-affiliate-api-dead-letter-bucket_**

4.Permissions to put objects in the "Dead Letter Bucket". In the "iamRoleStatements" that is into "provider" section, there is a structure (statement) with an "Action" property with "s3:PutObject" like value, in the "Resource" property of that structure you must use the variable created in step 3 to name the "Dead Letter Bucket", for example:

**_Resource:_**
  **_- arn:aws:s3:::${self:provider.environment.AFFILIATE\_API\_DEAD\_LETTER\_BUCKET}/\*_**

5.DLQ configuration. In the "Resources" section within "resources" section you must look for a resource with type "AWS::SQS::Queue" that HAS NOT the "RedrivePolicy" property. Its structure is like following example:

**_AffiliateApiDeadLetterQueue:_**
  **_Type: "AWS::SQS::Queue"_**
  **_Properties:_**
    **_QueueName: ${self:provider.environment.AFFILIATE\_API\_DEAD\_LETTER\_SQS}_**
    **_MessageRetentionPeriod: 1209600_**

In this structure you must configure the "DLQ" with a unique resource id like principal node (in the example is "AffiliateApiDeadLetterQueue"). In the "QueueName" property within "Properties" you must use the variable created in step 3 to name the "DLQ".

6.Queue configuration. In the "Resources" section within "resources" section you must look for a resource with type "AWS::SQS::Queue" that HAS the "RedrivePolicy" property. Its structure is like the following example:

**_AffiliateApiQueue:_**
  **_Type: "AWS::SQS::Queue"_**
  **_Properties:_**
    **_QueueName: ${self:provider.environment.AFFILIATE\_API\_SQS}_**
    **_VisibilityTimeout: 90_**
    **_MessageRetentionPeriod: 86400 # default 345600(4 days)_**
    **_RedrivePolicy:_**
      **_deadLetterTargetArn: !GetAtt AffiliateApiDeadLetterQueue.Arn_**
      **_maxReceiveCount: 5_**

In this structure you must configure the "Queue" with a unique resource id like principal node (in this example is "AffiliateApiQueue"). In the "QueueName" property within "Properties" you must use the variable created in step 3 to name the "Queue".
In the "deadLetterTargetArn" property within "RedrivePolicy" is used the "GetAtt" function to get the "Arn" of the "DLQ" (configured in step 5), so that you must modify here using the unique resource id configured in the "DLQ”.

7.Create topics which the new adapter listen. If the new adapter listens only existent topics (topics already used by the others adapters) continue to step 8, if not view "Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-29077](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-29077))" for every one of the new events.

8.Configure Queue policy. In the "Resources" section within "resources" section you must search for a resource with type "AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy". Its structure is like the following example:

**_AffiliateApiQueuePolicy:_**
  **_Type: AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy_**
  **_Properties:_**
    **_PolicyDocument:_**
      **_Statement:_**
        **_- Sid: "allow-sns-messages"_**
          **_Effect: Allow_**
          **_Principal: "\*"_**
          **_Resource: !GetAtt AffiliateApiQueue.Arn_**
          **_Action: "SQS:SendMessage"_**
          **_Condition:_**
            **_ArnEquals:_**
              **_"aws:SourceArn": \[_**
                **_'${self:provider.environment.OPERATION\_COMPLETED\_TOPIC\_REF}',_**
                **_'${self:provider.environment.USER\_CREATED\_TOPIC\_REF}'_**
              **_\]_**
    **_Queues:_**
      **_- Ref: AffiliateApiQueue_**

In this structure you must configure the policy with a unique resource id like principal node (in this example es "AffiliateApiQueuePolicy"). In the "Resource" property within "Statement" is used the "GetAtt" function to get the "Arn" of the "Queue" configured in step 6, so that you must modify here using the unique resource id configured in the "Queue".
In the "Queues" property within "Properties" is used the "Ref" function to get the reference of the "Queue" configured in step 6, so that you must modify here to use the unique resource id configured in the "Queue".
In the "ArnEquals" property that is into "Condition" property is possible to view there is an array that will be configured later, in this step you must left empty like is showed in the next example:

              **_"aws:SourceArn": \[_**
              **_\]_**

9.Configure initial topic subscription. In the "Resources" section within "resources" section you must look for a unique resource id with type "AWS::SNS::Subscription" (there is at least one). In this step, to start the adapter, if there are more than one with that type, delete them, that is, just leave one:

**_AffiliateApiQueueToOperationCompletedTopicSubscription:_**
  **_Type: "AWS::SNS::Subscription"_**
  **_Properties:_**
    **_Endpoint: !GetAtt AffiliateApiQueue.Arn_**
    **_Protocol: sqs_**
    **_RawMessageDelivery: true_**
    **_TopicArn: ${self:provider.environment.OPERATION\_COMPLETED\_TOPIC\_REF}_**

In this structure you must configure the subscription with a unique resource id like principal node (in this example is "AffiliateApiQueueToOperationCompletedTopicSubscription"). In the "Endpoint" property is used the "GetAtt" function to get the "Arn" of the "Queue" configured in step 6, so that you must modify here to use the unique resource id configured in the "Queue".
In the "TopicArn" property you must delete the current value because it will be configured in the next step.

10.Subscribe topics to new adapter. View "Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-29017](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-29017))" to configure the subscription of each topic of the new adapter.

11.Dead Letter Bucket configuration. In the "Resources" section within "resources" section you must look for a unique resource id with type "AWS::S3::Bucket". Its structure is like show the following example:

**_AffiliateApiDeadLetterBucket:_**
  **_Type: AWS::S3::Bucket_**
  **_Properties:_**
    **_BucketName: ${self:provider.environment.AFFILIATE\_API\_DEAD\_LETTER\_BUCKET}_**
    **_BucketEncryption:_**
      **_ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:_**
        **_- ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:_**
            **_SSEAlgorithm: AES256_**
    **_LifecycleConfiguration:_**
      **_Rules:_**
        **_- ExpirationInDays: 180_**
          **_Status: Enabled_**
  **_DeletionPolicy: Retain_**

In this structure you must configure the "Dead Letter Bucket" with a unique resource id like principal node (in this example is "AffiliateApiDeadLetterBucket"). In the "BucketName" property within "Properties" you must use the variable created in step 3 to name the "Dead Letter Bucket”.

12."pushData" function configuration. Go to "functions" section and look for the function with "handler.pushData" like handler value. Its structure is like the following example:

**_pushDataAffiliateApi:_**
  **_handler: handler.pushData_**
  **_environment:_**
    **_ENDPOINTS: '\["${file(../Common/endpoints-${opt:stage, self:provider.stage}/endpoints.yml):AFFILIATE\_API\_ENDPOINT}"\]'_**
  **_timeout: 30_**
  **_events:_**
    **_- sqs:_**
        **_arn: !GetAtt AffiliateApiQueue.Arn_**
        **_batchSize: 1_**

In this structure you must configure the function with a unique resource id like principal node (in this example is "pushDataAffiliateApi"). In the "arn" property within "sqs" is used the "GetAtt" function to get the "Arn" of the "Queue" configured in step 6, so that you must modify here to use the unique resource id configured to the "Queue”.
Inside "environment" property there is a "ENDPOINTS" variable with an array as value, that array contains the endpoints that the adapter will call, for more detail about how create endpoints view "Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-29057](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-29057))”.

13."putDeadLetterEvent" function configuration. Go to "functions" section and look for a function with "handler.putDeadLetterEvent" like handler value. Its structure es like show the next example:

**_putDLQEvtAffiliateApi:_**
  **_handler: handler.putDeadLetterEvent_**
  **_environment:_**
    **_BUCKET\_NAME: ${self:provider.environment.AFFILIATE\_API\_DEAD\_LETTER\_BUCKET}_**
  **_timeout: 30_**
  **_events:_**
   **_- sqs:_**
        **_arn: !GetAtt AffiliateApiDeadLetterQueue.Arn_**
        **_batchSize: 1_**

In this structure you must configure the function with a unique resource id like principal node (in this example is "putDLQEvtAffiliateApi"). In the "arn" property within "sqs" is used the "GetAtt" function to get the "Arn" of the "DLQ" configured in step 6, so that you must modify here to use the unique resource id configured to the "DLQ".
Inside "environment" property there is a "BUCKET\_NAME" variable that must have as value the variable created in step 3 (it was created to name the "Dead Letter Bucket").

After you have configured the elements described from step 1 to 13, you must deploy the adapter. To do it, is necessary go to the new adapter module path (for example "EventSystemV2/ WalletApiAdapter/" to “WalletApiAdapter”) in a terminal and execute the next command:

**_$ serverless deploy_**

# Create and add a new Endpoint to an adapter

1\. Create a new endpoint. You must create a variable in the "endpoints.yml" file that correspons to every one of the several existing stages (dev, qa, etc.). For example, to create a variable for "dev" stage, you must go to “EventSystemV2/Adapters/Common/endpoints-**dev**/” folder, and the path suffix will change depends on stage. Whitin every folder, for every stage, it must exist an "endpoints.yml" file containing the variables with the existing endpoints, for example :

**_CASH\_NETWORK\_INTERFACE\_API\_ENDPOINT:_** [**_https://dev.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/handleevent_**](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/handleevent)
**_AFFILIATE\_API\_ENDPOINT:_** [**_http://dev.sacbepayments.com:440/numi/api/affiliates/handleevent_**](http://dev.sacbepayments.com:440/numi/api/affiliates/handleevent)
**_MONEY\_EXCHANGE\_BC\_ENDPOINT:_** [**_http://dev.sacbepayments.com:80/Numi/api/operations/handleevent_**](http://dev.sacbepayments.com:80/Numi/api/operations/handleevent)
**_OPERATION\_API\_ENDPOINT:_** [**_http://dev.sacbepayments.com:440/operations/handleevent/_**](http://dev.sacbepayments.com:440/operations/handleevent/)
**_STORE\_PAY\_API\_ENDPOINT:_** [**_https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/handleevent_**](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/handleevent)
**_STORE\_PAY\_INTERFACE\_API\_ENDPOINT:_** [**_https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface/handleevent_**](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface/handleevent)
**_WALLETS\_API\_ENDPOINT:_** [**_http://dev.sacbepayments.com:440/wallets/handleevent_**](http://dev.sacbepayments.com:440/wallets/handleevent)

So, for add a new endpoint you must add it below the last existing variable, you must respect the same variable name in the several "endpoints.yml" files for every stage (it’s clear that the value could be different by every stage).

**_…_**
**_WALLETS\_API\_ENDPOINT:_** [**_http://dev.sacbepayments.com:440/wallets/handleevent_**](http://dev.sacbepayments.com:440/wallets/handleevent)
**_WALLETS\_API\_ENDPOINT\_2:_** [**_http://dev.sacbepayments.com:440/wallets/handleevent2_**](http://dev.sacbepayments.com:440/wallets/handleevent2)

2\. Adding a new endpoint to an adapter. In the "serverless.yml" file of the module which add the new endpoint (for example "EventSystemV2/Adapters/WalletsApiAdapter/serverless.yml" for the "WalletsApiAapter" module) go to "functions" section and look for the function that has "handler.pushData" like handler value. In that function is an "ENDPOINTS" variable, within "environment" property, that contains an array where you must add the new variable created in step 1, for example :

**_ENDPOINTS: '\["${file(../Common/endpoints-${opt:stage, self:provider.stage}/endpoints.yml):WALLETS\_API\_ENDPOINT}","${file(../Common/endpoints-${opt:stage, self:provider.stage}/endpoints.yml):WALLETS\_API\_ENDPOINT\_2}"\]'_**

After have configured the elements described in step 1 and 2, you must deploy the module which was added the endpoint. To do it, is necessary go to the adapter module path (for example "EventSystemV2/ WalletApiAdapter/" for “WalletApi” adapter) in a terminal and execute the next command :

**_$ serverless deploy_**

# Event Creation

[1.In](http://1.In) the "serverless.yml" file of the "CommonResources" module, go to the "custom" section. There, create a variable with the name for the new topic, for example:

**_WALLET\_UPDATED\_TOPIC: ${self:provider.environment.APIDYNPREFIX}-wallet-updated-topic_**

[2.In](http://2.In) the same section, create another variable that will be used as the name of the "output" (step 5) that will store the reference to the new topic so that it can be referenced from other modules. An example of this variable creation is:

**_WALLET\_UPDATED\_TOPIC\_REF: ${self:provider.environment.APIDYNPREFIX}-wallet-updated-topic-ref_**

[3.In](http://3.In) the "Resources" section that is into "resources" section you must configure the topic with a unique resource id like principal node, and in the "TopicName" property we must use the variable created in step 1, for example:

**_WalletUpdatedTopic:_**
    **_Type: AWS::SNS::Topic_**
    **_Properties:_**
      **_TopicName: ${self:custom.WALLET\_UPDATED\_TOPIC}_**

[4.In](http://4.In) the "apiGatewayServiceProxies" section that is into "custom" section, it must be configured the proxy that will trigger the notification of the new topic.
In the "topicName" property of the proxy configuration is used the function "GetAtt" to get the topic name configured in step 3, for example:

**_\- sns:_**
    **_path: ${self:provider.environment.FUNCTION\_PATH\_PREFIX}/WalletUpdated_**
    **_method: post_**
    **_topicName: { 'Fn::GetAtt': \['WalletUpdatedTopic', 'TopicName'\] }_**
    **_cors: true_**
    **_authorizationType: 'CUSTOM'_**
    **_authorizerId: { Ref: 'DefaultAuthorizer' }_**

The "path" property value must have like suffix the event name **as will be used from the different services** (in this sample is "/WalletUpdated").

[5.In](http://5.In) the "Outputs" section that is into "resources" section, is necessary to configure the reference to the new topic, which will be used from adapters modules. This configuration must have a unique resource id like principal node. In the "Value" property will be done reference to the unique reference id of the new topic created in step 3, and in the "Name" property that is into "Export" it must be used the variable created in step 2, for example:

**_WalletUpdatedTopicRef:_**
  **_Value: !Ref WalletUpdatedTopic_**
  **_Export:_**
    **_Name: ${self:custom.WALLET\_UPDATED\_TOPIC\_REF}_**

After configuring the items described from step 1 to 5, is necessary to deploy the "CommonResources" module. To do it, is necessary go to the path “EventSystem2/CommonResources” in a terminal and execute the following command:

**_$ serverless deploy_**

6.Add new topic to “Analytics” module.

6.1. Go to "serverless.yml" file of "Analytics" module (EventSystemV2/Analytics). In that file, go to "custom" and create a new variable that store the reference to just created new topic in "CommonResources" module, for example:

**_WALLET\_UPDATED\_TOPIC\_REF: !ImportValue ${file(../CommonResources/serverless.yml):custom.WALLET\_UPDATED\_TOPIC\_REF}_**

6.2. In the "Resources" section that is into "resources" section, you must search for a resource with the type "AWS::SNS::Subscription" and copy it below all existing resources, so that it works as a basis for the subscription of the "putEventToBucket" function of the "Analytics" module to the new topic. Its structure is like the following example:

**_PutEventToBucketFunctionToWalletUpdatedTopicSubscription:_**
      **_Type: AWS::SNS::Subscription_**
      **_Properties:_**
        **_Endpoint: !GetAtt \[ PutEventToBucketLambdaFunction, 'Arn' \]_**
        **_Protocol: lambda_**
        **_TopicArn: ${self:custom.WALLET\_UPDATED\_TOPIC\_REF}_**

In this structure you must configure the subscription with a unique resource id like principal node (in the example is "PutEventToBucketFunctionToWalletUpdatedTopicSubscription"), if is used existing one it will be received an error when the module update deploy was done. In the "TopicArn" property it must be used the variable created in step 6.1. The other properties must be left with the values that were copied.

6.3. In the same "Resources" section now you must search for a resource with the type "AWS::Lambda::Permission" and copy it below all existing resources, so that works as a basis for the necessary permissions so that the new topic can call the "putEventToBucket" function. Its structure is like the following example:

**_WalletUpdatedTopicInvokeFunctionPermission:_**
      **_Type: AWS::Lambda::Permission_**
      **_Properties:_**
        **_Action: lambda:InvokeFunction_**
        **_Principal:_** [**_sns.amazonaws.com_**](http://sns.amazonaws.com)
        **_SourceArn: ${self:custom.WALLET\_UPDATED\_TOPIC\_REF}_**
        **_FunctionName: !GetAtt \[ PutEventToBucketLambdaFunction, 'Arn' \]_**

In this structure you must configure the permission with a unique resource id like principal node (in the example is "WalletUpdatedTopicInvokeFunctionPermission"). In the "SourceArn" property that is within "Properties", you must configure the variable created in the step 6.1. The other properties must be left with the values that were copied.

After you have configured the elements described from step 6.1 to 6.3, you must deploy the "Analytics" module. To do it, is necessary go to the module path ("EventSystemV2/Analytics/") in a terminal and execute the next command:

**_$ serverless deploy_**

# Subscribing an adapter to an event

1\. In the "serverless.yml" file of the module that will be subscribed to the topic (for example "EventSystemV2/Adapters/WalletsApiAdapter/serverless.yml" for the "WalletsApiAdapter" module), go to the "environment" section within "provider" section. There you create a variable in which you must import the reference to the topic to which the adapter is going to subscribe (this reference was created in "CommonResources" module, view step 2 of "Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-29077](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-29077))"), for example:

**_USER\_UPDATED\_TOPIC\_REF: !ImportValue ${file(../../CommonResources/serverless.yml):custom.USER\_UPDATED\_TOPIC\_REF}_**

2\. Assignment of permissions in the SQS to receive the new topic. In the "Resources" section that is into "resources" section you must look for the resource with type "AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy" and in that resource identify the "ArnEquals" property that is within the "Condition" property. In that property there is an array like the following :

**_"aws:SourceArn": \[_**
  **_'${self:provider.environment.USER\_CREATED\_TOPIC\_REF}'_**
**_\]_**

In that array you must add a new item putting a "," in the final of the current last item (if exists) and like new item you must put the variable created in step 1, for example:

**_"aws:SourceArn": \[_**
  **_'${self:provider.environment.USER\_CREATED\_TOPIC\_REF}',_**
  **_'${self:provider.environment.USER\_UPDATED\_TOPIC\_REF}'_**
**_\]_**

3\. Subscription of the SQS to topic. Now, in the same "Resources" section, where you found "AWS::SQS::QueuePolicy" in step 2, you must look for a resource with type "AWS::SNS::Subscription" (there is at least one). Where finish the last resource than you found with type "AWS::SNS::Subscription" you must add one new to create the subscription copying and pasting the structure of an existing one (if the "TopicArn" property in the existing structure has not value you must use that resource without necessity of create another). The structure is like the following example:

**_WalletsApiQueueToUserUpdatedTopicSubscription:_**
  **_Type: "AWS::SNS::Subscription"_**
  **_Properties:_**
    **_Endpoint: !GetAtt WalletsApiQueue.Arn_**
    **_Protocol: sqs_**
    **_RawMessageDelivery: true_**
    **_TopicArn: ${self:provider.environment.USER\_ UPDATED \_TOPIC\_REF}_**

In this structure you must configure the subscription with a unique resource id like principal node (in the example is "WalletsApiQueueToUserUpdatedTopicSubscription", if you use existing one you will receive an error when you deploy the module update. In the "TopicArn" property, you must use the variable that was created in step 1. The other properties must be left with the values that were copied.

After have configured the elements described from step 1 to 3, you must deploy the modified module. To do it, is necessary go to the adapter module path (for example "EventSystemV2/ WalletApiAdapter/" for “WalletApi” adapter) in a terminal and execute the next command:

**_$ serverless deploy_**

# Handle events

Expose an Https endpoint to handle a “POST” with this body:
string EventType
string Payload

Authentication:
Take the JWT from the header “LFAuth” and validate the Issuer, Audience and the sign (symmetric key)

# NesMonitor

# Status
### Request
Get [https://nesmonitor.h-way.com/api/status](https://localhost:32788/api/Status)
### Respose
True if all events are delivered. False if there is one or more dead letter buckets with events inside

# Remediation
## Buckets with undelivered events
### Request
Get [http://nes](http://nes)[monitor.h-way.com/api/buckets](http://monitor.h-way.com/api/buckets)
### Response

```auto
{
  "<<bucket name>>": <<event count>>,
  "evtsys-hwayqa-callback-interface-dead-letter-bucket": 0,
  "evtsys-hwayqa-cash-network-dead-letter-bucket": 0,
  "evtsys-hwayqa-cash-network-interface-dead-letter-bucket": 254,
}
```

### Resend events
Requeue events (the process could be executing beyond timeout. See logs to ensure the process results)
Post http://nesmonitor.h-way.com/api/buckets

```auto
{
  "bucketName": "<<bucket name>>",
  "since": "2023-02-27T16:34:05.762Z",
  "to": "2023-02-27T16:34:05.762Z",
  "take": 1000,
  "reference": "string"
}
```

##### Required fields
bucketName, reference
##### Conventions
Take: 1.000 is the max number of event requeue. That is the default if the parameter is ignored.
Since: if null will take everything
to: if null will take everything

### Response
Http status 200

# Services

# Creation
To create a new service you need:
*   A Config for all the environments in the Configuration Manager 
*   Jenkins build job
*   Jenkins deployment job for the diferent environments
*   Coordinate a service Url for the production environment
*   Create the Event System Adapter

# Health Status
The services should expose and endpoint to reveal the health status. The status have to include all dependencies status like DBs and services.
### Library
The HealthCheck library could be use to expose the endpoint. This library expose the endpoint "/status"

**Method:** GET

| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| SubscriberId |  | string | Optional - Required one of these |
| UserId |  | string | Optional - Required one of these |
| MobilePhone |  | string | Optional - Required one of these |
| Email |  | string | Optional - Required one of these |

## Response

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Accounts |  | IList<AccountResponse> |  |

| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Type |  | AccountType |  |
| Cards |  | IList<Card> |  |

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Type |  | AccountCardType |  |

# Accounts Geolocalization Limits

Limitations for new user personal account’s creation based on geographical coordinates.

# Backend Configuration Data Dictionary for Accounts Geolocalization - Hway

| **Description**<br> | **Key**<br> | **Value for DEV/QA**<br> | **Value for PROD**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Geographical radius within which limitations apply for user personal account’s creations | RadiusForCloseAccounts | 0.1 (Km) | 0.5 (Km) |
| Maximum number of user personal accounts allowed to be created within the specified radius | MaxCloseAccounts | 9999 | 50 |
| Range of days within which the number of created user personal accounts are counted | GeolocalizationRolling | 10 | \- |

# SPEI IN - Wallet Load Request

The Service provided to the External Services that will Load the Wallet trhoguth SPEI IN providing a CLABE as identificator, that needs to be matched by the Platform with the User that helds that CLABE/Account
This Service will Receive the Amount, call the proper mechanism to Analyze the Limits and either Accept Or Decline the in coming Transaction.
Note: to execute this Service the LFAUTH Authentication must be provided first
## Example of Request:

```auto
curl --location --request POST 'https://qa.sacbepayments.com/callbackinterface/walletLoadRequest' --header 'LFAuth: eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..NTygnQe4faQkyn98hvHpUyj_rHFtozJvcGCMXbQEFcI' --header 'Content-Type: application/json' --data-raw '{
  "authorizationId": "00000747489",
  "senderAccount": "5215513109404",
  "senderName": "Vanesa",
  "senderRfcCurp": null,
  "paymentConcept": "Carga SPEI NESA",
  "beneficiaryAccount": "646180152700164384",
  "beneficiaryName": "Luis",
  "beneficiaryRfcCurp": null,
  "amount": 15000,
  "trackingKey": "987654322",
  "counterpartInstitution": null,
  "accountNumber": null,
  "customerId": null
}'
```

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/431d4899-e751-44d2-aca6-dd9f2b7338c5/image-20210518-140829.png)
## Example of Responses
When the Execution is **Successfull**:

```auto
{
    "Status": "Received"
}
```

When the field `authorizationId` is **repeated**:

```auto
{
    "Status": "AlreadyUsedCode"
}
```

When the Execution is **Rejected**

```auto
{
    "Status": "Rejected"
}
```

This Response will be the one used when the limits mechanisms in the Platform do not allow the SPEI-IN to be accepted
The scenarios for this error are as follows:
*   When the Limits are surpased
*   When the Time in Miliseconds to analyze the limits is surpased: Current Configuration: 5 miliseconds

# SubscribersApi - Add Beneficiary and Enable Account

# Retry Steps

El sistema de reintentos costa de un paso base dispuesto en **BaseOperationFlow**

```auto
///Flow to Retry
            this.Flow.Configure(OperationState.RetryState)
                .PermitDynamic(ActionToBeDone.CheckRetryState, this.CheckRetryState);
                
```

Este código adicional hace posible implementar en los _PASOS_ de la maquina de estados que se requiera interceptando el **_OperationState_** que simbolizaría el fallo del _PASO_ usando una función dispuesta en el **_BaseOperationFlow_** nombrada como **_UpdateOperationToRetry_** a la cual se le pasa como parámetro el estado de falla

```auto
///ORIGINAL

  OperationState operationState;
  switch (response.Status)
  {
      case RequestStatus.Completed:
          operationState = OperationState.RecipientWalletFunded;
          break;

      default:
          operationState = OperationState.RecipientWalletFundingFailed;
          this.FailureReason = this.GetProviderFailureReason(_accountTransactionManager.GetProviderName(this.Operation), response, operationState);

          break;
  }

  return operationState;
///RETRY STEP

  OperationState operationState;
  switch (response.Status)
  {
      case RequestStatus.Completed:
          operationState = OperationState.RecipientWalletFunded;
          break;

      default:
          operationState = this.UpdateOperationToRetry(OperationState.RecipientWalletFundingFailed);
          this.FailureReason = this.GetProviderFailureReason(_accountTransactionManager.GetProviderName(this.Operation), response, operationState);

          break;
  }

  return operationState;
```

Al usar `UpdateOperationToRetry` toma la configuración desde AppConfig llamado `RetriesTimesInMinutes`el cual se configura con valores separados por comas los cuales simbolizan 2 cosas, la cantidad de números agregados representará el número de veces que debes reintentar y el valor de cada representará la cantidad de minutos que se debe esperar para efectuar ese intento en particular
En el _configuration manager_
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/f6a0dd18-ee7f-4627-8b3e-ef24a8e89313/image-20220916-134113.png)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/ef8cc1a1-0045-427c-9adf-225094585e48/image-20220916-134008.png)

Esto se hace posible debido a que durante el avance natural de la maquina de estado se va dejando registro del paso que se acaba de ejecutar, exceptuando un estado nuevo llamado **_RetryState_** que es el encargado de comandar que estado se va a ejecutar.

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/8f692da3-595f-4fea-82a7-80527eadc0c8/ProjectGif-20220916-164708.gif)

# Incluvida Interface API

# SACBE Payments Wallet Update through Remittances Interface API
The following API is developed as part of the integration with Incluvida to allow the Remittances information to be set in the SACBE Platform Wallet and the exchange of information of the Transactions involved.
##   

## General Info
**Connection**:
URL: [https://dev.sacbepayments.com/](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/)
API Key: c481e824-5c06-4c61-80ca-ea12b5b75df8

## Data Requirement
The requirement for the data to be exchanged in the different APis is detailed in the spreadsheet containing the information of the meetings held to identify and detail such data:

# Authentication
Used for authentication on the API
### Request
Path: /stsbc/auth/apikey
**Headers:**
*   Content-Type: application/json
**Method: Post**
Body:

```auto
{
	"ApiKey": "c481e824-5c06-4c61-80ca-ea12b5b75df8",
	"TTLMinutes": 50000
}
```

### Response

```auto
eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..MFdSd7Z0nCJ8TYI6aBqdzIDMPe8jDDWvgFGQ4T80X0A
```

### Request
Path: /incluvidainterface/CancelOperationRequest
Headers:
*   Content-Type: application/json
*   LFAuth: <<Token from Authentication response>>
Method: Post
Body:

```auto
{
	"Id": "US2000",
	"CancelationDate": "2020-06-24 09:45:00"
}
```

### Response
Returns a HTTP Response code 204 for successful communication
# Transaction/Cancellation Status Callback
Used to obtain the Status of a Transaction or the Status of a Cancellation
### Status

```auto
{    
    //Fund request rejected by recipient (the one to fund it)
    Rejected,
    //Operation not completed. Failure
    Failed,
    //Operation Cancel process completed. 
    CancelationCompleted,
    //Operation Cancel process rejected. 
    CancelationRejected,
    //Operation Cancel process failed. 
    CancelationFailed,
    //Operation Cancel TxId not found or invalid 
    CancelationInvalidTransactionId
}
```

## Transaction Completed Callback
Used to obtain the Data of a Transaction that was properly completed
### Transaction Completed Information

# Changes to Set Funds in Wallet

## General Details
FOR SACBE in June 2021
As part of a business strategy certain existing business flows related to customer that receive remmitances are going to be migrated to SACBE
Allow Cartera Dinamica User Information in SACBE
In Order to Generate this Mechanism the following is required to be set in Place
*   When Receiving a Remittance in Wallet (set Funds in Wallet)
    *   There will be a new field indicating a new data that needs to be kept as part of the user information
    *   This field needs to be kept to be later displayed in the Card Info Section
*   Ensure that the following mechanism is in place already
    *   When the user creates its account if he already had a remittance “waiting” those funds from the remittance will be automatically sent to its wallet

# Pay in Store API

This API provides the means to perform the communication between the SACBE Platform and the Third Party that will receive and handle the funds from a SACBE user's account as a way of payments (through a QR Code) for the goods or services sold by a Store
For Details on the User-Story Review:  [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-620)[SAC-620](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-620) - Pay in Store Blocked

# Processing Flow

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/414a44a9-352b-49f2-be33-31f4b94dee66/image2020-10-6_19-13-51.png)

# Entities 

# Methods
In each section below, you'll find the different methods available to be accessed, with a description of the purpose and the information of each field involved
## Authentication Login
### **Description**:
The following details are needed in order to perform authentication to communicate with SACBE Platform APIs.
The method Apikey provides the LFAuth token, which needs to be sent in the header element, as credential to access the SACBE Platform APIs
### URI:
_The following Uri is provided to be accessed from a postman service tool_
Uri: https://qa.sacbepayments.com/stsbc/auth/apikey

```auto
{
"ApiKey":"0000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 "
}
```

*   **ApiKey:** information provided by SACBE.

## Get Reference Code Info
### **Description**:
this API provides, based on the identification code, relevant information about the user, 
To be Consumed by: POS Terminal.
### **Uri:** 
(Uri can be tested when accessed from a postman service tool)
[https://qa.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface/](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface/sendFundsStorePay)[getReferenceCodeInfo](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface//SendFundsStorePay)
### **Request**:

```auto
Headers:
{
"LFAuth":""
}

Request:
{
"RefCodeId": "",
"Amount": "100.50"
}


```

**Request Field Info**
*   RefCodeId: obtained from Subscriber identity information provided by Mobile app scanning the Barcode
*   Amount: the purchase amount to pay
###   

### **Response**:

```auto
Response

{
"refCodeId":
"subcriberId":
"firstName":
"lastName":
"expirationDate":
}


```

**Response Field Info:**
*   refCodeId: obtained from Subscriber identity information provided by Mobile app scanning the Barcode
*   subcriberId: internal id of the payer
*   firstName: sender's frist name
*   lastName: sender's last name
*   expirationDate: date util reference code is valid

## Send Funds to Merchant
### **Description**:
With the information of the Reference Code, previously acquired, the terminal can use this method to send a payment order to the user that was previously identified. 
To be Consumed by: POS Terminal.
### **Uri**: 
_The following Uri can be tested when accessed from a postman service tool_
[https://qa.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface/sendFundsToMerchant](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface/sendFundsToMerchant) 
### **Request**:

```auto
Headers:

{
"LFAuth":""
}

Request:

{
  "refCodeId": "4d143f1c-a668-45f9-aa7b-ac4b3e41de5b", 
  "merchantId": "e8120b1a-7afe-4a85-a1ec-c5c39b402ed4",
  "amount": 10.0,
  "currency": "USD", 
  "networkId": "031306ba-4f54-45f3-9722-1342a9b4f192", 
  "branchOfficeId": "031306ba-4f54-45f3-9722-1342a9b4f192",
  "customData": "",
  "ExternalTransactionId" : ""
}
```

**Request Fields Info:**
*       *   RefCodeId: obtained from Subscriber identity information.
    *   MerchantId: is the store(receiver's) Id from corporate user, this user will receive the money in his wallet.
    *   Amount: the amount of money to pay the bill on store.
    *   Currency: will be ISO 3 currency (USD,MXN)
    *   NetworkId:  will be the network's id
    *   BranchOfficeId: will be the a location from the network.
    *   CustomData: information in Json format from store, e.g.  the detail of the bill (list of goods) to be payed
    *   ExternalTransactionId: Information of bill number
### **Response**:
###   

```auto
Response

{
"requestStatus": 
"transactionId":
"transactionCode":
}


```

**Response Fields Info:**
*       *   RequestStatus: can be Success, ExpiredCode, AlreadyUsedCode, NotExistingCode, Failed
    *   TransactionId: is the operation's Id, will be used for get status information from transaction
    *   TransactionCode: is the short Id from transaction for User friendly  view.

**Error Codes:**
In RequestStatus response field you can find:
0: Success occurs when the operation could be created correctly.
1: ExpiredCode occurs when reference code has its time expired.
2: AlreadyUsedCode occurs when reference code has been used in a payment before, references codes can be used on only 1 payment.
3: NotExistingCode occurs when reference code cannot be found on PayInStore database.
4: Failed occurs when ExternalTransactionId field is empty or null.

##   

## Return Funds From Merchant
### **Description**: 
This needs to be used in any scenario the funds need to be returned, to send money back to the user that was previously identified.
To be consumed by: POS terminal.
### **Uri**: 
The following Uri can be tested when accessed from a postman service tool
[https://qa.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface/](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface/sendFundsStorePay)[returnFundsFromMerchant](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface//SendFundsStorePay)
### **Request**

```auto
Headers:
{
"LFAuth":""
}

Request:
{
 "refCodeId": "4d143f1c-a668-45f9-aa7b-ac4b3e41de5b", 
  "merchantId": "e8120b1a-7afe-4a85-a1ec-c5c39b402ed4",
  "amount": 10.0,
  "currency": "USD", 
  "networkId": "031306ba-4f54-45f3-9722-1342a9b4f192", 
  "branchOfficeId": "031306ba-4f54-45f3-9722-1342a9b4f192",
  "customData": ""
}
```

**Request Fields Info:**
*       *   RefCodeId: obtained from Subscriber identity information.
    *   MerchantId: is the store(receiver's) Id from corporate user, this user will receive the money in his wallet.
    *   Amount: the amount of money to pay the bill on store.
    *   Currency: will be ISO 3 currency (USD,MXN)
    *   NetworkId:  will be the network's id
    *   BranchOfficeId: will be the a location from the network.
    *   CustomData: information in Json format from store, e.g.  the detail of the bill.

### **Response**:

```auto
{
"requestStatus": 
"transactionId":
"transactionCode":
}
```

**Response Fields Info:**
*       *   RequestStatus: can be Success, ExpiredCode, AlreadyUsedCode, NotExistingCode, Failed
    *   TransactionId: is the operation's Id, will be used for get status information from transaction
    *   TransactionCode: is the short Id from transaction for User friendly  view.
    *   
**Error Codes:**
In RequestStatus response field you can find:

0: Success occurs when the operation could be created correctly.
1: ExpiredCode occurs when reference code has its time expired.
2: AlreadyUsedCode occurs when reference code has been used in a payment before, references codes can be used on only 1 payment.
3: NotExistingCode occurs when reference code cannot be found on PayInStore database
4: Failed occurs when ExternalTransactionId field is empty or null.

## Get Transaction Information
**Description**:
used by the terminal to check the current status of the transaction previously sent. 
To be Consumed by: POS Terminal
### **Uri**: 
_The following Uri can be tested when accessed from a postman service tool_
#### [https://qa.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface/](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface/sendFundsStorePay)[requestOperationStatus](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface//SendFundsStorePay)

### **Request**:

```auto
Headers:
{
"LFAuth":""
}


Request:
{
TransactionId: 
}
```

**Request Field Info:**
*   TransactionId: obtained from SendFundsToMerchant

### **Response:**

```auto
{
"transactionId":
"operationStatus": 
"status": 
}
```

**Response Field Info**
*   "transactionId": transaction Id
*   "operationStatus": Completed for operation success, can be Failed, and other status is in transit.
*   "status": Succeed is for transaction's found and Not Found.

**Error Codes:**
In operationStatus response field you can find:
1: Succees occurs when transactionId is found on PayInStore database and its information can be displayed
2: NotFound occurs when transactionId is not found on PayInStoreDatabase

# Cash In/Out in Store API

## The aim of this doument is to describe the APIs set in place from SACBE Platform, to be consumed by third parties that will receive the information of SACBE user's account in order to:
*   be able to [Withdraw Cash](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/pages/resumedraft.action?draftId=45976518#PayinStoreAPI-CashInCashOut)  from/in their SACBE accounts. (Sacbe Card/Wallet)

# **Authentication Login**
The following details are needed in order to perform authentication to communicate with SACBE Platform APIs.
The method Apikey provides the LFAuth token, which needs to be sent in the header element, as credential to access the SACBE Platform APIs
#### URI:
#### **The following Uri is provided to be accessed from a postman service tool**
Uri: https://qa.sacbepayments.com/stsbc/auth/apikey

```auto
{
"ApiKey":"0000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 "
}
```

*   **ApiKey:** information provided by SACBE.

#   

# Cash In / Cash Out in Store
This functionality is available through an API that allows SACBE Platform to connect with the different devices, technologies/platforms, available in the Stores, to send/retrieve the information of Cash Related Transactions of the SACBE Card/Wallet.
The user will start the transaction in its own device, performing a Self Transfer Out to Cash, or use the Cash Payment Method to Load funds into his SACBE Card/Wallet. He will also be able to Fund with Cash a Transfer Funds Transaction to another user.
The final step is connect the SACBE Platform information with the third party that will handle the Cash Transaction to either receive a Cash Deposit or allow the user to WithDraw Money from the Store , identifying the user and the transaction thru the QR Code provided by the SACBE Platform.

**get Reference Code Info**
**Description:** This Api provides Barcode / QR information generated in Get Reference Code Api, it contains general info about operation.
**Uri**: 
_The following Uri can be tested when accessed from a postman service tool_
[https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode)[g](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface//SendFundsStorePay)[etReferenceCodeInfo](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode)

**Headers:**
{
"LFAuth":""
}
**Request:**
{
"RefCodeId"
}
**Request Field Info:**
*       *   RefCodeId: obtained from Get Reference Code

**Response:**
{
"RefCodeId"
"OperationId"
"TransactionCode"
"Amount"
"SenderAmount"
"SenderName"
"ReceiverName"
"CashProvider"
"TransactionType"
"DateGeneration"
"ExpDate"
"Status"
}
**Response Field Info:**
*       *   RefCodeId: Reference code for operation.
    *   OperationId: Id of operation
    *   TransactionCode: shot Id of operation
    *   Amount: amount of operation.
    *   SenderAmount: amount of money of operation including fees
    *   SenderName: sender full name
    *   ReceiverName: receiver full name
    *   CashProvider:  can be (Numi, Greendot) and its obtained from CashNetwork.
    *   TransactionType: can be (Load,Withdraw)
    *   DateGeneration: date of code creation (YYYY/MM/DD)
    *   ExpDate: date ultil Code expires (YYYY/MM/DD)
    *   Status: Success if Reference code id is found.
**Error Codes:**
In Status response field you can find:

0: Success occurs when the operation could be created correctly.
2: AlreadyUsedCode occurs when reference code has been used in a payment before, references codes can be used on only 1 payment.
3: NotExistingCode occurs when reference code cannot be found on CashNetwork database.

# **Activate Reference Code**
**Description**: This Api uses the Reference code obtained from Barcode/QR in order to complete the operation.
##### **Uri**: 
##### The following Uri can be tested when accessed from a postman service tool
[https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode)

**Headers:**
{
"LFAuth":""
}
**Request:**
{
"refCodeId": null,
"operationId": null,
"cashProvider": null,
"networkId": null,
"locationId": null,
"externalData": null
}
**Request Field Info:**
*       *   refCodeId":  Obtained from get reference code id
    *   operationId":  Obtained from get reference code id
    *   cashProvider": Obtained from get reference code id
    *   networkId":  network provided by the Terminal
    *   locationId":  network provided by the Terminal
    *   externalData":  Json format string from Terminal

**Response:**
{
"transactionId": null,
"status": 0,
"errorMessage": null
}

**Response Field Info:**
*       *   TransactionId: Operation Id
    *   Status:  can be Success of Failed
    *   ErrorMessage: empty if no error occurs

**Error Codes:**
In Status response field you can find:

0: Success occurs when the operation could be created correctly.
1: ExpiredCode occurs when reference code has its time expired.
2: AlreadyUsedCode occurs when reference code has been used in a payment before, references codes can be used on only 1 payment.
3: NotExistingCode occurs when reference code cannot be found on CashNetwork database.
4: WrongNetwork occurs when the networkId from store is different of the networkId piked by the user when reference code was generated, only can be 1 reference code at time.

# **Activate Payment Code (Incluvida)**

**Uri:** [**https://dev.sacbepayments.com/**](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface//SendFundsStorePay)[**cashnetworkinterface/activatePaymentCode**](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/activatePaymentCode)
Body:
{
"paymentCode": null, 
"status": 0,
"reason": null,
"networkId": null,
"externalData": null,
"locationId": null
}
*   **PaymentCode\***: Sent previously on Operation creation.
*   **Status\***: 0 for success, 1 for failed.
*   Reason: Open text to explain why its failed
*   **NetworkId\***: must be same network sent on Operation creation.
*   ExternalData:  Json format string from Terminal
*   LocationId: must be same network sent on Operation creation.
**(\*) Obligatory**
Response
{
"transactionId": null,
"status": 0,
"errorMessage": null
}

*   TransactionId: Operation Id
*   Status:  can be Success, ExpiredCode, AlreadyUsedCode, NotExistingCode, WrongNetwork, Failed
*   ErrorMessage: empty if no error occurs

# Chedraui Cash Networks

## Detalles Generales
En base a los requerimientos y relevamientos realizados por chedraui/incluvida:

[Espcificaciones Chedraui Comentarios(1).docx](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/857b15a9-ba76-420f-81e6-3e080ee43acd/Espcificaciones%20Chedraui%20Comentarios(1).docx)

Se detallan a continuación la problematica / trabajo a realizar identificados en
**1)Validar si es necesario generar un nuevo tipo de Cash Provider**
**2) Generar un nuevo codigo de barra**
1. Actualmente nuestro codigo solo tienen el ide Operacion
    1. se debe agregar el monto, que es requerido por chedraui
    2. Implicaciones de la extension-tamaño del codigo
        1. como se vería en el device
        2. Actualmente el que tenemos tiene un “short guid” = 22 caracteres
            1. A eso agregar +5 para el monto
    3. Implicaciones de agregarle información especifica
**3) Cuando se genera el codigo de barras se debe hacer un hold de fondos (en Galileo)**

**4) Generar un API Específico para la confirmación de Pago**
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-2351](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-2351)
1. Necesitan una respuesta especifica:
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/e94e9c8d-c952-491f-abb4-710f7d5251a9/image-20210701-184115.png)
**5) El mecanismo queda en espera de cancelación por una determinada cantidad de tiempo**
**6) Si la Cancelación no se envia se considera completada la operacion**

## Especificación Técnica: One Step Cash-Out

## Especificación de QA Testing: : One Step Cash-Out

# Zoho - Tzune Integration Api's

In order to perform payments using Tzune Platform user must follow this process.
1. Authenticate
2. query for payment methods from sender and recipient
3. perform send funds.
This steps are described below:

**Step 1. Authentication**
[https://qa.tzune.com/stsbc/auth/apikey?ApiKey=029b3c7e-39e2-4136-8fc5-a9c64c97303e&TTLMinutes=10](https://qa.tzune.com/stsbc/auth/apikey?ApiKey=029b3c7e-39e2-4136-8fc5-a9c64c97303e&TTLMinutes=10)
*   ApkiKey: this key is provided by development team for Qa environment is (029b3c7e-39e2-4136-8fc5-a9c64c97303e)
*   TTLMinutes= this is life time of key provided, 10 minutes suggested
The expected response for this authentication should look like the example below:

```auto
eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..MePfvAzWijFbPlbmvDvhCBiF__xOGf41_7wlxIqSci0
```

**Step 2. Call get user payment methods**
Once Authorization key is ready, you can proceed to call actual query GetUserPaymentMethods with this structure:
Uri: https://qa.tzune.com/messagingbc/subscribermessages/query
Headers:

```auto
LFAuth: eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9...
```

Body:

```auto
{
	"MessageRequest": {
		"PostedAt": "2021-03-19T21:34:39.4035132Z",
		"Payload": "{\"Username\":\"+5213310120342\"}",
		"MessageType": "GetUserPaymentMethods",
		"MessageId": "34190baa-72ee-4258-9d48-01ff1a90fe09"
	}
}
```

PostedAt: Date time on UTC format
Payload: Json object with UserName with format “+52“ country code, “1“ international calling code and mobile number
MessageType: must be `"GetUserPaymentMethods"`
MessageId: Must be a new Guid

**Step 3. Perform send funds**
Once payment method ready, you can proceed to perfom send funds with this structure:
Uri: https://qa.tzune.com/messagingbc/subscribermessages/forward
Headers:

```auto
LFAuth: eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9...
```

Body:

```auto
{
"Message":{
    "PostedAt":"2021-07-12T21:34:39.4035132Z",
    "Payload":"{\"RecipientAmount\":\"30.00\",\"RecipientCurrency\":\"MXN\",\"SourcePaymentMethod\":{\"AccountId\":\"a7d08bc7-234a-4ccb-ab8c-f127d23f531e\",\"Country\":\"MX\"},\"ExchangeRate\":0,\"FeeType\":0,\"SendTo\":{\"CountryCode\":\"MX\",\"MobilePhone\":\"+5215513109833\"},\"Amount\":\"30.00\",\"Ip\":\"123.456.789.000\",\"DestinationFee\":0,\"TransactionFee\":0,\"DestinationPaymentMethod\":{\"AccountId\":\"23b9b673-f8ee-4389-aa3e-28b8a69dc5be\",\"Country\":\"MX\"},\"Code\":\"Code-014\",\"CallLocationLongitude\":\"-99.140818\",\"ProcessAt\":\"2021-07-21T09:21:01.089Z\",\"CallLocationLatitude\":\"19.315157\",\"SenderAmount\":\"30.00\",\"Currency\":\"MXN\",\"SenderCurrency\":\"MXN\",\"CorrelationId\":\"a7195b48-8803-4f2d-ab82-cd70571a6628\",\"SourceFee\":0}",
    "MessageType":"SendFunds",
    "MessageId":"a7195b48-8803-4f2d-ab82-cd70571a662b"
    }
}
```

PostedAt: Date time on UTC format
Payload: Json object with structure described below (sendFunds).
MessageType: must be `"SendFunds"`
MessageId: Must be a new Guid

**SendFunds**

```auto
{
  "processAt": null,
  "destinationFee": 0.0,
  "transactionFee": 0.0,
  "sourceFee": 0.0,
  "feeType": 0,
  "recipientCurrency": null,
  "recipientAmount": 0.0,
  "senderCurrency": null,
  "senderAmount": 0.0,
  "currency": null,
  "amount": 0.0,
  "destinationPaymentMethod": null,
  "sourcePaymentMethod": null,
  "code": null,
  "correlationId": "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000",
  "sendTo": null,
  "exchangeRate": 0.0,
  "ip": null,
  "CallLocationLongitude":0,
  "CallLocationLatitude":0
}
```

*   ProcessAt: current date time in Utc format
*   DestinationFee, TransactionFee, SourceFee: must be 0.0
*   FeeType: Must be 0
*   ReciepientCurrency: “MXN“
*   RecipientAmount: amount of money for recpient
*   SenderCurrency: “MXN“
*   SenderAmount: amount of money to be discounted for sender
*   Currency: “MXN“
*   Amount: amount of money to be transfered
*   DestinationPaymentMethod: payment method of recipient
*   SourcePaymentMethod: payment method of sender
*   Code: Alpha numeric value 6 characters (Unique)
*   CorrelationId: new guid value
*   SendTo: recipient user.
*   ExchangeRate: must be 0
*   Ip: ip address of sender
*   CallLocationLongitude: georeferenced sender longitud (decimal)
*   CallLocationLatitude: georeferenced sender latitude (decimal)
**PaymentMethod (SourcePaymentMethod, DestinationPaymentMethod)**

```auto
{
  "accountId": null,
  "Country":"MX"
}
```

*   Account Id: this is from user’s payment method mobile wallet of sender or recipient field (Id)

**SendTo**

```auto
{
  "countryCode": null,
  "mobilePhone": null,
}
```

*   CountryCode: ”MX”
*   MobilePhone: with format “+52“ country code, “1“ international calling code and mobile number (`+5213310120342`)

# LC and MoneyExchangeBC APIs



# Business Accounts

### Request LC Token
Obtiene token de autenticación para usarse en los llamados hacia Leapcentral
Dev Url: https://dev.tzune.com/stsbc/auth/apikey
QA Url: https://qa.tzune.com/stsbc/auth/apikey
Prod Url: https://lcnext.tzune.com/stsbc/auth/apikey
Content-Type → application/json
Variables:
Dev ApiKey → `020bc204-0c2b-4464-a3af-ad66673efc0c`
QA ApiKey → `020bc204-0c2b-4464-a3af-ad66673efc0c`
Prod ApiKey →

```auto
POST
{
	"ApiKey":"020bc204-0c2b-4464-a3af-ad66673efc0c"
}
```

### Request Business Account QR Image Url
Obtiene la Url del código QR de la cuenta Business de un usuario.
Dev Url: https://dev.tzune.com/mexbc/subscribermessages/query
QA Url: https://qa.tzune.com:3437/subscribermessages/query
Headers:
LFAuth → Token from LC
Content-Type → application/json
Variables:
Dev accountId → `b2f7f394-94e3-4d12-a06e-2b63239e18b2`
QA accountId → 6797901b-31fe-43ca-8bf9-ac75bc30988a
UserId → Dueño de la cuenta Business de la que se quiere el QR.

```auto
POST
{
  "accountCode": "Numi",
  "accountId": "b2f7f394-94e3-4d12-a06e-2b63239e18b2",
  "applicationName": "Tzune",
  "subscriberId": "04a48878-59b5-4471-a948-7accf1a3bf2d",
  "username": "+5215514200072",
  "originIP": "167.58.129.110",
  "messageId": "38d83c4b-66c8-4323-9944-25241895df9b",
  "messageType": "RequestBusinessAccountImageCodeUrl",
  "payload": "{'UserId':'+5213310120410','ContractVersion':'1.0.3.4','UserLanguage':null}"
}
```

### Send data (user and password) to charge by card in Business Account
Guarda user y password que se usarán para identificar la cuenta Business de un usuario cuando se hacen cargos con tarjeta y su destino es la cuenta Business.
Dev Url: https://dev.tzune.com/messagingbc/subscribermessages/query
QA Url: https://qa.tzune.com/messagingbc/subscribermessages/query
PROD Url: https://lcnext.tzune.com/messagingbc/subscribermessages/query
Headers:
LFAuth → Token from LC
Content-Type → application/json
Variables:
UserId → Dueño de la cuenta Business a la que se ligaran el user y password.

```auto
{
  "messageRequest": {
    "messageId": "1771217a-850f-4b6f-b455-9ebab705b4de",
    "messageType": "SendDataToChargeByCardInBusinessAccount",
    "payload": "{\"UserId\":\"+5215513109921\",\"UserToCharge\":\"userTest\",\"PasswordToCharge\":\"passwordTest\",\"ContractVersion\":\"1.0.3.4\",\"UserLanguage\":null}",
    "postedAt": "2021-08-17T14:10:15.306Z",
    "ttl": 6000
  }
}
```

# User levels modification

# SetUserLevelToSoftfailed (Query message)
## Request
**MessageType:** SetUserLevelToSoftfailed
**Parameters in Payload:**

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Mandatory**<br> | **Type**<br> | <br>**Sample**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| SubscriberId |  id of the user | conditional (if UserId is empty) | string |  |
| UserId | phone of the user | conditional (if suscriberId is empty) | string |  +1234567890 |
| Level | Level to Soft Fail | yes | int | 0,1,2 |
| SoftfailType | Type of Soft Fail (will trigger the corresponding actions in the app) | yes | enum | `InvalidEMail`<br>InvalidAddress<br>UnreadableDoc<br>ProvideAdditionalInfo<br>InvalidPersonalData |
| DocumentType | Legacy field, for hway 2023 always use “Id” | yes | enum | None<br>Id<br>ProofOfResidence<br>ProofOfIncome<br>ProofOfLife<br>ProofOfAddress |
| DocumentSubtype | Used when DocumentType = Id |  | enum | Passport,<br>NationalId,<br>StateId,<br>ResidentCard,<br>ForeignId,<br>DriverLicense,<br>HealthId,<br>MilitaryId,<br>WorkPermit,<br>VisaInPassport,<br>SocialSecurityNumber,<br>MatriculaCard,<br>NativeAmericanTribalId,<br>INE\_IFE,<br>ConsularId,<br>ITIN |

##  Response

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Status |  | enum | Succeeded<br>Failed |
| success |  | boolean |  |
| StatusMessage |  | string |  |

| ---| ---| ---| --- |

| success |  | boolean |  |
| StatusMessage |  | string |  |

**Method:** Post

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| SubscriberId |  | string |  |

# Users Geolocalization Limits

Limitations for new user’s creation based on geographical coordinates.

# Backend Configuration Data Dictionary for Users Geolocalization - Hway

| **Description**<br> | **Key**<br> | **Value for DEV/QA**<br> | **Value for PROD**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Geographical radius within which limitations apply for user’s creations | RadiusForCloseUsers | 0.1 (Km) | 0.5 (Km) |
| Maximum number of users allowed to be created within the specified radius | MaxCloseUsers | 9999 | 50 |
| Range of days within which the number of created users are counted | GeolocalizationRolling | 10 | \- |

This virtual account has the purpose of handle all account movements (log, in, out, locks and unlock money)

{
"Id":"",
"ExternalClientId":"",

"LastUpdate":"",

"AvailableBalance":0.0,
"LockedBalance":0.0
}

"ReferenceId":"",
"TransactionType":"",
"TransactionStatus":"",
"Currency":"",
"CreatedAt":"",
"UpdatedAt":"",
"Amount":"",
"AccountId":"",

"LinkedTransactionId":"",
}

Debit,
Credit

Pending,

}

# CallbackInterfaceApi

this API is used when events/messages from any provider need to be received
To review the details please select eacu provider details

# Incomm Call Back Api

Incomm provides a solution for load with cash using barcode
The messages received from Incomm will be the information of a transacion performed by a user that affects the balance (incresase the balance) of a user’s account

**URL**
The base Url for the call back will be as follows
[https://dev.h-way.com/callbackinterface/IncommCallback](https://dev.h-way.com/callbackinterface/IncommCallback)
This url also works a as ping service (to check the connection)
**Authentication**
Incommm will be authenticated into the CallBack Interface with a Basic Authentication
The following user and password needs to be provided to Incomm
Usr Incomm2022
Psw iy56ta6fdqioiwem

**Messages**
The Messages that the call back interface will listen as well as the details of the parameters and data types received are detailed in the Provider’s documentation referenced in the provider’s page from this space
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/1871511553/Incomm#Transactions-Mesagging-Exchange](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/1871511553/Incomm#Transactions-Mesagging-Exchange)

# TurnOnOffOnHoldTransactionToUser (Query message)

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Mandatory**<br> | **Type**<br> | <br>**Sample**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| SubscriberId |  id of the user obained from the console (field ID, you have to click to copy) | yes | string |  e43abd22-ea4f-47f0-9376-bb6a745fdedd<br> |
| UserId | phone of the user | yes | string |  +1234567890 |
| TurnOnOff | Enable or disable this flag | yes | boolean |  true (sets the flag ON<br>false (sets the flag OFF) |

##  Response

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Status |  | enum | Succeeded<br>Failed |
| WasModifiedSuccessfully |  | boolean |  |
| StatusMessage |  | string |  |

| Activate | Enable or disable this flag | yes | boolean |  true (secondary check pending)<br>false (secondary check not pending) |

# RegisterThresholdForUser (Query message)
## Request
**MessageType:** RegisterThresholdForUser
**Parameters in Payload:**

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Mandatory**<br> | **Type**<br> | <br>**Sample**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| SubscriberId | id of the user obained from the console (field ID, you have to click to copy) | yes | string |  e43abd22-ea4f-47f0-9376-bb6a745fdedd |
| UserId | phone of the user | yes | string |  +1234567890 |
| Amount |  amount to set the threshold for | yes | float | 20.00 |
| TimeType | tipe of time to control for the threshold to be achieved | yes | enum<br>(one of the following values) | Rolling,<br>Weekly,<br>Monthly,<br>Annual,<br>Whole |
| Time | Only used if TimeType = Rolling, amount of days | yes | int | 1 |
| Action |  | yes | enum<br>(one of the following values) | TurnOnHoldOperation<br>TurnOnSecondaryCheck |

##  Response

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Status |  | enum | Succeeded<br>Failed |
| StatusMessage |  | string |  |

# FindRuleByUser (Query message)
## Request
**MessageType:** FindRuleByUser
**Parameters in Payload:**

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Mandatory**<br> | **Type**<br> | <br>**Sample**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| SubscriberId |   id of the user obained from the console (field ID, you have to click to copy) | yes | string |  9da2c092-cfa7-4749-be53-7a3ab7c4e0c9 |
| UserId | phone of the user | yes | string |  +1234567890 |

##  Response

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> |  | **Type**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Status |  |  | enum |
| StatusMessage |  |  | string |
| Rules\[\] | Array of elemements with the following inforamtion: |
|  | **field** |  **value** | **Definition** |
|  | RuleType | Sum |  Type of Rule |
|  | Max |  | Rule’s max value |
|  | TimeType | Rolling<br>Weekly<br>Monthly<br>Annual<br>Whole | Time applied to the rule |
|  | Time | 7 | When timetype is rolling, the “Time” value is equals to days number |
|  | ActionLimit | TurnOnHoldOperation<br>TurnOnSecondaryCheck | Action to be done when rule fail |
