# Testing

Keep in mind please that we should be picky selecting what to test, to find the balance between code coverage and time invested in tests maintenance
# Core Integrations
The goal of this type of testing is to verify that the main activities of the system remains ok. This will be achieve with one or few testing scenario covering this activities across the entire platform.
Example:
*   Create an User
*   Upgrade to level 1
*   Upgrade to level 2
*   Request Wallet
*   Send Money
When talking about core integration, this is not intended to cover all the scenarios (e.g. not to cover all the Operations Flows, for that we will have Service Integration, explained in this article too) but to test that the core business functionality in terms of the objects interactions are working as expected (Flow with providers and services).
# Services Integration
## Services Infrastructure
Checking if the lay ground in which the rest of testing will be performed is working as expected
1. Services are up and running (Health check)
2. Services authentication (Token validation)
## Business Logic Tests
This kind of testing is focused in one Service at the time and not in the interaction between services. 
1. Per-services tests, ordered by business relevance, e.g.:
    1. Operation API:
        1. All the active flows reaching the Completed status
    2. WalletsAPI
        1. Virtual Wallet created OK
2. Per-services tests, more granular, ordered by business relevance, e.g.:
    1. Operation API:
        1. Testing different operation flows scenarios
    2. Wallets API:
        1. Testing different wallet creation scenarios
# Unit Testing
The mindset is as follows: Test core business functions that are most likely to be erroneous and are critical for the business (e.g. amounts calculators to determine in a given operation the Sender Amount, and the Recipient Amount).

# APIs for Risk Assessment Services

In order to execute the APIs provided for Risk Assesment Services, it is necessary to perform a query call to the IEP with the corresponding parameters for case
The following instructions are intended to guide you through the executing of these apis in a postman or similar tool
**The parameters that are important to note that will be modified are:**
messageType = the type of message in each case
payload = add the necessary parameters for each case

**Reminder :**
The LFFAuth token provided as a header parameter, is used to authenticate in the Platform, and needs to be obtained in case is expired. See [Authentication for HWAY Services](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/HWAY/pages/2004549633/Authentication+for+HWAY+Services)
  
## Example Curl for QA
The following is a **baseline** **example for QA Environment** CURL, that will change to have the corresponding parameters for each service to be used.
**Tip**: Use this curl to create a request in postman and use it as baseline for the rest

```swift
curl --location "https://qa.h-way.com/mexbc/subscribermessages/query" ^
--header "LFAuth: eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..u8NvREHhmBSB-3sWUv7tB4YY5bo70pq2wup6rGjTlmg" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/json" ^
--data "{
  \"accountCode\": \"Numi\",
  \"accountId\": \"a9c2df6c-81df-4011-9694-e8edd9d63a50\",
  \"applicationName\": \"HWay\",
  \"subscriberId\": \"b35701b6-7739-46de-85bb-a61a8d708ad1\",
  \"username\": \"+19163015875\",
  \"originIP\": \"167.58.129.110\",
  \"messageId\": \"38d83c4b-66c8-4323-9944-25241895dt9b\",
  \"messageType\": \"TurnOnOffOnHoldTransactionToUser\",
  \"payload\": \"{'UserId':'+19163599958','TurnOnOff':'false','ContractVersion':'1.0.3.4','UserLanguage':null}\"
}"
```

  
# For PROD Environment
### The Endpoint
for PROD is the following
[https://lcnext.h-way.com/messagingbc/subscribermessages/query](https://lcnext.h-way.com/messagingbc/subscribermessages/query)
### The Credentials
For the LFAUTH Token select the production api-key as displayed in [Authentication for HWAY Services](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/HWAY/pages/2004549633/Authentication+for+HWAY+Services)
#   

# List of Messages for Each Scenario
Below is the descriptions of the specific data to be user in each scenario

## Turn On/Off OnHoldTransaction To User
Use this API to enable or disable the HoldTransaction flag for a user
**Body Parameters Example for Postman:**

**Tip**: create a new request in postman as a copy from the baseline and change the body

```plain
{
  "messageType": "TurnOnOffOnHoldTransactionToUser",
  "payload": "{'UserId':'+19166019033','TurnOnOff':'false','ContractVersion':'1.0.3.4','UserLanguage':null}"
}
```

**See Parameters definition:**
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2009563137/Risk+Assessment#TurnOnOffOnHoldTransactionToUser-(Query-message)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2009563137/Risk+Assessment#TurnOnOffOnHoldTransactionToUser-(Query-message))

## Activate Or Deactivate Secondary Check To User

Use this API to enable or disable the “Secondary Check Pending” flag for a user.
When true, it will set the Secondary Check Pending to “Pending”. When false, it will set the Secondary Check Pending to “Done”
**Body Parameters Example for Postman:**

**Tip**: create a new request in postman as a copy from the baseline and change the body

```plain
{
  "messageType": "ActivateOrDeactivateSecondaryCheckToUser",
  "payload": "{'UserId':'+19166019033','Activate':'false','ContractVersion':'1.0.3.4','UserLanguage':null}"
}
```

**See Parameters definition:**
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2009563137/Risk+Assessment#ActivateOrDeactivateSecondaryCheckToUser-(Query-message)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2009563137/Risk+Assessment#ActivateOrDeactivateSecondaryCheckToUser-(Query-message))

## Register Threshold For User
Use this API to set a NEW THRESHOLD for the user (different than the ones in the regular process)
**Body Parameters Example for Postman:**

**Tip**: create a new request in postman as a copy from the baseline and change the body

```plain
{
    "messageType": "RegisterThresholdForUser",
    "payload": "{'UserId':'+19166019033','Amount':'10000','TimeType':'Monthly','Action':'TurnOnHoldOperation','ContractVersion':'1.0.3.4','UserLanguage':null}"
}
```

**See Parameters definition:**
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2009563137/Risk+Assessment#RegisterThresholdForUser-(Query-message)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2009563137/Risk+Assessment#RegisterThresholdForUser-(Query-message))

  
# Postman Collection
The following postman collection can be a useful resource to have the baseline calls in each scenario

[NUMI - Risk and CIP.postman\_collection.json](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/f7bd3c25-0fd4-4ae5-9cc0-3cf3f716c9d1/NUMI%20-%20Risk%20and%20CIP.postman_collection.json)

# APIS for CIP Services

In order to execute the APIs provided for CIP Services, it is necessary to perform a query call to the IEP with the corresponding parameters for case.
The following instructions are intended to guide you through the executing of these apis in a postman or similar tool

**The parameters that are important to note that will be modified are:**
messageType = the type of message in each case
payload = add the necessary parameters for each case

**Reminder :**
The LFFAuth token provided as a header parameter, is used to authenticate in the Platform, and needs to be obtained in case is expired. See [Authentication for HWAY Services](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/HWAY/pages/2004549633)
  
## Example Curl
The following is a **baseline** **example** CURL, that will change to have the corresponding parameters for each service to be used.
**Tip**: Use this curl to create a request in postman and use it as baseline for the rest

```auto
curl --location "https://qa.h-way.com/mexbc/subscribermessages/query" ^
--header "LFAuth: eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..u8NvREHhmBSB-3sWUv7tB4YY5bo70pq2wup6rGjTlmg" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/json" ^
--data "{
  \"accountCode\": \"Numi\",
  \"accountId\": \"a9c2df6c-81df-4011-9694-e8edd9d63a50\",
  \"applicationName\": \"HWay\",
  \"subscriberId\": \"b35701b6-7739-46de-85bb-a61a8d708ad1\",
  \"username\": \"+19163015875\",
  \"originIP\": \"167.58.129.110\",
  \"messageId\": \"38d83c4b-66c8-4323-9944-25241895dt9b\",
  \"messageType\": \"TurnOnOffOnHoldTransactionToUser\",
  \"payload\": \"{'UserId':'+19163599958','TurnOnOff':'false','ContractVersion':'1.0.3.4','UserLanguage':null}\"
}"
```

# Add User To Whitelists

Use this API to add a user to the whitelist in the platform (to prevent having the user failed for Black List validation)
**Body Parameters Example for Postman:**

**Tip**: create a new request in postman as a copy from the baseline and change the body

```auto
{
  "messageType": "AddUserToWhitelists",
  "payload": "{'UserId':'+19166019033'}"
}
```

**See Parameters definition:**
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2010447873/WhitelistsApi#AddUserToWhitelists-(Query-message)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2010447873/WhitelistsApi#AddUserToWhitelists-(Query-message))

# Remove User From Whitelists
Use this API to REMOVE a user to the whitelist in the platform (to allow having the user failed for Black List validation if applicable)
**Body Parameters Example for Postman:**

**Tip**: create a new request in postman as a copy from the baseline and change the body

```auto
{
  "messageType": "RemoveUserFromWhitelists",
  "payload": "{'UserId':'+19166019033'}"
}
```

**See Parameters definition:**
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2010447873/WhitelistsApi#RemoveUserFromWhitelists-(Query-message)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2010447873/WhitelistsApi#RemoveUserFromWhitelists-(Query-message))

# Search User In Whitelists
Use this API to search for a user to the whitelist in the platform
**Body Parameters Example for Postman:**

```auto
{
  "messageType": "SearchUserInWhitelists",
  "payload": "{'UserId':'+19166019033'}"
}
```

**See Parameters definition:**
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2010447873/WhitelistsApi#SearchUserInWhitelists-(Query-message)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2010447873/WhitelistsApi#SearchUserInWhitelists-(Query-message))

# Set User Level To Softfailed
Use this API to force the softFail in a specific CIP Level.
This API is intended to be user by operations when is necessary to set a user in a specific point of the CIP process to provide specific data
**Body Parameters Example for Postman:**

**Tip**: create a new request in postman as a copy from the baseline and change the body
**The level to softfail will always be 2**

```auto
{
  "messageType": "SetUserLevelToSoftfailed",
  "payload": "{'UserId':'+19166019036', 'Level':'2','SoftfailType':'InvalidAddress','DocumentType':'Id' ,'DocDocumentSubtype':'SocialSecurityNumber'}"
}
```

**See Parameters definition:**
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2010513422/User+levels+modification#SetUserLevelToSoftfailed-(Query-message)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2010513422/User+levels+modification#SetUserLevelToSoftfailed-(Query-message))

# Set User Level To Success
Use this API to force the softFail in a specific CIP Level.
This API is intended to be user by operations when is necessary to set a user in a specific point of the CIP process to provide specific data
**Body Parameters Example for Postman:**

**Tip**: create a new request in postman as a copy from the baseline and change the body

```auto
{
  "messageType": "SetUserLevelToSuccess",
  "payload": "{'UserId':'+19166019036', 'Level':'2'}"
}
```

**See Parameters definition:**
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2010513422/User+levels+modification#SetUserLevelToSuccess-(Query-message)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/2010513422/User+levels+modification#SetUserLevelToSuccess-(Query-message))

# Definition of Done

This is a pragmatic-approach definition of done with all the steps to consider a User Story / Task / Bug as Done from the Backend perspective.
This document is iterative in nature.
## Conditions to meet for considering a User Story / Task / Bug as “Done” to Backend
1. It works
2. Clear on business value-added
    1. Therefore satisfy the acceptance criteria defined within the User Story by the Business
3. The codebase is in a better state than before
4. Development Team is satisfied with test coverage
5. No apparent bugs
6. Release notes are up-to-date and published
    1. Deploy document was updated
7. The Mobile Contracts are updated
    1. Into the NumiTPE Packages
        1. For the old .Net Framework
        2. For the .net standard
    2. Into the NumiTPE
        1. The MC packages were updated to the latest version
8. It is available in the Development environment (Dev.Sacbepayments) which means
    1. The code was merged to the develop branch and
    2. Deployed into the Development Environment
9. It was tested with the mobile team into the Development environment (Dev.Sacbepayments)
10. It was added as part of the delivery items to be shown as part of the Demo session

# Deployment Checklist

### Proveedores Externos
*   Validar que el ambiente productivo de ellos este listo
*   Disponibilizar credenciales, urls, y configuraciones
### Backup bases de datos
*   GES
*   Raven
*   FireStore (Firebase)
### Numi Event System
*   nuevas configuraciones
*   Analizar impacto de rollback (mas grave es el borrar un evento)
*   Anotar los impactos para rollback
*   deploy
### Configuraciones
*   Agregar nuevas a produccion
*   Validar valores
### Validar servicios a deployar
(Que servicios se deployan)
*   Backend (IEP / servicios)
*   Claudia (LC / consola)
### Migraciones Central
*   Validar con clau (deploy manual)
### Migraciones
*   Validar datos nuevos productivos
*   Deployar y correr
### Deploy
*   LC (manual)
*   IEP (manual)
*   Servicios (todos son automáticos)

# Limit Rules Changes Procedure

1. In case of needing a new tab, create it by making a copy of the most recent one (duplicate option).
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/9d2b6053-8cf9-427a-9c02-7a9a23fdd74c/image-20211006-165557.png)

2. 3. 4. Procede making the necessary changes.

**Some considerations to take into account:**
*   For new limits, get a new Guid.
*   At the right side of the main table (the one with the limit values) there are two more tables. The one on the right has one complete rule for each cell (only the rules enabled will appear in this table)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/b955e952-d1d2-4de0-9628-4c6c58275fe4/image-20220907-033338.png)

Below this table there will be a smaller table with only one cell. This one has the full JSON already generated.
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/e110bd4f-3529-45a0-9ad4-dc1fdd5c34cd/image-20220907-033603.png)

Copy that cell and paste it an empty cell using the “Values Only“ option
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/c6308f38-8c26-4f25-b21a-e83e3d803fd9/image-20220907-033711.png)

### Rules.json
1. Copy the text gotten from the cell of the last step.
2. In the corresponding project, go to Tools > Numi.Migrations > Base > {{Name of the Project}}
3. Replace (in Develop, QA and Production) the content of Rules.json file with the copied text in step 1.
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/7a5f2bf7-d66a-4ad0-a1a4-8cf2ec94c673/image-20211006-165939.png)

4. ![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/ef1ef039-e2e4-435f-a234-03a47d04ed4b/image-20211006-170028.png)

5. 6. 7. Save the changes.
### Migration
1. In the corresponding project, go to Tools > Numi.Migrations > Migrations.
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/a61cea6b-c31a-4129-ac22-5845a658eadf/image-20211006-170204.png)

2. 3. 4. Generate a new migration, taking the current date and time, for the Rules update in the database (Raven)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/6ee3311c-7381-4c4a-966b-369a1a8034d2/image-20211006-170337.png)

5. 6.

# Unlock users (manually)

El desbloqueo manual aplica para aquellos usuarios que quedaron bloqueados antes de liberar la tarea [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZUN-787](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZUN-787)
El procedimiento consiste en revisar el nivel en que falló el usuario e identificar en kibana el paso en el que falló, porque ese valor deberá actualizarse en documento del usuario (UserView: Level1FlowStep o Level2FlowStep, según sea el caso)
Para nivel 2, actualizar en raven
TPEServiceAPIPart0
UserLevelView: En Level 2
Status: UpgradeSoftFailed
StatusDescription: UnreadableDoc
DocumentType:Id
SubType:13
UserView
Level2FlowStep: 4
Lanzar el evento o actualizar la VT subscriber\_info\_virtual

Para nivel 1 falta.

# Repository management

## General
Every service (API) will have their own repository in Gitlab or Github.

## Development
During the development face the team will work on “master” branch. Depending on the feature the team will choose between work and push on the “master” branch or create a feature branch and merge when the feature is finish.
When a feature is completed and pushed to the repository, an automatic process will execute the automated testing and deploy the new code to all the develop environments.

## QA
Once the expected requirements are complete, the code will be merge to the branch “QA” and then on demand will be deployed to the correspond environment.
All the QA environments will be deployed from the “QA” branch and this will be the branch that will be use for merge to prod environments.

## Production
Each production environment will have a specific branch. This branches will take the code from “QA” branch an will be tagged on every deploy.

## Fixes
#### QA
Small fixed detected during the QA work, could be fixed in “QA” branch the deployed and merged to “master” branch. For bigger fixes or we should use a feature branch from “master” and then merged to “master”, tested in develop and then cherry pick to “QA”
#### Production
On fixed for production environment we could make the fix on the production branch for quick release. Then we should take the fix to “QA”, to other productions environments if necessary, and to “master”

## Diagram
[https://app.diagrams.net/#G1he2em\_2xljIpE6-7rmvTlxEkTpLxM4xL](https://app.diagrams.net/#G1he2em_2xljIpE6-7rmvTlxEkTpLxM4xL)
Check the “Repository” page

## Github
### Token github
[https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/keeping-your-account-and-data-secure/managing-your-personal-access-tokens)
*   Generate token (clasic)
*   Assign permissions of read:packages

### Local config
npm set @leapfinancial:registry [https://npm.pkg.github.com](https://npm.pkg.github.com)
npm set [//npm.pkg.github.com](http:////npm.pkg.github.com):\_authToken <<token>>

<<Attention>>
Do not use Powershell, because it doesn't work.

#### Docker
docker login [ghcr.io](http://ghcr.io) -u <<username github>>
The console will request the password. Insert the token generater before.
If this doesn´t work, try:
docker login [ghcr.io](http://ghcr.io) -u <<username github>> -p <<token>>

#### VS Code
Install the plugin "GitHub Actions"

#### Environment Variables
Maintain a `.env` file for each environment (e.g., `.env.dev`, `.env.test`). For convenience during development, you can store these files in the repository **except for production**, where sensitive data should never be committed.
To securely use these environment variables in GitHub Actions, add a secret named `ENV_FILE` for each environment. You can do this easily using the GitHub CLI:

```plain
gh secret set ENV_FILE --body "$(cat .env.dev)" --env dev
```

> 💡 Make sure to install and use the [GitHub CLI](https://cli.github.com/) (`gh`) to run this command.
in powershell

```plain
Get-Content .env.dev -Raw
gh secret set ENV_FILE --body "$envContent" --env lf-dev
```

# Support users

Steps to register support users for access to the Console
According to the LCNext installation, you should obtain the authentication and add the JWT in the header request.

| **Steps**<br> |
| --- |
| **Create a named user**<br>Endpoint https://**\[lcnextURL\]**/subscriberbc/subscribers<br>Header: LFAuth:**\[JWT obtained by APIKey\]**<br>Method: POST<br>`{`<br>`"UsernameFormat": "Free",`<br>`"Username": "xxxxxxx@xx.xxx",`<br>`"Password": "999999",`<br>`"SubscriberType": "Management",`<br>`"AccountCode": "Numi",`<br>`"UID": "e6bcc5e1-62e3-4d43-8bff-3925a2e89995",`<br>`"DevAccountId": "",`<br>`"StoreIdentifier": "com.leapfactor.enterprise.numi",`<br>`"Profile": {`<br>`"FirstName": "Xxxxxxxx",`<br>`"LastName": "Xxxxxxxx",`<br>`"Email": "xxxxxxx@xx.xxx",`<br>`"CountryCode": "MX"`<br>`},`<br>`"ProfileExtensions": [ ]`<br>`}` |
| **Assign Clearance LCAdministration**<br>Endpoint https://**\[lcnextURL\]**/subscriberbc/subscribers/Numi/**\[subscriberId\]**/clearances<br>Header: LFAuth:**\[JWT obtained by APIKey\]**<br>Method: POST<br>`{`<br>`"ClearanceReq": {`<br>`"Realm": "LCAdministration",`<br>`"Ring": "0",`<br>`"ExpiresAt": "2081-01-26T17:31:41.7602767Z"`<br>`}`<br>`}` |
| **Assign Clearance Administration**<br>Endpoint https://**\[lcnextURL\]**/subscriberbc/subscribers/Numi/**\[subscriberId\]**/clearances<br>Header: LFAuth:**\[JWT obtained by APIKey\]**<br>Method: POST<br>`{`<br>`"ClearanceReq": {`<br>`"Realm": "Administration",`<br>`"Ring": "0",`<br>`"ExpiresAt": "2081-01-26T17:31:41.7602767Z"`<br>`}`<br>`}` |
| **Create clearance a new clearance and assign to user (optional)**<br>Endpoint https://**\[lcnextURL\]**/accountbc/accounts/**\[accountId of Numi\]**/realms<br>Header: LFAuth:**\[JWT obtained by APIKey\]**<br>Method: POST<br>`{`<br>`"Name": "Operations",`<br>`"Description": "Operations"`<br>`}`<br><br>**You can obtain account Id of Numi**<br>Endpoint https://**\[lcnextURL\]**/accountbc/accounts/**Numi**<br>Header: LFAuth:**\[JWT obtained by APIKey\]**<br>Method: GET<br>Through this API, you can know the details of the account, for example, the realms, rings and endpoints. |

# Sacbé Realms

There are two default realms defined in Leapfactor account, which are the basic realms for the users of support console.

| **Account Code**<br> | **Realm Name**<br> | **Realm Description**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| Leapfactor | Administration | Leapcentral administration |
| LCAdministration | Leapcentral administration |

The realms of Numi account were defined for specific actions in the console.

| **Account Code**<br> | **Realm Name**<br> | **Realm Description**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| Numi | Compliance | Compliance |
| Operations | Operations |
|  | SACBEUS | Leapcentral identifier to filter user view<br>The users that contains this mark only can be look up the information about US users |
|  | SACBEMX | The users that contains this mark only can be look up the information about Mexican users |

The console’s users can be have only one mark SACBEUS or SACBEMX,
If someone need look up about MX and US; He/She must be have two users

A console user must have minimally assigned realms Administration and LCAdministration. Add one of Numi’s realms for specific actions.

## Compliance Actions:
### Accept OFAC
Durante el proceso de CIP del usuario es posible que el servicio que consulta la puntuación OFAC responda que el usuario debe ser rechazado; y esto puede ser un falso positivo, debido a esto se habilita la funcionalidad de “Accept OFAC”con el propósito de que un funcionario capacitado pueda, a pesar del resultado, hacer que el usuario continúe con su proceso de registro
### Accept RISK
Durante el proceso de CIP del usuario es posible que el servicio que consulta la puntuación del nivel de Riesgo responda que el usuario debe ser rechazado; sin embargo esta respuesta puede ser reconsiderada por un funcionario capacitado, debido a esto se habilita la funcionalidad de “Accept RISK”con el propósito de que un funcionario pueda, a pesar del resultado, hacer que el usuario continúe con su proceso de registro
### Accept OFAC / Reject Operations
Una funcionalidad de mismas proporciones que se da al momento de que un usuario se encuentre operando en la plataforma; puede que durante la revisión de OFAC mientras transacciona de como respuesta la inviabilidad de este usuario para con OFAC, en consecuencia la plataforma frenará toda transacción debido a esto; sin embargo en este punto el usuario capacitado de cumplimiento podrá dar el aval para que la transacción continúe
### RejectedUser
Después del usuario estar bloqueado por el proceso de CIP u OFAC, permite al usuario de compliance negar toda posibilidad de que el usuario pueda continuar con el registro o uso de la aplicación.

## Operations Actions:
### LockUserByPin
Permite al usuario de Operaciones, bloquear al usuario por el Pin

# LeapCentral



# Register Named User

This method is used to register a user in the Leap Central engine, with the full data required, to crate a user in the Platform
Note: In the <<[<<LC URL>>](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/subscriberbc/subscribers%22) portion of the URL you must provide the domain for the corresponding implementation and the corresponding environment (DEV, QA, etc)

**URL:** [https://<<LC URL>>/subscriberbc/subscribers](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/subscriberbc/subscribers%22)
**Method:** POST
**Description of Parameters**

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  **Format or Fixed Value**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| UsernameFormat |  | string |  `"0"` |
| username |  | string |  MobilePhone normalizado |
| password |  | string | Pin de 6 digitos |
| subscriberType |  | string | `Enduser` |
| accountCode |  | string | `Numi` |
| uid |  | Guid | `xxxx00001` |
| storeIdentifier |  | string | `com.leapfactor.enterprise.numi.p2p` |
| profile | see below | **Profile** |  |
| profileExtensions | see below | **ProfileExtension** |  |

## Profile
**Description of Parameters**

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> | <br> **Format or Fixed Value**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| firstName |  | string |  |
| lastName |  | string |  |
| email |  | string |  |
| rememberPIN |  | bool |  |
| language |  | sting | ES-EN |
| dateOfBirth |  | string | yyyy-MM-dd |
| gender |  | string | M-F |
| primaryZIP |  | string |  |
| mobilePhone |  | string |  |
| country |  | string |  |

## ProfileExtension
**Description of Parameters**

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> | <br> **Format or Fixed Value**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| KeyName |  | string |  |
| KeyValue |  | string |  |
| KeyType |  | string |  |
| Application |  | string | P2P |

## Required Profile Extensions
See below the required profile Extentions

```auto
{"KeyName": "Activated","KeyValue": "Succeed","KeyType": "String","Application": "P2P"}

{"KeyName": "AlertsConfiguration","KeyValue": "{"ReceiveByEmail":true,"ReceiveBySms":true,"ReceiveByPush":true,"ContractVersion":\\"1.0.3.4","UserLanguage":null},"KeyType": "String","Application": "P2P"}

{"KeyName": "ExtendedProfile","KeyValue": "{"CountryCode":\\"MX","CityId":null,"CityDescription":\\"MIGUEL HIDALGO","StateId":null,"StateDescription":\\"Ciudad de Mexico","Address2":\\"","Address3":\\"Merida","Address4":\\"San Jose, Sn Jose Tecohf.","PhoneNumber2":null,"SocialProfileIds":[null],"LastName2":\\"Barroso","BirthState":\\"MX.09","Nationality":\\"MX"}","KeyType": "JSONEncoded","Application": "P2P"}]}
```

## Example
See below example in CURL of how to use the endpoint

```auto
curl --location --request POST "https://dev.sacbepayments.com/subscriberbc/subscribers" --header "Content-Type: application/json" --data-raw "{"usernameFormat": "0","username": "+0521331012203","password": "123456","subscriberType": "Enduser","accountCode": "Numi","uid" : "xxxx00001","storeIdentifier": "com.leapfactor.enterprise.numi.p2p","profile":{"firstName": "Luis","lastName": "Osio","email": "correo@correo.com","rememberPIN": false,"language": "ES","dateOfBirth": "1988-07-25T00:00:00","gender": "M","primaryZIP": "53100","mobilePhone": "+0521331012203","country": "México","mailingAddress": "","mobileWorkPhone": "+0521331012203"

},"profileExtensions": [{"KeyName": "Activated","KeyValue": "Succeed","KeyType": "String","Application": "P2P"},{"KeyName": "AlertsConfiguration","KeyValue": "{\"ReceiveByEmail\":true,\"ReceiveBySms\":true,\"ReceiveByPush\":true,\"ContractVersion\":\\"1.0.3.4\",\"UserLanguage\":null}","KeyType": "String","Application": "P2P"},{"KeyName": "ExtendedProfile","KeyValue": "{\"CountryCode\":\\"MX\",\"CityId\":null,\"CityDescription\":\\"MIGUEL HIDALGO\",\"StateId\":null,\"StateDescription\":\\"Ciudad de Mexico\",\"Address2\":\\"\",\"Address3\":\\"Merida\",\"Address4\":\\"San Jose, Sn Jose Tecohf.\",\"PhoneNumber2\":null,\"SocialProfileIds\":[null],\"LastName2\":\\"Barroso\",\"BirthState\":\\"MX.09\",\"Nationality\":\\"MX\"}","KeyType": "JSONEncoded","Application": "P2P"}]}"
```

# Docker

## Dockerfile
Lo dividimos en 2 etapas
1. Build
2. Ejecución
En la etapa 1 instalamos dependencias y compilamos / generamos la aplicación.
En la etapa 2 preparamos la imagen que se va a ejecutar con lo minimo requerido. Esto puede ser copiar el build de la etapa 1 o quizas tambien instalar solo las dependencias de producción.
#### Aclaraciones
`WORKDIR /app` indica el path base sobre la que se va a trabajar en la imagen
`.` referencia el path sobre el que se esta ejecutando. Este puede ser el directorio local o el de la imagen
`COPY package.json .` copia el archivo packege.json desde el directorio local al directorio . (punto) de la imagen. Ver punto anterio para mas detalle
`COPY . .` copia el directorio base local al directorio base de la imagen.
`COPY` primero va el directorio local, luego el de la imagen
`EXPOSE 3000` puerto en el que escucha la imagen. Meramente declarativo, la applicación puede estar escuchando en otro puerto
`COPY --from=builder /app/build ./build` el --from permite especificar otra imagen para asi tomar el build de la imagen de la etapa 1 y copiarlo a la etapa 2
`ARG APP_TYPE` parametro que se envia al dockerfile al ejecutarlo.
`$APP_TYPE` uso de un parametro
`ENV NODE_ENV=production` seteo de variable de entorno en la imagen

```auto
ENV APP_TYPE=$APP_TYPE
CMD if [[ -z "$APP_TYPE" ]]; then yarn start; else yarn start:$APP_TYPE; fi
```

Los parametros no se reemplazan en los CMD, es necesario primero setearlo como variable de ambiente en la imagen.

## Docker-Compose
Se utiliza para agrupar varios servicios en un unico comando de build o start.
Dentro de `services` se listan los servicios a correr con sus configuraciones.
`networks` esta seccion es necesaria y se deben listar todas las redes utilizadas por los servicios antes listados.

#### Aclaraciones

```auto
ports:
      - "${PARTNER_PORT}:3000"
```

Mapeo de puertos entre primero el puerto de la maquina host y luego el puerto de la imagen.
`${PARTNER_PORT}` variable que se toma el archivo .env por default.

```auto
networks:
  public:
    name: proxy-pub
    external: true
```

Si la red se aclara external: true quiere decir que ya tiene que existir previamente y no se crea. Caso contrario la crea si no existe.

# Steps and configurations for a new bundle id

See

| **Step**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Owner**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| Add app in Firebase Console | Go to Project settings / General and define a new app for platforms iOS and Android.<br>Complete at least steps 1 and 2 from firebase wizard<br>Download and share google-service.json for Andriod<br>Download and share GoogleService-Info.plist for iOS | [Claudia Bolanos](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/557058:4d2023d6-97c0-4108-ba2b-cb62c1b0531c?ref=confluence)<br>[Alfredo Vina](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/557058:e0e8fe2e-3f6d-4338-a529-6c028c4a250a?ref=confluence) |
| Config an APNs authentication for Firebase Cloud Messaging | Go to Project settings / Cloud Messaging / Apple app configuration<br>Upload the APNs Authentication Key (\*.p8) only for iOS app<br>You should provide Key ID and Team ID provided by Apple. | [Hector Candia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/5bec3273fbe1467261e65c69?ref=confluence) Generates the APNs Auth Key<br>[Claudia Bolanos](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/557058:4d2023d6-97c0-4108-ba2b-cb62c1b0531c?ref=confluence) |
| Prepare asset file for LCMigration | Edit \[env\]Bundle.json at assest/\[brand\] directory and include new bundle.<br><br><br>When a bundle is required to be deleted, it must be removed directly in central, using the API ApplicationBC:<br><br><br><br> | [Claudia Bolanos](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/557058:4d2023d6-97c0-4108-ba2b-cb62c1b0531c?ref=confluence) |
| Generate Bundle ID Keys for Microblink Products if Applies |  | T.A. |

# Url Shortener

# Tool
Shlink
[https://shlink.io/](https://shlink.io/)

# Configuration
### Apikey
The only way to access to api keys in connected to the docker container
##### List keys
docker exec <<containerId>> shlink api-key:list
##### Generate admin key (works for all domains)
docker exec <<containerId>> shlink api-key:generate
##### Generate domain key (works only in the specific domain)
docker exec <<containerId>> shlink api-key:generate -m <<name>> -d <<domain>>
example
docker exec <<containerId>> shlink api-key:generate -m hway -d [links.h-way.com](http://links.h-way.com)

### Short Url
#### Generate with specific api key
The short link includes the domain set in the api key
POST /rest/v2/short-urls HTTP/1.1
Host: [links-dev.h-way.com](http://links-dev.h-way.com)
X-Api-Key: <<domain api key>>
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Length: 108
{
"longUrl": "[https://www.leapfinancial.com",](https://www.leapfinancial.com%22,)
}

#### Generate with admin api key
The short link includes the default domain, unless send the domain in the body
POST /rest/v2/short-urls HTTP/1.1
Host: [links-dev.h-way.com](http://links-dev.h-way.com)
X-Api-Key: <<domain api key>>
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Length: 108
{
"longUrl": "[https://www.leapfinancial.com",](https://www.leapfinancial.com%22,)
"domain": "[links-dev.h-way.com",](https://www.leapfinancial.com%22,)
}

# Alert Flags for App Status

The alerts are available in Firebase. in the virtual table configs\_virtual. These are stored in configs\_virtual collection.
The app shows a notification when the attribute **Enabled** is **false**.

| **Notification key**<br> | **Description**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| NotificationAvailableCardBillingNoTime |  |
| NotificationAvailableNetwork |  |
| NotificationAvailableNetworkNoTime |  |
| NotificationAvailableSpei |  |
| NotificationAvailableSpeiNoTime |  |
| NotificationAvailableSpeiWithTime |  |
| NotificationAvailableUpgradeLevelNoTime |  |
| NotificationBlocked |  |
| NotificationBlockedNoTime | **This notification is used when you need to block full application** |
| NotificationDownNoTime |  |

# CashNetworkApi

# **Model**

# **GetCashOperators**
**Description:** This API return all information about operators registered and it is consumed from the Mobile App
**Type:** Query Message

**Request:** GetCashOperators

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| CountryCode |  | string |  |
| OperationAmmount |  | decimal |  |

*   CountryCode: Country from which you want to get operators
*   OperationAmmount: The amount of the operation for which the operators are required

**Response:** GetCashOperatorsResponse

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| CashOperator |  | IEnumerable<CashOperators> | CashOperators<br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br>→ CashNetwork<br><br><br><br><br><br>→ CashProvider (Enum)<br><br><br><br><br><br><br>→ CashLocation<br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br>→ CashAccountType (Enum)<br><br><br> |

*   **CashOperator**
    *   id: Unique Identifier of CashOperator
    *   networkId: Unique Identifier of the CashOperator’s Store Chain.
    *   network: List of Networks associated to this operator
    *   country: Country code of this Operator
    *   name: Name of this operator
    *   imageUrl: URL of image to identify this operator
    *   location: List of Locatios associated to this operator
    *   cashAccountType:  Enum that identifies the type of network {CREDIT, PREPAID} 
    *   imageCircleUrl: URL of image to identify this operator (circle shape)
*   **CashNetwork**
    *   id: Unique Identifier of CashNetwork
    *   name: Name of Network
    *   cashProvider: Enum that identifies the type of provider use {Numi, Greendot, Inpamex, NumiCashDelivery, Incomm} 
    *   imageUrl: URL of image to identify this network
*   **CashLocation**
    *   id: Unique Identifier of CashLocation
    *   enable: Identify if location is enabled
    *   cashOperatorId: Id of operator associated
    *   name: Name of Location
    *   addressLine1: Address
    *   addressLine2: Address
    *   state: State code
    *   city:  City Code
    *   longitude: Expression to include longitude value
    *   latitude: Expression to include latitude value
    *   phone: Telephone number
    *   image: URL of image to identify this Location

# **GetReferenceCode**

**Description:** This Api provides Barcode/QR information in order to complete operations in Cashload and it is consumed from the Mobile App
**Type:** Query Message

**Request:** GetReferenceCode

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| OperationId |  | string |  |
| OperationCode |  | string |  |
| Amount |  | decimal |  |
| Currency |  | string |  |
| SenderName |  | string |  |
| ReceiverName |  | string |  |
| NetworkId |  | string |  |
| LocationId |  | string |  |
| OperationType |  | OperationType | <br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br> |
| CashProvider |  | CashProvider | <br><br><br><br><br> |

*   OperationId: internal Id of operation
*   OperationCode: short id of operation 
*   Amount:  amount of money 
*   Currency: Iso 3 for Curency (USD,MXN)
*   SenderName: sender full name
*   ReceiverName: receiver full name
*   NetworkId: this can be obtained from GetCashNetworks
*   OperationType:  can be (SendFunds, RequestFunds, WalletLoad, WalletTransferOut, TopUp, RequestTopUp, SendFundsVirtualAgent, RequestFundsVirtualAgent, StorePayment, SubscriptionFee)
*   CashProvider: can be (Numi, Greendot, Inpamex, NumiCashDelivery, Incomm) and its obtained from CashNetwork.

**Response:** GetReferenceCodeResponse

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Code |  | string |  |
| ExpirationDate |  | DateTime |  |
| CodeType |  | CodeType | <br><br> |

*   Code: string with Code Reference Id, will be used for Get Reference Information
*   ExpirationDate: date ultil Code expires (YYYY/MM/DD)
*   CodeType: Barcode or QR
# **get Reference Code Info**
## This Api provides Barcode / QR information generated in Get Reference Code Api, it contains general info about operation.
**The following Uri is provided to be accessed from a postman service tool.**
Uri: [https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode)[g](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface//SendFundsStorePay)[etReferenceCodeInfo](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode)
Request:
{
"RefCodeId"
}
*   RefCodeId: obtained from Get Reference Code

Response:
{
"RefCodeId"
"OperationId"
"TransactionCode"
"Amount"
"SenderAmount"
"SenderName"
"ReceiverName"
"CashProvider"
"TransactionType"
"DateGeneration"
"ExpDate"
"Status"
}

*   RefCodeId: Reference code for operation.
*   OperationId: Id of operation
*   TransactionCode: shot Id of operation
*   Amount: amount of operation.
*   SenderAmount: amount of money of operation including fees
*   SenderName: sender full name
*   ReceiverName: receiver full name
*   CashProvider:  can be (Numi, Greendot) and its obtained from CashNetwork.
*   TransactionType: can be (Load,Withdraw)
*   DateGeneration: date of code creation (YYYY/MM/DD)
*   ExpDate: date ultil Code expires (YYYY/MM/DD)
*   Status: Success if Reference code id is found.

# **Activate Reference Code**
## This Api uses the Reference code obtained from Barcode/QR in order to complete the operation.
Uri: [https://dev.sacbepayments.com/](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface//SendFundsStorePay)[cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode)
[Uri: https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode)

{
"refCodeId": null,
"operationId": null,
"cashProvider": null,
"networkId": null,
"locationId": null,
"externalData": null
}
*   refCodeId":  Obtained from get reference code id
*   operationId":  Obtained from get reference code id
*   cashProvider": Obtained from get reference code id
*   networkId":  network provided by the Terminal
*   locationId":  network provided by the Terminal
*   externalData":  Json format string from Terminal

Response
{
"transactionId": null,
"status": 0,
"errorMessage": null
}

*   TransactionId: Operation Id
*   Status:  can be Success, ExpiredCode, AlreadyUsedCode, NotExistingCode, WrongNetwork, Failed
*   ErrorMessage: empty if no error occurs

# **Activate Payment Code (Incluvida)**

**Uri:** [**https://dev.sacbepayments.com/**](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface//SendFundsStorePay)[**cashnetworkinterface/activatePaymentCode**](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/activatePaymentCode)
Body:
{
"paymentCode": null, 
"status": 0,
"reason": null,
"networkId": null,
"externalData": null,
"locationId": null
}
*   **PaymentCode\***: Sent previously on Operation creation.
*   **Status\***: 0 for success, 1 for failed.
*   Reason: Open text to explain why its failed
*   **NetworkId\***: must be same network sent on Operation creation.
*   ExternalData:  Json format string from Terminal
*   LocationId: must be same network sent on Operation creation.
**(\*) Obligatory**
Response
{
"transactionId": null,
"status": 0,
"errorMessage": null
}

*   TransactionId: Operation Id
*   Status:  can be Success, ExpiredCode, AlreadyUsedCode, NotExistingCode, WrongNetwork, Failed
*   ErrorMessage: empty if no error occurs

# **Activate CashDelivery Code**
## This Api uses the CashDelivery code obtained from Barcode/QR in order to complete the operation.

Dev Uri: [https://dev.sacbepayments.com/](https://dev.sacbepayments.com/storepay/storepayinterface//SendFundsStorePay)[cashnetworkinterface/activateCashDeliveryCode](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode)
QA [Uri: https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/activateCashDeliveryCode](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/cashnetworkinterface/ActivateReferenceCode)

Body:
{
"RefCodeId": null, 
"TrakingNumber": null,
"CashProvider": null,
"NetworkId": null,
"LocationId": null,
"ExternalData": null,
"Status": 0,
}
*   **RefCodeId\***: Obtained from GetReferenceCode Id
*   **TrackingNumber\***: Tracking number of the code mentioned above
*   CashProvider: Obtained from GetReferenceCode Id
*   **NetworkId\***: Network provided by the Terminal
*   ExternalData: Json format string from Terminal
*   LocationId: Location provided by the Terminal
*   **Status\***: 0 for Success, 1 for Failed

**(\*) Obligatory**

Response
{
"TransactionId": null,
"Status": 0,
"ErrorMessage": null
}
*   TransactionId: Operation Id
*   Status:  can be Success, ExpiredCode, AlreadyUsedCode, NotExistingCode, WrongNetwork, Failed
*   ErrorMessage: empty if no error occurs

    }

# CIP - User Levels

## Response Info
The status of level operations will be in the "subscriber\_info\_virtual" virtual table, in the "UserLevels" field.
The "UserLevels" field contains a json list of "UserLevel".

### User Level
The level contains:
*   Level number (0, 1, 2)
*   Level status
*   Level status details (this will contains info in certain scenarios)
*   Additional info
### Level Status
*   Completed = 0,
*   UpgradePending = 1,
*   UpgradeInProgress = 2,
*   UpgradeFailed = 3,
*   UpgradeSoftFailed = 4

### Level Status Details
*   None = 0,
*   InvalidAddress = 1,
*   InvalidEMail = 2,
*   UnreadableDoc = 3,
*   ProvideAdditionalInfo = 4,
*   InvalidPersonalData = 5,
*   OtpPrivacyMissing = 6,
*   OtpContractMissing = 7,
*   OfacFailed = 8,
*   ComplianceBlocked = 9,
*   GeolocationFailed = 10
### Document Type
*   None = 0
*   Id = 1
*   ProofOfResidence = 2
*   ProofOfIncome = 3
*   ProofOfLife = 4
*   ProofOfAddress = 5
### Additional Info
*   Id
*   Proof of residence
*   Proof of income
### UserIdentityDataType
*   DocumentIdFrontImage
*   DocumentIdBackImage
*   DocumentIdVisaImage
*   SelfieVideo
*   FaceRecognitionData
*   MRZData1
*   MRZData2
*   MRZData3
*   DocumentMimeType
*   DocumentImage
*   DocumentData
*   DocumentAddressProofImage
### DocumentDataKeys
*   Address
*   DateOfBirth
*   DateOfExpiry
*   DateOfIssue
*   DateOfExpiryPermanent
*   DocumentNumber
*   FirstName
*   FullName
*   LastName
*   Sex
*   LocalizedName
*   AdditionalNameInformation
*   AditionalAddressInformation
*   PlaceOfBirth
*   Nationality
*   Race
*   Religion
*   Profession
*   MaritalStatus
*   ResidentialStatus
*   Employer
*   PersonalIdNumber
*   DocumentAdditionalNumber
*   IssuingAuthority
*   DriverLicenseDetailedInfoRestrictions
*   DriverLicenseDetailedInfoEndorsements
*   DriverLicenseDetailedInfoVehicleClass

The user is in the highest completed level number.
The UpgradePending or UpgradeInProgress status show that backend is working on it and must await until a completed status
UpgradeSoftFailed implies that we need to ask for other information or retry the upgrade.
UpgradeFailed implies that user is not acceptable.

# Configurations

The configs will be stored on the "configs\_virtual" virtual table

*   **WalletCountryMode (Only Sacbe)**
    *   This config sets the wallet mode per country
    *   Wallet Modes:
        *   Auto
        *   SemiAuto
    *   If a country doesn't have a value, implies that won't be a Numi card in that country
    *   *   
*   **SessionTimeOut**
    *   This configuration sets the maximum duration of the session.
    *   Time in seconds.

*    **BeneficiaryCountries**
    *   List of countries ISO code alpha 2

*   **BankRegistrationCountryMode**
    *   Modes:
        *   Plaid
    *   If a country doesn't have a value, implies that we'll register the bank without external system.

*   **AccountCountryMode (Only Tzune)**
    *   This config sets the Account mode per country
    *   Account Modes:
        *   Auto
        *   SemiAuto

*   **CardCountryMode (Only Tzune)**
    *   This config sets the Card mode per country
    *   Enabled: 
        *   true
        *   false
    *   Card Modes:
        *   Auto
        *   SemiAuto
    *   _Sample Json: {"ModesByCountry":{"MX":{"Enabled":true,"CardMode":"SemiAuto"}}}_

*   **BlinkIdTypes**
    *   Replace BlinkID-Types.json

*   **CashDepositCountryData**
    *   Sample json:
    *   {
    *       "DataByCountry": {
    *           "US": {
    *               "Currency": "USD",
    *               "LowerLimit": 25.0,
    *               "UpperLimit": 500.0,
    *               "FeeAmount": 1.99,
    *               "MonthlyLimit": 2500.0
    *           }
    *       }
    *   }

*   **TrialMode**
    *   ConfigValue: Set 1 when the app allows add personal accounts (case Friends&Family). Set 0, when the app must work without that restriction. 

*   **IncommEnabled**
    *   ConfigValue: Set 0 to hide Cash Load  and Cash Deposit in the add account lists. Set 1 to enable options.
    *   Note: The corridors must be active only in backend. You don't need register the corridor in configs\_virtual 
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/4eb2bc83-0dfc-44a9-93d5-6e9800a86566/IncommEnabled.png)

*   

*   **RewardsEnabled**
    *    ConfigValue: Set 1 when the app enables "Rewards" and "Cash Hunter" in the home page. Set 0 to disable sections.
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/a223f7fd-7d58-447f-a98b-367edb7fd6c7/rewardsEnabled.png)

*   *   **StoreURL**
    *   Setting for Apple Store and Google Play. 
    *   ConfigValue: {"AppleStoreURL":"[https://apps.apple.com/us/app/h-way/id1635450512](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/h-way/id1635450512)", "PlayStoreURL":"[https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.leapfinancial.hway.numi.android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.leapfinancial.hway.numi.android)"}

# DeepLink

# General info
To see the link information you could use this url [https://channel.leapcentral.net](https://channel.leapcentral.net)[/links](https://channel.code2go.mobi/links)/{id} where the id is the las part of the url. Ej [https://channel.leapcentral.net](https://channel.leapcentral.net)[/l/numi/FMX](https://channel.code2go.mobi/l/numi/FMX) to [https://channel.leapcentral.net](https://channel.leapcentral.net)[/links/FMX](https://channel.code2go.mobi/links/FMX)

### **Affiliate conventions**
Organic user: when the **affiliatePartnersId** property of the attribution link is null, string empty or has the value: **EypwPIjtEk-hy-\_m2psZ8A**; the user must be considered as organic.
Branded user: when the **affiliatePartnersId** property of the attribution link has a value different of: **EypwPIjtEk-hy-\_m2psZ8A**, the user is branded.

Deep link app actions
Deep link may contain a property call “Action”. This property indicates witch action should perform the app and could be one of these values:
*   ActivateNumiVirtualCard. App should start the virtual card activation flow
### Code
Action: DeepLinkAction
DeepLinkAction {
ActivateNumiVirtualCard
}

# Deep link operations
Deep links generated on operations process contains the following data:
*   **operation** (base64 encoded operation JSON)
*   **AffiliateId**
*   **CampaignId**
*   **AccountCode**
*   **affiliatePartnersId**
*   **affiliatePartnersData**

# Numi Card

Virtual Card
*   Request
    *   RequestNumiVirtualCard (Forward)

## Physical Card
*   Request
    *   RequestNumiPhysicalCard (Forward)

## Activate Card
*   Request
    *   ActivateNumiCard (Forward)

## Set Pin
*   Request
    *   CreateNumiCardPin (Forward)

## Card Pickup
*   Request
    *   GetNumiCardPickupNetworks (Query)
    *   GetNumiCardPickupLocations (Query)
*   Response
    *   GetAffiliateCashNetworksResponse (will be renamed)
    *   GetAffiliateCashLocationsResponse (will be renamed)

## Card Details
*   Request
    *   GetNumiCardDetails (Query)
*   Response
    *   GetNumiCardDetailsResponse

# Operations

# Amounts
*   Amount: The amount that user enters on the app on send or request
*   SenderAmount: The amount that will be charged on sender payment method. This could cotains fees or not depending on FeeType
*   RecipientAmount: The amount that will be pushed to recipient payment method. This could contais fees or not depending on FeeType.

```auto
 public class StorePaymentRequest : BaseDataRequest
    {
        public Guid CorrelationId { get; set; }
        public Contact SendTo { get; set; }
        public string Code { get; set; }
        public PaymentMethod SourcePaymentMethod { get; set; }
        public PaymentMethod DestinationPaymentMethod { get; set; }
        public decimal Amount { get; set; }
        public string Currency { get; set; }
        public decimal SenderAmount { get; set; }
        public string SenderCurrency { get; set; }
        public decimal RecipientAmount { get; set; }
        public string RecipientCurrency { get; set; }
        public OperationFeeType FeeType { get; set; }
        public decimal SourceFee { get; set; }
        public decimal DestinationFee { get; set; }
        public decimal TransactionFee { get; set; }
        public decimal BonusAmount { get; set; }
        public DateTime? ProcessAt { get; set; }
        public Dictionary<OperationDataType, string> OperationData { get; set; }
        public string DebtCollector { get; set; }
        public string Ip { get; set; }
        public string RelatedQuoteId { get; set; }
    }
    
     public enum OperationDataType
    {
        TopUpProductId,
        IsSenderAmount,
        StorePayData,
        AuthorizationCode,
        OperationSign,
        IsCashBackOperation,
        SupportTicketId
    }
```

```auto
        "ChargeType": "Destination",
        "ChargeMethod": "RelativeAmount",
        "Amount": 2
      }
    ],
    "Taxes": [
      {
        "ChargeType": "Destination",
        "ChargeMethod": "RelativeAmount",
        "Amount": 1
      }
    ],
    "CorridorRequirements": [
      {
        "Type": "NumiCard",
        "UserRole": "Recipient"
      },
      {
        "Type": "UserLevel2",
        "UserRole": "Recipient"
      },
      {
        "Type": "UserLevel2",
        "UserRole": "Sender"
      }
    ]
  }
```

# Pay in Store

#   

**GetStorePayNetworks** 

## This API return all the information about operators registered
## **To be consumed from:** Mobile App
**Request:**
{
"CountryCode":
}
*   CountryCode : Country from which you want to get operators

**Response:**

{
"storePayNetwork": \[
{
"id": null,
"externalId": null,
"name": null,
"countryCode": null,
"imageUrl": null,
"enable": false,
"storePayBranchOffices": \[
{
"id": null,
"externalId": null,
"networkId": null,
"name": null,
"addressLine1": null,
"addressLine2": null,
"imageUrl": null,
"enable": false
}
\]
}
\]
}

*   StorePayNetworks
    *   id: Unique Identifier of Network
    *   externalId: ReferenceId of Network  
    *   name: Name of network
    *   countryCode: Country code of this Network
    *   imageUrl:  URL of image to identify this branchOffice
    *   enable: Identify if Network is enabled
    *   storePayBranchOffices: List of branchOffice associated 

*   StorePayBranchOffice
    *   id:  Unique Identifier of CashOperator
    *   externalId:  Reference Id of BrachOffice 
    *   networkId: Id of network associated
    *   name: Name of branch Office
    *   addressLine1: Address
    *   addressLine2: Address
    *   imageUrl: URL of image to identify this branchOffice
    *   enable: Identify if branchOffice is enabled

## **GetIdentityCode** 
## This api provides a code which will be used to consult the user identification information.
## **To be consumed from:** Mobile App.

**Request**:
{
      "SubscriberId": "1bae6b46-119d-4767-a37c-73d5fd496e46",
}
*   SubscriberId: sender Id

**Response**:
{
"Code":"",
"ExpDate":"",
"CodeType":""
}
*   Code: Base 64 string with Code Reference Id, will be used for SendFundsToMerchant
*   ExpDate: date ultil Code expires (YYYY/MM/DD)
*   CodeType: Will be Barcode or QR.

Properties must be sent on Operation
*   `PaymentCode` added as a OperationData using StorePayData(3) enum
*   `AccountId` added on DestinationPaymentMethod on AccountId

# Payment Methods

# Founding Methods
When users wants to fund an operation, they need a funding payment method. This payment method will be on the virtual table:  "source\_of\_funding\_virtual". 
# Adding a payment method
To add a payment method is necessary make a forward to RegisterPaymentMethod. Backend will register this new payment method to the virtual table "source\_of\_funding\_virtual".
Depending on the funding method, the registration can be specialized, example, for bank account see Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-31657](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-31657)) for more details.
# Receiving Methods
On P2P flows (ProductType: Funds) the receiver user needs a payment method. This payment method will be on the virtual table:  "source\_of\_funding\_virtual". See "Adding a payment method" for more details.

# Removing a payment method
PaymentMethodAPI manages the user's payment methods, when a payment method is deleted its status changes to Removed. While in the other services and in the virtual table it is physically removed. In the case of a transaction the payment method data is in the operation itself.

# BankAccount

## Get Bank URL
**Type:** POST
**Path:** /getbankurl
**Request:** GetBankURLRequest

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| SubscriberId |  User identifier | Guid |  |
| UserLanguage |  | string |  |
| CallLocationLongitude |  | double |  |
| CallLocationLatitude |  | double |  |
| ContractVersion |  | string |  |

**Response:** GetBankURLResponse

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| URL | Identificator | string |  |
| RedirectUri | Redirect Url | string |  |
| Status | Response status | enum | Valid values:<br>Succeeded = 0,<br>Failed = 1,<br>Queued = 2 |
| UpdateMode | **true** when user requires update credentials or reconnect current paymentMethod.<br>**false** when is net new user. | bool |  |
| StatusMessage |  | string |  |

## Add Bank Account
**Type:** POST
**Path:** /add/bankAccount
**Request:** AddBankAccountRequest

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| SubscriberId |  Subscriber identifier | Guid |  |
| BankName | Bank name | string |  |
| PublicToken | Public token | string |  |
| PaymentMethodId |  Payment method identifier, **only is reconnection** | string |  |
| UserLanguage |  | string |  |
| CallLocationLongitude |  | double |  |
| CallLocationLatitude |  | double |  |
| ContractVersion |  | string |  |

**Response:** BankAccountResponse

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| Success |  | boolean |
| ResponseDetail | Some values:<br>_BankAccountSubtypeNotValid_<br>_BankAccountBalanceInsufficient_<br>_BankAccountIsOwnerNameFailed_ | string |
| PaymentMethod | Payment method added | PaymentMethodResponse |

**PaymentMethodResponse Definition**

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| Id | Payment method id | string |
| UserId | User id (mobile phone) | string |
| Name | Payment method name | string |
| Type | Payment method type, in this case **BankAccount** | enum<br>MobileWallet = 0,<br>BankAccount = 1,<br>DebitCard = 2,<br>CreditCard = 3,<br>CashLoadLocation = 4,<br>CashPayoutLocation = 5,<br>MTOLoad = 6,<br>None = 7,<br>StorePay = 8,<br>AppleDigitalWallet = 9,<br>GoogleDigitalWallet = 10 |
| Country |  | string |
| AvailableBalance |  | decimal |
| Currency |  | string |

# StatusCode Status codes for Account Lock and PIN expiration

| Titulo | Codigo | Subtitulo | Mensaje |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Account Lock | 110 | Due to Inactivity | To reactivate your account please call support |
| Account Lock | 310 | Due to multiple incorrect PIN entries | To reactivate your account please call support |
| Account Lock | 210 | Due to the simultaneous use of devices to login | To reactivate your account please call support |

| Titulo | Codigo | Subtitulo | Mensaje | Campos Adicionales |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Warning | 200 | Account logged into another device (1° attempt) | Login into both devices simultaneously will block your account |  |
| Warning | 200 | Account logged into another device (2° attempt) | Login into both devices simultaneously will block your account |  |

| Titulo | Codigo | Subtitulo | Mensaje | Campos Adicionales |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Warning | 300 | Account logged (1° attempt) | 1st Invalid PIN- Account will be blocked after 3 attempts | Number of attempt and total number of possible attempts |
| Warning | 300 | Account logged (2° attempt) | 2nd Invalid PIN- Account will be blocked after 3 attempts | Number of attempt and total number of possible attempts |

| Titulo | Codigo | Sesión |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| PIN LifeSpan | 400 | El usuario es autenticado |  |
| PIN Expired | 401 | El usuario es autenticado para hacer posible el cambio de PIN |  |
|  |  |  |  |

LFDK response failed
{
"StatusCode" : 110,
"Attempt": 0,
"TotalAttempts": 0
}

LFDK response success
{
"StatusCode" : 0,
"Attempt": 0,
"TotalAttempts": 0
}

### General Information

```auto
public enum Status
    {    
        /// <summary>
        ///     Operation completed. Money transferred from source to destination
        /// </summary>
        Completed,
        /// <summary>
        ///     Fund request rejected by recipient (the one to fund it)
        /// </summary>
        Rejected,
        /// <summary>
        ///     Operation not completed. Failure
        /// </summary>
        Failed,
        /// <summary>
        ///     Operation Cancel process completed. 
        /// </summary>
        CancelationCompleted,
        /// <summary>
        ///     Operation Cancel process rejected. 
        /// </summary>
        CancelationRejected,
        /// <summary>
        ///     Operation Cancel process failed. 
        /// </summary>
        CancelationFailed
    }
```

# Google Location Features

## General Features
The following piece of information is received in the Mobile App by Google Location Services information when performing the autocomplete validation of the data provided by the user:

```swift
{
   "html_attributions" : [],
   "result" : {
      "address_components" : [
         {
            "long_name" : "296",
            "short_name" : "296",
            "types" : [ "street_number" ]
         },
         {
            "long_name" : "Calle Avenida Dos",
            "short_name" : "Calle Av. Dos",
            "types" : [ "route" ]
         },
         {
            "long_name" : "San Pedro de los Pinos",
            "short_name" : "San Pedro de los Pinos",
            "types" : [ "sublocality_level_1", "sublocality", "political" ]
         },
         {
            "long_name" : "Ciudad de México",
            "short_name" : "México D.F.",
            "types" : [ "locality", "political" ]
         },
         {
            "long_name" : "Ciudad de México",
            "short_name" : "CDMX",
            "types" : [ "administrative_area_level_1", "political" ]
         },
         {
            "long_name" : "Mexico",
            "short_name" : "MX",
            "types" : [ "country", "political" ]
         },
         {
            "long_name" : "03800",
            "short_name" : "03800",
            "types" : [ "postal_code" ]
         }
      ],
      "adr_address" : "\u003cspan class=\"street-address\"\u003eCalle Av. Dos 296\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"extended-address\"\u003eSan Pedro de los Pinos\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"region\"\u003eBenito Juárez\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"postal-code\"\u003e03800\u003c/span\u003e \u003cspan class=\"locality\"\u003eCiudad de México\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"region\"\u003eCDMX\u003c/span\u003e, \u003cspan class=\"country-name\"\u003eMexico\u003c/span\u003e",
      "formatted_address" : "Calle Av. Dos 296, San Pedro de los Pinos, Benito Juárez, 03800 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico",
      "geometry" : {
         "location" : {
            "lat" : 19.3857871,
            "lng" : -99.1844325
         },
         "viewport" : {
            "northeast" : {
               "lat" : 19.3871443302915,
               "lng" : -99.18315441970849
            },
            "southwest" : {
               "lat" : 19.3844463697085,
               "lng" : -99.18585238029151
            }
         }
      },
      "icon" : "https://maps.gstatic.com/mapfiles/place_api/icons/geocode-71.png",
      "name" : "Calle Av. Dos 296",
      "place_id" : "ChIJwZRWLX__0YUR0Sf4pjolYf0",
      "reference" : "ChIJwZRWLX__0YUR0Sf4pjolYf0",
      "scope" : "GOOGLE",
      "types" : [ "premise" ],
      "url" : "https://maps.google.com/?q=Calle+Av.+Dos+296,+San+Pedro+de+los+Pinos,+Benito+Ju%C3%A1rez,+03800+Ciudad+de+M%C3%A9xico,+CDMX,+Mexico&ftid=0x85d1ff7f2d5694c1:0xfd61253aa6f827d1",
      "utc_offset" : -300,
      "vicinity" : "San Pedro de los Pinos"
   },
   "status" : "OK"
}
```

With this information it´s necesary to provide a map to the Address Object in the Platform
Firstly, a new field will be created to save all this piece of information
> . de lo que devuelve el servicio de Google se enviará al backend todo lo que se reciba en “result” en el nuevo campo a agregar **PlaceDetail**
That mapping will happen as follows:
> . dentro de “address\_components” se agregará un ítem extra con types = custom\_level\_1 en donde se cargará el primer valor ”region” encontrado en “adr\_address”
el valor “region” se enviará aparte al backend en el campo Address3
> el valor “`sublocality`“ se enviará al backend en el campo Address4

The object/nugget created in mobile layer to handle this map, is called **PlaceDetails**

**Current (HWAY and RAAS) format of PlaceDetails object in Mobile**

The fields in the platform are completed with the information of the PlaceDetails object / nugget

**Address1** = the google location service address 1 field
**Address2** \= an empty string
**Address3** = PlaceDetail.CustomLevel1;
**Address4** = PlaceDetail.Colony;
**City** \= [PlaceDetail.City](http://PlaceDetail.City);
**State** = PlaceDetail.State;
**ZipCode** = PlaceDetail.ZipCode;
**Country** \= PlaceDetail.CountryCode;

## Features Affected by this changes (legacy project)
*   CIP process when the user provides or updates his address information, to be adressed in the story: [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1644](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1644)
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
Request Physical Card when the user provides or updates the address when he wants his physical card delivered, to be addressed in the story: Provide Google Location Information for Physical Card Delivery Address
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1691)*   [SAC-1691](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1691) CLOSED
*   When the user is entering the information of a Beneficiary in any flow, if he need to provide or update the beneficiary address, to be addressed in the story: Provide Google Location Information for Beneficiary Address

# Upload Files Service

### Upload Signature to AWS
this api works to get the file Url to upload signatures at amazon.
Dev Base Url: https://dev.tzune.com/userFileUploadService/userfiles/signUpload

```auto
    [Route("/userfiles/signUpload", Verbs = "POST", Summary = "Sign operation upload url. It is valid for 5 minutes. Use signed URL and PUT request.")]
    public class UploadOperationSign : CQRS.Command, IReturn<SignedUploadUrlResponse>
    {
        public string OperationCode { get; set; }
        public string IndexFile { get; set; }
        public string FileExtension { get; set; }
    }
```

### Upload Documents to AWS
this api works to get the file Url to upload images at amazon.
Dev Base Url: https://dev.tzune.com/userFileUploadService/userfiles/operation

```auto
    [Route("/userfiles/operation", Verbs = "POST", Summary = "Sign the upload file url. It is valid for 5 minutes. Use signed URL and PUT request.")]
    public class SignedUploadUrl : CQRS.Command, IReturn<SignedUploadUrlResponse>
    {
        public string OperationCode { get; set; }
        public string IndexFile { get; set; }
        public string FileExtension { get; set; }
    }
```

### Upload Documents to File System
this api works to get the file Url to upload to Server.
Dev Base Url: https://dev.tzune.com/userFileUploadService/userfiles

```auto
    [Route("/userfiles", Verbs = "POST")]
    public class UserFileUpload : CQRS.Command, IReturn<UserFileUploadResponse>
    {
        [ApiMember(Name = "File", ParameterType = "body", DataType = "file")]
        public object File { get; set; }

        public string FileType { get; set; }
        public string FileExt { get; set; }
        public string FileExtension { get; set; }
        public string ContentType { get; set; }
    }
```

### Upload QR of a business account to AWS bucket
This api works to upload a qr (in svg) of a business account to AWS bucket (QRImagesBucket)
Dev Base Url: https://dev.tzune.com/userFileUploadService/accountfiles/putBusinessAccountQRToBucket

```auto
    [Route("/accountfiles/putBusinessAccountQRToBucket", Verbs = "POST")]
    public class PutBusinessAccountQRToBucket : CQRS.Command, IReturn<PutBusinessAccountQRToBucketResponse>
    {
        public string SVGText { get; set; }
        public string DestFileName { get; set; }
    }
```

# Notification_virtual

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Example**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Id | Identificator | string |  |
| Read | El read lo setea mobile, a bkd no le importa | bool |  |
| Date |  | Datetime |  |
| Action |  | NotifiactionAction | <br><br><br><br> |
| Data | Alt 1:<br>{Nombre: “Pepe”, Mensaje: “hola”}<br><br>Alt 2<br>\[“Pepe”, “hola”\] | string |  |
| Title | Depende del diseño | string |  |
| Description | Depende del diseño | string |  |
| Image | Depende del diseño | string |  |

### Alternativa 1
El mensaje sale de backend en el idioma correspondiente, configurado en algun lado por idioma. El problema que presenta es que ante un cambio del idioma, las notificaciones anteriores al cambio estan en el idioma anterior

### Alternativa 2
El mensaje se encuentra en una VT de notificaciones en los idiomas correspondientes, y mobile toma el que corresponde segun el idioma del usuario

# GetMerchantAccountConfiguration

its a Query Message “GetMerchantAccountConfiguration“
Url: http://dev.tzune.com/numi/api/operations/subscribermessages/query
Headers:
LFAuth
Request:

```auto
    public  class GetMerchantAccountConfiguration : BaseDataRequest
    {
        public string AccountId { get; set; }
    }
```

Response:

```auto
    public class MerchantAccountConfiguration : BaseResponse
    {
        public string AccountId { get; set; }
        public AccountStatus Status { get; set; }
        public bool ChargeWithCardEnabled { get; set; }
    }
    
    public enum AccountStatus
    {
        Requested,
        InProgress,
        Failed,
        Completed,
        Blocked,
        OtpMissing = 999
    }
```

# Authorization Token (OTP)

this Api provide send Token by sms process

Url: http://dev.tzune.com:440/tpe/api/authorizationTokenRequest
this Api will send OTP Token to `MobileNumber` and **requires** LFAuth Key.

```auto
 public class AuthorizationTokenRequest
    {
        public string MobileNumber { get; set; }
        public string CountryCode { get; set; }
        public AuthorizationType AuthorizationType { get; set; }
    }
    
     public enum AuthorizationType
    {
        PersonalAccount,
        MerchantAccount
    }
```

Url: http://dev.tzune.com:440/tpe/api/tokenValidationRequest
this Api will send receive Token from authorization token request from `MobileNumber` and will validate if token is correct and **requires** LFAuth key.

```auto
  public class TokenValidationRequest 
    {
        public string MobileNumber { get; set; }
        public string Token { get; set; }
        public string CountryCode { get; set; }
        public AuthorizationType AuthorizationType { get; set; }
    }
    
     public enum AuthorizationType
    {
        PersonalAccount,
        MerchantAccount
    }
```

Response:

```auto
public class TokenValidationResponse : ProviderResponse
    {
        
    }
    
     public class ProviderResponse
    {
        public ProviderRequestStatus Status { get; set; }
        public string StatusDescription { get; set; }
    }
    
    public enum ProviderRequestStatus
    {
        Completed,
        Failed,
    }
```

# Cancel operation

**Type:** Query Message
**Request:** CancelOperationRequest
**API to cancel a cash delivery code**. The status response indicates if the operation is cancellable, although the cancelation happens in background.

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| OperationId |  Id operation | string |
| ReferenceCode | Reference code<br>(Cash delivery code) | string |

**Response**:

| **Name**<br> | **Definition**<br> | **Type**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| Status |  Status | bool |  |
| TicketId | Support ticket | string | When the operation is not cancellable, generates a support ticket. |
| ErrorCode | Error code | string |  |
| ErrorDescription | Error description | string |  |

```auto
public class MobileChargeRequest : BaseDataRequest
{

    public string Email { get; set; }

}
```

# UpdateMerchantAccount Api

**Description:** An Api to modify User’s Merchant Account type.
The Request’s Payload must include the UserId and the fields which values need to be modified.
In case of a Request’s Payload that ONLY includes the UserId, the Api will be used as a query service for the User’s MerchantAccount type.
The Response comes with the corresponding status (Succeeded or Failed) and, in case of Succeded, with the information related to the User’s Merchant Account type.

**Url Dev:** [https://dev.tzune.com/mexbc/json/reply/QuerySubscriberMessageRequest](https://dev.tzune.com/mexbc/json/reply/QuerySubscriberMessageRequest)
**Url QA:** [http://qa.tzune.com:80/numi/api/operations/json/reply/QuerySubscriberMessageRequest](http://qa.tzune.com/numi/api/operations/json/reply/QuerySubscriberMessageRequest)
**Url Prod: Pending until de Prod ambient is restored by ABC**

**Type:** POST
**Headers**:
*   Content-Type → application/json
*   response-content-type → json
*   Authorization:
Key: LFAuth
Value (Dev & QA):
eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..7c7vaEkkr6DPJmHU9XviRSYH8Gl5rQt2uOuhCo2ly3g

**Value (Prod): Pending until de Prod ambient is restored by ABC**

**Request Fields:**

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> | <br>**Example**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| UserId **(required)** | string | `'+5217651841504'` |
| MerchantType | MerchantType | Valores permitidos:<br>'NoMerchant'<br>'TzuneMerchant'<br>'OwnMerchant'<br> |
| Subscription | Subscriptions | `'{\"Id\":\"d0366ce4-f089-4f0f-b49b-dc537100fc32\", \"From\":\"2009-01-05T00:00:00.0000000\",\"Until\":\"2023-12-13T00:00:00.0000000\"}'` |
| Verified | VerifiedStatus | Valores permitidos:<br>'NotVerified'<br>'Verified'<br> |

**Request Body (Example for Dev & QA):**
`{     "messageType": "UpdateMerchantAccount",     "payload": "{'UserId':'+5213310120075', 'Verified': 'Verified', 'Subscription': '{\"Id\":\"09366ce4-f089-4f0f-b49b-dc537100fc32\", \"From\":\"2009-01-05T00:00:00.0000000\",\"Until\":\"2023-12-13T00:00:00.0000000\"}'}" }`

**Request Body (Example for Prod): Pending until de Prod ambient is restored by ABC**

**Response Fields:**

| **Name**<br> | **Type**<br> |  **Values**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| StatusMessage | string |  |
| Status | ResultStatus | <br><br><br> |
| AccountId | string |  |
| MerchantType | MerchantType | <br><br><br> |
| Subscriptions | Subscription |  |
| Verified | VerifiedStatus | <br><br> |

**Response Body (Successful Example):**
`{     "messageId": "a2dad4a2c77143598528875873701421",     "messageType": "UpdateMerchantAccount",     "payload": "{\"accountId\":\"dd6d3976-c4b6-4abd-85fd-1c06d76a9010\",\"merchantType\":\"OwnMerchant\",\"subscription\":{\"id\":\"09366ce4-f089-4f0f-b49b-dc537100fc32\",\"from\":\"2009-01-05T00:00:00.0000000-06:00\",\"until\":\"2023-12-13T00:00:00.0000000-06:00\"},\"verified\":\"Verified\",\"status\":\"Succeeded\",\"statusMessage\":null}",     "remarks": null }`

# UpdateUser Api

**Description:** An Api to modify User’s data. The Request’s Payload must include the UserId and the CountryCode, which are mandatory, and the fields which values need to be modified. The Response, in case of success, comes with the User’s data after changes were applied.
