# Atomic

Atomic is a company that povides financial analuysis and through it advice on investments to end users, and the corresponding steps necessary to allow the users to execute those investments
As per March 2023 Leap financial integration with Atomic is intended to allow **hway** users to have a specific module to get advise and invest
The following link contains information specific to the integration between Leap Financial and Atomic:

The work that needs to be done to achieve this integration is concentrated in the following Epic = [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/epic.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1569)[HWAY-1569](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1569) - Investments Module with Atomic Backlog
## Technical Details
In order to integrate with Atomic module the following needs to be achieved
**Pre- Conditions:**
there will be a section in the **hway app** to access the Atomic module, called “Investment Module” from the App Home
*   UX Details to be provided by [Valeria Ruiz](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/5cf14e2ae68f3d0fab740870?ref=confluence)
**Steps to Achieve**
1. When the user enters for the first time to the Investments Module:
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
Create an Investment Module User
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1568)[](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1568)1. [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1568)[HWAY-1568](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1568) - Create an Investment Module User and relate it to the Hway User IN PROGRESS using Atomic Apis , and retrieve the Atomic user id created
2. When the user is created, the data sent to Atomic will be part of what is called the “Identity Verification”, the information that was not entered by the user in hway, such as tax id, country of citizenship and residency status, needs to be entered in the Atomic Module
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
The user needs to access the Atomic Module by an Specific URL that will be created with a token session ID, for the specific ID of the Usercreated in the step a.
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1743)[](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1743)1. [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1743)[HWAY-1743](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1743) - Generate user specific Investment WebView URL IN PROGRESS
2. When the onboarding is not yet completed, the user will be allowed to continue using the Atomic Module.
    1. It is needed to verify that the user has not yet completed its onboarding / identity data, inentity status not completed of onboarding: _this will be achieved by validtaing that there was no update in the identity verification and the initial status “verification not started” remains the same_
3. When the onboarding is completed, the user will have a verification period done By atomic, during that period, a specific screen will be displayed for the user to let him know he cannot access Atomic yet, because the revision is undergoing
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
It is needed to verify the verification process has started by Atomic: _This will be achieved by Analyzing the “verification\_processing” status provided in the Events emmited by Atomic into the webhook that hway platform has_
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426)[](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426)1. [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426)
    [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426)1. [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426)[HWAY-2426](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426) - Handle Event of Identity Verification Status Backlog
2. When the onboarding is verified, it can be successfull or failed, the user will have to be updated to reflect this situation,
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
It is needed to verify the outcome of the verification status: _This will be achieved by Analyzing the “user\_verification\_succesfull or “user\_verification\_failed” status provided in the Events emmited by Atomic into the webhook that hway platform has_
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426)[](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426)1. [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426)
    [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426)1. [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426)[HWAY-2426](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2426) - Handle Event of Identity Verification Status Backlog
2. If the Identity Verification was successfull, the user will be considered as completed, so two things will happen
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
Connection of the account in Atomic with the information of the **hway account** of the user: using Atomic Apis, with the information of the hway user: Create external investment account
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1729)[](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1729)1. [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1729)
    [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1729)1. [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1729)[HWAY-1729](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1729) - Create external investment account IN PROGRESS
    2. Update the status of the user’s verification status to allow him to have the access to the webview everty time he wants to
2. If the Identity verification was failed, the user will be considered as not allowed to access the Invesmtments Module, so the following needs to happen:
    1. Update the status of the user’s verification status to disable the access to the web view permanently
    2. _In a further phase II the verificatin failed will undergo a different process that Atomic Calls “KYC-Retry” but this process is out of scope for the current integration_
**Considerations:**
1. When the user already has an Investment Module User, every time he wants to access the module, it is necesary to obtain the id of the Investment Module user, in order to create the url for the specific user and the specific session
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
Identify the id of the Investment Module User for the hway user
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1744)[](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1744)1. [](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1744)[HWAY-1744](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1744) - Identify the id of the Investment Module User for the hway user IN PROGRESS
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)
When the user accesses the Atomic Module it will be displayed with specific User Experience as detailed in the following story
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1588)2. [HWAY-1588](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1588) - Display the Module in a WebView Backlog

## Authentication Details
Please refer to the following guideline provided by Atomic on how to implement the Authentication in Each Environment (from = [https://docs.atomicvest.com/docs/security](https://docs.atomicvest.com/docs/security))
### IMPLEMENTATION GUIDE
Every request to the API (regardless of whether you are using Sandbox, or Live environment) must include the header:
Text

```auto
Atomic-ID
```

As mentioned above, this can be obtained in **Organization > Keys** in the dashboard UI.
## SANDBOX ENVIRONMENT
The sandbox environment does not require any other header. This environment should purely be used for testing and quick prototyping. Use the Sandbox environment for an environment that mirrors the final Live environment.
## LIVE ENVIRONMENTS
Both of these environments must include two additional headers. The first header is:
Text

```auto
Atomic-Timestamp
```

This is a string denoting seconds since the unix epoch. The second header is:
Text

```auto
Atomic-Signature
```

This header is a cryptographically signed digest of the request. To generate this header, create a sha256 HMAC signature with the Atomic API Secret for the string concatenation of:
Text

```auto
`timestamp` + `method` + `request path` + `request body`
```

Follow this by base64 encoding the digest:
Text

```auto
Base64(HMAC-SHA-256(atomic_api_secret, timestamp + method + request_path + request_body))
```

Timestamp is the number of seconds since Unix Epoch (the same value set for the header `Atomic-Timestamp`). The timestamp must be within 2 minutes of the API request or the request will be considered expired. If you believe there may be clock skew beyond the 2-minute expiration window please contact technical support. Note: using milliseconds will result in an invalidated request, this signature expects the timestamp header to be in seconds.
The requests that require `content-type: multipart/form-data` do not require a signature check.
## **Sandbox Credentials**
Please note sandbox does not need the secret to be used in the API Call when executing from example from Postman
NOTE _to copy the keys look in the attachments_

# Create and Retrieve Users in Atomic

Atomic provides the ability to create a user in their system by using an endpoint, that can also be used to retrieve the information of the users
`http://sandbox.api.atomicvest.com/users`
## Create a User
`POST http://sandbox.api.atomicvest.com/users`
The Model that needs to be honored and the mandatory data **to create a user** is detailed in the following link
[https://docs.atomicvest.com/docs/capture-user-identity#model](https://docs.atomicvest.com/docs/capture-user-identity#model)
With the information currently existing in the platform a user needs to be created using this endpoint with the **POST** method
Below is a map between the information that atomic needs and what information from the platform should be used:

| **Field**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Field in Platform**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| `identity.first_name` | The user's legal first name. | ✓ | First Name of hway app User |
| `identity.last_name` | The user's legal last name. | ✓ | Last Name of hway app user |
| `identity.email` | The user's verified email address. | ✓ | email of hway app user |
| `identity.phone_number` | The user's verified phone number, including country and area codes. | ✓ | phone of hway app user |
| `identity.date_of_birth` | The user's date of birth as present in government-issued identification in YYYY-MM-DD format. | ✓ | date of birth provided by the user |
| `identity.address` | The user's permanent residence address.<br>The address is an object with the following format:<br>"address": { "country\_code": "USA", "street": "3101 SW 22ND AVE", "additional": "suite 202", "city": "MIAMI", "region": "FL", "postal\_code": "33133" },<br> | ✓ | The users address with the following correspondence of the fields:<br><br>\- **country code** = country of the address provided by the user following the ISO Alpha-3 country code standard.<br>\- **street** : address1 of the user<br>\- **additional**: address2 of the user<br>\- **city**: City of the user<br>\- **region**: State of the User. For USA and Canada only it should be following the ISO Alpha-2 state code standard.<br>\- **postal\_code:** postal code of the user<br> |
| `identity.government_ids` | The government ids provided by the user.<br>The government id Is an object containing the following information:<br>      {         "country": "ARG",         "type": "passport",         "number": "AAH689538"       }<br> | ✓ | The user id information (if available for the user) with the following correspondence of the fields<br>**country** \= ISO three letter code of the country of the user’s provided id (during CIP) - Passport should display the country of issuance of the passport. National legal USA documents should display USA. To Be Confirmed other id types<br>**type** \= the following image depicts the accepted types<br>![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/c58fb70e-c7ce-42e4-9fc0-203e67dc9019/image-20231114-003813.png)<br>**number** \= the number of the user’s provided id (during CIP)<br>**Mapping of Types in the platform**<br>\- Passport = Passport<br>\- Drivers Licence = Drivers Licence<br>\- identification\_card = state id<br>\- tax\_id = SSN or ITIN<br>If the hway user provided a different form of id not listed above, this field will not be sent to Atomic |
| `identity.gender` | gender of the user | ✓ | gender of the user |
| `custom_user_id` | the id the user will be identified by in the hway system | ✓ | suscriber\_id of the user in the platform |
|  |  |  |  |

## Retrieve a User
`GET location https://sandbox.api.atomicvest.com/users?custom_user_id=3c7a21b0-2b9b-43a8-ac23-d0b85a53c963`
With the same endpoint, used as a **GET location** method providing the custom user id of an existing user, as a query url paremeter, a user can be retrieved

| **URL Parameter**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Field in Platform**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| `custom_user_id` | id used to identify the user | suscriber id |
|  |  |  |

## Endpoint Information
The API documentation for this specific endpoint can be reviewed here
[https://docs.atomicvest.com/reference/usermanagerservice\_createuser](https://docs.atomicvest.com/reference/usermanagerservice_createuser)
## EXAMPLES
### Create User
###   

### Example Request

```auto
curl --request POST \
     --url http://sandbox.api.atomicvest.com/users \
     --header 'Atomic-ID: Oksb8iRx8spF6vkrdKY6iQMzY87Wkd' \
     --header 'accept: application/json' \
     --header 'content-type: application/json' \
     --data '
{
  "identity": {
    "address": {
      "country_code": "USA",
      "street": "3101 SW 22ND AVE",
      "city": "MIAMI",
      "region": "FL",
      "postal_code": "33133",
      "additional": "suite 202"
    },
    "government_ids": [
      {
        "country": "ARG",
        "type": "passport",
        "number": "AAH689538"
      }
    ],
    "first_name": "Vanesa",
    "last_name": "Vilas",
    "email": "vanesa@leapfinancial.com",
    "phone_number": "19166019000",
    "date_of_birth": "1978-02-06",
    "gender": "female"
  },
  "custom_user_id": "3c7a21b0-2b9b-43a8-ac23-d0b85a53c963"
}
'
```

### Example Response

```auto
{
  "id": "9c675f1a-dd59-4d4c-af70-13a695746c30",
  "created_timestamp": "2023-03-20T21:37:26.637612357Z",
  "last_modified_timestamp": "2023-03-20T21:37:26.637612357Z",
  "custom_user_id": "3c7a21b0-2b9b-43a8-ac23-d0b85a53c963",
  "identity": {
    "first_name": "Vanesa",
    "last_name": "Vilas",

}
```

### Retrieve a User
### Example Request

```auto
curl --location "https://sandbox.api.atomicvest.com/users?custom_user_id=3c7a21b0-2b9b-43a8-ac23-d0b85a53c963" ^
--header "Atomic-ID: Oksb8iRx8spF6vkrdKY6iQMzY87Wkd" ^
--header "accept: application/json"
```

### Example Response

```auto
{
    "elements": [
        {
            "id": "9c675f1a-dd59-4d4c-af70-13a695746c30",
            "created_timestamp": "2023-03-20T21:37:26.638728Z",
            "last_modified_timestamp": "2023-03-20T21:37:26.638728Z",
            "custom_user_id": "3c7a21b0-2b9b-43a8-ac23-d0b85a53c963",
            "identity": {
                "first_name": "Vanesa",
                "last_name": "Vilas",
                "email": "vanesa@leapfinancial.com"
            }
        }
    ]
}
```

according to [https://www.patriotsoftware.com/blog/payroll/what-is-a-taxpayer-identification-number-tin/](https://www.patriotsoftware.com/blog/payroll/what-is-a-taxpayer-identification-number-tin/)

# Retrieve User ID and Generate Session for Web View

The following steps are a guide on how to get the Atomic User ID and then retrieve and use the configuration object -including the session token- to create the specific web view URL for the user
The session token will allow to create a web view specifically for the selected user, and the configuration will define the user experience including which screens should not be displayed and how the module should behave.

1. **Retrieve the user with the custom user id (suscriber ID from hway)**
2. **Example in curl for sandbox**
3. **Request**
4. EXAMPLE using the following custom\_user\_id from an hway QA user subscriber id =805cd6f2-4d39-454c-9a18-91b8eb4063c9

```auto
curl --location "https://sandbox.api.atomicvest.com/users?custom_user_id=805cd6f2-4d39-454c-9a18-91b8eb4063c9" ^
--header "Atomic-ID: Oksb8iRx8spF6vkrdKY6iQMzY87Wkd" ^
--header "accept: application/json"
```

5. 6. **Response**

```auto
{
    "elements": [
        {
            "id": "68feaac0-9b71-4d4b-99f4-f9fea91ad815",
            "created_timestamp": "2023-11-16T23:29:39.652349Z",
            "last_modified_timestamp": "2023-11-16T23:49:17.034210Z",
            "custom_user_id": "805cd6f2-4d39-454c-9a18-91b8eb4063c9",
            "identity": {}
        }
    ]
}
```

7. 8. **get the Token for the specific user ID (atomic user ID)**
9. 10. [https://sandbox.api.atomicvest.com/auth/onetime-tokens](https://sandbox.api.atomicvest.com/auth/onetime-tokens)
EXAMPLE In the above example the atomic user ID is =

```auto
"id": "68feaac0-9b71-4d4b-99f4-f9fea91ad815",
```

**Example in curl for sandbox**
**Request**

```auto
curl --location "https://sandbox.api.atomicvest.com/auth/onetime-tokens" ^
--header "Accept: application/json" ^
--header "Atomic-ID: Oksb8iRx8spF6vkrdKY6iQMzY87Wkd" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/json" ^
--data "{
    \"user_id\":\"68feaac0-9b71-4d4b-99f4-f9fea91ad815\" 
}"
```

**Response**

```auto
{
    "token": "QC4ACC66TWBFH4GMSJF3PDRFQGK6PZVYXQ3LM6JNGD5ZYWN2KFFAENZDVK4LTFCZEIESEXTLC5LAE2QPO3LXEACM6F4NEFYIKUYU4KY"
}
```

NOTE **this token needs to be used as part of the configuration object.**
**RESTRICTIONS**
The token expires every 30 seconds.
Also, be aware that the token is “one use only”
1. **With the token obtained, generate the config object to encode**
**note:** to encode, remove the external brackets first

```auto
{
"token": "XKSFXKBLAAMT7NQBEY2DVSXOBCN3BT3I5GNQ23JLQZXZGAADSBBRR2HA4KQF7FO6CMWZJTGFGLYZ7HLFS3MTZJELFKXSFMJ7Z22IGAA",
"origin": "https://www.h-way.com/ ",
"shouldCloseAfterOnboarding": false,
"excluded_onboarding_pages": [
 "page-verify-identity",
 "page-personal-info",
 "page-contact-info",
 "page-address"
 "page-get-started",
 "page-connect-account"
]
}
```

NOTE The configuration object will have the following info

| **Field**<br> | **Details**<br> | **Value to set**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| token | the token obtained from that user | the resulting token from calling one time token with the user id from atomic for the hway user |
| origin | the originating page | `https://www.h-way.com/` |
| shouldCloseAfterOnboarding | determines if the module should close | `false` |
| excluded\_onboarding\_pages | the pages that will be excluded form the atomic module´s user experience.<br>It’s an array of names of pages as defined in atomic’s documentation:<br>[https://docs.atomicvest.com/docs/retail-onboarding-module-pages](https://docs.atomicvest.com/docs/retail-onboarding-module-pages) | Wtin the array the following names of pages should be set (to avoid to display them unnecesesarily)<br>**Note:**<br>none of this pages needs to be displayed because all the data is already collected in the creation of the user with data that hway platfomr pass trhough to atomic |

1. **Encode the response config objet to url, and pass it as a parameter of the module URL**

```auto
curl '<https://module.cdn.atomicvest.com/atomic-module/latest/index.html?config=<url-encoded> config object>`
```

**URL base for sandbox**
[https://module.cdn.atomicvest.com/atomic-module/latest/index.html?config=](https://module.cdn.atomicvest.com/atomic-module/latest/index.html?config=)
## **Example of encoded config object complete url**
[https://module.cdn.atomicvest.com/atomic-module/latest/index.html?config={%22token%22%3a%22J5Z3Z2HKFDBQBANSHFFU4Z7UHK25X76S3XIAH3B7RVM2TYKAO2TF2JROUVJQRFMQVWRVDS73A3U6CBL6WOPGUO2F3Z7L2BB7LCDX6BA%22%2c%22origin%22%3a%22https%3a%2f%2fwww.h-way.com%2f%22%2c%22shouldCloseAfterOnboarding%22%3afalse%2c%22excluded\_onboarding\_pages%22%3a%5b%22page-verify-identity%22%2c%22page-personal-info%22%2c%22page-contact-info%22%2c%22page-address%22%2c%22page-get-started%22%2c%22page-connect-account%22%5d}](https://module.cdn.atomicvest.com/atomic-module/latest/index.html?config={%22token%22%3a%22J5Z3Z2HKFDBQBANSHFFU4Z7UHK25X76S3XIAH3B7RVM2TYKAO2TF2JROUVJQRFMQVWRVDS73A3U6CBL6WOPGUO2F3Z7L2BB7LCDX6BA%22%2c%22origin%22%3a%22https%3a%2f%2fwww.h-way.com%2f%22%2c%22shouldCloseAfterOnboarding%22%3afalse%2c%22excluded_onboarding_pages%22%3a%5b%22page-verify-identity%22%2c%22page-personal-info%22%2c%22page-contact-info%22%2c%22page-address%22%2c%22page-get-started%22%2c%22page-connect-account%22%5d})

# Connect hway account to Atomic Module



# Atomic Events

## General Details
Atomic provides a series of events in different categories that can be used to determine the status or the situation for several information of the users.
As part of the 2023 integration, it was stated that the events should be handled at platform level to serve as information to implement different logic according to the status of the user, and its identity verification, to be able to determine what the user can and cannot do in relation to the atomic module web view
## Event Handling
Basically the logic of the Event Handling should be as follows
1. When the user is created, the id verification status is still not started, as part of the response indicates:

```auto
      "verification_status": {
          "identity_verification": {
              "code": "IDENTITY_VERIFICATION_NOT_STARTED"
          },
          "document_verification": {
              "code": "DOCUMENT_VERIFICATION_NOT_STARTED"
          }
      }
  }
```

**While the user has not yet completed their onboarding, or has started entering the data but not finished, no event will be triggered, so this initial status should be stored in the user’s information, to allow the platform to indicate the mobile layer that the user can access the web view to complete their data.**
1. When the user completes their data in the atomic module web view, he will be prompted to wait while their identity verification (with the data he provided) is being processed. This will be indicated by one of the Atomic Events, whose endpiont is /verification and whose message should be “**verification\_processing**” (see technical details section below for the details of the event)
**While this event is present, the platform should implement a mehcanism to let the mobile layer know that the user should be displayed with a screen in the hway app, indicating that the identity verification process is still ongoing, and he needs to wait to access the web view, and not allow the user to enter the webview**
1. When the Atomic Team completes the identity verification, and the verification is succesfull a new Atomic Event will be emmited, that can be identified in the /user endpoint and whose message should be “**user\_identity\_verification\_success**” (see technical details section below for the details of the event)
**When this event is detected, the platform should implement a mehcanism to update the status of the verification process, and let the mobile layer know that the user is now allowed to enter the webview**
**At this point also the hway account -if present- should be linked within the Atomic System (see** Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-23977](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-23977)) **)**
1. When the Atomic Team completes the identity verification, but the verification is FAILED a new Atomic Event will be emmited, that can be identified in the /user endpoint and whose message should be “**user\_identity\_verification\_failed**” (see technical details section below for the details of the event)
**When this event is detected, the platform should implement a mehcanism to update the status of the verification process, and let the mobile layer know that the user WILL NOT BE allowed to enter the webview, so it should display a message accordingly**
There will be a phase II handling of this the verification failed, that Atomic calls “KYC - Retry” but it will be implemented in 2024

## Technical Details
The following links contain the information from Atomic related to Events
1. Register the Webhook where the events will be sent = [https://docs.atomicvest.com/docs/registering-your-endpoint](https://docs.atomicvest.com/docs/registering-your-endpoint)
2. Events listing = [https://docs.atomicvest.com/docs/overview-2](https://docs.atomicvest.com/docs/overview-2)

# SPEI Payments with Arcus

THIS DOCUMENT IS NOT COMPLETED AND NEEDS REVIEWEING UPDATING

Arcus provides a solution in Mexico to allow [**SPEI**](https://www.banxico.org.mx/services/interbanking-electronic-payme.html) **Payments**, this is, allow to send funds to someone in **Mexico** throught their **bank account,** using the identification number of such account ([CLABE](https://bank.codes/guides/clabe-numbers-guide/))
# Technical Details
The mechanisms to perform a SPEI Transaction trhough Arcus, involves the creation of a series of elements in Arcus systems and the interaction with Arcus to receive notifications.
**See the following Graphic for the Overall Process**

1. **Leap Financial Global Account (Main and default) created in Arcus**
    1. There will be a Customer entity created in Arcus records for one time only. This Customer will contain the information of the account where Leap Financial will set the funds available for the Transactions.
    2. When the customer is created, also a Transaction Instrument is created. In this case, the TI can be re-used for future disbursements
    3. The Leap customer id in Arcus will be the “default customer”.
    4. The default customer is always the “source” of the transactions.
2. **Creation of the Transaction ”Instrument” for each Recipient**
    1. In Arcus, each transaction will correspond to an entity called Transaction instrument, with specific elements, that allows to generate a SPEI Transaction within the Mexican Banking Network. The Transaction Instrument will contain:
        1. The source account will be Leap’s default Customer
        2. The destination account, will be the user that receives the funds. The information of the destination account will be completed with the CLABE and personal information of the mexican user receiver of the transaction.
    2. Once the Transaction Instruments are created, the TI need to be used in each Transaction that contains the same destination, Arcus provides a series of webhooks to notify the status of such transaction
3. **Execution and Status of the Transaction**
    1. The SPEI transaction itself is called Payout in Arcus
    2. The Transaction will be executed using the TI created, or re-using an existing TI if the destination account already existed
    3. Arcus will execute the Transaction within the SPEI network and provide the status of the sPEI Transaction in a webhook
    4. Leap will consume the webhook and process it in the Platform accordingly to the coresponding status received in the webhook

## Implementation Details
The current implementation for this integration is going to be applied in [RAAS](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NMC/pages/1887764487/General) project
*   The creation -and validations- of the customer and the transaction, are provided through a series of APIs.

*   The web hook needs to be configured providing the url of the callback to be used as listener, and the and then provides the information of the status of the transaction.
*   In Numi Platform, SPEI Payment is included as a specific payment method = UBN = Unified Bank Number

# Arcus PAAS API

The PAAS API has the following Documentation Hub
[https://developers.arcusfi.com/docs/paas/bfe1c8b2dfdbe-quick-start](https://developers.arcusfi.com/docs/paas/bfe1c8b2dfdbe-quick-start)

**Example PRODUCTION Transaction Completed Event**
(information from the Emails in the Certification Phase)

El evento de transaction completed, no arroja los datos. Lo cual nos deja únicamente la opcion de usar la información del CEP para la confirmación de los datos del usuario, 

{\\"Id\\":\\"EV\_2605dbf481a0\\",\\"object\\":\\"event\\",\\"type\\":\\"payments.status.completed\\",\\"data\\":{\\"id\\":\\"PAY\_857cfc27ec6b\\",\\"object\\":\\"payment\\",\\"type\\":0,\\"status\\":{\\"status\\":0,\\"code\\":\\"\\",\\"message\\":\\"\\"},\\"customer\_id\\":\\"CUS\_830282618768\\",\\"source\\":\\"TI\_31e38c4d4b51\\",\\"destination\\":\\"TI\_fcd40b9d32dd\\",\\"currency\\":\\"MXN\\",\\"amount\\":1,\\"metadata\\":{\\"description\\":\\"Tx al usuario Test Prod Hector Candia\\",\\"trx\_reference\\":\\"0813241\\",\\"tracking\_id\\":\\"20240813907061W43QKCYAZWSZJ23\\",\\"CEP\\":{\\"beneficiary\_name\\":\\"\\",\\"beneficiary\_id\\":\\"\\",\\"URL\\":\\"[https://www.banxico.org.mx/cep/go?i=90706&s=20211117&d=oDPInwQheu8FukwQsgmCGDUpqX0nayfwaWUVR63DPqoTQjbK%2FvGILJyg6JJ7Z6TB7n6vAzmogwJBr02ww3XLe6RYcckPFdKkUuA79uFqEms%3D\\](https://urldefense.com/v3/__https:/www.banxico.org.mx/cep/go?i=90706&s=20211117&d=oDPInwQheu8FukwQsgmCGDUpqX0nayfwaWUVR63DPqoTQjbK*2FvGILJyg6JJ7Z6TB7n6vAzmogwJBr02ww3XLe6RYcckPFdKkUuA79uFqEms*3D*5C__;JSUl!!NDdRaFrjhKsg!p30Fh5K3w3KGBKIfeAl-MWsrlCEkjwoOuQJYga9yMiUCpx6uXuuK2ofSwRvW7YL6Br6Ew_m3AEJ5AhIA7n95Vj3UXOk$)"},\\"digital\_signature\\":\\"072fc5fe91200e5355a3489e41f42a2b817e229f5e9808104a749c7e1b17ea6e\\"},\\"creation\_date\\":\\"2024-08-13T16:58:41Z\\",\\"update\_date\\":\\"2024-08-13T16:58:51Z\\",\\"date\\":null},\\"event\_date\\":\\"2024-08-13T16:58:51.686Z\\"}

# Enhacements for Remmitance Transaction - Type 36

As per the following enforcement required by Arcus (see pdf original email attached)

> We will be enabling Transaction Type 36 as a payment type for Arcus transactions effective October 23, 2025. This new transaction type allows payments to be properly identified as remittance within the SPEI system, ensuring appropriate handling by participating institutions in accordance with Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Compliance procedures established by Banxico.  
>    
> Starting October 27, 2025, the use of Transaction Type 36 will be mandatory for all remittance-related payments.  
>    
> To ensure compliance, we kindly request confirmation of your readiness to support these transactions and implement the required data fields by the effective date.  
>    
> For a transaction to be categorized as Type 36, the following data fields must be included in the payment:  
>    
> • remittance\_type  
> • remittance\_id  
> • country  
> • currency  
> • foreign\_provider  
> • national\_provider  
> • ex\_rate  
>    
>    
> Documentation[Link](https://developers.arcusfi.com/docs/payments---spei-remittances/wz3hipv4tfwam-create-spei-remittances-payment)  
>    
> Failure to include the above fields may result in certain SPEI participants rejecting payments originating from remittances, and we cannot guarantee their continued acceptance without full compliance and additional actions to be evaluated by Arcus

1) It is necessary to add the mentioned fields in the payment API call
*   See below image with considreations on the information of the new fields to be added

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/723f7612-e429-47d3-9420-d16c399e93e4/image.png)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/b9bc5d86-4eab-429d-909d-da9c2eb11543/image.png)

The Full Curl examñle with the mentioned fields will be as follows

```plain
curl --location "https://paas-sdbx.arcusfi.com/v1/payments" ^
--data "{
    \"customer_id\": \"CUS_f2b612199682\", 
    \"source\": \"TI_0fd6fa6b06d8\",
    \"destination\": \"TI_bee2ac8fc54c\",
    \"amount\": 85559,
    \"metadata\": {
       \"description\" : \"Tx al usuario 1234-215-59654-14523\",
       \"trx_reference\": \"1908242\",
       \"remittance_type\": \"inbound\",
    \"remittance_id\": \"23poi-1235-9098766rhfjgk-4893939kl\",
    \"country\": \"US\",
    \"currency\": \"USD\",
    \"foreign_provider\": \"Leap Financial\",
    \"national_provider\": \"IFPE KLU TECH\",
    \"ex_rate\": \"19.2500\"
    }
}"
```

2) The Response will be handled as currently with no changes,
3) The response will contain an "echo" off the mentioned fields, as described in the reference documenation

[

developers.arcusfi.com

https://developers.arcusfi.com/docs/payments---spei-remittances/wz3hipv4tfwam-create-spei-remittances-payment

](https://developers.arcusfi.com/docs/payments---spei-remittances/wz3hipv4tfwam-create-spei-remittances-payment)

> Fix: Stack trace de-obfuscation when type namespace information is missing.Fix: The configured PDB password is not used at command line when using the Babel Obfuscator NuGet package.Fix: Stack trace de-obfuscation added support for type generic parameters, and stack frame without namespace information.Fix: PDB source file information is not encrypted for .NET Core assemblies.Fix: Fixed crash of obfuscated application in certain scenario when enum type removal is enabledFix: Assemblies could be identified as already obfuscated if types are found in the global namespaceFix: Fixed warning \[W00002\] cannot parse BAML resource stream '...baml': Unable to read beyond the end of the stream, occurring in certain scenario when merging assemblies with XAML resources.  
>    
> Babel Encrypt Plugin 1.1.0.0  
>    
> Hash string encryption  
>    
> Fix: Output of encrypted string in obfuscation log can cause the truncation of the log file.Fix: When useCodeEncryption is set to true the application crash.Fix: application crash when enabling dynamic proxy calls.  
>    
> Should you have any question about this release feel free to contact us at [support@babelfor.net](mailto:support@babelfor.net)  
> Thank you again and best wishes.  
>    
> Kind Regards,  
> [_babelfor.NET_](http://babelfor.NET)

# Cybersource

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/73b91ed2-d9ac-4041-ab94-8abfd7d1c67e/image-20200717-001948.png)
## General Details
Cybersource is a partner to VISA that allows the OCT Transactions to be performed from one card or Account To a Credit or Debit Card
[https://www.cybersource.com/en-us.html](https://www.cybersource.com/en-us.html)
The usage and details of Cybersource “Payout” mechanism can be found in the following link
[https://developer.cybersource.com/api-reference-assets/index.html#payouts\_payouts\_process-a-payout](https://developer.cybersource.com/api-reference-assets/index.html#payouts_payouts_process-a-payout)

IN SACBE Cybersource was integrated to be able to access the “payment to a card” feature, and in this manner, be able to process a cross border payment from a USA User’s wallet, to a MEX User’s debit card (and hence, its wallet)
Cybersource can be implemented to perform an OCT to any other debit card no matter what country.
Enhancements to Platform to support using Cybersource to to OCT from the a Platform account to any international Debit Card [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/NUMI-53)[NUMI-53](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/NUMI-53) - Enhancements to Platform to support using Cybersource to to OCT from the a Platform account to any international Debit Card Backlog
## Technical Details
When moving to PRODUCTION ENVIRONMENT the urls will change as follows:
[api.cybersource.com](http://api.cybersource.com/)
### VGS Outbound Route for Cybersource
To complete the integration, In order to be able to send the information to Cybersource, the VGS proxy needs to be setted up with an outbound route to Cybersource, to be able to complly with the CPI regulations and always handle the Tokenized information for the Card.
### Testing Account Information:
The information to connect with the credentials of the account created by Cybersource to use their APIs in testing mode are detailed in the following Page:
Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24197](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24197))
### Examples of API Usage
The following information was sent by Fidel Palacios from Cybersource
> Developer Center Payout Sample:  
> [https://developer.cybersource.com/api-reference-assets/index.html#payouts\_payouts\_process-a-payout\_samplerequests-dropdown\_payout-card-not-token](https://developer.cybersource.com/api-reference-assets/index.html#payouts_payouts_process-a-payout_samplerequests-dropdown_payout-card-not-token)  
> Bin Lookup and Payouts Sample attached.  
> Finally attached is the POSTMAN collection I am using. It includes samples of many different types of requests.  
> Step by Step guide to make Cybersource POSTMAN Collection work on your machine with your mid.  
> Login to EBC [https://ubctest.cybersource.com/ebc2/app/Home](https://ubctest.cybersource.com/ebc2/app/Home)  
> Generate Key in EBC 2 see key.txt attached to this page  
> Be sure to select API Keys in filter  
> ·       Save Shared Secret  
> ·       Copy Key Serial Number, this will be key id  
> OPEN MY\_REST.postman\_environment file that is in .zip file attached to this article.  
> ·       Ctrl + f 'REPLACE\_WITH\_KEY\_SERIAL\_NUMBER' and replace with Key Serial Number copied in previous step. (3 instances)  
> ·       Ctrl + f 'REPLACE\_WITH\_SECRET\_KEY' and replace: with Secret Key (2 instances)  
> ·       Ctrl + f  'REPLACE\_WITH\_YOUR\_TEST\_MID' and replace with your test mid (2 instances)  
> ·       Save  
> OPEN the file MY\_RESTAPI.postman\_collection  
> ·       Ctrl + f and Replace 'REPLACE\_WITH\_YOUR\_TEST\_MID' with your test mid (64 instances)  
> ·       If testing Token Management API, Ctrl + f and Replace 'REPLACE\_WITH\_YOUR\_OTS\_PROFILEID' with the ConfigDB value for field ots\_profileid of your test MID. If no value exists, your test mid has not been configured properly for TMS. Configure for TMS before proceeding.  
> ·       Save  
> Download and install Postman. Open the Postman application on your desktop.  
> Go To: Files → Setting → proxy and turn OFF Use System Proxy and Global Proxy Configuration  
> Import Postman collection and Environment  
> ·       MY\_REST.postman\_environment  
> ·       MY\_RESTAPI.postman\_collection  
>   
> Choose environment – MY\_REST as shown in screenshot  
> Click on  
> ·       CYBERSOURCE-RESTAPI  
> ·       Goto: PayOuts→ PayOut.  
> note -- If you don’t see the sidebar as pictured below, got to View → Toggle Sidebar  
> 10.   Click send.  
> 11.   You should see a response similar to below, indicating that you were able to make a successful REST API Authorization request

## Documents for Integration July 2020

# Cybersource APIs Information and Resources

## How to generate the information for Authentication
[https://developer.cybersource.com/docs/cybs/en-us/payments/developer/all/rest/payments/GenerateHeader/httpSignatureAuthentication.html](https://developer.cybersource.com/docs/cybs/en-us/payments/developer/all/rest/payments/GenerateHeader/httpSignatureAuthentication.html)
## Response Codes Information
[https://support.cybersource.com/s/article/What-does-this-response-code-mean](https://support.cybersource.com/s/article/What-does-this-response-code-mean)

## Payout Response Codes
The first round of Testing for SACBE Implementation resulted in the following response in our Platform

```json
{"clientReferenceInformation":{"code":"da38b531-a3d5-452b-af51-3c5d39febb4b"},"id":"6069465713206728004237","processorInformation":{"systemTraceAuditNumber":"918205","responseCode":"64"},"status":"DECLINED"}
```

This was the response fro Support on Code 64
You may learn a bit more about this by reviewing Code 64 on page 31 of the Payouts Using the Simple Order API guide found here: [https://developer.cybersource.com/library/documentation/dev\_guides/payouts\_SO/Payouts\_SO\_API.pdf](https://developer.cybersource.com/library/documentation/dev_guides/payouts_SO/Payouts_SO_API.pdf)

# Bin Lookup

### **_/bin/v1/binlookup_**
> [https://developer.cybersource.com/docs/cybs/en-us/bin-lookup/developer/all/rest/bin-lookup.html](https://developer.cybersource.com/docs/cybs/en-us/bin-lookup/developer/all/rest/bin-lookup.html)
Fields

| CardNumber | Número de tarjeta tokenizado |
| ---| --- |
| PayoutInquiry | Cuando se encuentra en true, como respuesta se incluira la información relativa a los payouts |

## Example Request
**Note**:
to execute in postman you must have configured a proxy with VGS crednetials : Private (https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27717)

```plain
curl --location --request POST 'https://apitest.cybersource.com/bin/v1/binlookup' \
--header 'V-C-MERCHANT-ID: lpfp2p000000001' \
--header 'Date: 1662058034' \
--header 'Host: apitest.cybersource.com' \
--header 'Digest: SHA-256=cOUpwcFTVTNQJJW+9S3VJpd4VNX/Fa7z7O+nAXHLbxE=' \
--header 'Signature: keyid="b4a35208-54ab-4793-ae6b-f7c438a024f7", algorithm="HmacSHA256", headers="host date (request-target) digest v-c-merchant-id", signature="3qTkcPnyPNzEwArOzaz8nw6vm98JHm8gcPP+6s98n/w="' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0' \
--header 'X-Secret-Key: CY0sqYGb4IS0JPac8qtA3e5wEL5X7NGmE9fKvYZgz3E=' \
--header 'X-Key-Id: 3bf402db-0443-4e60-b487-ad0afb86901e' \
--data-raw '{
    "paymentInformation": {
        "card": {
            "number": "tok_sandbox_tENi684FotKkWtCnheu42m_1111"
        }
    },
    "processingInformation": {
        "payoutOptions": {
            "payoutInquiry": true
        }
    }
}'
```

You may also use the full PAN (w/o tokenizing) but the proxy needs to be present

```plain
curl --location "https://apitest.cybersource.com/bin/v1/binlookup" ^
--header "V-C-MERCHANT-ID: lpfp2p000000001" ^
--header "Date: 1662058034" ^
--header "Host: apitest.cybersource.com" ^
--header "Digest: SHA-256=eLeNyaDVPD8GYQLM7jCfG25LOgNlb6vWs8VKR36w+AQ=" ^
--header "Signature: keyid=\"dcf6792e-158d-4e6b-8b30-295a01dcb144\", algorithm=\"HmacSHA256\", headers=\"host date (request-target) digest v-c-merchant-id\", signature=\"ngaBL/TOOiWp7Ndq6upmX3R4JdK4Pcl/r2Qr4FmBMpo=\"" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/json" ^
--header "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0" ^
--header "X-Secret-Key: CY0sqYGb4IS0JPac8qtA3e5wEL5X7NGmE9fKvYZgz3E=" ^
--header "X-Key-Id: 3bf402db-0443-4e60-b487-ad0afb86901e" ^
--data "{
    \"paymentInformation\": {
        \"card\": {
            \"number\": \"5033961989100023\"
        }
    },
    \"processingInformation\": {
        \"payoutOptions\": {
            \"payoutInquiry\": true
        }
    }
}"
```

[https://developer.cybersource.com/docs/barclays/en-us/bin-lookup/developer/all/rest/bin-lookup.html](https://developer.cybersource.com/docs/barclays/en-us/bin-lookup/developer/all/rest/bin-lookup.html)

## Response Handling of Fields

the following image depicts what are the current response information that will allow to identify if the payment method is valid for the OCT Transaction
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/4b07e46e-76f6-4d06-9b12-090ae02b22d7/image-20230816-173258.png)
## Description of each Fields of the response
(this information is provided for each field individually in cybersource documenation = [https://developer.cybersource.com/docs/cybs/en-us/bin-lookup/developer/all/rest/bin-lookup/bin-lookup-api-fields.html](https://developer.cybersource.com/docs/cybs/en-us/bin-lookup/developer/all/rest/bin-lookup/bin-lookup-api-fields.html)

| **Field**<br> | **Obs.**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| moneyTransferFastFundsCrossBorder | Indicates whether cross-border money transfer OCTs (fast push funds) are allowed.<br>Possible values:<br>Y: Cross-border money transfer Fast Fund OCTs are enabled<br>N: Cross-border money transfer Fast Fund OCTs are not enabled |
| moneyTransferFastFundsDomestic | Indicates whether domestic money transfer OCTs (fast push funds) are allowed.<br>Possible values:<br>Y: Domestic money transfer Fast Fund OCTs are enabled<br>N: Domestic money transfer Fast Fund OCTs are not enabled |
| moneyTransferCrossBorder | Indicates whether cross-border money transfer OCTs (push funds) are allowed.<br>Possible values:<br>Y: Cross-border money transfer OCTs are enabled<br>N: Cross-border money transfer OCTs are not enabled |
| moneyTransferDomestic | Indicates whether domestic money transfer OCTs (push funds) are allowed.<br>Possible values:<br>Y: Domestic money transfer OCTs are enabled<br>N: Domestic money transfer OCTs are not enabled |
| nonMoneyTransferFastFundsCrossBorder | Indicates whether cross-border non-money transfer OCTs (fast push funds) are allowed.<br>Possible values:<br>Y: Cross-border non-money transfer Fast Fund OCTs are enabled<br>N: Cross-border non-money transfer Fast Fund OCTs are not enabled |
| nonMoneyTransferFastFundsDomestic | Indicates whether domestic non-money transfer OCTs (fast push funds) are allowed.<br>Possible values:<br>Y: Domestic non-money transfer Fast Fund OCTs are enabled<br>N: Domestic non-money transfer Fast Fund OCTs are not enabled |
| nonMoneyTransferCrossBorder | Indicates whether cross-border non-money transfer OCTs (push funds) are allowed.<br>Possible values:<br>Y: Cross-border non-money transfer OCTs are enabled<br>N: Cross-border non-money transfer OCTs are not enabled |
| nonMoneyTransferDomestic | Indicates whether domestic non-money transfer OCTs (push funds) are allowed.<br>Possible values:<br>Y: Domestic non-money transfer OCTs are enabled<br>N: Domestic non-money transfer OCTs are not enabled |
| domesticParticipant | Domestic indicator for pull funds (AFT).<br>Supported only in the US for domestic transactions involving the Push Payments Gateway Service.<br>Possible values:<br>true<br>false |
| crossBorderParticipant | Cross-border indicator for pull funds (AFT).<br>Supported only in the US for domestic transactions involving the Push Payments Gateway Service.<br>Possible values:<br>true<br>false |
| domesticParticipant | Domestic indicator for pull funds (AFT).<br>Supported only in the US for domestic transactions involving the Push Payments Gateway Service.<br>Possible values:<br>true<br>false |
| crossBorderParticipant | Cross-border indicator for pull funds (AFT).<br>Supported only in the US for domestic transactions involving the Push Payments Gateway Service.<br>Possible values:<br>true<br>false |

## Handling and Storing the BIN for future reference
The information provided by this feature needs to be handled to allow to provide information for the payment validation desition making
When the Bin Lookup is Completed you will see the following elements in the response

```json
   "submitTimeUtc": "2023-06-15T10:08:33Z",
    "status": "COMPLETED",
```

### The Following values in the “payoutInformation” object of the response, will indicate that the BIN is “Good” for Cross Border
### Example 1

```json
    "payoutInformation": {
        "pushFunds": {
            "domesticParticipant": "true",
            "crossBorderParticipant": "true"
```

where `"crossBorderParticipant": "true"` is returned
### Example 2

```json
    "payoutInformation": {
        "pushFunds": {
            "moneyTransferFastFundsCrossBorder": "N",
            "moneyTransferFastFundsDomestic": "N",
            "payoutInformation": {
        "pushFunds": {
            "moneyTransferFastFundsCrossBorder": "N",
            "moneyTransferFastFundsDomestic": "N",
            "moneyTransferCrossBorder": "Y",
            "moneyTransferDomestic": "Y",
            "moneyTransferDomestic": "Y",
```

where `"moneyTransferCrossBorder": "Y",` is returned
### The folowing values in the “issuerInformation” object of the response, will provide the information of the Issuer

```json
  "issuerInformation": {
        "name": "CREDOMATIC DE HONDURAS S.A.",
        "country": "HN",
        "binLength": "6",
        "accountPrefix": "55426567"
    },
```

**When the response returns the payout information object, with the values mentioned above,**
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)
there will be an extra step in the validation of the payment method, a “micro-transaction” will be executed: see Execute a Micro Transaction to Add Logic to the Payment Method Validation
](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2269)*   [HWAY-2269](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2269) - Execute a Micro Transaction to Add Logic to the Payment Method Validation Blocked
*   If the result of the micro transaction is ACCEPTED, then the BIN is overal “**Good for Crosborder Payout**”
*   If the result of the micro transaccion is DECLINED, then the bin is overal “**Bad for Crossborder Payout**”
**When the response returns the payout information object,with different values than the ones mentioned above,**
*   The bin is overal “**Bad for Crossborder Payout**”
### Data to store in the Platform
Once the logic above was defined, the elements to be stored in the Platform are also extracted from the response:

```json
    "issuerInformation": {
        "name": "CREDOMATIC DE HONDURAS S.A.",
        "country": "HN",
        "binLength": "6",
        "accountPrefix": "55426567"
    },
```

Below are suggested field names to be stored and the value that needs to be obtained
**IssuerBankname** \= [IssuerInformation.name](http://IssuerInformation.name) , this will be a string field (this field could be empty)
**IssuerBankCountry** \= [issuerInformation.country](http://issuerInformation.country). this will be a two characters field, corresponding to the two letter ISO code of a country
**IssuerBankBinPrefix** = issuerInformation.accountPrefix, this can be a string of a variable lenght depending on issuerInformation,binLenght
**isBinGoodForCrossBorder** \= True / False = the restul of the logic above mentioned = Good / Bad for Cross Border Payout
**dateOfBinLookup** \= the date the bin lookup was executed `"submitTimeUtc": "2023-06-15T10:08:33Z",` in the response.

The request begin to this information:
[https://developer.cybersource.com/api-reference-assets/index.html#payouts\_payouts\_process-a-payout](https://developer.cybersource.com/api-reference-assets/index.html#payouts_payouts_process-a-payout)

| { |  |  |
| ---| ---| --- |
|   "clientReferenceInformation" : { |  |  |
|     "code" : "1234567890" | <50 | Merchant-generated order reference or tracking number. It is recommended that you send a unique value for each transaction so that you can perform meaningful searches for the transaction. |
|   }, |  |  |
|  "pointOfSaleInformation" : { |  |  |
|     "cardPresent" : "false", |  |  |
|     "catLevel" : "6", |  |  |
|     "terminalCapability" : "4" |  |  |
|   }, |  |  |
|   "orderInformation" : { |  |  |
|     "billTo" : { |  |  |
|       "country" : "US", |  |  |
|       "firstName" : "RTS", |  |  |
|       "lastName" : "VDP", |  |  |
|       "address1" : "901 Metro Center Blvd", |  |  |
|       "postalCode" : "40500", |  |  |
|       "locality" : "Foster City", |  |  |
|       "administrativeArea" : "CA", |  | State or province of the billing address. Use the State, Province, and Territory Codes for the United States and Canada.<br>For Payouts: This field may be sent only for FDC Compass |
|       "email" : "[test@cybs.com](mailto:test@cybs.com)" |  |  |
|     }, |  |  |
|     "amountDetails" : { |  |  |
|       "totalAmount" : 98, | <=19 | Grand total for the order. This value cannot be negative. You can include a decimal point (.), but no other special characters. CyberSource truncates the amount to the correct number of decimal places. |
|       "currency" : "USD" | <=3 | Currency used for the order. Use the three-character ISO |
|     } |  |  |
|   }, |  |  |
|   "paymentInformation" : { |  |  |
|     "card" : { |  |  |
|       "expirationYear" : "2031", |  | Four-digit year in which the payment card expires.<br>Format: YYYY. |
|       "number" : "4111111111111111", | <=20 | The customer’s payment card number, also known as the Primary Account Number (PAN). You can also use this field for encoded account numbers. |
|       "expirationMonth" : "12" |  | Two-digit month in which the payment card expires.<br>Format: MM.<br>Valid values: 01 through 12. Leading 0 is required. |
|     } |  |  |
|   } |  |  |
| } |  |  |

# SACBE Implementation for Cybersource

## SACBE Implementation
This is the story for the current Cybersource Integration [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1478)[SAC-1478](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1478) - Cybersource Integration CLOSED
### Merchant “Real” Account Information
The information to create with the credentials for the merchant id created and that are the real ones to be used later on with the Production environment are in the following page:
[Merchant ID Account Information](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/349503491/Merchant+ID+Account+Information)
This is the story to update the configuration from testing to real account: Configure real merchant id in Cybersource [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1575)[SAC-1575](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1575) - Configure real merchant id in Cybersource Cancelled
When moving to PRODUCTION ENVIRONMENT the urls will change as follows:
[api.cybersource.com](http://api.cybersource.com/)
### VGS Outbound Route for Cybersource
To complete the integration, In order to be able to send the information to Cybersource, the VGS proxy needs to be setted up with an outbound route to Cybersource, to be able to complly with the CPI regulations and always handle the Tokenized information for the Card.
This is the task to configure the VGS Outbound Route: [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1574)[SAC-1574](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1574) - Configure Specific Outbound Route to reach Cybersource through VGS CLOSED
### Testing Account Information:
The information to connect with the credentials of the account created by Cybersource to use their APIs in testing mode are detailed in the following Page:
Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24197](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24197))

# Cybersource SACBE Authentication Information

Cybersource grants authentication through the configuration of a Merchant ID, and then each mercchang needs to access the Dashboard and create the keys for accessing through API (API Keys)
**Dashboard for Sandbox**
[https://ubctest.cybersource.com/ebc2/app/Home](https://ubctest.cybersource.com/ebc2/app/Home)
**Dashboard for PROD**
[https://ubc.cybersource.com/ebc2/app/Home](https://ubc.cybersource.com/ebc2/app/Home)
Once the API Key credentials are created in any of this environments, the execution of the Payout API needs to be updated to reflect the credentials for Each Implementation

# Leap Financial Integration for Cybersource

## General Information
Cybersource is as December 2022 the provider for OCT Transactions throghout the Platform
It is currently used in the following implementations
*   Hway
*   hway.send

# VGS proxy for Cybersource

La configuración de VGS se hace de la manera tradicional, agregando que campos se deben desvelar mediante la configuración de filtros.

Una vez configurado los filtros de manera tradicional hay que agregar un filtro de especial que incluya la ejecución de scripts donde se hará el firmado del request, se debe hacer de esta manera ya que la información del request se encontrara revelada a partir de esta se debe calcular la firma.

> El procesado de este script es case sensitive en el nombre de las llaves dispuestas en el HEADER deben estar tal cual como se llamen en este SCRIPT  
>   
> Tambien es necesario que todos los HEADERS pre existan desde donde se este haciendo el llamado ya que el script no es capaz de crear nuevos Headers, solo es campaz de modificar el valor a los que ya existen

Este código es el que se encarga de firmar:

```auto
                  load("@stdlib//base64", base64="base64")
                  load("@stdlib//codecs", codecs="codecs")
                  load("@stdlib//binascii", binascii="binascii")
                  load("@stdlib//json", json="json")
                  load("@vendor//Crypto/Hash/SHA256", SHA256="SHA256")
                  load("@vendor//Crypto/Hash/HMAC", HMAC="HMAC")

                  def process(input_http, ctx):
                      headers = dict(input_http.headers)
                      new_headers = {}
                      for k in input_http.headers:
                          new_headers[k] = input_http.headers[k]
                      keyId = headers.get("X-Key-Id")
                      secret = headers.get("X-Secret-Key")
                      host = headers.get("Host")
                      endPoint = input_http.path
                      merchantId = headers.get("V-C-MERCHANT-ID")
                      date = headers.get("Date")
                      body = input_http.body
                      body_utf8 = bytes(body, encoding="utf-8")
                      digestStr = "SHA-256=" + base64.b64encode(SHA256.new(body_utf8).digest()).decode("utf-8")
                      new_headers["Digest"] = digestStr
                      sigParamHeadersStr = "\n".join(["host: " + host,"date: " + date,"(request-target): post " + endPoint,"digest: " + digestStr,"v-c-merchant-id: " + merchantId])
                      sig_value_utf = bytes(sigParamHeadersStr, encoding="utf-8")
                      secret = base64.b64decode(secret)
                      hash_value = HMAC.new(secret, sig_value_utf, digestmod=SHA256)
                      sigParamHmac = base64.b64encode(hash_value.digest()).decode("utf-8")
                      new_headers["Signature"] = str('keyid="'+ keyId+ '",algorithm="HmacSHA256",headers="host date (request-target) digest v-c-merchant-id",signature="'+ sigParamHmac+ '"')
                      
                      #new_headers.pop('X-Key-Id')   
                      #new_headers.pop('X-Secret-Key')   

                      input_http.headers = new_headers
                      return input_http
```

El código YAML completo del filtrado seria asi:

```auto
data:
  - attributes:
      created_at: '2022-08-26T16:44:57'
      destination_override_endpoint: '*'
      entries:
        - classifiers: {}
          config:
            condition: AND
            rules:
              - expression:
                  field: PathInfo
                  operator: matches
                  type: string
                  values:
                    - /pts/v2/payouts
              - expression:
                  field: ContentType
                  operator: equals
                  type: string
                  values:
                    - application/json
          id: 6ec2a1a3-fba4-4fd0-942c-3404e509fec7
          id_selector: null
          operation: ENRICH
          operations: null
          phase: REQUEST
          public_token_generator: UUID
          targets:
            - body
          token_manager: PERSISTENT
          transformer: JSON_PATH
          transformer_config:
            - $.paymentInformation.card.number
          transformer_config_map: null
        - classifiers: {}
          config:
            condition: AND
            rules:
              - expression:
                  field: PathInfo
                  operator: matches
                  type: string
                  values:
                    - /pts/v2/payouts
              - expression:
                  field: ContentType
                  operator: equals
                  type: string
                  values:
                    - application/json
          id: c84e8a2c-5ebc-4899-adba-e7c15c553614
          id_selector: null
          operation: ENRICH
          operations: null
          phase: REQUEST
          public_token_generator: UUID
          targets:
            - body
          token_manager: VOLATILE
          transformer: JSON_PATH
          transformer_config:
            - $.paymentInformation.card.securityCode
          transformer_config_map: null
        - classifiers: {}
          config:
            condition: OR
            rules:
              - expression:
                  field: PathInfo
                  operator: begins_with
                  type: string
                  values:
                    - /pts/v2
              - expression:
                  field: PathInfo
                  operator: begins_with
                  type: string
                  values:
                    - /reporting/v3
              - expression:
                  field: PathInfo
                  operator: begins_with
                  type: string
                  values:
                    - /tss/v2/transactions/
          id: 46b75d91-06a1-429c-a2a0-a866ce5ee41b
          id_selector: null
          operation: ENRICH
          operations:
            - name: github.com/verygoodsecurity/common/compute/larky/http/Process
              parameters:
                script: |
                  load("@stdlib//base64", base64="base64")
                  load("@stdlib//codecs", codecs="codecs")
                  load("@stdlib//binascii", binascii="binascii")
                  load("@stdlib//json", json="json")
                  load("@vendor//Crypto/Hash/SHA256", SHA256="SHA256")
                  load("@vendor//Crypto/Hash/HMAC", HMAC="HMAC")

                  def process(input_http, ctx):
                      headers = dict(input_http.headers)
                      new_headers = {}
                      for k in input_http.headers:
                          new_headers[k] = input_http.headers[k]
                      keyId = headers.get("X-Key-Id")
                      secret = headers.get("X-Secret-Key")
                      host = headers.get("Host")
                      endPoint = input_http.path
                      merchantId = headers.get("V-C-MERCHANT-ID")
                      date = headers.get("Date")
                      body = input_http.body
                      body_utf8 = bytes(body, encoding="utf-8")
                      digestStr = "SHA-256=" + base64.b64encode(SHA256.new(body_utf8).digest()).decode("utf-8")
                      new_headers["Digest"] = digestStr
                      sigParamHeadersStr = "\n".join(["host: " + host,"date: " + date,"(request-target): post " + endPoint,"digest: " + digestStr,"v-c-merchant-id: " + merchantId])
                      sig_value_utf = bytes(sigParamHeadersStr, encoding="utf-8")
                      secret = base64.b64decode(secret)
                      hash_value = HMAC.new(secret, sig_value_utf, digestmod=SHA256)
                      sigParamHmac = base64.b64encode(hash_value.digest()).decode("utf-8")
                      new_headers["Signature"] = str('keyid="'+ keyId+ '",algorithm="HmacSHA256",headers="host date (request-target) digest v-c-merchant-id",signature="'+ sigParamHmac+ '"')
                      
                      #new_headers.pop('X-Key-Id')   
                      #new_headers.pop('X-Secret-Key')   

                      input_http.headers = new_headers
                      return input_http
          phase: REQUEST
          public_token_generator: UUID
          targets:
            - body
          token_manager: PERSISTENT
          transformer: JSON_PATH
          transformer_config:
            - $.paymentInformation.card.securityCode
          transformer_config_map: null
          type: null
      host_endpoint: apitest.cybersource.com
      id: 54fea738-aee0-46e6-9671-bf1692a4630b
      ordinal: null
      port: 80
      protocol: http
      source_endpoint: '*'
      tags:
        name: cybersource-any-method
        source: RouteContainer
      updated_at: '2022-08-30T20:43:25'
    id: 54fea738-aee0-46e6-9671-bf1692a4630b
    type: rule_chain
version: 1
```

# DOSH

## General Details
DOSH is a provider that provides the retailer advertisement of Rewards and benefits that may apply for transactions executed with specific details (e.g. paid with specific debit card in a specific store)
The notification to Dosh occurs by calling their notify transaction endpoint, with the details of the Puchase Transaction, obtained from the Event that Galileo Sent
At Dosh systems, the transaction is analyzed to get the rewards, if applicable, depending on the details of the operation such as: in what store the purchase was made, the amount, etc
If Dosh identifies that the transaction is subject of rewards, will notifify back the reward to the Platform through a callback.
Dosh provides an status for each reward that is notified to the Platform.
When the Reward is notified in a Payout Confirmed Status, the Platform will add the reward as a pending operation for the Rewards Account, and the amount of the rewards will be added to the pending rewards total.
Per Dosh Contract, the Rewards will be settled to Leap (in an specific report) at the first day of the next month they where set to payout confirmed. At this moment the Platform will complete the operation and will add the amount of the rewards as available to spend
The Actual payment of the rewards into Leap financials account happens on the second month the reward payout was confirmed by dosh. This means that Leap will cover the amount for the user for the first month, until the money is actually deposited by Dosh.
Because of this, the value of “when” the amount of the reward will be available to spend for the user needs to be configurable. For now its the first day of the next month. But it can change if in the actual flow this configuration is not adequate for Leap’s finance handling
## Technical Details
The following graphic depicts the overall flow of the Dosh integration
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/f6214853-f83f-4ffe-97e1-f9f2741dd676/image-20220425-162513.png)
## Integration Details
The integration is perfomed at two levels
1) Displaying the user the market place information where the user can review the stores where he can get the rewards / cash back by using its hway account to perform transactions
2) Exchanging Transaction information with Dosh at API . Sending the information of the transaction when the user does a purchase in a store (and thus, an Event from Galileo is triggered to our Platform) And receving the information of any applicable rewards of the transactions sent, trhough a callback , to be able to inform the user that their purchases had a reward
_Specifically at Platform level, the amount of the reward is handled as explained inthe general details sections_
### Mobile Layer Level
1. The Mobile layer level includes the display of the web view of the Market Place assigned by dosh (see section Dosh Hway Market Place)
    1. The following Link includes details on how the WebView is handled [https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/web-experience.html](https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/web-experience.html)

### Platform Level
1. The integration needs to be performed at Platform level allowing the events/messaging exchange.
2. The first part of the messaging exchange is the generation of a JWT to be exchanged with Dosh
    1. Review the following documentation by the provider to ensure the JWT is generated according to their guidelines [https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/api-reference-overview.html#dosh-jwt-authorization-token](https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/api-reference-overview.html#dosh-jwt-authorization-token)
3. Once the JWT generation is peformed, it will be userd in the the work to integrate with the APIs is required. In the following lik you will find the API Full Reference [https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/api-reference-overview.html](https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/api-reference-overview.html)
    1. The Specific Endpoint to Nofity Transactions is the following: [ttps://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/api-reference-notify-transaction.html](https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/api-reference-notify-transaction.html)
    2. The examples of how to test the notify transaction endpoint for SANDBOX is the following: [https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/api-reference-sandbox-notify-transactions.html](https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/api-reference-sandbox-notify-transactions.html)
    3. Finally when the transaction is reported, it is necessary to provide to dosh a “call back listener” endpoint, for dosh to be able to notify the rewards and its different statuses along their lifecycle. To provide de events, the reference for the rewards messages to be listened information is provided in the following link : [https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/api-reference-notify-reward.html](https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/api-reference-notify-reward.html)

## Dosh HWAY Market Place
[https://poweredby.dosh.com/partners/hway/](https://poweredby.dosh.com/partners/hway/)
## Documentation Drive

# Dosh Market Place for Leap Financial

The market place ulr is exclusive for each vendor.
The web experience is tied to the Leap Finanacial specific credentials, to provide specific offers.
Also the web exeperience contains a session jwt to identify the specific user
The generation of the web experience ulr is is achieved by executing the steps described by Dosh in the following page:
Note: please take in consideration the steps mentioned in the section = **Example: Web Experience**
[https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/user-experience-authentication.html](https://poweredby.dosh.com/doc/user-experience-authentication.html)
## Example of URL
Here is an example of the the full url structure to initialize you web experience:
[https://poweredby.dosh.com/partners/hway/setup/%](https://poweredby.dosh.com/partners/hway/setup/%)
[eyJqd3QiOiJleUpoYkdjaU9pSklVekkxTmlKOS5leUpwYzNNaU9pSm9kMkY1T21aa056a3labUl4TFdNeU5EZ3RORFJqTkMxaE5tUTJMV0l3WmpVd1lUWXhZV1ZrTVNJc0luTjFZaUk2San eWpBM056VmtNV015WW1FeVpEZzRNelZsWVRVME5UVTJOMkpsWWpFNVpqVmpJaXdpWlhod0lqb3hOelV4TURRM05UTXhMQ0pxZEdraU9pSmhZVGcxWW1KbU9TMDJPV000TFRRNFlqa3RPR0l4Tnkxa1lUVm1PREV6WW1JMllXRWlmUS5NWmlYaFAzWE9aUXViUHducUQyV2x0MWJzTGJyQ2g1M0h2TFA4VjRyTnljIiwiZGVidWdMb2dnaW5nRW5hYmxlZCI6dHJ1ZX0g](https://poweredby.dosh.com/partners/hway/setup/%)

## Elements of the URL
The previous url is formed by the following elements
**1 . The core/base urls** that can be used to initialize you web experience or take a look at the web experience checklist:
*   [https://poweredby.dosh.com/partners/hway/setup/](https://poweredby.dosh.com/partners/hway/setup/)
*   [https://poweredby.dosh.com/partners/hway/checklist/](https://poweredby.dosh.com/partners/hway/checklist/)

**2 . A valid JWT** that is encoded in a `jwt` wrapper.
The following Instructions were given by Dosh
For example -
Base JWT using your sandbox credentials:

```auto
eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJod2F5OmZkNzkyZmIxLWMyNDgtNDRjNC1hNmQ2LWIwZjUwYTYxYWVkMSIsInN1YiI6IjA3NzVkMWMyYmEyZDg4MzVlYTU0NTU2N2JlYjE5ZjVjIiwiZXhwIjoxNzUxMDQ3NTMxLCJqdGkiOiJhYTg1YmJmOS02OWM4LTQ4YjktOGIxNy1kYTVmODEzYmI2YWEifQ.MZiXhP3XOZQubPwnqD2Wlt1bsLbrCh53HvLP8V4rNyc
```

Decoded base JWT

```auto
{
"iss": "hway:fd792fb1-c248-44c4-a6d6-b0f50a61aed1",
"sub": "0775d1c2ba2d8835ea545567beb19f5c",
"exp": 1751047531,
"jti": "aa85bbf9-69c8-48b9-8b17-da5f813bb6aa"
}
```

Unencoded `jwt` wrapper + base JWT:

```auto
{"jwt":"eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJod2F5OmZkNzkyZmIxLWMyNDgtNDRjNC1hNmQ2LWIwZjUwYTYxYWVkMSIsInN1YiI6IjA3NzVkMWMyYmEyZDg4MzVlYTU0NTU2N2JlYjE5ZjVjIiwiZXhwIjoxNzUxMDQ3NTMxLCJqdGkiOiJhYTg1YmJmOS02OWM4LTQ4YjktOGIxNy1kYTVmODEzYmI2YWEifQ.MZiXhP3XOZQubPwnqD2Wlt1bsLbrCh53HvLP8V4rNyc","debugLoggingEnabled":true}
```

Base 64 encoded `jwt` wrapper + base JWT:

```auto

```

Full web experience request url with encoded `jwt` wrapper + base JWT:

```auto
https://poweredby.dosh.com/partners/hway/setup/

```

# Dosh API integration

The integration with DOSH APIs is necessary to
1. Notify the Transactions to be analyzed / matched with possible rewards
    1. **Example**
2. Receive the notifications of the “rewards” available based on the transaction sent in the previous steps and details, througout the lifecycle of the reward
    1. **Example**

```auto
  curl --location --request POST "https://qa.h-way.com/callbackinterface/DoshCallback" ^
  --header 'Content-Type: application/json' ^
  --data-raw '{
      "eventId": "3a07bfe3-9fe1-4074-8070-78c33248287a",
      "Event": "PAYOUT_CONFIRMED",
      "eventTimestamp": "2022-11-29T21:02:33.452Z",
      "userId": "86e87a4b-f280-48b1-90b1-ce7638eda613",
      "rewardId": "51cd3b23-923f-44c5-b8d5-08f28f02e7f8",
      "paymentNetworkId": "MASTERCARD",
      "transactionId": "265944",
      "transactionAmount": 4000,
      "transactionCurrency": "USD",
      "transactionTimestamp": "2022-11-28T21:01:05.415Z",
      "paymentMethodId": "14714e94-4c43-4129-beaf-2452e84c6f9f",
      "last4": "4750",
      "merchant": "DOSH LAUNDRY",
      "icon": "https://dosh-cash-res.cloudinary.com/image/upload/v1579886873/brand%20assets/DOSHCAFE/DEFAULT_BRANDING/logo/1579892831.png",
      "color": "#F5821F",
      "currency": "USD",
      "amount": 200,
      "notificationTimestamp": "2022-11-28T21:02:40.584Z",
      "pushNotificationTitle": "You just got $1 cash back!",
      "pushNotificationBody": "Congratulations!"
  }'
```

# Dt-One (former Transfer To)

Transfer to is an airtime operator that was originally (legacy) integrated with Numi Platfofm
It was noticed that the company changed its name to DTone when contacted in 2020 again for the integration with the SACBE Implementation
## API Documentation Site
[https://api.transferto.com/docs.html](https://api.transferto.com/docs.html) - Legacy API (implemented in SACBE)
[https://dvs-api-doc.dtone.com/#section/Overview/Sandbox](https://dvs-api-doc.dtone.com/#section/Overview/Sandbox) - NEW API (implemented in hway)

*   NumiTPE (iep)
*   OperationApi

The login is provided when the services are hired, and the token can be generated within the DTone Account Dashboard

# Legacy T-Shop Details

## Technical Details
The API that Dtone provides as a solution for the airtime/top up transactions is:
[https://airtime-api.dtone.com/cgi-bin/shop/topup](https://airtime-api.dtone.com/cgi-bin/shop/topup)
The Endpoint is the same for al Requests, the usage of one of it parameters: “action” is intended for identifying the type of operation the API needs to perform.
Review the specification below from page 16 for the list of available “actions” for each operation type.
The Specification for the API is not available online, it has to be requested specifically to Customer Support
### Developers Information Dashboard:
[https://tshop-app.dtone.com/cgi-bin/developer\_page](https://tshop-app.dtone.com/cgi-bin/developer_page)

### Platform Services in Use for this Integration
*   NumiTPE (iep)
*   OperationApi

## API Documentation
The following Document contains the complete Specification for this API as sent by the Customer Support team in **November 2020**

## Authentication Method
The method for authentication is by a set of credentials named login and a Token
The login is provided when the services are hired, and the token can be generated within the DTone Account Dashboard
## Credentials Test
To test the credentials the method to be used is a simply ping:
[https://airtime-api.dtone.com/cgi-bin/shop/topup?login=login&key=key&md5=md5&action=ping](https://airtime-api.dtone.com/cgi-bin/shop/topup?login=login&key=key&md5=md5&action=ping)
The explanation of each parameter required is explained in the reference document, as this:
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/ba4b6568-806c-49db-a8aa-65fba3973110/image-20201124-221152.png)![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/8c4944dd-d645-4569-a51f-93e335df7af1/image-20201124-221453.png)
IMPORTANT Please review the Authentication Details per each implementation

# Galileo

## [https://www.galileo-ft.com/](https://www.galileo-ft.com/)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/e2672703-fafd-4d06-a838-60bfecffd2b8/GalileoLogo.jpg)
## General Details:

Galileo is the Numi Baseline Feature Card and Banking Core Processor.
Although the Platform is ought to be prepared to basically receive the information from any Processor,  Galileo is the Processor that has been used in the past, so somehow the solution is "pre wired" to be connected with Galileo default,
The main goal of the integration with Galileo is the Handling of the Program (this is, ALL the User's Debit Card Related Features) and the corresponding relation with the oustide world (this is, the updates of the Balance, the Bank Account). It also provides some other features to allow the Cards in the program to have its Balance "Loaded" with two methods: 1) A Partnership with Green Dot, which is a Cash Network, to allow a Cash-In through in any store by giving Cash to the Cashier and loading the Amount to the Card Balance by using Card Swipe functionality and 2) Enables the card to use ACH channels (this is, in USA, the bank compensation network) to also provide Cash-In for the cards through a Bank Deposit.

Galileo provides a Sandbox "free" environment for General Testing of its APIs, but when the _Actual Program_ is started (e.g. SACBE Program, by signing a contract including payment with Galileo), there's a Connection and Program Configuration that needs to take place in order to actually receive a _customized_ environment for a Real Integration Testing, which Galileo Calls "Customer Verification" Environment, or CV for short.

## **Important Note: Since Galileo Changed their documentation site, some of the links in these pages might be outdated**
## Documentation :
The following links contain documentation important for the on going integration:
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#program-api-intro](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#program-api-intro)

# PROGRAM APIs
**Integration Web Service**
The Web Services software is built to interact with cardholder information. Whether doing a batch process of accounts via another client application, a real-time web request through the Galileo interface, or a terminal transaction through a closed network, clients can use Web Services to meet a wide variety of needs.
