## Events APIs
Events API (also known as Alerts API) is a collection of webhooks that clients
can use to get events from Galileo. For example, when a card authorization happens,
Galileo can send an event to the client's authorization event webhook with information
about the authorization.
### List of Events APIs available in Galileo:
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#api-reference-events-api-about-events-api](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#api-reference-events-api-about-events-api)
### For the detailed information of the Events in use in our platform please review: Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24617](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24617))

# Set PIN Feature

## General Details
Galileo provides a mean to allow the user of a Physical Card to set the PIN (ATM usage) through accessing a specific content that should be accessed through a URL formatted with a TOKEN, and needs to interact with the Mobile APP, providing different ways to set the parameters and the response back to Galileo
[https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/pin-set-procedures](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/pin-set-procedures)
## Technical Details
### Implementation Options

Galileo Present two ways of implementing the Set PIN Feature, one is by POST , and one is by DIRECT RENDER
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/direct-post-pin-set-procedure](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/direct-post-pin-set-procedure) - POST
[https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/direct-render-pin-set-procedure](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/direct-render-pin-set-procedure) -RENDER
The current way selected for all Numi implementations is the RENDER

### Generate URL
To be able to generate the URL to obtain the PIN web component (html form) that needs to be retrieved from galileo the Platform needs to make the call to the following API,
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post\_getcardpinchangekey](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post_getcardpinchangekey)
With the ussuall credentials data for the program, and providing the field accountNo with the number of the Galileo Account that the physical card is associated to accountNo=284101000347
> C:\\Program Files\\Git\\mingw64\\bin>curl --location --request POST "[https://api-sacbe.pd.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/getCardPinChangeKey%22](https://api-sacbe.pd.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/getCardPinChangeKey%22) --header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" --data-urlencode "apiLogin=arVc6n-0155" --data-urlencode "apiTransKey=29GCdrLe5CmFcGf" --data-urlencode "transactionId=testing" --data-urlencode "providerId=543" --data-urlencode "accountNo=284101000347"
This API retrieves amongst it fields the “Token” :<token>YQtJoyg1Raz7tBaylBZM0ZA1jTZJcjtccaWzWghCeBX2WQNHri</token>
> <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>  
> <response><status\_code>0</status\_code><status>Success</status><system\_timestamp>2020-09-09 15:16:59</system\_timestamp><response\_data><token>YQtJoyg1Raz7tBaylBZM0ZA1jTZJcjtccaWzWghCeBX2WQNHri</token></response\_data><processing\_time>0.166</processing\_time><echo><transaction\_id>testing</transaction\_id><provider\_transaction\_id/><provider\_timestamp/></echo></response>
With that information, plus the submitter ID provided by Galileo for this specific **Program**, plus the Token generated for the specific **account**, the url is created to call the iframe element
NOTE = these url might be different depending if the program is on **Premise** or on **Cloud** for Galileo’s servers
Example Client Validation
[https://secure-agserv.ua.gpsrv.com/agserv/direct/pin/en\_US/19-0001/50CharacterRandomTokenFromTheApiThatWillBeAppended](https://secure-agserv.ua.gpsrv.com/agserv/direct/pin/en_US/19-0001/50CharacterRandomTokenFromTheApiThatWillBeAppended)
Example Production
[https://secure-agserv.pd.gpsrv.com/agserv/direct/pin/en\_US/19-0001/50CharacterRandomTokenFromTheApiThatWillBeAppended](https://secure-agserv.pd.gpsrv.com/agserv/direct/pin/en_US/19-0001/50CharacterRandomTokenFromTheApiThatWillBeAppended)

### Generate Stylesheet for Render
For each implementation, the Stuyle of the Set PIN form presented by Galilleo can be customized to certain extent
Please review [https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/direct-render-pin-set-procedure#stylesheets](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/direct-render-pin-set-procedure#stylesheets) for details on the stylesheets
The documentation folder contains a baseline example of Galileo´s stylesheets under “html examples”
## Documentation
The following folder contains all the documentation related to this feature:

# Transaction History

In order to be able to retrieve the information of **all** the transactions There are a couple of methods in Galileo to retrieve the transaction history
With a date range:
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post\_gettranshistory](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post_gettranshistory)
All the Transaction history (actually this APIs brings the last 3 years of Transactions)
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post\_getalltranshistory](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post_getalltranshistory)

### Transaction History Implementation for SACBE August 2020
The Transaction history for SACBE[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1623](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1623) needs to be displayed as part of the User Experience related to the User´s Account information
The user should be able to review the history of all Transactions from the last 90 days
The Mobile Appilication will display the basic data for the last 90 days of transactions
The Platform should be able to provide a service with the information from the getTransHistory API from Galileo
### **The list of fields and its corresponding content as follows:**
*   **In the Title section:**
    *   Available Balance: The Balance of the Wallet, indicating also the corresponding currency
*   **In each entry of the list:**
    *   The name of the transaction: is the <details> field in the API
    *   The date below the name of the Transaction: the <post\_tx> field in the API
    *   The amount of the Transaction: the <ant> field in the API
    *   The calculated balance below the amount of the transaction: the <calculated\_balance> field in the API
*   In the detail of each Transaction
    *   The name of the transaction: is the <details> field in the API
    *   The name of the Merchant: is the <<formatted\_merchant\_desc>> field in the API
    *   The Transaction ID is the <external\_trans\_id> field in the API
    *   The Category is the <type\_description> field in the API

### **In the following image a mapping between the response of the GetTranHistory API and the Screen information is provided**
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/ad0369ef-d15f-4b4d-9b21-bc3bfe04c6da/image-20200820-012026.png)

# Events API Callbacks

NOTE= THIS PAGE IS OBSOLETE
## General Details
This APIs are aimed to interact with the GALILEO services to listen all that involves the cards in the program outside SACBE's environment
The following is a technical description of the APis and its intended for a technical audience.
**Please review and update the information below @Alvaro**
# Get Authorization code

The security of the API needs one extra parameter in each request; this parameter is an additional header called LFAuth, this header is obtained by performing a request to the next link.
This request is POST
[https://qa.sacbepayments.com/stsbc/auth/apikey](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/stsbc/auth/apikey)
**Galileo Api Key**:  [c481e824-5c06-4c61-80ca-ea12b5b75df8](http://dev.sacbepayments.com:8080/studio/index.html#databases/edit?&id=c481e824-5c06-4c61-80ca-ea12b5b75df8&database=AuthenticationPart0&list=ApiKeys&item=0)

It's a body for this request

{
"ApiKey":"\[Application Key\]"
}

When \[Application Key\] is a key assigned to authorize each request in the future.
The answer obtained is a value to set in LFAuth header

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/bf3a1e87-3230-4307-b1a7-8956614b2d3f/LEPwRML1Vlk32HRbVLtB4QkcOzW6vyMADzUA9Fwf9c0woH3bwx5NtuciHoIpY-G4iAyap7Dcv_y1vMMFQ2wCoyGelU7PSmyXNPJbNCf-tvxVYIFasbYEFKfvxIhH49OuFIXrylqH)

With this value you set  the Header
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/208cf41e-15c9-4c91-9973-ee071d030e4d/xSuDgcB24R4RDVL9BfMKXjfwWa7o7v-7jItUHEnahYuUGbrIA2eBsFTCBdqWaWCDqB4SUgXUn-OMcWY4rpW5MWM5yBMlctvk6VTsa2Iay-NkzgS7AXpCpjGjDLd22FPUo_3CyE38)

# Settlement transactions
 The information of the webHook from Galileo is in the following Page:
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#settlement-events-webhook](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#settlement-events-webhook)
in order to activate Galileo callback transactions, Galileo must send a Post order to this api, using the authorization number previously obtained.
Url: [https://qa.sacbepayments.com/callbackinterface/galileoEvents/Settlement](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/callbackinterface/galileoEvents/Settlement)
Header:
Content-Type: application/json
LFAuth (API Key Value):
eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..IfkKkQq1gFLzhGq04BjRAtqQOj68adhSqpUoAkrLDWE
Body:
{
  "type": "",
  "account\_id": "",
  "act\_type": "",
  "amount": "",
  "auth\_id": "",
  "balance": "",
  "card\_id": "",
  "currency": "",
  "local\_currency": "",
  "local\_currency\_amount": "",
  "mcc": "",
  "merch\_loc": "",
  "merch\_name": "",
  "merch\_num": "",
  "multi\_sequence\_count": "",
  "multi\_sequence\_number": "",
  "network": "",
  "original\_auth\_id": "",
  "otype": "",
  "prn": "",
  "prod\_id": "",
  "prog\_id": "",
  "settle\_currency": "",
  "settle\_currency\_amount": "",
  "timestamp": "",
  "tran\_id": "",
  "trans\_amount": ""
}

 **Example**: 

curl --location --request POST '[https://qa.sacbepayments.com/callbackinterface/galileoEvents/Settlement](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/callbackinterface/galileoEvents/Settlement)' \\
*   \-header 'LFAuth: eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9..L6sw5qJjxQgA3XA6wn3wpV\_vYNIjZXKKoChUsD6Rg9Q' \\
*   \-header 'Content-Type: application/json' \\
*   \-data-raw '{
  "type": "",
  "account\_id": "",
  "act\_type": "",
  "amount": "",
  "auth\_id": "",
  "balance": "",
  "card\_id": "",
  "currency": "",
  "local\_currency": "",
  "local\_currency\_amount": "",
  "mcc": "",
  "merch\_loc": "",
  "merch\_name": "",
  "merch\_num": "",
  "multi\_sequence\_count": "",
  "multi\_sequence\_number": "",
  "network": "",
  "original\_auth\_id": "",
  "otype": "",
  "prn": "",
  "prod\_id": "",
  "prog\_id": "",
  "settle\_currency": "",
  "settle\_currency\_amount": "",
  "timestamp": "",
  "tran\_id": "",
  "trans\_amount": ""
}'

# Authorization Events
 The information of the webHook from Galileo is in the following Page:
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#authorization\_event\_post](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#authorization_event_post)

# Payments and Adjustments

## General Details
See Galileo Docs
[https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/creating-a-payment](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/creating-a-payment)
[https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/about-adjustments](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/about-adjustments)

When performing Inbound transactions to the Platform Wallet or Outbound Transactions from the Platform Wallet, it is necessary to settle the Platform Wallet Balance with the Galileo Accocunt Balance
To achieve those processes, there are two main APIs to use
1. **createPayment** : for the Inbound Transactions
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post\_createpayment](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post_createpayment)
2\. **createAdjustments:** for the Outbound Transactions
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post\_createadjustment](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post_createadjustment)
The call for the APIs needs to be exercuted with the proper _“type configuration”_
## Type Configuration
To achieve the proper registration of the Inbound and Outbound flows (and hence the funds flow) in all the Settlement Related processes (both inside the Platform and Galileo, and outside, for example in the bank records) the proper configuration needs to be provided in the Payments and Adjustments.
The configuration is decided in the early stages of the project with Galileo and the Business/Processes team agreement and approval and it is registered in the Funds Flow discussion, alongisde other transaction types
**_Review each implementation information for the Type Configuration_**
In order to translate such configuration into its usage in the Platform, within the Payments and Adjustments API calls, you need to consider the following:
> **The Activity is defined by the type of API Call**  
> **\* createPayment = PM**  
> **\* createAdjustment = AD**  
> **The second part of the string (the “otype”) needs to be configured in the "Type" Field of each API Parameters**

For the **createPayment**, the field has the following detail:
**type\***string
Payment types are configurable per client. Contact your account representative to determine which types you have implemented.
Pattern: 2 character alphanumeric
Example: `"2"`
For the **createAdjustments** the field has the following detail:
**type\***string
F=Web service adjustment, additional custom configured types available.
Pattern: 2 character alphanumeric
Example: `"F"`

# Events API

This page contains specific information of how to handle the Events API and the different alternatives available to “Listen” te Events from Galileo
The following document was provided by Galileo as a side to the documentation available in their site.
IMPORTANT Specific attention is required to the detail of how the “Authentication” needs to be configured (Authentication section of the document) in order to be able to obtain the specific events of the Program and Provider ID, thru the use of **HMAC** credentials

### Event for Getting the Settled Transactions of the Card:
**Settlement Events Webhook**
Galileo `POST`s to `/Settlement` to send Settlement Events to the client.
A Settlement Event is triggered when a card financial transaction is
settled or force posted.
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#settlement-events-webhook](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#settlement-events-webhook)
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1497](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1497)
Clarifications sent from Galileo
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/abf688da-28fa-4f3a-9ec3-013160fc243b/image-20201106-135530.png)
## Transaction Events
### Transaction Events Webhook
Galileo `POST`s to `/Transaction` to send Transaction Events to the client.
A Transaction Event is triggered for all other financial transactions
that occur against an account, but don''t go through an association
(fee, payments, adjustments, etc.).
[https://sandbox.gpsrv.com/docs/events#category4transaction](https://sandbox.gpsrv.com/docs/events#category4transaction)
### Event for Getting a Payment or Payout
Receive a Payment Posted to a Card in the Program: **The Event you are looking for is the Transaction message with the alert name “pmt”.** The code is BPMT.
### Web Hook Detail
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#transaction-events-webhook](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#transaction-events-webhook)
### Payment Event Detail

[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#api-reference-events-api-bpmt-pmt](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#api-reference-events-api-bpmt-pmt)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/e04594d1-3dfa-40d6-8062-79a87e8006e4/image-20200723-225659.png)
Further documentation in Sandbox site:
[https://sandbox.gpsrv.com/docs/events#category4transaction](https://sandbox.gpsrv.com/docs/events#category4transaction)
Jira Task for SACBE: Update Galileo Events Listeners to make sure the Payments Transactions are Processed[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1922](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1922)

# Special Data Points to Handle in Galileo Events

Cash Back in Purchase Data Points

Incremental Clearing Data Points
The following needs to be analyzed in order to identfy the “Incremental Clearing” authorization and settle events.
The “Incremetnal Clearing” is given when a merchant sends one original authorization event, and then several settles divided for the total amount, for example, when the purchase is payed in installments
*   `original_multiclearing_auth_id` — The auth\_id of the original preauthorization that arrived over network rails
*   `bookkeeping_auth_id` — The auth\_id of the bookkeeping authorization record that is next in the series
*   `remaining_amount` — The amount of the preauthorization that has not been cleared yet

# Get Account Cards

this endpoint will retrieve the collection of cards associated to a spedific account
this endpoint should be invoked using the PRN
## Information of the Endpoint in Galileo
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post\_getaccountcards](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post_getaccountcards)
## Details on the Response
Below is a full example of a reponse from the endpoint
In the Accoutns array you will find all the accounts related to that PRN,

```auto
accounts": [
            {
                "active": "Y",
                "application_date": "2021-06-17",
                "bill_cycle_day": null,
                "galileo_account_number": "691",
                "group_id": null,
                "pmt_ref_no": "284101016921",
                "product_id": "2148",
                "start_date": "2021-06-18",
                "status": "N",
                "cards": [
```

from this section of the response the importan information is identifyint the `"product_id"` this will indicate the type of product the accounts belongs to (e.g. phusical, virtual) and the `"pmt_ref_no":` this will indicate unvoquely the account
Finally the `"cards": [` array will cointain the information of the cards that belong to each account,

```auto
"cards": [
                    {
                        "card_id": "1138",
                        "card_number": "555842XXXXXX2253",
                        "card_status": "Y",
                        "expiry_date": "",
                        "external_card_id": null,
                        "pin_fail_count": "0",
                        "pin_fail_date": null,
                        "freeze_info": {
                            "status": "Unfrozen",
                            "start_date": null,
                            "end_date": null
                        },
```

the information to notice here is the `"card_status"` that will allow to determine the status of the card and the `"card_number":` that will allow to identify univoquely the card with the last 4 when the information is non pci- sensitive
WARNING **warning this example was produced using sacbe implementation credentials**

# Get Card

This API is intentend to be used to retrieve the Card information, depending on the needs, the call to this API can be perfomed through the PCI compliance call using the VGS proxy configuration, or withouth it, wich will bring the PCI data, “masked”
See also: Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27617](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27617))
**Old Projects Info**
For **SACBE implementation** the Jira to apply this use is
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1810)[SAC-1810](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1810) - Provide the Card Details with the proper Galileo API Call CLOSED
For **Tzune Implementation**
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1313)[TZN-1313](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/TZN-1313) - CLONED FROM SACBE - Provide the Card Details with the proper Galileo API Call TO DO

## Details of this Endpiont
Galileo Documentation [https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_getcard](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_getcard)
### Example Request
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/83a55cdb-4ddd-413c-80d9-2c60d645fe3f/image-20231018-233432.png)

```auto
curl --location "https://api-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/getCard" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=XZ5JgK-0253" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=LUbgxR3XcQ6Tax6" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=7ff32158-0c87-4bc0-84d9-5b051c199313" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=381101090730"
```

### Important Fields to consider
[Vanesa V. Vilas](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/5cd5c60bf348990dbc3c2063?ref=confluence) to complete

### Status of the Card
According to the contents of the response field
`"status": "Y",`
the following table provides information of the description of such status

| **Status**<br> | **Description**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| A | Lost card waiting for sufficient funds |
| B | Blocked. May be an un-issued instant issue card that is used at a point of sale before the card has been sold, the card reached the maximum number of PIN retries, or the bounce-mail flag is set to Yes. |
| C | Canceled |
| D | Disabled. May indicate that a card has been reissued or replaced. |
| L | Lost Card. If reported lost with Modify Status type 3, a replacement card is issued with a new PAN. See the [Lost, Stolen, or Damaged Cards](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/lost-stolen-damaged-cards) guide for more information. |
| N | Active (normal). A card and its account must be in this status for a card transaction to be authorized. The exception is an advice or force post, which must be accepted regardless of account status. |
| O | Operations Hold. May indicate that the embosser has run out of plastic to emboss cards. |
| Q | Delinquent |
| R | Charged Off |
| S | Stolen Card. If reported stolen with Modify Status 4, a replacement card is issued with a new PAN. See the [Lost, Stolen, or Damaged Cards](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/lost-stolen-damaged-cards) guide for more information. |
| V | Voided |
| W | Waiting for Payment. The default status of a newly created card, or a replacement card that does not have sufficient funds to cover a replacement fee. |
| X | Set to Emboss. The card will be picked up by an automated process and sent to the embosser. |
| Y | Shipped/Ready to Activate. The card order has been sent to the embosser. |
| Z | Canceled without Refund. |

# Modify Status

This endpoint is intended for several uses when the status of the Account or the Card in Galileo needs to be changed either due to the workflow of the lifecycle of the account/card needs it or due to an external situation that needs to be adressed
## Considerations on the Status of The Card / Account in Galileo
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/about-cards](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/about-cards)

[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/about-cards#freezing-cards](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/about-cards#freezing-cards)
The specific date for freeze / un freeze account needs to be set up using the following fields
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/4aa40d52-293b-4939-98ae-61e7fc749cc3/image-20210908-223026.png)

[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post\_modifystatus](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post_modifystatus)

# Instant Issue Cards (Bulk Cards)

## General Details
Gallieo allosw to manage bulk cards by a specific process of calling different endpoints

See Galileo Docs for this feature

[

docs.galileo-ft.com

https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/setup-for-instant-issue

](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/setup-for-instant-issue)

**Legacy Implementation (SACBE - TZUNE)**

It is necesary to create a Location, and then create a Bulk Card Order indicating the Location
Then, once the Order is created, by using the getOrder endpiont the list of cards (with all its information, either PCI or non PCI -masked-) will be available
The List of Cards of the Order needs to be Stored to be able to later associate the cards with accounts.
For Mexico implementations it is necesary to create the Waybill for each prn of the list of cards in the Card Order, with the same address in each card, that will be the location address, to complete the emboss requirements,

## Technical Information
### Create Location
*   [https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_createlocation](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_createlocation)
    *   create location endpoint
        *   unique ID
        *   tied to Galileo System / internal id from program
        *   1 = program / 0 = Galileo
    *   Name/Address/Details
    *   Hierarchy: Parent-Branches
    *   modify location endpoint to change the details
### Request

```kotlin
curl --location --request POST "https://api-bancacao.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/createLocation" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--header "Cookie: TS015a1273=012fd6b1286a6d797c6b8823ec3c3dd73e0570b0d15a9e5d7bbd0189459525666a962a7b5567768e6e1999cd4fc8f130c07676d178" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=QB6xKS-0193" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=AZjJEf2jc497Wmc" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=600" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=9d661c62-accf-4a78-91fd-6d337f2fe776" ^
--data-urlencode "name=Oficina_Tzune" ^
--data-urlencode "address1=avenida 2" ^
--data-urlencode "address2=292" ^
--data-urlencode "city=Zapopan" ^
--data-urlencode "state=JA" ^
--data-urlencode "postalCode=45030" ^
--data-urlencode "countryCode=484" ^
--data-urlencode "parentLocation=5" ^
--data-urlencode "parentLocationType=2"
```

### Response

```json
{
    "status_code": 0,
    "status": "Success",
    "processing_time": 0.237,
    "response_data": {
        "location": "4"
    },
    "echo": {
        "provider_transaction_id": "",
        "provider_timestamp": null,
        "transaction_id": "68fbd984-bcea-4f18-9204-6ece799a542b"
    },
    "system_timestamp": "2021-10-20 15:44:22",
    "rtoken": "0b0a7bf8-fd32-478f-9a7c-002de0a436b2"
}
```

### Create Bulk Card Order
*   [https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_createbulkcardorder](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_createbulkcardorder)
    *   Create Bulk Card Order
        *   Number of Cards
        *   Location (sent to)
            *   Galileo/Custom location ID
*   Once the order is generated, it creates the number ordered of “emboss records” for the Embosser (e.g. 200 orders if the number of cards above is 200)
    *   In CST they will have all the 200 cards not associated to any account
    *   Blank User Name, etc
    *   They Batch the all together and get sent to the embosser
    *   The embosser sents them to the location
### Example Request

```kotlin
curl --location --request POST "https://api-bancacao.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/createBulkCardOrder" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--header "Cookie: TS015a1273=012fd6b12896c683aab2ea9a1ed2625f0de7f85bc1144c222f34811abba312bb796f5ef04e1443a2c01e4e3a35c3427fbc7aea86bd" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=QB6xKS-0193" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=AZjJEf2jc497Wmc" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=600" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=569f620a-1053-4568-9d41-3acc5cd6c73a" ^
--data-urlencode "prodId=2424" ^
--data-urlencode "numberOfCards=20" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToName=Tzune Offices" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToAddress=Avenida 2, 292" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToCity=Ciudad de Mexico" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToStateOrProvince=MX" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToPostalCode=03800" ^
--data-urlencode "location=4" ^
--data-urlencode "locationType=0"
```

### Example Response

```json
{
    "status_code": 0,
    "status": "Success",
    "processing_time": 0.183,
    "response_data": {
        "order_id": "8"
    },
    "echo": {
        "provider_transaction_id": "",
        "provider_timestamp": null,
        "transaction_id": "3ef13781-2d79-4def-ac8d-9851b91a3dbb"
    },
    "system_timestamp": "2021-10-20 16:14:58",
    "rtoken": "236747b5-42f1-4d16-a2e6-7d2767d42f57"
}
```

### Get Bulk Card Order
*   Returns the Specified Order
*   Returns a collection of Cards with PRNs
*   Each PRN can be referenced and associated to a User using CreateAccount endpoint
    *   The accountNumber field parameter needs to be filled with the PRN identified of the bulk card
*   It is necesary to determine the locationtype
*   [https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference#post\_getbulkcardorder](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference#post_getbulkcardorder)
### Example Request

```kotlin
curl --location --request POST "https://api-bancacao.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/getBulkCardOrder" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--header "Cookie: TS015a1273=012fd6b1286cff2652859e27aa9e0acadba0231e24c8480e72f705b2b95eb6353390ebb56cbb5918904c4189acd4098dea4977274a" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=QB6xKS-0193" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=AZjJEf2jc497Wmc" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=600" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=5ec12705-82e7-463a-9f5f-c9576c40cb1b" ^
--data-urlencode "orderId=8" ^
--data-urlencode "locationType=2" ^
--data-urlencode "returnAllCards=1"
```

### Please note
The following parameter **must be provided “as is”**
`"returnAllCards=1"`
### Example Response
### Example 1 = the order has not been processed yet
Note the following fields:
`"status": "N",` - The Status is NOT Processed
`"cards": null` The Array of Cards is Null (the cards are not yet created)

# Associate an Instant Issue Card with the existing primary Account

## General Details
The bulk card is created originally as a “stand alone” product.
When the order is generated, the cards are created and embossed but they are not associated to any “primary” account/product
In order to activate and allow the card holder to use this type of card, the bulk card needs to be associated to an account and this account needs to be “child” of the user’s primary account
To achieve such association and link the Galileo specific endpoints needs to be used in a specific manner

## Technical Details
The endpoint to be used is createAccount
note the set of the following parameters
`"accountNo=003101000549" ^` this must mandatory be the prn of an instant issue product. So in this case the prn of the bulk card to be activated/associated needs to be used.
`"primaryAccount=003101003485" ^` this must mandatory be the prn of the “main” product. So in this case it coule be either a prn of a Funding Account or a Virtual Account, depending on the implementation
`"prodId=26376" ^` this parameter **MUST MATCH the prodId** of the instant issue card that is being used to create the account. See Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-25417](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-25417))
See: How to Identify the ProdID of a Bulk Card
### Example of Request

```plain
curl --location --request POST "https://api-bancacao.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/createAccount" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--header "Cookie: TS015a1273=012fd6b12873d68fe8f572fce73bfecb3bec35b6ecc9b05305511b5b23c494ca0d789c3a391fe41f6995122e533f4c151fced0fe7d" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=QB6xKS-0193" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=AZjJEf2jc497Wmc" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=600" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=0d135e2e-7e00-4c53-b969-cc0ffb808bff" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=003101004202" ^
--data-urlencode "prodId=26376" ^
--data-urlencode "primaryAccount=003101003501" ^
--data-urlencode "sharedBalance=1"
```

## Example of Response

# How to Identify the ProdID of a Instant Issue Card

Because the creation of a bulk card does not involve the creation of its related product account, and for Galileo the account is the one element in its system used to identify fully either type of product (either a funding, savings account or a virtual, or physical card), when a bulk card is going to be associated to a User Profile, it needs to get the product id correponding account first
To create the Account for aBulk Card, it is necesary to use the createAccount endpoint, but, unlekiley the creation of any other main product account, for the bulk cards it is mandatory to indicate the parent Account
Because the createAccount endpoint also has as mandatory field, the ProductdID (this is, the product kind that which account is being created) it is necessary to previously have identified the product for the bulk card which the account is being created for.
Since the instant issue card is a card “on the loose” not really connected to anytihing in Galileo yet, the prod Id is only available from the Order the bulk belongs to
So to obtain the Prod ID with the only data of the Instant Issue PRN it is necessary to perform the following steps:
*   Identify the PRN from the Isntant Issue Card within the Orders already saved in the platform
*   Identify the order the prn was included into
*   Obtain the Product ID from the Order
*   Use the obtained prodID in the create Account endpoint

# 2026 Via Americas Details

# General Details
this page aims to depict the specific details for the Via Americas implementation for Bulk Cards

Via Americas will provide instant issue cards to their customers usgint their system. For this they need to be able to invoke the creation of the bulk card orders.

The cards will be associated inmediatly to the user / account by requirement of the CRB sponsor bank: the user will be required to download the app and perform their CIP process, and have the account / virtual card created (and hence the onboarding in CRB system) and after that he will be offered with the instant issue card

Also, they expect to be able to fund the cards they bive to customers, so they need to be able to have a mean to load the cards, it was agreed with Galileo that a specific Otype will be provided for this specific type of movement.

## Items still pending confirmation
1) What will be the prodId to be used -TBC
2) Which is the otype to be used to load the cards \- TBC
3) Address for the Via Americas Location and order shipping

Items to be integrated
1) Location Creation
the location will only be a main location for the time being
See below the curl for the location creation

# Authentication Credentials

## General Details
for each program that Galileo sets in place there´s a set of credentials that need to be used for authentication and foe identifying in each API Call the program and the type of types of product that is accessed
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#authentication](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#authentication)

| **Values of Authentication / Program / Product**<br> |
| --- |
| providerId |
| Product Physical ID |
| Product Virtual ID |
| apiLogin |
| api\_user\_ID |
| apiTransKey |
| Provider |
| Database |

## Accessing Galileo
there will be a set of URLs for accessing the APIs
For each implementation it will be provided:
*   URL FOR CV with its credentials
*   URL FOR PROD with its credentials
## Galileo Test Connection Example
The first to test the connectivity is to try the **ping** method
In the request body parameters the parameters apiLogin, apiTranskey and providerId need to be set with the values for the implementation. The TransactionId parameter needs to be set to any character string

```plain
curl --location --request POST 'https://api-sacbe.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/ping' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
--header 'Cookie: TS01385eab=012fd6b128b20dd723f7907eaf8d83c87c1b23bbe68c886ea8e72d21c0f04262bd60375a138f471ebdfff92ae100b71312033d5668; TS015a1273=012fd6b128804c468d23808629104e190a5f122f5f6c2140fadee6d9a7537b588c317fd98130879c092997338b3c9cf56fc94ad912' \
--data-urlencode 'apiLogin=bwVcZ8-0155' \
--data-urlencode 'apiTransKey=8LWA8RfXuRSGhAf' \
--data-urlencode 'providerId=543' \
--data-urlencode 'transactionId=9847dk-39fdk3fj3-4483483489'
```

# Operation / Transaction / Activity Types Codes

## General Details
The activity types are codes that are set up during the funds flows discussion to address any registration that needs to be done from Galileo to the program or from the Program to Galileo.
This information is aimed to be used during the conciliation and settlement processes to identify properly all the payments and adjustments
This information is also used in the Galileo Analytics tool
For each Implementation/Program there will be a set of the activity types
Some of them are default for defined flows, others need to be defined together with the team in charge of operation/conciliation processes to be able to set the information to be used properly outside the platform

## Technical Details
The activity types are used in the createAdjustment and createPayment Methods
See Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24597](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24597))
**Full list of Activity Types from Galileo**
[https://sandbox.gpsrv.com/docs/events#activitytype](https://sandbox.gpsrv.com/docs/events#activitytype)
**More Details on Activity Types from Galileo**
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/about-transactions#activity-type-and-transaction-type-in-the-galileo-system](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/about-transactions#activity-type-and-transaction-type-in-the-galileo-system)

# Simulate Authorization and Settle

_In_ order to be able to simulate in CV environment the puchases (Auth) and the corresponding settlement of those movements, Galleo provides two endpoints to execute.
Below is the updated Guide provided directly by Galileo
[https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/simulating-card-transactions](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/simulating-card-transactions)
## **Create Simulate Auth:**
Used to simulate an Authorization for a Galileo Card, ussually used to simulate a purchase
[https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_createsimulatedcardauth](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_createsimulatedcardauth)

### Parameter Definitions
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/aed75dd9-6b6a-442d-a1d5-6965d8fe8244/image-20220107-201703.png)

1. 

**NOTE**
You need to provide the SPECIFIC PARAMETERS for each program to execute this simulation
##   

## Examples
Example for Tzune Program
### Request

```plain
curl --location --request POST "https://api-bancacao.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/createSimulatedCardAuth" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--header "Cookie: TS015a1273=012fd6b128ae7a06a9eaf6fc1b4c2ed6d7d89cbcab7ec0b2897e04c65b36c936d01bd2c721f5cb68eb9757599608c3194e516a75ae" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=QB6xKS-0193" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=AZjJEf2jc497Wmc" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=600" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=f6d627eb-21d7-4615-8fb5-73089c018965" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=003101008708" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=testing_vanesa20012022_3" ^
--data-urlencode "locale=es_MX" ^
--data-urlencode "association=mc_auth" ^
--data-urlencode "authId=123456" ^
--data-urlencode "currencyCode=484" ^
--data-urlencode "amount=50" ^
--data-urlencode "merchantName=Jugueteria Tio Oscar"
```

### Response

```json
{
    "status_code": 0,
    "status": "Success",
    "processing_time": 0.859,
    "response_data": {
        "auth_response_code": "00",
        "auth_response_description": "Success",
        "auth_id": 80004
    },
    "echo": {
        "provider_transaction_id": "",
        "provider_timestamp": null,
        "transaction_id": "03eaa3df-011c-41b5-9a09-5661469b36ce"
```

Used to simulate a Settlement for a Galileo Card, ussually used to simulate the settlement of an _already existing authorization_
[https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_createsimulatedcardsettle](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_createsimulatedcardsettle)

## Response Codes

**a SUCCCES response does not mean the transaction has been executed normally (this is, the purchase and settle has been simulated correctly)**

# Updated Examples 2025

Example 2025

1. createSimulateCardAuth
to simulate a purchase
**Request**

```javascript
curl --location "https://api-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/createSimulatedCardAuth" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=XZ5JgK-0253" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=LUbgxR3XcQ6Tax6" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=4e0d7863-9195-41f6-b28d-e6034c081507" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=627101000081" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=simulated_purchase13012025" ^
--data-urlencode "locale=en_US" ^
--data-urlencode "association=mc_auth" ^
--data-urlencode "authId=121213" ^
--data-urlencode "currencyCode=840" ^
--data-urlencode "amount=5.22" ^
--data-urlencode "merchantName=Baby World Clothing"
```

**Reponse**

```json
{
    "echo": {
        "provider_timestamp": null,
        "provider_transaction_id": "",
        "transaction_id": "fcb8fcc2-f845-4b22-960f-587f9e4821ae"
    },
    "processing_time": 1.905,
    "response_data": {
        "auth_id": 1029755,
        "auth_response_code": "00",
        "auth_response_description": "Success"
    },
    "rtoken": "c82495ab-f106-4a95-8e05-cf0aa1e07e08",
    "status": "Success",
    "status_code": 0,
    "system_timestamp": "2025-01-14 08:17:11"
}
```

# Virtual card image

## General Details
In order to be able to display the proper card image and the information of the specific user (this is, the PCI format of the card´s PAN, the expiry date, etc) Galileo requests the images to be sent and configured in their environment first, and to be handled by the Platform in the way described in the following document
For **August 2020** the card images will be a TRANSPARENT PNG IMAGES that will allow the Platform to display the proper card art in the background.

## Technical Details
in order for the Virtual Card Image to work the url needs to be created to retrieve such image from Galileo services.
To create the URL the Platform must provide a security token related specifically with the card the data is going to be displayed and the identification of the config of each program
The security token is obtained by calling the createImgToken endopoint, see=
The config parameter is specific to each program
**example of URL** **_in CV_** **environment**
https:[//assets.ua.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=\[GENERATED TOKEN\]&config=](https://assets.ua.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=qXWUhQL8zwi3uJTykXuInGw7JbVoGf30bhPMNJLlc_nZekwvAQ&config=test)\[specific config\]
**example of URL** **_in PROD_** **environment**
[https://assets.pd.gpsrv.com/asset/image?](https://assets.pd.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=qXWUhQL8zwi3uJTykXuInGw7JbVoGf3)[token=\[GENERATED TOKEN\]&config=](https://assets.ua.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=qXWUhQL8zwi3uJTykXuInGw7JbVoGf30bhPMNJLlc_nZekwvAQ&config=test)\[specific config\]

**As Per August 2022 Leap Financial HWAY implementation was migrated to Cloud** so the URLS for this feature has changed:
**For CV**
[https://asset-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=](https://asset-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=%7B0%7D&config=%7B1%7D)[\[GENERATED TOKEN\]&config=](https://assets.ua.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=qXWUhQL8zwi3uJTykXuInGw7JbVoGf30bhPMNJLlc_nZekwvAQ&config=test)\[specific config\]
**For PROD**
[https://asset-leapfin.pd.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=](https://asset-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=%7B0%7D&config=%7B1%7D)[\[GENERATED TOKEN\]&config=](https://assets.ua.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=qXWUhQL8zwi3uJTykXuInGw7JbVoGf30bhPMNJLlc_nZekwvAQ&config=test)\[specific config\]
## Card Art
In order to display in the Mobile App, the proper card art as _the background_ of the transparent virtual card there are different images called Card Art that need to be applied for _each brand implementation_

In order to complete the configuration of the Virtual Image, the following needs to occur:
*   send to Galileo the Transparent Image
*   send to Galileo the image that displays how the card looks
*   sent to Galileo the font, if its not within the Galileo available fonts, for the virtual card (this process is called Customer Custom Font in Galileo)
*   get Galileo to confirm the configuration of the transparent image
*   get from Galileo the “config” configured to be used in the url that is created to display the virtual card image
**_Review in each implementation´s section within this three, the information regarding the configuratin id for the virtual image_**

## Documentation for Virtual Image Process by Galileo
[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1DKx2TRR7EWKiPquMpcO30aXgM0KBlE5O/view?usp=sharing](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1DKx2TRR7EWKiPquMpcO30aXgM0KBlE5O/view?usp=sharing)

# Practical Example on how to create the card image url

## Pasos para botener el card image url para PROD
**Nota:**
tener en cuenta que todos los ejemplos estan basados en la informacion de la implementacion de hway, para otros programas se cambian las url de las apis, los elementos de configuracion, las credenciales, etc.
Para ver la informacion de configuracion y authenticacino de cada programa ver en la correspondiente sección de éste confluence

1. **Ejecutar el CURL en COMMAND LINE de WINDOWS** en el servidor whitelisteado en prod para galileo
para hway el servidor whitelisteado en prod es “services”
**Donde =**
"accountNo=381101203937"
en este parametro se debe poner poner el PRN de la cuenta o el CAD (card ID) que se obtiene del CST de Galileo
PRN = cuando la cuenta tiene solo una tarjeta asociada al prn
CAD = cuando la cuenta tiene mas de una tarjeta asociada al mismo prn (ej, tiene tarjetas reportadas como extraviadas)
**Ej CURL con PRN para hway**

```auto
curl --location "https://api-leapfin.pd.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/getAccessToken" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=2EZqu3-0253" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=u8CjdMgGWMkJhHb" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=46f2c29f-f037-46d8-b906-6d004ef7db9c" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=381101203937"
--data-urlencode "type=0"
```

**Ej CURL con con CAD para hway**

```auto
curl --location "https://api-leapfin.pd.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/getAccessToken" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=2EZqu3-0253" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=u8CjdMgGWMkJhHb" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=86bdbfa9-cafc-445c-8db6-b9cf33cc851d" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=44274" ^
--data-urlencode "type=0"
```

**Ejemplo de Respuesta**

```auto
{"status_code":0,"status":"Success","processing_time":0.061,"response_data":{"token":"mpmGtnH5bHnm_yt2umPrn5JC5Qdvgd0ysBRSRikYfbAtwLb0Dh","expires":"2023-11-02 08:44:55"},"echo":{"provider_transaction_id":"","provider_timestamp":null,"transaction_id":"20d3c1b7-b27a-4c75-8390-56e8c9303c41"},"system_timestamp":"2023-11-02 08:39:55","rtoken":"6ed69985-d481-484c-a573-83da150bd2c5"}
```

**Donde =**
"token":"mpmGtnH5bHnm\_yt2umPrn5JC5Qdvgd0ysBRSRikYfbAtwLb0Dh"
es el dato que nos interesa
tener en cuenta que tiene tiempo de expiración = expires":"2023-11-02 08:44:55"
En la URL que se utilizará en el navegador
[https://asset-leapfin.pd.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=\[GENERATED](https://asset-leapfin.pd.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=%5BGENERATED) TOKEN\]&config=\[specific config\]
Reemplazar \[GENERATED TOKEN\] por el valor del parametro token
Reemplazar \[specific config\] con la configuración del programa correspondiente
**Ejemplo de link del programa hway**
[https://asset-leapfin.pd.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=mpmGtnH5bHnm\_yt2umPrn5JC5Qdvgd0ysBRSRikYfbAtwLb0Dh&config=leaptrans](https://asset-leapfin.pd.gpsrv.com/asset/image?token=mpmGtnH5bHnm_yt2umPrn5JC5Qdvgd0ysBRSRikYfbAtwLb0Dh&config=leaptrans)
**Este link deja de funcionar cuando se cumple el tiempo de expiracion**

# RDF File

## General Details
The main source of information from Galileo’s Account / Card and Transactions is a File extract that Galileo produces every day with all the daily “updates” of the inforamtion (e.g. external transactions, authorizations, changes, settlements)
This files are called RDF File and its available for the Program Managers to process and make sure the Information of Galileo and the Information from the Platform of the Program Managers is consistent.
Note: from our Platform Point of view, SACBE is one Program Manager, Bancacao/Tzune is another program manager, and so on. So for each Implementation in NUMI Platform corresoponds one program manager
There are different types of RDFs files depending on the usage the Program Managers:
*   postedtransactions
*   lookup
*   accountcard
*   authorizedtransactions
*   customermasterfile → **We use only this in this case**
The naming convention for each file is   'FileName + “\_” + DateOfTransfer + “.txt”'.
Note: DateOfTransfer format: yyyyMMdd
Los archivos estarán encriptados con PGP(Pretty Good Privacy).
Please read below for the information of the RDF Files for each implementation.
## Technical Information
### Galileo’s Documentation
[https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/about-the-raw-data-files-rdfs](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/about-the-raw-data-files-rdfs)

### RDF Transmission
The RDF Files are sent to the SFTP by Galileo
See Each Implementation for the Authentication Information of RDF SFTP
*   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-25077](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-25077))
## Documentation
The following folder contains the information for this feature

# SACBE Implementation Information for RDF

As per September 2020 the information of the SACBE Implementation is as follows:
The story that addresses the work is: Update Platform Information with RDF File Information [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1745)[SAC-1745](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1745) - Update Platform Information with RDF File Information TO DO
Se buscará cada cuenta encontrada en el archivo proporcionado por Galileo en nuestro OperationApi en las WalletViews por el campo BankAccountNumber. Posteriormente se comparará el CurrentBalance de la misma vista(WalletViews) contra el obtenido del archivo de Galileo(customermasterfile); de no ser iguales se reemplazaremos el valor en nuestro sistema con el leído desde del archivo.
Información de Cambios a Realizarse luego de Aclaraciones de Galileo
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1894)[SAC-1894](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1894) - Enhaces to Consolidation of Balance and Transactions from RDF File Backlog
1. El RDF File tiene la información del dia calendario anterior, no va a tner información del dia siguiente
2. se procesa y se entrega durante la mañana del dia siguiente
3. si hay transacciones posteriores del dia siguiente (de la madrugada) no estarán refleadas en el RDF
4. lo que hacen los clientes es validar que el balance del dia anterior matchea con el RDF,
5. no se debería intentar matchear el balance del dia siguiente con el del RDF, porque nuestro balance puede tener los datos mas actualizados
\--

# Physical Card

## General Details
When the user of a wallet request a Physical Card, there are certain elements that need to be updated to the the user’s account information, because the requested Card is added/associated to the user’s existing account.
Also, the physical PIN should be set thru the mechanism provided by Galileo (WebView), either in the moment the card is generated (request card flow) o in the moment the card is activated (activation flow), depending on the Implementation.
Depending on the type of card, the Embossing information and Shipping Information needs to be registered and updated either in Galileo’s records, in Embosser update files, or in both.
Finally, also depending on the implementation, it is needed, or not, to set the proper status for the card to be set to emboss
The following graphic depicts the overall flow within Numi Platform of the Physical Card Creation process with Galileo
[https://app.diagrams.net/#G13Dde392nuYCes2HHDcOXLGGS8WR9-xpE](https://app.diagrams.net/#G13Dde392nuYCes2HHDcOXLGGS8WR9-xpE)

## Technical Details
The method to create the Physical Card to be associated it to the users account is **_addAccount_**
[https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_addaccount](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_addaccount)

The response of the AddAccount Method retrieves the PRN of the new account created
> <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>  
> <response>  
>     <status\_code>0</status\_code>  
>     <status>Success</status>  
>     <system\_timestamp>2020-09-28 16:15:29</system\_timestamp>  
>     <response\_data>  
>         <pmt\_ref\_no>284101001253</pmt\_ref\_no>  
>         <product\_id>2148</product\_id>  
>         <galileo\_account\_number>141</galileo\_account\_number>  
>     </response\_data>  
>     <processing\_time>0.414</processing\_time>  
>     <rtoken>361a8a42-1818-4d38-8ccb-276f4128e5b9</rtoken>  
>     <echo>  
>         <transaction\_id>vanesaPhysical2809</transaction\_id>  
>         <provider\_transaction\_id/>  
>         <provider\_timestamp/>  
>     </echo>  
> </response>

The method to Update the Information for the Physical Card once created is the Following:
**Update Account**
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post\_updateaccount](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post_updateaccount)
In this field the information of the shipping address, is the most important one, because it was requested to the user in the process of the request of the physical card
### Fields to be Updated for the Delivery Address for Physical Card
> **shipToAddress1**string  
> Ship to address line 1.  
> Pattern: Maximum length -- 30 characters  
> Example: `"33 business parkway"`  
> **shipToAddress2**string  
> Ship to address line 2.  
> Pattern: Maximum length -- 30 characters  
> Example: `"Suite 400"`  
> **shipToCity**string  
> Ship to city.  
> Pattern: Maximum length -- 30 characters  
> Example: `"Salt Lake City"`  
> **shipToState**string  
> Ship to state.  
> Pattern: Valid 2 character state or province abbreviation.  
> Example: `"UT"`  
> **shipToPostalCode**string  
> Ship to postal code (zip code).  
> Pattern: Valid postal code  
> Example: `"841213333"`  
> **shipToCountryCode**string  
> 3 digit ISO numeric UN M49 country code; Example USA=840, Canada=124.  
> Pattern: Three digit country code  
> Example: `"840"`

Other fields may be updated in this endpoing depending on the implementation
The Process to update the PIN either within the Request Card Process or the Activation Process, is depicted in the following link Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24557](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24557))
The process to update the status of the card, when applicable, is depicted in the following link
Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24577](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24577))

# Details Address File for Embosser for Mexico Programs

## General Details
For the Mexico Programs for Galleo , it is necessary to update a n Address File to the embosser company (G+D) with all the Physical Cards created every day.
This is due to the inhability for Galileo To process a Postal Address for Mexico as of Today (December 2020)
This needs to be performed by a automatic process that appends to a file every time a card physical card is created in Galileo and set to Emboss.
The following story is the one that covers this requirement for SACBE Implementation: Create/Update Details File to Embosser for Mexico Program[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1732](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1732)
## Technical Details
**The information that the embosser needs is in the following detail:**
`BatchNumber|Reference|Peso|CodigoPostalOrigen|DireccionOrigen1|DireccionOrigen2|CiudadOrigen|MunicipioOrigen|EstadoOrigen|ColoniaOrigen|CodigoPostalOrigen2|TelefonoOrigen|CelularOrigen|ContactoOrigen|RazonSocialOrigen|DireccionDestino1|DireccionDestino2|CiudadDestino|MunicipioDestino|EstadoDestino|ColoniaDestino|CodigoPostalDestino|TelefonoDestino|CelularDestino|ContactoDestino|RazonSocialDestino`
The file to be created is a txt file to have an “append” for each card is created, each field of each new line needs to be a fixed size
**The folllowing contains an example of two lines of the file:**

```auto
003101002842	003101002842	                  0102110AV SAN PABLO XALPA            331                           CIUDAD DE MEXICO                                  AZCAPOTZALCO                                      CIUDAD DE MEXICO                                  SAN PABLO XALPA                                   021105599999999                              CLM Modulo 4                  CLM Modulo 4                                      Calle Av. Dos 296                                           Ciudad de México                                  Benito Juárez                                     Ciudad de México                                  San Pedro de los Pinos                            03800+5213310120267      +5213310120267                                                                                                       
003101002859	003101002859	                  0102110AV SAN PABLO XALPA            331                           CIUDAD DE MEXICO                                  AZCAPOTZALCO                                      CIUDAD DE MEXICO                                  SAN PABLO XALPA                                   021105599999999                              CLM Modulo 4                  CLM Modulo 4                                      Calle Av. Dos 296                                           Ciudad de México                                  Benito Juárez                                     Ciudad de México                                  San Pedro de los Pinos                            03800+5213310120267      +5213310120267                                                                                                       
```

### **The details of the requirement data are described roughly in the following documents:**
###   

### Fields Lenght and Content
###   

### First Examples

### Connection Requirements

# Fields Mapping
**The mapping of the fields to be retrieved from Galileo to be added to this file is the following:**

| **Field in Platform for Personalized Cards**<br> | **Field in Platform for Bulk Cards**<br> | **Field in File**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| AccountNumber | AccountNumber | BatchNumber (\*) |
| AccountNumber | AccountNumber | Reference (\*) |
| 01 | 01 | Peso (\*) |
| 02110 | 02110 | CodigoPostalOrigen (\*) |
| AV SAN PABLO XALPA | AV SAN PABLO XALPA | DireccionOrigen1 (\*) |
| 331 | 331 | DireccionOrigen2 |
| CIUDAD DE MEXICO | CIUDAD DE MEXICO | CiudadOrigen (\*) |
| AZCAPOTZALCO | AZCAPOTZALCO | MunicipioOrigen |
| CIUDAD DE MEXICO | CIUDAD DE MEXICO | EstadoOrigen |
| SAN PABLO XALPA | SAN PABLO XALPA | ColoniaOrigen (\*) |
| 02110 | 02110 | CodigoPostalOrigen2 (\*) |
| [UserView.Contact](http://UserView.Contact) | [UserView.Contact](http://UserView.Contact) | TelefonoOrigen (\*) |
| 5599999999 | 5599999999 | CelularOrigen |
| CLM Modulo 4 | CLM Modulo 4 | ContactoOrigen |
| CLM Modulo 4 | CLM Modulo 4 | RazonSocialOrigen (\*) |
| user.Address.Address1 |  | DireccionDestino1 (\*) |
| String.Empty |  | DireccionDestino2 (\*) |
| [user.Address.City](http://user.Address.City) |  | CiudadDestino |
| user.Address.Address3 |  | MunicipioDestino |
| user.Address.State |  | EstadoDestino(\*) |
| user.Address.Address2 |  | ColoniaDestino (\*) |
| user.Address.ZipCode |  | CodigoPostalDestino(\*) |
| user.UserId | Empty (spaces) | TelefonoDestino |
| user.UserId | Empty (spaces) | CelularDestino(\*) |
| user?.FirstName + ' ' + user?.LastName + ' ' + user?.LastName2 ?? "" | Empty (spaces) | ContactoDestino(\*) |
| user?.FirstName + ' ' + user?.LastName + ' ' + user?.LastName2 ?? "" | Empty (spaces) | RazonSocialDestino |

# Card Activation with NON-PCI Data

## General Details
In case the usage of non-pci data is required by the implementation the process to activate the card needs to be provided without the need to scan the card, and have the user enter the last four and the date of expiration instead.
The UI needs to provide the user a Form to input the data:
*   Last 4
*   Expiration Date
The Platform needs to retrieve the PRN of the User’s already Requested Card and compare the information with the one provided by the user (**_Note, for Bulk Cards this Step is not available)_**
The comparison that needs to take place is the last 4 entered by the user with the last 4 provided by either the the Get Card endpoint of the get Account Cards endpoint
Once the card is found univoquely in the system the Activation process can be set in place
See Page Activate a Card
## Technical Details and Scenarios
This is an example of how the data is retrieved by the get Card endpoint with no PCI data.
WARNING The Example below was executed with sacbe implementation credentials

```auto
?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<response>
    <status_code>0</status_code>
    <status>Success</status>
    <processing_time>0.109</processing_time>
    <response_data>
        <card_number>555842XXXXXX2253</card_number>
        <expiry_date></expiry_date>
        <card_security_code></card_security_code>
        <status>Y</status>
        <card_id>1138</card_id>
        <external_card_id></external_card_id>
        <pmt_ref_no>284101016921</pmt_ref_no>
        <first_name>Jose Christian</first_name>
        <middle_name></middle_name>
        <last_name>Carreon Alvarez</last_name>
        <encrypted_card_number></encrypted_card_number>
        <encrypted_expiry_date></encrypted_expiry_date>
        <embossed_cards>
            <embossed_card>
                <status>Y</status>
                <created_date>2021-08-24</created_date>
                <emboss_date>2021-08-24</emboss_date>
                <expiry_date></expiry_date>
                <shipping_type>standard</shipping_type>
            </embossed_card>
        </embossed_cards>
        <freeze_info>
            <status>Unfrozen</status>
            <start_date></start_date>
            <end_date></end_date>
        </freeze_info>
        <pin_fail_count>0</pin_fail_count>

    </response_data>
    <echo>
        <provider_transaction_id></provider_transaction_id>
        <provider_timestamp></provider_timestamp>
        <transaction_id>VANESA_TEST_0810</transaction_id>
    </echo>
    <system_timestamp>2021-10-08 07:56:25</system_timestamp>
    <rtoken>65e0f926-4d2e-498e-ab27-1d7e5c3ff083</rtoken>
</response>
```

In certain cases it is possible that using the PRN to call the Get Card provides more than one card (e.g. in the case the user has the card reissued from some reason)
This is the Response from the GetCard Endpoint when the PRN has more than one card
WARNING The Example below was executed with sacbe implementation credentials

```auto
<response>
    <status_code>101</status_code>
    <status>Multiple cards found when search by PRN</status>
    <processing_time>0.10512089729309082</processing_time>
    <response_data></response_data>
    <echo>
        <provider_transaction_id></provider_transaction_id>
        <provider_timestamp></provider_timestamp>
        <transaction_id>VANESA_TEST_0810</transaction_id>
    </echo>
    <system_timestamp>2021-10-08 07:52:15</system_timestamp>
    <rtoken>8698d8bd-415b-45a2-918d-2f702998de54</rtoken>
</response>
```

If this is the case the usage of getAccountCards is necessary, using the same PRN of the user’s requested card, to identify the specific card within all the cards that are related to the PRN
Please review the page Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24637](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24637))
In this scenario, the Status will be the mean to determine which card is the correct one that is being activated, the "Ready To Activate" Status is the one to aim for.
To Work with Status plese review Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24577](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-24577))

# Card Art / Marketing Kit

The card art / marketing kit for the embosser to apply, is defined in Galileo in the moment the Physical product is created

In Leap financial's programs, the physical card is created as a added product to a main account.

The endpoint that creates the physical card is AddAccount, adding the physical product to a main product.

In the moment the physical card is created, using the mentioned endpoint, the "shipping address" needs to be updated (the address where the card is going to be sent)

In order to to this, the UpdateAccount endpoint is used

In this moment is also when the marketing kit is defined, by a specific set of information set in a spedific field, that is common practice for galileo to use for this specific feature

The information to be set is the combination of two fields in the api call
id2 and idtype2

the field idtype2 will always be 14, this settles the type to "customer generated" to ensure the value is picked up later by the galileo process that gets this information into the embosser file

The field id2 will contain the information previously agreed with Galileo and the embosser, that will identify the marketing kit

For example for hway the following was the information for the two types of cards / marketing kits that will be used

Following an example request with the information to request the h.way regular purlple card

```plain
curl --location "https://api-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/updateAccount" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=XZ5JgK-0253" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=LUbgxR3XcQ6Tax6" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=ed5d4343-6955-46dc-8190-917fab859fa9" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=627101000081" ^
--data-urlencode "idType2=14" ^
--data-urlencode "id2=HWAYPURP" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToAddress1=any 123" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToAddress2=" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToCity=City" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToState=PP" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToPostalCode=123456" ^
--data-urlencode "shipToCountryCode=484"
```

# Accounts, Product Types and its usage

Galileo handles different concepts for Accounts and Products, and different Product Types
The Account is the User Enrollement information
Tha Account might have products, that can be of type bank account, virtual card, physical card
More information on the Accounts and Products From Galileo:
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/add-account-procedure](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/add-account-procedure)
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/about-accounts#create-account-vs-add-account](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/docs/about-accounts#create-account-vs-add-account)

**How to create an account and not use the card**
*   Galileo advices
    *   Create the physical product (create Account)
    *   It will hold the information of the CLABE and be the main PRN
    *   Don’t issue the actual card if the user does not want to (don´t set to emboss)
    *   Add the virtual product if needed (add Account)

**How it works for stolen/lost**
*   no need to create another card
*   modify status call with “lost or stolen”
*   add card call using an “instant issue card”
*   the prn remains the same

**Raw Concepts**
*   Configuración de tipo de producto = Holding Account con Galileo
*   Mapear cuenta de banco directamente con cuenta de Galileo
*   No se necesita crear la tarjeta,
*   "Concepto de separar la cuenta de la tarjeta"
*   Por ahora no se debe crear la cuenta de Galileo
*   Cada creación de Cuenta con Galileo nos cuesta
*   por ahora para ABC no intentaremos crear cuenta con Galileo hasta que sea necesario

# ACH

## General Information
The ACH Transactions allow to load or transfer out money from/to Galileo account from/to a specific Bank Account in USA, that were previously linked to the Galileo acount.
The functionality works as following:
*   The Bank account needs to be associated to the Galileo account via an specific API, providing the routing number and the accoutn number of the Bank Account.
*   Gallileo will associate the Bank Account and provide an internal “ach\_account\_id”
*   Once the “ach\_account\_id” exists, the transactions can be executed
*   The ACH Transaction “direction” will be determined by the “debit credit indicator” Parameter
*   The way the balance gets updated depends on the “direction”
*   The following is the basic detail of how it works:
    *   **D : Debit: Pull**
        *   Funds from the Bank Account into Galileo's account
        *   Our trigger to update our platform Balance is the BMPT AC Event
        *   It takes up to three days in our program
        *   When it fails: Fails that would be the ACHR Event, when an external account tries to take money off of Galileo and Galileo denies it.
    *   **C: Credit Push:**
        *   Funds from Galileo's account out to External Bank Account
        *   Galileo automatically updates the Balance in the Open to Buy
        *   Our platform needs to update the platform wallet balance with galileo's balance
        *   When it fails: Fails that would be ACHC Event, when there is a request to credit the Galileo account and that is denied by the external bank account.

*   Note: When an external ACH occurs, and it fails: For the FACH that is if we try to pull funds into Galileo, there is a return file and can't add the funds in.

### Events Considerations
*   For any Transactions that start somewhere else to deposit money into Galileo have MN, so for ACH Transactions **started outside** the Event will will be BPMT = PM MN
*   For ACH in transactions, **initiated inside thorugh the program API,** the Event is a is a BPMT = PM AC
*   For ACH out transactions, , **initiated inside thorugh the program API,** the Event is a BADJ = AD S
*   The expected transaction time (also called hold parameter) for an ACH out is 3 days, and there iS NO EVENT that confirms that the external bank received the funds , SIMPLY AFTER THE HOLD PARAMETER EXPIRES, GALILEO EXECUTES THE TRANSACTION and sends the events in the item above.
*   If there is a **fail** in any of these previous events, during the HOLD PERIOD, started from gallileo (AC or S), IT WILL BE AN EVENT with the **same detail (ach\_trans\_id)** and the **opposite value** (either negative or positive), to cancel it out
*   if the processing fails after Galileo Execution with the external Bank (after the hold days), we will got  the Events as follows
    *   ACHR, when an external account tries to take money off of Galileo and Galileo denies it.
    *   ACHC, when there is a request to credit the Galileo account and that is denied by the external bank account.
    *   FACH that is if we try to pull funds into Galileo, there is a return file and can't add the funds in.
## Technical Information
The first API is to create the account reference in Galileo: Add ACH Account
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post\_addachaccount](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post_addachaccount)
**Request Data Considerations**
The Request information is general to all the APIs, the specific parameters to keep in consideration are
This field will be the PRN of the account to associate the ACH account to:
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/ba5fc51b-1d26-44de-b89e-9a82f0bad676/image-20201214-205515.png)
These fields need to be completed with the information of the Bank Account to Be added
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/76434bb1-0da8-4f65-aa39-c6ac3e14e5cf/image-20201214-190742.png)
**Response Data Considerations:**
The reponse data retrieves an internal Account ID reference of the account added
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/6a52382d-5141-4424-90da-6b94865ef2a5/image-20201214-210739.png)
2\. The account id reference returned by the first API is needed to actually create the ach transaction API: Create ACH Transaction
[https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post\_createachtransaction](https://galileo-ft.readme.io/galileo-pro/reference#post_createachtransaction)
*   DebitCredit Indicator = this parameter indicates the “direction”
*   Since is the same api for pull and push transactions, this parameter is important to indicate wheter it’s an ACH OUT OR AN ACH IN
*   same day flag = it should be set to Y to allowe the ACH to kick the same day if possible

## Flow Diagram for ACH-IN / ACH-OUT scenarios

**Q/A with Richard Mortensen 2-2-20201**
1. When the ACH Transaction with Debit Credit Indicator D - Fails - we get a FACH event?
2. When the ACH Transaction with Debit Credit Indicarot C -Fails - what event to we get?-
3. For example 2, What is the time that can happen between the balance gets debited and then credited?
4. What is the time setted in our program for ACH credit transactions (Christian believed it was 3 days but never confirmed). When this time passes do we need to consider the tranaction as executed succesfully or need to wait for an event as well?
1 Yes that is the event that you would receive
2 The event you would receive is the ACHR event.
[3 So](http://3.So) the time you expect is about 3 business days is when you will see the account debited and then credited.
4 It is 3 days. I would wait for the event to come through to ensure that the funds have been put onto the card
**Clarification From Richard 2-4-2021**
So I got more clarity on this. For the Debit Credit Indicator D - Fails that would be the ACHR, when an external account tries to take money off of Galileo and Galileo denies it. For the Debit Credit Indicator C- Fails that would be ACHC, when there is a request to credit the Galileo account and that is denied by the external bank account. For the FACH that is if we try to pull funds into Galileo, there is a return file and can't add the funds in.
