# Round-Up to Savings Feature

## General Details
This feature allows the card holder to have a separated account to save the round-ups (the difference in cents) in their purchases.
This separated account can also be used as a savings account to transfer money into from the main account, or transfer out money to the main account or to external accounts (using ach bank transfer)
The savings account is a secondary product to the principal product of the card holder and hence needs to be associtated with it when it is created, through the “addAccount” feature
Also, the roundoup to savings is defined as an “account feature” of the principal product, so it needs to be enabled specifically by the user. When the user (at UI level) chooses to enable this feature, the “set Account Feature” functionality must be used to specifically set the roundup to savings feature for the cardholders account.
**Notes:**
1. .This feature needs to be specifically enabled by Galileo per Program, because Galileo creates a Product ID specifically for the “Savings” Product
2. As Per June 2022 this feature is implemented for leap financial / hway program. Only for one savings account

## Galileo Documentation
The following google drive Folder contains the main information provided by Galileo on this feature

The following Graphic Depicts the Architecture of the Accounts including the savings account for Leap Financial Program
**Please bare in mind the graphic contains an error, the add Account for the Guardadito must contain the parameter balance=0**

## Technical Details
### **1\. Create Savings Account**
When the user chooses to have a savings account the account will need to be created, related to the main account -and hence the account holder profile-
The Savings account must be created used the **addAccount** endpoint, indicating the following parameters that will be specific per each Program:
**accountNo** \= the number of the main account the Savings account will be related to
**prodId** \= the product corresponding to the savings product of the account being created
**sharedBalance**\= 0 / indicating that this account DOES NOT shares blance with the main account
For the corresponding **ProdID** please check the details of each implementation in the product hierarchy section:
**FOR LEAPFINANCIAL/HWAY** Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-25477](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-25477))

**Note:** the step below was deprecated by Galileo so it must not be used to enable or disable the roundup, see notes in step 3 instead
### **2.** **Enable/Disable roundup**
The Rondup is an “Account Feature” that needs to be setup at Main Account Level using the setAccountFeature Endpoint
The parameter that needs to be setup are=
**featureType = 11**
**featureValue = Y**
For more details on the endpoint you may review Galileo Docs: [https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_setaccountfeature](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_setaccountfeature)

### **3\. Set The Savings Account as the Roundup**
Once the savings account has been created and the roundup enabled for the main account, it is needed to set the destination for the roundup savings
For this there is an specific API called setRoundupAccount
This account needs to be a specific product for savings
For each implementation, the product will be set up for the program
The specific parameters for this call will be as follows
**accountNumber**\= primary PRN of the main account
**linkedAccounts=** PRN of the account created for savings
**expire = N as default,** and when the roundup needs to be **enabled / Y** when the reoundup needs to be **disabled**
If in any case the roundup account should be more than one, the linkedAccounts parameter can be steup as an array, setting up also the percentage to be rrounded for each account, the percentage must total 100
**accountNumber**\= primary PRN of the main account
**linkedAccounts**\= Json format setting “PRN of savings 1” : Percentage of savings1, “PRN of savings2” : Percentage of savings2 (and so on) Example:" { ' 123456789012' :
58, ' 123456789000' : 42}

To see the full documentation for this endpoint in Galileo Docs: [https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_setroundupaccounts](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_setroundupaccounts)

### **4\. Get The Savings Account set to Roundup**
Theres a method available to get the accounts that are cuirrently configured for roundup for a main account
the method is Get Roundup Accounts
The specific parameters for this call will be as follows
**accountNumber**\= primary PRN of the main account
To see the full documentation for this endpoint in Galileo Docs: [https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_getroundupaccounts](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_getroundupaccounts)

### **5\. How the roundup works**
When the savings account is setup and associated to the main account, and the roundup feature enabled in the main account, the roundup will start working every time the user spends money in a purchase with their cards, and there´s a cents differentce
When the roundup is applied, Galileo will trigger two events
1. an Adjustments for the main account deducting the cents, with the otype =ru
2. a Payment for the savings account, sending the cents with the otypee = RU
The following image depicts the specific flow (extracted from the roundup full process depicted in Galileo Documentation [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/1878982657/Round-Up+to+Savings+Feature#%5BinlineExtension%5DGalileo-Documentation](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/1878982657/Round-Up+to+Savings+Feature#%5BinlineExtension%5DGalileo-Documentation) section of this page)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/2394eb43-72c5-4ec9-a6cd-723cb5bf7eb4/image-20220701-140353.png)

# PCI Sensitive Data Access

The way Galileo allows the access the PCI Sensitive Data is through the access throug a PCI Compliance third party
The third party should be the one making the call to the getCard() method, with specific credentials that are set by Galileo for this specific purpose
Also, the credentials will only work witht the program that signed the specific “connection agreement” with VGS
The third party for Numi is VGS
VGS allows the configuration of the routes to access the Get Card Method and tokenize, de-tokenize the information to be sent for any third party that requires it (e.g. Cybersource / TabaPay)

For Current Sacbe Implementation the credentials
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/283ac393-7706-4995-ba2e-cb8303217a39/image-20201019-154043.png)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/6b77c6da-1f39-4cd7-bc7b-1dcc5bde056c/image-20201019-154100.png)

# Statements

## General Details
Galileo provides the monthly statements for the end users

The statement process is executed montly and it´s made available to the program

The implementation by NUMI platform is aimed to obtain the montly statement thorugh a url exposed by galileo, using the specific PRN of the user's account which statement is desired (e.g. it couyld be the prn of the main account, savings account etc). The statement is obtained in JSON format and formatted to PDF in the platform, with a spedific template according to the program.

The information of how to implement this feature from Galileo is in the following link
[https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/statements#json-fields](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/docs/statements#json-fields)

## Technical Details
The statement JSON is exposed by Galileo through their "asset" feature. The assets are urls that deliver certain objects (either a json or other such as images)
The url for the statements is similar to the url of any "asset" based information to be retrieved from Galileo.
It cointains:
*   a base url specific for the program,

Example = [https://asset-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/asset/](https://asset-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/asset/statement/account)

*   a subdomain specific for the feature (in this case the statement)

Example = [statement/account](https://asset-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/asset/statement/account)

*   a config parameter also specific for the program,

Example = `&config=leapfins`

*   a token that needs to be generated thorugh an API call with the specific PRN that will be the source of the data

Example = `token=gq8FTlv8lJTw7HvHH9w_gwU9eIESrCOzCYzNFDEqwM_B0ZGvB8`

*   a set of parameters spedific for the feature
    *   date of the statement: MMDD
    *   statement output = json
    *   type of stateme = a one letter code = D
    *   Example:
    `&date=0824&statementType=D&output=json`

As per August 2024 **h.way** has implemented statement for Main Account and Savings Account

**Example of URL for h.way program in CV environment**
[https://asset-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/asset/statement/account?token=gq8FTlv8lJTw7HvHH9w\_gwU9eIESrCOzCYzNFDEqwM\_B0ZGvB8&config=leapfinst&date=0824&statementType=D&output=json](https://asset-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/asset/statement/account?token=gq8FTlv8lJTw7HvHH9w_gwU9eIESrCOzCYzNFDEqwM_B0ZGvB8&config=leapfinst&date=0824&statementType=D&output=json)

### How to Test Statements information
1) The token needs to be generated with the provided API = getAccessToken using the PRN of the accoutn to get the statemen from in the accountNo field
Example CURL

```plain
curl --location "https://api-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/getAccessToken" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=XZ5JgK-0253" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=LUbgxR3XcQ6Tax6" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=d86aaa08-05ef-4067-b0d1-1b775c463db1" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=627101000412" ^
--data-urlencode "type=1"
```

2) Once the token is generated, use the following url in a browser, replacing the date you want the statement from, and the token generated (bear in mind the token has a life span of 5 minutes)

[https://asset-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/asset/statement/account?token=](https://asset-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/asset/statement/account?token=){REPLACE WITH GENERATED TOKEN}&config=leapfinst&date={MMYY}&statementType=D&output=json
3) You should be getting a json in the browser
See screenshot below of how it looks
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/933ec29a-7c57-4d0c-8974-3a04ac1b2f8c/image.png)

# Old (Monies) Information

Galileo provides a free Sandbox for base testing and the tables below contain _the historic information used_ to access this environment and the first configuration for Monies
**not for testing the actual configuration of the Implementations.**

## Overview

*   **Galileo** is the new processing partner, that will provide Numi with all the functionality needed to create, manage, operate and transact with Numi prepaid debit cards.
*   All communication is done via a secure channel (SSL), with specific client addresses permitted (whitelisting), and with HTTP-header based authentication.
*   Methods are standard POST HTTP actions, with the request payload sent in the body, as **x-www-form-urlencoded** ( ☛ [see example](https://stackoverflow.com/a/4073451/2146521)).
*   Response data is received as a JSON payload, as long as the custom request header is set:

> response-content-type: json
## Connecting to test environment

*   Galileo provides what they refer to as “_staging_”or _“CV” (customer validation)_ environment, which closely matches the production site, excluding some functionality which is not available, and that will have to be (carefully) tested on production (e.g. ACH).
*   Dev. team can use this environment to implement the prepaid card use cases and perform integration testing.
*   Base url for test environment is the following. _This must be made configurable, and it will be environment-dependent_.
    *   [https://api-monies.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/](https://api-monies.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/)
*   Test environment is whitelisted to a specific IP address, therefore, all connections to such API endpoints must be done via an HTTP/S proxy.
*   _This must be made configurable, and it will be environment-dependent_.
    *   Proxy host: **169.46.50.35:83**
    *   Username: **\*\*\* request to manager \*\*\***
    *   Password: **\*\*\* request to manager \*\*\***
*   All API calls, unless noted otherwise, must include the following fixed request parameters, which represent the credentials associated to the Numi program.
*   _This must be made configurable, and they will be environment-dependent_.

**This values goes in the request body**

| Header | Value |
| ---| --- |
| apiLogin | **cUWBw8-0087** |
| apiTransKey | **xW54B4lqY7NaLqM** |
| providerId | **458** |

## SACBE Sandbox configuration

| Header | Value |
| ---| --- |
| URL | [https://sandbox-api.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/](https://sandbox-api.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/) |
| apiLogin | **7KqKxh-9999** |
| apiTransKey | **l4GKAWQrBD** |
| providerId | **911** |
| Test Product ID | **5094** |
| Test Program ID | **511** |

*   Each and every API call must include a request parameter, uniquely representing the transaction, by a 30-chars. length alphanumeric string. The caller must not reuse a previously used transaction id.
*   This value serves also as a useful tool for logging and troubleshooting purposes.

| Header | Value |
| ---| --- |
| transactionId | **{unique-30-char-alphanumeric-string}** |

## Other remarks

*   All request values, unless explicitly stated otherwise, must not include non-latin standard characters (e.g. á é í ñ, etc.). Those values should be transcribed to their regular version, instead (a-z A-Z n).

# Physical Card Features

**_For SACBE October 2020 Implementation:_**
*   The PIN will be setted up in the Web View within the request card flow
*   There will be 2 types of Embossing for Phyisical Cards available:
    *   Personalized
    *   Non-Personalized
*   There will be 2 types of delivery available
    *   To a Specific Address
    *   To a Location
The story that addresses the PIN feature is the following: [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1726](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1726)
The story that addresses the file to be produced for the embosser for each new card that is created is the following: [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1732](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1732)
The story that addresses the update in the shipment information is the following: [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1772](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1772)
The story that addresses the update in the user’s account to add the Physical Car is the following: [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1773](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1773)

The story that addressed the _General Changes_ to be able to use the AddAccount Method proplery is the following: [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1793](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1793)

# Details File for Embosser for SACBE Mexico Program

## General Details
For the Mexico Program for SACBE, it is necessary to update a details file to the embosser company (G+D) with all the Physical Cards created every day.
This needs to be performed by a automatic process that appends to a file every time a card physical card is created in Galleo
The following story is the one that covers this requirement: Create/Update Details File to Embosser for Mexico Program[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1732](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1732)
## Technical Details
**The information that the embosser needs is in the following detail:**
`BatchNumber|Reference|Peso|CodigoPostalOrigen|DireccionOrigen1|DireccionOrigen2|CiudadOrigen|MunicipioOrigen|EstadoOrigen|ColoniaOrigen|CodigoPostalOrigen2|TelefonoOrigen|CelularOrigen|ContactoOrigen|RazonSocialOrigen|DireccionDestino1|DireccionDestino2|CiudadDestino|MunicipioDestino|EstadoDestino|ColoniaDestino|CodigoPostalDestino|TelefonoDestino|CelularDestino|ContactoDestino|RazonSocialDestino`
The file to be created is a txt file to have an “append” for each card is created, each field of each new line needs to be a fixed size
**The folllowing contains an example of two lines of the file:**

```auto
284101002483LSB145SPLS                    0102110AV SAN PABLO XALPA            331                           CIUDAD DE MEXICO                                  AZCAPOTZALCO                                      CIUDAD DE MEXICO                                  SAN PABLO XALPA                                   02110          5599999999                    CLM Modulo 4                  CLM Modulo 4                                      BLVD.BELISARIO DOMINGUEZ      1691 LOC-1                    TUXTLA GUTIERREZ                                                                                    CHS                                               JARDINES DE TUXTLA    C.COM. PLAZACRISTAL         29020         19616154295                    DISTRIBUIDORES Y VENDEDORES  DMARIA DE LOS ANGELES GUTIERREZ RICO
284101002491LSB146SPLS                    0102110AV SAN PABLO XALPA            331                           CIUDAD DE MEXICO                                  AZCAPOTZALCO                                      CIUDAD DE MEXICO                                  SAN PABLO XALPA                                   02110          5599999999                    CLM Modulo 4                  CLM Modulo 4                                      BLVD.BELISARIO DOMINGUEZ      1691 LOC-1                    TUXTLA GUTIERREZ                                                                                    CHS                                               JARDINES DE TUXTLA    C.COM. PLAZACRISTAL         29020         19616154295                    DISTRIBUIDORES Y VENDEDORES  DDISTRIBUIDORES Y VENDEDORES  DEL CENTRO SA DE CV
```

**The details of the requirement data are described roughly in the following documents:**

# Fields Mapping
**The mapping of the fields to be retrieved from Galileo to be added to this file is the following:**

| **Field in Platform**<br> | **Field in File**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| AccountNumber | BatchNumber (\*) |
| AccountNumber | Reference (\*) |
| 01 | Peso (\*) |
| 02110 | CodigoPostalOrigen (\*) |
| AV SAN PABLO XALPA | DireccionOrigen1 (\*) |
| 331 | DireccionOrigen2 |
| CIUDAD DE MEXICO | CiudadOrigen (\*) |
| AZCAPOTZALCO | MunicipioOrigen |
| CIUDAD DE MEXICO | EstadoOrigen |
| SAN PABLO XALPA | ColoniaOrigen (\*) |
| 02110 | CodigoPostalOrigen2 (\*) |
| [UserView.Contact](http://UserView.Contact) | TelefonoOrigen (\*) |
| 5599999999 | CelularOrigen |
| CLM Modulo 4 | ContactoOrigen |
| CLM Modulo 4 | RazonSocialOrigen (\*) |
| user.Address.Address1 | DireccionDestino1 (\*) |
| String.Empty | DireccionDestino2 (\*) |
| [user.Address.City](http://user.Address.City) | CiudadDestino |
| user.Address.Address3 | MunicipioDestino |
| user.Address.State | EstadoDestino(\*) |
| user.Address.Address2 | ColoniaDestino (\*) |
| user.Address.ZipCode | CodigoPostalDestino(\*) |
| user.UserId | TelefonoDestino |
| user.UserId | CelularDestino(\*) |
| user?.FirstName + ' ' + user?.LastName + ' ' + user?.LastName2 ?? "" | ContactoDestino(\*) |
| user?.FirstName + ' ' + user?.LastName + ' ' + user?.LastName2 ?? "" | RazonSocialDestino |

## Embosser Sftp Server
The following info contains user and password to access embosser Sftp server
embosser frecuency setup can be found on WalletsApi appconfig:
<add key="Cron\_Embosser" value="\* \* \* \* \*" /> every minute
<add key="Cron\_Embosser" value="0,10,20,30,40,50,60 \* \* \* \*" /> every 10 minutes
<add key="Cron\_Embosser" value="30 23 \* \* \*" /> every day at 23:30
### QA Server

**SftpPassword**:'7\_#h@VQ7=2'
**SacbeSftpHost**:'3.226.114.233'

# Transaction, Activity, Otypes Types for SACBE

The activity types are used throughout the internal consolidation, money movement and settlement of Funds in Galileo’s processes.
They need to be used properly within the Platform in the createPayment and createAdjustmens API calls in each program, to be able to match with the definitions that Galileo requires for its processes and to reflect the proper movements /transactions definitions in the Account Statement of the user
Latest Updates to Activity Types [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1942)[SAC-1942](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1942) - Latest Updates to Activity Types to register Payments and Adjustments In QA
**Explanations/ Usage of Activity Types in each program**

**Funds Flow Diagrams with Explanation for flows and its activity types**
1. Original
2\. Updated to October 2020

## Otypes Descriptions
New Requirement to Add the description in each API call for the Activity Types / Otypes

The storiesto address this changes for SACBE implementation are the following
[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-2579)[SAC-2579](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-2579) - Add a description for the Transactions in the Galileo Adjustments and Payments In QA
: [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1636)[SAC-1636](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1636) - Provide the Proper API Configuration for Galileo when updating the Wallet Account CLOSED

# PCI Sensitive Data Access for SACBE

## General Details
The way Galileo allows the access the PCI Sensitive Data is through the access throug a PCI Compliance third party
The third party should be the one making the call to the getCard() method, with specific credentials that are set by Galileo for this specific purpose
Also, the credentials will only work witht the program that signed the specific “connection agreement” with VGS
The third party for Numi is VGS
VGS allows the configuration of the routes to access the Get Card Method and tokenize, de-tokenize the information to be sent for any third party that requires it (e.g. Cybersource / TabaPay)
## Credentials
For Current Sacbe Implementation the credentials for each environment are detailed in the following images follows:
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/e89824af-438a-432a-99d7-58d698f889ce/image-20201019-154043.png)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/2d60adf7-c4fc-43eb-acf5-edb590aa4184/image-20201019-154100.png)

The Jira to address the update of this configuration to be able to set the PROD environment is the following: Update Credentials for VGS Production access to PCI Compliance Data in Galileo [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1844)[SAC-1844](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1844) Dev Complete

## Customer Support Tool
the CST Tooll is the console where the Galileo Operation can ge reviewed. It is intended for the user of customer support team but it is useful for the development team for validations and verifications related to the Cards/Accounts held in Galileo
You need to Contact your Scrum Master to get credentials for this site
### In CV Environment the URL is
[https://tools-bancacao.cv.gpsrv.com/tool/index.php?s=2](https://tools-bancacao.cv.gpsrv.com/tool/index.php?s=2)
### In Prod Environment the URL is
[https://tools-bancacao.pd.gpsrv.com/tool/query.php](https://tools-bancacao.pd.gpsrv.com/tool/query.php)

# General Information

## General Details
For the Tzune Implementation the "Main" Product is a Funds Product, and both the Virtual and the Physical Card will be Products Added to the Main Funds Account
The Three Products will share the balance
See: Funds Product

For more information on the implementation visit the specific Space: [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BCO/overview](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/BCO)

# Testing in PROD APIs

1. Create Account
2. Visualize Image URL (white)
    1. 
3. Add Account
4. Get Pin URL
    1. submitter\_id = 600-0193
    2. 
5. Set pin using URL
6. Visualize Image URL (black)
    1. 
7. Commit PIN
8. Create Payment SPEI to step 1
9. Create Payment SR Pago step 1
10. Validate Balance in CST
11. Create Account
12. Visualize Image URL
13. Transfer from Account step 1 to Account Step 11
14. Validate Balance in CST
15. Create Adjustment SPEI Account Step 11

# Tzune Funding Account Products and Card Products

For Tzune implementation a New Product Type from Galileo was introduced to be used as a base product, to be the main product for a user account, and then add the card products to this one.
For this reason, the creation of the account in Galileo needs to be made using this product as ProdID parameter
The createAccount Endpoint call needs to be made with the full information of the Platform user’s profile and the prodId corresponding to the Funds Product `21980`

```auto
curl --location --request POST "https://api-bancacao.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/createAccount" ^
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' ^
--header 'Cookie: TS015a1273=012fd6b12871a1c4396fc753e66efe2d19051f484f9915c93ff278b37e03a96e7ebfd73c23bcb91593c67b11f48431d55cb5300066' ^
--data-urlencode 'apiLogin=QB6xKS-0193' ^
--data-urlencode 'apiTransKey=AZjJEf2jc497Wmc' ^
--data-urlencode 'transactionId=Testing_Tzune_FundingPd_06062021_1' ^
--data-urlencode 'providerId=600' ^
--data-urlencode 'prodId=21980' ^
--data-urlencode 'firstName=Vanesa' ^
--data-urlencode 'lastName=Vilas del Diestro' ^
--data-urlencode 'dateOfBirth=1978-06-02' ^
--data-urlencode 'address1=W. 0 A. Mozart' ^
--data-urlencode 'city=Zapopan' ^
--data-urlencode 'postalCode=45030' ^
--data-urlencode 'primaryPhone=3310120378' ^
--data-urlencode 'mobilePhone=3310120378' ^
--data-urlencode 'email=vanesaviilas@yahoo.com' ^
--data-urlencode 'userData=AFFILx1' ^
--data-urlencode 'idType2=14' ^
--data-urlencode 'id2=MEXICO' ^
--data-urlencode 'idType=14' ^
--data-urlencode 'id=CURP-ABC123456789' ^
--data-urlencode 'state=JAL' ^
--data-urlencode 'countryCode=484'
```

Following when the user request a Card, for the first time, the Product ID `19112`

```auto
curl --location --request POST "https://api-bancacao.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/addAccount" ^
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' ^
--header 'Cookie: TS015a1273=012fd6b128b455dce86c2bfed00e7f4f075922235af48539485cacc33205bd3f7ef63251e0f8e58a64c650205532e32e6269c46326' ^
--data-urlencode 'apiLogin=QB6xKS-0193' ^
--data-urlencode 'apiTransKey=AZjJEf2jc497Wmc' ^
--data-urlencode 'transactionId=Testing_TzuneAddVirtual_03062021' ^
--data-urlencode 'providerId=600' ^
--data-urlencode 'prodId=19112' ^
--data-urlencode 'accountNo=003101000689'
```

Or when the user request a Physical Personalized Card: `8519`

```auto
curl --location --request POST "https://api-bancacao.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/addAccount" ^
--header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' ^
```

## FUNDS FLOW ANALYSIS 23-02-2022
Basado en la siguente documentacion

# Fraud Features

The following documentation was shared by Galileo Team regarding the Fraud Features

# Products and Hierarchy

For Hway the Parent Product will be the funding account = ProdID= 25342
When a user creates an account this is the main PRN so it´s the first that needs to be created
**As per Galileo’s Advice on Meeting on March 1st of 2022 the changes over the previous statement are being displayed in blue:**
*   The Main Account is advisable we use the Virtual Card Product
    *   **H.way Virtual Card ProdID = 25304**
*   This is achieved with the **createAccount** endpoint
*   The Main Account which is a Virtual, can be used as an account (PRN) for the user and not displaying the Card Information (PAN, exp date: card image overlay) to the user
*   If the user requests it, he can be displayed with the Virutal Card information in the card image overlay format
*   When the user request a physical card, the Physial Acount is added as a secondary account
*   This is achieved with the **addAccount** endpoint
*   After the creation of the main PRN product can be added to the account, if the user user requested a physical or virutal account, for a “PERSONALIZED” Card
    *   **H. Way 25446- Personalized MasterCard**
**TBD = how will HWAY handle differnt card types? The options are:**
*   different card art for the same product
*   different products
\*
*   When the user wants to setup a **savings account** (Guardadito): use the addAccount Method with the main account PRN, the sharedBalance must be NO (sharedBalance=0) and the following PRODID=
    *   **Guardaditos (25342)**
## Examples in Postman
1. **Create Main Account**
    1. Request
    2. _Please note, the amount of parameters to be provided depends of the implementation, for example the Address related information, can either be added here in the_ **_createAccount_** _endpoint or afterwars using the_ **_updateAccount_** _endpoint. In this example only the idtype, and id related fields, and the name related fields, are provided_

```auto
  curl --location --request POST "https://api-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/createAccount" ^
  --header "response-content-type: {{responseContentType}}" ^
  --header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
  --header "Cookie: TS015a1273=012fd6b128ccf072ba39799dc41729eeae76f48ceb7e8e59f9c90f5863323f6b37dbe8462cb379361424fca2fa2b7d3f1cbbcca5bf" ^
  --data-urlencode "apiLogin=XZ5JgK-0253" ^
  --data-urlencode "apiTransKey=LUbgxR3XcQ6Tax6" ^
  --data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
  --data-urlencode "transactionId=1723b7a4-0db3-4e1e-a98a-11afcf1ca1a0" ^
  --data-urlencode "idType=4" ^
  --data-urlencode "id=26417769M|05-02-2032" ^
  --data-urlencode "idType2=14" ^
  --data-urlencode "id2=ARG" ^
  --data-urlencode "firstName=Vanesa" ^
  --data-urlencode "middleName=Viviana" ^
  --data-urlencode "lastName=Vilas" ^
  --data-urlencode "dateOfBirth=1978-02-06" ^
  --data-urlencode "countryCode=484" ^
  --data-urlencode "prodId=25304"
```

# Secondary Account/Cards Technical Details

The following images contain information of the secondary cards products created for HWAY Program

## **Primary (Virtual) Secondary**
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/e522444e-19d9-495e-b3af-f3fc5cd086a7/image-20230529-193208.png)
## **Personalized (Physical) Secondary**
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/03dc9918-0867-4120-8b38-f6f59ca4ae2f/image-20230529-193308.png)
## Creating Secondary Account API
The API to use is `createAccount`
Example CURL for creating a Secondary Account = Virtual Secondary Primary Product `prodId=2897"`

```dsconfig
curl --location "https://api-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/createAccount" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=XZ5JgK-0253" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=LUbgxR3XcQ6Tax6" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=50c3fb54-d239-42a2-9a2b-af06e7759d2c" ^
--data-urlencode "firstName=Macarena" ^
--data-urlencode "middleName=Elizabeth" ^
--data-urlencode "lastName=del Diestro" ^
--data-urlencode "dateOfBirth=1972-01-01" ^
--data-urlencode "address1=Wallaby 42" ^
--data-urlencode "city=Sidney" ^
--data-urlencode "postalCode=333333" ^
--data-urlencode "countryCode=840" ^
--data-urlencode "prodId=2897" ^
--data-urlencode "primaryAccount=381101010449" ^
--data-urlencode "sharedBalance=0"
```

## Parameters to highlight
The following are the specific parameters for the secondary account to keep in consisderation in the usage of the Create Account endpoint
`"primaryAccount=381101010449" ^` - the number of the main account on which the secondary account will depend
 `"prodId=2897" ^`the product id of the secondary account
`"sharedBalance=0"` the shared balance is = means NOT SHARED

### Example Response

```json
{
    "status_code": 0,
    "status": "Success",
    "processing_time": 1.479,
    "response_data": [
        {
            "pmt_ref_no": "381101080368",

}
```

## Creating Secondary Physical Card API
The API to use is `addAccount`
Example CURL for creating a Secondary Account = Secondary Physical Product `prodId=2895"`

```dsconfig
curl --location "https://api-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/addAccount" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=XZ5JgK-0253" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=LUbgxR3XcQ6Tax6" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=c216c161-2ac2-492a-8a55-1c21320a5eea" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=381101080368" ^
--data-urlencode "prodId=2895" ^
--data-urlencode "firstName=Vanesa" ^
--data-urlencode "middleName=Viviana" ^
--data-urlencode "lastName=Vilas" ^
--data-urlencode "dateOfBirth=1978-02-06" ^
--data-urlencode "sharedBalance=1"
```

## Parameters to highlight
The following are the specific parameters for the secondary account to keep in consisderation in the usage of the Add Account endpoint
`"accountNo=381101080368" ^` - the number of the **secondary** “account on which the physical secondary card will be added
 `"prodId=2895" ^`the product id of the secondary physical card
`"sharedBalance=1"` the shared balance is =1 means SHARED
### Example Response

```json
{
    "status_code": 0,
    "status": "Success",
    "processing_time": 1.598,
    "response_data": {
        "pmt_ref_no": "381101080418",
        "product_id": "2895",
        "galileo_account_number": "6153",
        "card_id": "7230",
        "card_number": "525579XXXXXX8909",
        "expiry_date": null,
        "card_security_code": null,
        "emboss_line_2": null,
        "billing_cycle_day": null
    },
    "echo": {
        "provider_transaction_id": "",
        "provider_timestamp": null,
        "transaction_id": "a4684f0c-e57a-4429-aaf3-25882588e4ee"
    },
    "system_timestamp": "2023-05-29 15:15:33",
    "rtoken": "5ce146d5-1661-4a37-856b-e1fcfc399735"
}
```

# Re-Activation of Accounts and Virtual Cards

In certain scenarios where the Main Account product has been suspended and the _virtual card associated to -them_ has been disabled, there might be the need to set Back to Active the Account and the Virtual Card
Note = this procedure **is not** applicable for physical cards products
Having the PRN of the Account that is needed to set back to Active Status, there is a specific status type that needs to be set using the modify status endpoint, in order to be able to re-activate them
## Pre-conditions
1- Make sure the Account / Card belongs to the product “main account” = H-Way Primary Account (25304)
2- Make sure that the **Card is in Status Disabled (D)** and the **Account is in status Suspended (K)**
### Ways to Verify
In CST in the primary information of the Account
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/f50d2454-ff33-4d4f-b66c-29282ffafe9c/image-20240418-170414.png)

In CST in User Notes
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/0065a7af-fd53-4063-8747-059d8e410b72/image-20240418-171225.png)

## Set The Account and Card to Active Status
The /modifyStatus ([https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_modifystatus](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_modifystatus) ) endpoint needs to be **called twice** in order to set the active status for the virtual card first and the account second.
In each case the corresponding parameter “type” needs to be the following
*   **type = 7 will activate the card**
**Example CURL Request**

```plain
curl --location "https://api-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/modifyStatus" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=XZ5JgK-0253" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=LUbgxR3XcQ6Tax6" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=1ff8787b-c352-4c11-a480-68e32a088fa6" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=381101085268" ^
--data-urlencode "type=7" ^
--data-urlencode "startDate=" ^
--data-urlencode "endDate=" ^
--data-urlencode "bypassRepFee="
```

**Example Response**

```json
{
    "status_code": 0,
    "status": "Success",
    "processing_time": 0.062,
    "response_data": {
        "pmt_ref_no": "381101085268",
        "account_status": "K"
    },
    "echo": {
        "provider_transaction_id": "",
        "provider_timestamp": null,
        "transaction_id": "432d0551-8687-46ea-ba4d-f17df687b578"
    },
    "system_timestamp": "2024-04-18 10:17:35",
    "rtoken": "8fd22f9f-7840-4e54-bed0-c68189ba50d8"
}
```

The Response should return the element Account Status changed successfully
`"account_status": "N"`

*   **type = 11 will activate the account**
**Example CURL Request**

```plain
curl --location "https://api-leapfin.cv.gpsrv.com/intserv/4.0/modifyStatus" ^
--header "response-content-type: json" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "apiLogin=XZ5JgK-0253" ^
--data-urlencode "apiTransKey=LUbgxR3XcQ6Tax6" ^
--data-urlencode "providerId=741" ^
--data-urlencode "transactionId=c1f5b482-27de-4e55-aa4a-99d61e6e7f6d" ^
--data-urlencode "accountNo=381101085268" ^
--data-urlencode "type=11" ^
--data-urlencode "startDate=" ^
--data-urlencode "endDate=" ^
--data-urlencode "bypassRepFee="
```

**Example Response**

```json
{
    "status_code": 0,
    "status": "Success",
    "processing_time": 0.095,
    "response_data": {
        "pmt_ref_no": "381101085268",
        "account_status": "N"
    },
    "echo": {
        "provider_transaction_id": "",
        "provider_timestamp": null,
        "transaction_id": "cc3652a8-a61a-4314-81d7-cb9e05b5690f"
    },
    "system_timestamp": "2024-04-17 14:11:29",
    "rtoken": "0758fa46-76be-4168-a21f-f5dbe7f0a721"
}
```

The Response should return the element Account Status changed successfully
`"account_status": "N"`
## Post - Conditions
1 - Make sure that the **Card is in Status Active (N)** and the **Account is in status Active (N)**
### Ways to Verify
Theres several ways to verify that the status has changed successfully
1 - In CST Checking the Main information of the Account (see screenshot example)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/78c515dc-3ccf-4686-b29c-00503278a079/image-20240418-163916.png)
2 - In CST Reviewing the “User Notes” section (see screenshot example)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/f9e31e0a-c193-4222-8140-423673ae8b20/image-20240418-164138.png)

3 - Using the getAccountCards endpoint = [https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post\_getaccountcards](https://docs.galileo-ft.com/pro/reference/post_getaccountcards)
The Accounts element will provide the status of the card
`"status": "N",`
and the list of cards associated, with their status as well
`"card_status": "N",`

# Green Dot

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/5b2f8d90-6a16-4d18-9296-06ca9635fa1f/1200px-Green_Dot_logo.svg.png)
[https://www.greendot.com/business/developer](https://www.greendot.com/business/developer)
## General Details
Green dot provides the Cash-In and Cash-Out Mechanisms in USA through a network of branches in different stores, that allows the users to either put or withdraw cash, using two mechanisms: 1) swipe through a physical credit card (service provided directly by Galileo) 2) Barcode Reading (a barcode image generated within our Platform through the directo connection with GreenDot APIs)

# Expect ID

ExpectID® is an identity verification service that is used to verify if an identity is real without requiring consumer interaction. ExpectID relies on IDology’s analytics to assess the components of an ID and return a result.

This guide is intended for IT teams responsible for integrating IDology’s services into an existing web site or application.
## Definitions

• **Client**: The party integrating its application with the IDology Web service.
• **Consumer**: The person or set of credentials that is the subject of the authentication process.
• **End User:** An individual using IDology’s verification services to verify a consumer’s identity.
• **IDCenter**: IDology’s web portal, which is used to process verifications and serves as an on-demand change management tool for the client.
• **Enterprise**: The client’s account, settings, and business rules established and maintained through the IDCenter.
• **Configuration**: The process of defining the client’s Enterprise business rules and settings in the IDCenter.
• **Integration**: The process of implementing IDology’s services, via the IDology API, within an existing application or web site.
• **Touch Points:** The point in the business process where ExpectID services are integrated. This business decision needs to be made in conjunction with the Enterprise Configuration, prior to beginning the integration of ExpectID services into the client’s application. The most common touch points for integration are point of entry, account origination, point of sale, and account modifications.
## Overview

Integrating both ExpectID and ExpectID IQ involves three basic steps:
1. Making a secure connection to the ExpectID service.
2. Submitting a request.
3. Receiving a formatted XML response.
## API Documentation from Idology API Reference
[https://web.idologylive.com/api\_portal.php#step-1-request-subtitle-step-1-expectid-iq](https://web.idologylive.com/api_portal.php#step-1-request-subtitle-step-1-expectid-iq)
## Step 1 – Request
> Sample Code

```bash
if [ "$#" -eq 8 ]; then
  curl -k --data-urlencode "username=$1" --data-urlencode "password=$2" --data-urlencode "firstName=$3" --data-urlencode "lastName=$4" --data-urlencode "address=$5" --data-urlencode "city=$6" --data-urlencode "state=$7" --data-urlencode "zip=$8" 'https://web.idologylive.com/api/idiq.svc'
else
  echo Usage: $0 "<username> <password> <firstName> <lastName> <address> <city> <state> <zip>"
fi
```

**Enable Product**
*   Configure required thresholds and rules through IDology's IDCenter to use various other features.
**Endpoint**
_XML Request_: `https://web.idologylive.com/api/idiq.svc?username={username}&password={password}&firstName={firstName}&lastName={lastName}&address={address}&zip={zip}`

_JSON Request_: `https://web.idologylive.com/api/idiq.svc?username={username}&password={password}&firstName={firstName}&lastName={lastName}&address={address}&zip={zip}&output=json`
**Request Parameters**

| Request Parameter | Description |
| ---| --- |
| username<br>**Required** | IDology API username (128 bytes). |
| password<br>**Required** | IDology API password (255 bytes). |
| invoice<br>**Optional** | Your invoice or order number. |
| amount<br>**Optional** | Order amount. |
| shipping<br>**Optional** | Shipping amount. |
| tax<br>**Optional** | Tax amount. |
| total<br>**Optional** | Total amount (sum of the above). |
| idType<br>**Optional** | Type of ID provided. |
| idIssuer<br>**Optional** | Issuing agency of ID. |
| idNumber<br>**Optional** | Number on ID. |
| paymentMethod<br>**Optional** | Payment method. |
| firstName<br>**Required** | First Name. |
| lastName<br>**Required** | Last Name. |
| address<br>**Required** | Street address. |
| city<br>**Conditional** | City. _City and State required if enabled_. |
| state<br>**Conditional** | State (2). _City and State required if enabled_. |
| zip<br>**Conditional** | 5-digit ZIP code (5). _ZIP Code required if enabled_. |
| country<br>**Conditional** | [ISO 3166 Alpha-3 country code.](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#search/code/).<br>_Note: This field is only required for the verification of international (non-US) customers._ |
| ssnLast4<br>**Optional** | Last 4 digits of SSN (4). _Results improve with the addition of this field_. |
| ssn<br>**Optional** | Full SSN (9). |
| dobMonth<br>**Optional** | Month of Birth (2). _Results improve with the addition of this field_. |
| dobYear<br>**Optional** | Year of Birth (4). _Results improve with the addition of this field. YOB is the minimum DOB_<br>_information accepted by IDology_. |
| dobDay<br>**Optional** | Day of Birth (2). |
| ipAddress<br>**Optional** | IP Address. _Include periods in the address, for example - 11.111.111.11_. |
| email<br>**Optional** | Email address (255). |
| telephone<br>**Optional** | Phone number (10). |
| sku<br>**Optional** | SKU. |
| uid<br>**Optional** | User ID (External application). |
| altAddress<br>**Optional** | Alternate street address. Submit a secondary address for verification, such as a shipping<br>address. |
| altCity<br>**Optional** | Alternate city. |
| altState<br>**Optional** | Alternate state (2). State in abbreviated format, i.e. for Georgia send "GA." |
| altZip<br>**Optional** | Alternate 5-digit ZIP code (5). |
| purchaseDate<br>**Optional** | Card purchase date. |
| captureQueryId<br>**Optional** | This `<id-number>`should be submitted if data is being sent from the ExpectID Scan Onboard<br>product. This field should be populated with the `<id-number>` provided in the API Response<br>from the Scan Onboard polling service. This will couple calls to the ExpectID IQ API service<br>to results from Scan Onboard. |
| score<br>**Optional** | Score. |
| c\_custom\_field\_1<br>**Optional** | Custom field 1. |
| c\_custom\_field\_2<br>**Optional** | Custom field 2 (True/False Value only). |
| c\_custom\_field\_3<br>**Optional** | Custom field 3. |
| output<br>**Conditional** | JSON<br>_Note: This field MUST be included in the request to receive JSON output._ |

**Notes**
*   IDology recommends submitting all of the fields (even blank values) for optimal performance and full change management functionality.
*   dobMonth, dobDay, dobYear, ssn, and ssnLast4 may or may not be required fields depending on the Enterprise configuration.
*   Fields are generally limited to 40 characters in length, except as noted above (Character length limits are in parenthesis after the field description).
*   While most of these values will be populated by the contents of a form from the client’s website, it is very important that the username and password fields be hard coded to prevent possible user tampering. These fields should never be included in the HTML source of a public web page.
*   The `country` parameter is only required for requests for international (non-US) customers and must be provided in the [ISO 3166 Alpha-3 country code.](https://www.iso.org/obp/ui/#search/code/) format.
*   If the response from the service needs to be in JSON format, then be sure to include `output=JSON` within the request to our service; without this parameter, responses will be returned in XML format.
## ↳ Critical Errors
> _Error Response - Invalid username and password_

```gherkin
<response>
   <error>Invalid username and password</error>
</response>
```

Password changes can be made for user IDs (including API Accounts) by using the IDCenter and take effect immediately. However, unless the password is also updated in the integrated application, the API requests will begin failing immediately and an error response will be returned in the response (“Invalid username and password”).

If separate application instances are maintained, all instances of the application must have access to the API account password. IDology clients are required to protect the password in a secure database that is accessible only to the application and selected administrators.
External IP addresses can change without warning if any of the following happen:
*   A network device is replaced.
*   Code is deployed to a new server.
*   The application infrastructure fails over to a different network device or a different IP on the same device.
*   A failover event occurs and the application is brought up in a redundant architecture.
> _IP address is not registered_

```plain
<response>
     <error>Your IP address is not registered. Please call IDology Customer Service (866-520-1234).</error>
</response>
```

Whatever the cause for the IP address change, if the integrated application is making requests to IDology from an IP address that is not included within a company’s specific IP Whitelist, the request will be denied. An XML response will be returned that explains the reasoning behind the decision.

### ↳ ↳ Disaster Recovery / Fail-Over Testing
IDology maintains test pages to our web services that can be utilized to ensure connectivity to the IDology system in the event of a fail-over or a switch to our DR site. The commands will test connectivity from client servers or employee workstations to [https://web.idologylive.com](https://web.idologylive.com) and [https://reports.idologylive.com](https://reports.idologylive.com) .
Either curl or wget may be used to test connectivity using the following commands:
**curl**
$ curl [ric-web.idologylive.com](http://ric-web.idologylive.com) --insecure
SUCCESS
$ curl [ric-reports.idologylive.com](http://ric-reports.idologylive.com) --insecure
SUCCESS
**wget**
$ wget -O- [ric-web.idologylive.com](http://ric-web.idologylive.com) --no-check-certificate -q
SUCCESS
$ wget -O- [ric-reports.idologylive.com](http://ric-reports.idologylive.com) --no-check-certificate -q
SUCCESS
Important: IDology recommends contacting our Customer Service team to request assistance for testing the services using live transactions to ensure that the proper resources are available to support all requests.
## Step 2.1 - Response without IQ Questions
The output is an xml or json object that contains the results of verification. Use the contents of the response to determine whether or not to proceed with the transaction and how to process the keys such as `<result>`, `<summary-results>`, `<questions>`, `<velocity-result>`, and others described below.
**Notes**
*   `<key>` - The `<key>` value is the non-configurable output from the search and is what developers use as identifiers.
*   `<message>` - The `<message>` contents are the user-definable text messages corresponding to the key results, which are specified in the IDCenter and used for configuring display messages in the ID Credential search tool or within the client's application.
*   `<results>` and `<summary-results>` are distinct and should be used according to the Enterprise Configuration. For example, if Summary Results are enabled,`<summary-results>` should be used and `<results>` should be ignored.
*   ID Notes also include warnings or informative messages such as **resultcode.coppa.alert** for subjects who are age 13 or under, or **resultcode.subject.deceased** in the event that a subject's records indicate that they are dead.

| Response Parameters | Description |
| ---| --- |
| response | Verification results come back in the response object. |
| id-number | Unique ID number user for reference, accounting, or troubleshooting purposes. |
| summary-result | Provides information on the success or failure to locate the record.<br>id.success<br>id.failure<br>id.partial<br>Contains following `key` and `message`.<br>_Note: A result of_ `id.partial` indicates that additional data is needed for a successful match (see Multiple Records Found under ExpectID Conditions). |
| ↳ key | Message identifier for `summary-result`. |
| ↳ message | Displays the message for `summary-result` : `PASS` or `FAIL`. |
| results | Returns the ExpectID result. This value indicates if the information submitted matched the information located, subject to IDology’s default tolerances and error-correcting logic (if enabled) on common typos in addresses or names.<br>In the non-configurable `<key>`, the result will be one of the following:<br>result.match<br>result.no.match<br>result.match.restricted<br>Contains following `key` and `message`. |
| ↳ key | Message identifier for `results`. |
| ↳ message | Message for `results` : `PASS` or `FAIL`. |

> _Subject Located_

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" ?>
<response>
  <id-number>2972309</id-number>
  <summary-result>
      <key>id.success</key>
      <message>Pass</message>
  </summary-result>
  <results>
      <key>result.match</key>
      <message>ID Located</message>
  </results>
</response>
```

**Example Response** : _Subject is located_

_Subject not Located_

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" ?>
<response>
  <id-number>2972309</id-number>
  <summary-result>
      <key>id.failure</key>
      <message>Fail</message>
  </summary-result>
  <results>
      <key>result.no.match</key>
      <message>ID Not Located</message>
  </results>
</response>
```

**Example Response** : _Subject is not located_

The **summary-result** and **results** tags are not the same.
> `<summary-result>` and `<results>` are not the same

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" ?>
<response>
   <id-number>2972309</id-number>
   <summary-result>
      <key>id.failure</key>
      <message>Fail</message>
   </summary-result>
   <results>
      <key>result.match</key>
      <message>ID Located</message>
   </results>
   <qualifiers>
      <qualifier>
         <key>resultcode.yob.does.not.match</key>
         <message>YOB Does Not Match</message>
      </qualifier>
   </qualifiers>
</response>
```

**Example Response**:
*   For each Enterprise, the Summary Results configuration in the IDCenter is a business decision that reflects business policies and goals and should not be considered part of the IDology integration. Using Summary Results allows an Enterprise to change its business rules in the IDCenter without having to complete any code changes.
*   The value returned via the `<summary-result>` tag is distinct from the value returned via the `<results>` tag and should be used only if Summary Results is enabled in the IDCenter.
*   The `<summary-result>` values are based on customizable rules. The `<results>` are based on IDology’s default logic.
*   In the example on the right, the Summary Results configuration in the portal had the YOB Does Not Match rule entry set to `Fail`. In the input for this example, the YOB was incorrect.

# Expect ID PA

ExpectID PA® (Patriot Act) helps businesses comply with the United States Patriot Act by accessing a group of government watch lists to help determine if an individual is included. ExpectID PA can be used not only to verify against all available Patriot Act-related lists, but it can also restrict results to the Office of Foreign Asset Control (OFAC) watch list only.
## Overview
Integrating ExpectID PA involves these basic steps:
1. Making a secure connection to the ExpectID PA service.
2. `ExpectID PA` should be enabled on IDology's IDCenter.
3. Submit a request.
4. Receive a formatted response via XML.
## Communication Flow
Submit an array of values to the ExpectID PA API service with the required parameters. The request is processed and the submitted attributes are matched against our databases. The service will then return a response to let you know if the customer's information is listed on any government watch lists.
## Step 1 - Request
### ExpectID PA (Standalone)
The standalone version of ExpectID PA can only be used once IDology has enabled the product for the enterprise, which provides the client-assigned administrators with the ability to view the ExpectID PA menu and customize settings within the IDCenter.
> Sample Code

```auto
// To use this code; download and integrate the Java helper library from  https://web.idologylive.com/api_portal.php#helper-libraries

package com.idology.helper;

public class ExpectIdPA {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        new Request("/api/pa-standalone.svc",
                new String[] { "username", "password", "firstName", "lastName", "address", "city", "state", "zip" })
                        .callAPI(args, System.out);
    }
}
```

**Enable Product**
*   `ExpectID PA` should be enabled on IDology's IDCenter.
**Endpoint**
`https://web.idologylive.com/api/pa-standalone.svc?username={username}&password={password}&firstName={firstName}&lastName={lastName}&address={address}&city={city}&state={state}&zip={zip}`
You must replace the {username} parameter with the required value. Do this for all subsequent parameters in brackets.
**Request Parameters**

| Request Parameter | Description |
| ---| --- |
| username<br>**Required** | IDology API username (16). |
| password<br>**Required** | IDology API password. |
| invoice<br>**Optional** | Your invoice or order number. |
| amount<br>**Optional** | Order amount. |
| shipping<br>**Optional** | Shipping amount. |
| tax<br>**Optional** | Tax amount. |
| total<br>**Optional** | Total amount (sum of the above). |
| idType<br>**Optional** | Type of ID provided. |
| idIssuer<br>**Optional** | Issuing agency of ID. |
| idNumber<br>**Optional** | Number on ID. |
| paymentMethod<br>**Optional** | Payment method. |
| firstName<br>**Required** | First Name. |
| lastName<br>**Required** | Last Name. |
| address<br>**Required** | Street address. |
| city<br>**Conditional** | City. _City and State required if enabled_. |
| state<br>**Conditional** | State (2). _City and State required if enabled_. |
| zip<br>**Conditional** | 5-digit zip code (5). _Zip Code required if enabled_. |
| ssnLast4<br>**Optional** | Last 4 digits of SSN (4). _Results improve with the addition of this field_. |
| ssn<br>**Optional** | Full SSN (9). |
| dobMonth<br>**Optional** | Month of Birth (2). _Results improve with the addition of this field_. |
| dobYear<br>**Optional** | Year of Birth (4). _Results improve with the addition of this field.YOB is the minimum DOB information accepted by IDology_. |
| ipAddress<br>**Optional** | IP Address. _Include periods in the address; for example - 11.111.111.11_. |
| email<br>**Optional** | Email address. |
| telephone<br>**Optional** | Phone number (10). |
| sku<br>**Optional** | SKU (stock keeping unit). |
| uid<br>**Optional** | User ID (External application). |

### ExpectID PA with ExpectID Verification
> Sample Code

```auto
// To use this code; download and integrate the Java helper library from  https://web.idologylive.com/api_portal.php#helper-libraries

package com.idology.helper;

public class ExpectIdPA {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        new Request("/api/idiq.svc",
                new String[] { "username", "password", "firstName", "lastName", "address", "city", "state", "zip" })
                        .callAPI(args, System.out);
    }
}
```

**Enable Product**
*   Enable `ExpectID PA` through IDology's IDCenter to use this API.
**Endpoint**
`https://web.idologylive.com/api/idiq.svc?username={username}&password={password}&firstName={firstName}&lastName={lastName}&address="address"&zip={zip}`
**Request Parameters**

| Request Parameter | Description |
| ---| --- |
| username<br>**Required** | IDology API username (16). |
| password<br>**Required** | IDology API password. |
| invoice<br>**Optional** | Your invoice or order number. |
| amount<br>**Optional** | Order amount. |
| shipping<br>**Optional** | Shipping amount. |
| tax<br>**Optional** | Tax amount. |
| total<br>**Optional** | Total amount (sum of the above). |
| idType<br>**Optional** | Type of ID provided. |
| idIssuer<br>**Optional** | Issuing agency of ID. |
| idNumber<br>**Optional** | Number on ID. |
| paymentMethod<br>**Optional** | Payment method. |
| firstName<br>**Required** | First Name. |
| lastName<br>**Required** | Last Name. |
| address<br>**Required** | Street address. |
| city<br>**Conditional** | City. _City and State required if enabled_. |
| state<br>**Conditional** | State (2). _City and State required if enabled_. |
| zip<br>**Conditional** | 5-digit zip code (5). _Zip Code required if enabled_. |
| ssnLast4<br>**Optional** | Last 4 digits of SSN (4). _Results improve with the addition of this field_. |
| ssn<br>**Optional** | Full SSN (9). |
| dobMonth<br>**Optional** | Month of Birth (2). _Results improve with the addition of this field_. |
| dobYear<br>**Optional** | Year of Birth (4). _Results improve with the addition of this field.YOB is the minimum DOB information accepted by IDology_. |
| ipAddress<br>**Optional** | IP Address. _Include periods in the address,for example - 11.111.111.11_. |
| email<br>**Optional** | Email address. |
| telephone<br>**Optional** | Phone number (10). |
| sku<br>**Optional** | SKU (stock keeping unit). |
| uid<br>**Optional** | User ID (External application). |

## Step 2 - Response
If the customer's name is located on any of the Patriot Act watchlists (or on THE OFAC list only, if that option is set in the IDCenter), then the ExpectID PA response will be enclosed in the `<restriction>` tag, as shown in the example to the right:
> Example: _Subject is located on the Patriot Act watchlists_

```auto
<response>
  <id-number>1454442</id-number>
  <restriction>
    <key>global.watch.list</key>
    <message>Patriot Act Alert<message>
  </restriction>
</response>
```

**Example Response**: Customer's Name Detected on Watchlist
### ExpectID PA DOB Enhancement
IDology's ExpectID PA product also features an optional enhancement to utilize the customer's input DOB information for enhanced scoring. When the input DOB is provided along with the name for processing, it will be matched against the located DOB of the name located on the Watch List.
This enhancement will also return an additional `<qualifier>` in the `<qualifiers>` tag indicating whether the DOB matches or if one is not available in the matching Watch List record. In instances where IDology is provided with the input DOB and it matches the name to a Watch List, then the located DOB is passed back in the `<pa>` key in the response.
Possible `<qualifiers>` for the PA DOBs feature include:

| Result Code | Description |
| ---| --- |
| resultcode.pa.dob.match | Indicates that the input DOB matches the located DOB on the PA Watch List record.<br>_Note: The located DOB will also be returned in the_ `<pa>` tag. |
| resultcode.pa.dob.does.not.match | Indicates that the DOB does not match the located DOB on the PA Watch List record.<br>_Note: the located DOB will also be returned in the_ `<pa>` tag. |
| resultcode.pa.dob.not.available | Indicates that there is no located DOB on the PA Watch List record to match against the input DOB. |

_Note: This feature is not enabled for your enterprise by default. The feature must be enabled by an IDology Customer Service representative to be utilized. Please contact Customer Service for additional information._

# Scoring of Results

The scoring is a tool provided by Idology within the API results to allow to qualify the results with a defined range of values that allow to evaluate the level of risk a possible response on the analysis of the given user data is given.
_IDology’s ExpectID Score is an analytical feature that allows clients to gauge the risk level of a transaction. This feature is fully customizable by the client and can be utilized to make decisions based on whether the transaction is low, medium, or high risk._
The following is an example of the Response with Expect ID Score:
> <response> <id-number>1108681053</id-number> <summary-result> <key>id.success</key> <message>Pass</message> </summary-result> <results> <key>result.match</key> <message>ID Located</message> </results> <qualifiers> <qualifier> <key>resultcode.address.does.not.match</key> <message>Address Does Not Match</message> </qualifier> <qualifier> <key>resultcode.street.number.does.not.match</key> <message>Street Number Does Not Match</message> </qualifier> <qualifier> <key>resultcode.street.name.does.not.match</key> <message>Street Name Does Not Match</message> </qualifier> <qualifier> <key>resultcode.low.risk</key> <message>Low Risk Score</message> </qualifier> </qualifiers> <idnotescore>123</idnotescore> </response>  
>   
For the Current **SACBE (June 2020) Implementation** the Enabled Services for Idology are:
*   Expect ID
    *   Authentication Method PI Only w/ Optional SSN4 & YOB (Personal Information Only)
*   ExpectID PA
    *   Match Score 92
    *   Search OFAC (Office of Foreign Asset Control)
    *   Search Politically Exposed Persons
    *   Search NonSDN Consolidated Sanctions (PLC, FSE, ISA, SSI)
Based on the above mentioned services, the following Google Sheet contains a list with description of the Id notes that are available for scoring.
It contains a sheet specifically for the configuration for **SACBE a per June 2020**
It also includes the full list of all possible ID Notes, which are enclosed in the `<qualifiers>` key as `<qualifier>` subkeys and may be returned.
Based on this score, the Platform should provide the following:
*   Validation for the result score
*   Validation for the continuity of the CIP process
    *   If the scoring is a low risk, is safe, it should continue the CIP process
    *   If the scoring is a medium risk, it should provide a stop to the process, with a soft fail result, allowing the UI to require further input of date to the user
    *   If the scoring is a hight risk, it should provide a stop to the process, with a hard fail result, informing the UI to disable the user to continue with the process
The Jira story related to this topic is:
[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1476](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1476)

# Testing ID Notes Scoring

### Testing ExpectID
When testing ExpectID, the following test case may be used to generate most result code.

Entering this test case into the ID Credential window using the information below exactly as written will generate a clean ID without returning any ID Notes.

**The following information will return a clean ID with the API**
First Name: JOHN
Last Name: SMITH
Street Address: 222333 PEACHTREE PLACE
City: ATLANTA
State: GA
ZIP: 30318
DOB: 02/28/1975
SSN: 112-22-3333

**Triggering ID Notes with Test Cases**
Certain ID Note result codes (such as Thin File and Single Address in File) require individual test cases to produce the result code.
Each IDology client has a different policy related to our ID Notes feature and how transactions are handled. Some clients are not concerned about the contents of ID Notes and focus solely on the `<results>` that are returned in the XML response.
The following list includes ID Notes that are available for testing, which are enclosed in the key as subkeys that can be returned during a search, as well as information on how to trigger each ID Note. Refer to the [Full List of ID Notes](https://web.idologylive.com/api_portal.php#full-list-of-id-notes) for a detailed description of each ID Note.

Considering the positive Case mentioned Above as baseline, changing the data according to the following table, should trigger the notes

| **ID Note**<br> | **API Result Code**<br> | **First Name**<br> | **Last Name**<br> | **Address**<br> | **City**<br> | **State**<br> | **ZIP**<br> | **DOB**<br> | **SSN last 4**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |

# Idology Authentication Information

## General Details
The authentication for the APIs is provided through the API username and password and the whitelisting of the IPs
In order to provide authentication specific for the API calls the “User Profile” to be created in Idology Dashboard needs to be of the type “API Account”
The Whitelisting needs to be requested to Idology Customer Support specifing the Enterprise Account

# Bridger Black Lists Incluvida API

## General Details
Bridger is the product that Incluvida uses to validate user Identity against Black Lists in Mexico
Incluvida provides an API to be able to connect with a user name and validate if such user names has “coincidences” in the Black Lists provided by Bridger
## Connectivity
The following document contain the specification for the connectivity for the API and other details

## Technical Details
The following document contain the specification for the usage of the API

# InPaMEX Cash Network API - Domestic Remittances

Impamex is a Affiliated Company to CI Banco, which is the sponsor Bank on the Mexican Side for SACBE
Impamex and Incluvida are two brands within of the same company
They will provide an API to allow the SACBE Platform to receive money transfers from the Remittances Network originated in the MTOs Offices and update the SACBE Platform User´s Wallet with such transfers.
There´s also the aim to allow the Remittances Network to be used as Cash Network Offices for Domestic Money Transfers, this is , a SACBE Platform User will be able to send money _domestically_ to be received by another User and _Cashed-Out_ taking advantage of the many Inpamex network offices.
## There’s two integrations that are related with Inpamex}
1. **Receive Remitances in Wallet**
2. **Use the Inpamex Cash Network as Cash Out Network**

# **1\. Receive Remittances in Wallet**
Receive Information of the remittances with SACBE Wallet as a destination method, that are handled by Inpamex (being the Correspondant to Intercambio Express) through Incluvida
The APIs that need to take place to perform the Information exchange that needs to take place between Incluvida (throgh Inpamex) and SACBE Platform is depicted at high level in the following Sheet.

The user story related to this requirement is: [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1340](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1340)
In the following graphic you can visualize the overal Flow that is involved in this scenario:

## **INPAMEX Web Service for money transfers**
URL:                 [http://187.174.203.210/SandBox/MoneyTransfers.asmx?wsdl](http://187.174.203.210/SandBox/MoneyTransfers.asmx?wsdl)              
AgentCode:       C9
UserName:        INPAMEX-SACBE
Password:          S5yDd328#2\*

[Inpamex - INPAMEX Web Service for money transfers](https://docs.google.com/document/d/14Rtlm4QLIRLDYDElN7-C4LMgaOdjzByE/edit)

# **2\. Integration of Cash Networks**
The second requirement is to connect to the APIS provided to use the Inpamex Stores Network as the SACBE Cash Network as described in the following document

The story to address this requirement is the following: SACBE Cash Networks Through Inpamex[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1348](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1348)
For the Appropriate Handling of the Logos and Names of the Cash Networks review the following Page: [5\. Cash Network Options-Assets](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SAC/pages/45981857/5.+Cash+Network+Options-Assets)

The following graphic depicts in high level the flow of information for the Cash Networks or “Domestic Remittances”

# Raw Requirements / Meeting Notes

**NOTAS DE MEETING 10/06**
*   se debe realizar la validación de limites y velocity
*   **definición pendiente:** reversa de operaciones en diversas situaciones
*   en las cuales no es válido la llegada de dinero al beneficiario.
*   **definición pendiente:** Cuestiones normativas de cumplimiento vs costo de devolver.
*   todos los acumulados de manejan por mes calendario y no por
*   acumulación de tiempo
*   identificación de transacción de la remesera, se usa en Leap. Falta obtener la información de id de tx de remesera
*   FALTA DEFINIR UN CATALOGO DE CODIGOS DE STATUS DE PARTE DE LEAP
*   Catalogos de Redes de Pago para la Recepción doméstica: cuando se envia
*   desde wallet a remesas domésticas. Son redes dirigidas.
*   **Definición pendiente:** incluir otros docs (cambio CIP del app)
*   Son diferentes de las reglas de pagos de efectivo en el contexto de la APP
*   Servicio de Pago a red de Remesa que entrega Incluvida
*   Servicio de cash-out de efectivo en redes de efectivo, (como se encuentra actualmente implementado por Leap) se necesita:
    *   API de código para remesa dirigida a una red de pago
    *   API de cancelación de código

# SPEI API

## General Details
This API is aimed to provide the mechanisms to allow the SACBE Platform Mexican users to perform Banking Transactions from and to their Wallets using SPEI Banking framework.
This API was produced by the Third Party Incluvida as a proxy to the connection with a third party named STP which is a Financial Solutions Provider for Incluvida.
The following document depicts the overall information for this API

# Detalles Técnicos

A continuación se describen detalles técnicos de la API de acuerdo a la documentación provista y aclaraciones identificadas en reuniones
## **Funcionamiento básico:**
La App de SACBE se conectará con La API de SPEI de CI Banco, que a su vez se conecta con STP (la Entidad que brinda el BIN para Certificar SPEI) , quien efectivamente ejecutará el proceso de transferencia interbanco a través de SPEI.
La información se identifica en dos escenarios básicos detallados a continuación
*   **Envio SPEI**
*   Envios desde la cuenta de un usuario de la Plataforma SACBE haceia una Cuenta de una Entidad Externa
    *   La API Recibe un CLABE valido desde la Plataforma SACBE y ejecuta el envio de dinero conéctandose a su vez con STP
    *   La Transacción mantiene un estado que STP actualiza de acuerdo al resultado de la misma
    *   LA API informa a la plataforma de SACBE de dicho estado, actualizando la información y saldos necesarios
*   **Recepción SPEI**
*   Envios desde una Cuenta de una Entidad Externa hacia una cuenta la Plataforma SACBE
    *   Desde uns Entidad que envia a una CLABE asociada a una Tarjeta/wallet dentro del programa SACBE
    *   La API obtiene la información de dicha Transacción desde STP
    *   La LA API informa a la plataforma de SACBE de la recepción, actualizando la información y saldos necesarios
## Detalles Identificados en Reunión 30-04
1. Identificación Recíproca de las Transacciones
    1. Cuales serán los datos que deben utilizarse para identificar de manera recíproca/unívoca las transacciones entre la API SPEI y .la Plataforma SACBE
    2. **Authorisation Number (número interno) + Tracking Key (numero de identificación entre las entidades) \_ Enviados por el API para la generación de la transacción**
2. No hay una Confirmación específica de Transacción Ejecutada desde STP
    1. Se debe esperar un X tiempo **(2 minutos)**
        1. Si hay un error STP devuelve notificación de error, con la devolución
        2. Se asume que si no hay error la TX fue exitosa
3. Cuando se recibe dinero de otras entidades, se debe “matchear” la información que envía STPE cno la información de los usuarios de SACBE
4. El STPE informa un _Cambio de Estado_ de la transacción en caso de errores
    1. Se dispone de un diccionario de errores de rechazo
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/b878ec63-eef6-45c5-bddf-26fa5ebab29c/image-20200828-184142.png)

1.     1. 
5. Se asume que la CLABE es correcta en cuanto a formato y validez “basica” en cuanto a sintaxis cuando se hace el envío a STPE
    1. Extensión de la CLABE
    2. Existencia del BIN de la Entidad a la que pertenece (Listado de Entidades Certificadas para participar en SPEI) Se puede obtener del método de la API _CounterpartInstitutions_/
    3. Algoritmo de dígito verificador

# Mexican User Banking Information

As part as the SPEI related information provided by third Party Incluvida, the banking information is available for the Platform to Obtain, Store and Use.
The Jira story that covers this requirement is : [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1537)[SAC-1537](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1537) - Provide Information for Users Banking Details in MEX Program CLOSED
In order to be able to obtain the CLABE to be stored as the Banking information for the Platform User, as well as part of the information for the SPEI transactions (both InBound and OutBound) the Platrofm needs to access the following Method from the SPEI API
> SACBE/Customers/  
> Método POST que da de alta un cliente, genera una cuenta y CLABE del cliente  
> Headers de  
>   
Then the information of the CLABE needs to be stored for each MEXICAN customer instead of the Routing Number

# Black Box Service

This services aimed to retrieve an analysus performed by the black box / risk profiler service that the Incluvida Bank has on their system, and return the risk profile the user has
It is merely a rest service that receives data and returns the type of risk
High
Medium
Low
For the details of the Functional Feature of Black Box please review: [Scoring Service "Black Box"](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SAC/pages/1775108097)
## Documentation
The following google Drive incluces the documentation shared by Incluvida with the Details of the Service for its Integrations

# Incode

Incode is the third party hired by SACBE that provides CIP validations solutions within a SDK

## General Information
Incode provides the services for capturing the data thru the OCR and perform the validations both of data and the required by law:
*   For Mexico: CURP, RENAPO
*   For USA: SSN, Address
It also provides the Selfie, Proof of Life and Signature Capture services
The way the Incode Integration works is basically creating a session for the data gathering. The session is called “Interview”. For each session the data is gathered through different incremental steps that involve the usage of the data scan elements and the extracion of the information throught the OCR.

## Technical Information
Incode provides different type of Integration
*   Xamarin Wrapper for Mobile Layer
*   API Rest Services

Below is a graphic that describes the process of the Incode Integration within the Mobile and Backend Platform
