I hope this is helpful to start picturing how you’d build out this MVP for your waitlisted customers.

# MX Widget Integration

# Mx Authentication for APIs
MX API requires Authentication via Token in the header “Authorization”.

```auto
headers: {
		"Authorization": "Basic ZmM0NDE1ZWItM2YxNS00NWNkLWFmOGEtOTU2N2U3MzMxZjAwOjI2MWJkNjFlMDQ5ODM0NDUyNzExNWY2N2ExZjdkY2ZmMDUwOWJiOWY=",
		"Accept": "application/vnd.mx.api.v1+json",
		"Content-Type": "application/json"
}
```

This value is a Base 64 conversion of UserName : Password style header. The value of the User Name is the **Client ID** provided by MX, the password is the **Api Key**. They can be consulted in `https://dashboard.mx.com` For easier conversion, you can use the Postman environment for MX, configure username and password with those values and then copy the header generated.
[https://docs.mx.com/api#authentication\_and\_security](https://docs.mx.com/api#authentication_and_security)
MX recommends that the Client ID and Api Key does not get used in a Front End environment or other public areas.
## Integration
The Header in the Code Example is the one used for Integration environment.
## Production
When working in production environment the Backend IP Address has to be whitelisted. In case it is outside the US, MX will do extra investigation. If not whitelisted, they will return `403 Forbidden error`.
# Host or Base URL
The value of the Base URL for Integration and Production environments are as follow.

| **Environment**<br> | **Host**<br> |  |
| ---| ---| --- |
| Integration | `https://int-api.mx.com` | For dev and QA environment. |
| Production | `https://api.mx.com` | For Production |

This needs to be configured in the Backend Code when deploying to production.

# API Get URL for Master Widget

## General description.
This API receives the phone number in international format as an ID and retrieves a URL from MX API to display the Master Widget. The ID has to be previously registered using the **API Register New MX User**.
This API calls “Request a Connect URL“[https://docs.mx.com/api#connect\_request\_a\_url](https://docs.mx.com/api#connect_request_a_url) to retrieve the URL using the corresponding GUID stored in a local Database.
The URL provided by MX is one-time use, so you’ll need a new one each time the page refreshes.
## Endpoint.
GET [https://{host}/mx-url/{id}](http://#)

| **Variable**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Example**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| host | Host for the API. | dev: [mx-dev.h-way.com](http://mx-dev.h-way.com)<br>qa: [mx-dev.h-way.com](http://mx-dev.h-way.com)<br>prod: [mx.h-way.com](http://mx.h-way.com) |
| id | Phone number in international format as is to be used by h.way app. | “+5215585804206”<br>”+5491161477570” |

**Validation**
id: the ID used has to be between 12 and 14 characters.
## Request.

```auto
let urlWidget = fetch('http://{host}/mx-url/{id}', {
  "mode": "cors"
  });
```

## Response.
All responses are returned in text format.
### Success:
The successful response is the URL for the widget. **Code: 200**
Example:

```auto
https://int-widgets.moneydesktop.
```

There are two environments for MX: Integration and Production. For integration environment the URL domain is [**_int-widgets.moneydesktop.com_**](http://int-widgets.moneydesktop.com)
### Error:
When the ID is not yet registered. **Code: 400**

```auto
Error: No está registrado el ID
```

When the ID has an invalid format. **Code: 400**

```auto
Error: Formato de ID no válido
```

When the MX API returns an error. **Code: 500**

```auto
Error en MX
```

When the local database returns an error. **Code: 500**

```auto
Error en consulta
```

# API Register New MX User

## General Description.
This API receives the ID to be used as a NUMI user, registers it as a MX User and stores the GUID in a local database which is the Primary Key to be used when using MX. This API calls “Create user” [https://docs.mx.com/api#core\_resources\_users\_create\_user](https://docs.mx.com/api#core_resources_users_create_user) to create the user.
The **ID** is the phone number in international format, the **GUID** is needed to use any other MX service such as a new URL to access MX Widget.
## Endpoint.
POST [https://{host}/mx-user/{id}](http://#)

| **Variable**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Example**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| host | Host for the API. | dev: [mx-dev.h-way.com](http://mx-dev.h-way.com)<br>qa: [mx-dev.h-way.com](http://mx-dev.h-way.com)<br>prod: [mx.h-way.com](http://mx.h-way.com) |
| id | Phone number in international format as is to be used by h.way app. | “+5215585804206” |

**Validation**
id: the ID used has to be between 12 and 14 characters.
## Request.

```auto
let urlWidget = fetch('https://mx-dev.h-way.com/mx-user/{id}',{
  "method": "POST",
  "mode": "cors"
});
```

## Response.
All responses are returned in text format. All errors start with the string “Error“.
### Success:
The user is successfully registered. **Code: 200**

```auto
Insercion satisfactoria.
```

### Error:
When the format of the ID is not valid. **Code: 400**

```auto
Error: Formato de ID no valido. ${id}
```

When local database is unavailable. **Code: 500**

```auto
Error en consulta.
```

When local database couldn’t insert. **Code: 500**

```auto
Error: No se pudo completar la peticion
```

When the user is already registered. **Code: 400**

```auto
Error: Usuario ya registrado. ${id}
```

When MX API returns an error. **Code: 500**

```auto
Error en MX
```

# Calling MX Widget from a Web page.

To display a widget in a Web Page, we need to insert some Javascript code. You can find MX documentation in the following link: [https://docs.mx.com/connect/guides/web#get\_url](https://docs.mx.com/connect/guides/web#get_url)
## Using Widget Loader.
Insert the following code before the </body> tag. Configure the fetch command properly, passing along the NUMI ID (phone number, international format) that has already been registered in the MX Platform using the **API Register New MX User**.

```auto
<script src="https://atrium.mx.com/connect.js"></script>
<script>
var mxConnect = new window.MXConnect({
  id: "connect-widget",
  iframeTitle: "Connect",
  onEvent: function (type, payload) {
    console.log("onEvent", type, payload);
  },
  config: {
    ui_message_version: 4
  },
  targetOrigin: "*",
});
// API Get URL for Master Widget: ID = phone number.
fetch(`https://mx-dev.h-way.com/mx-url/${id}`, {
  "mode": "cors",
  headers: {   }
  })
  .then(res => res.text())
  .then(url => {
    console.log(url);
    mxConnect.load(url);
  });

</script>
```

## Add an HTML element to display the Widget.
We need to define an HTML element where the Widget is going to appear. The **div** element is recommended. This element is going to have an **id** that is passed into the widget loader. In the example above the id is shown in line 4 with the value _“connect-widget”_, you can configure that.
Example:

```auto
<div id="connect-widget"> MX Widget goes here. </div>
```

The widget loader is going to need a new URL for each time it is accessed or refreshed.

# API Validate Telephone Number

## General Description.
This API receives a Telephone number, transforms it to give it international format and returns it in such format. Returns error in case the telephone number doesn’t have a valid length, between 12 and 14 characters including the + at the beginning. The only validation is length after eliminating other characters.
## Request.
POST https://{host}/validphone/
Body:

```auto
{ "tel": "+1 (619) 123-4567"}
```

| **Variable**<br> | **Description**<br> | **Example**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| host | Host for the API | dev: [mx-dev.h-way.com](http://mx-dev.h-way.com)<br>qa: [mx-dev.h-way.com](http://mx-dev.h-way.com)<br>prod: [mx.h-way.com](http://mx.h-way.com) |
| tel | Telephone number to validate and apply format. Accepts any string. | “521 55-4556-5707“<br>“+1 (619) 123-4567“ |

### Code example:

```auto
let validatedPhone = fetch(`https://mx-dev.h-way.com/validphone`,{
  "method": "POST",
  "mode": "cors",
  "headers": {"Access-Control-Allow-Origin": '*', 
            "contentType": "application/json"},
  "body": { "tel": "+1(619)123-4567" }
});
```

## Response.
All responses are returned in text format. All errors start with the string “Error“.
### Success:
The telephone number is returned in the proper format and has a length of 12 to 14 characters starting with +. **Status: 200**.

```auto
+16191234567
```

### Error:
When the length of the telephone number is not valid. **Status: 400**.

```auto
Error: Telefono no valido. ${tel}
```

# NUFI

## General Details
[Ana Mondragón](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/61ddd522125b12007143d425?ref=confluence) plese provide information on what is this provider used for

## Technical Details
The following services are going to be used from this provider
*   OCR INE/IFE: [https://docs.nufi.mx/docs/referencia-tecnica/b3A6NTA4NTcyMjQ-comparacion-ine-ife-vs-imagen-de-rostro](https://docs.nufi.mx/docs/referencia-tecnica/b3A6NTA4NTcyMjQ-comparacion-ine-ife-vs-imagen-de-rostro)

# Señor Pago

Gateway de Pagos de Mexico
[https://devcenter.srpago.com/](https://devcenter.srpago.com/)

## Reunión de Aclaraciones de Dudas 17/12/2020
## Señor Pago como Payment Gateway
*   Las Tx de load Funds de Tarjetas, quedan en una cuenta de Señor Pago
*   Tzune tiene una cuenta en Señor Pago (no es una cuenta Bancaria)
*   Tzune debe realizar “orden de Retiro” de la cuenta
*   Los Retiros pueden realizarse
    *   dia siguiente habil: Visa Master
    *   dos dias hábiles: Amex
*   Los viernes:
    *   lunes: Visa Master
    *   martes: Amex
## Tzune como Agregador de Señor Pago
*   Concepto de Submerchants
    *   Tzune es un sponsor conceptualmente
    *   Agrega “sub-merchants” que serían los comercios
*   **Explica Señor Pago: El esquema se llama “Join” - modelo ecommerce**
    *   Sub-Afiliados
    *   Tzune es la cabeza de la cuenta
    *   Se pueden ver los montos totales de cada sub-afiliado
    *   No se puede ver el detalle de transacciones
*   Como funciona el movivmiento de dinero
*   El sub-afiliado “no se entera” que está Señor Pago detrás
*   Con esta premisa, como se logra que el dinero del sub-afiliado llegue a la cuenta de cada sub-afiliado (discriminación de los montos y movimientos de cada sub-afliliado)
*   Señor Pago dice: Para disponer de los fondos el sub-afiliado debe “enterarse” que su “pasarela” es Señor Pago
    *   Para cobrar sus fondos puede ser a traves de una liga dentro de la APP movil de Tzune
    *   Se debe registrar el sub-afiliado, en Señor Pago, y allí se entera
*   Para permitir construilr la plataforma en “white label”
    *   Se puede hacer pero solamente con los accesos del cliente
    *   Tzune y Sr Pago tienen que hacer disclosure que estan accediendo a la información sensible de la cuenta
    *   Debe incluirse en los terminos y condiciones que el usuario permite a Tzune acceder a las credenciales

*   **Consultan si Tzune puede ser un agregador, sería un modelo de Agregador**
*   Señor Pago dice que no se puede hacer
    *   consulta si tienen licencia de Banco
*   Falta contexto e información en Señor Pago para responder si podemos hacerlo sin “dejar saber” a los submerchants que está señor pago detrás
    *   Señor pago no tiene preparado la infra o architectura para operar como agregador
    *   Se estan haciendo un proceso para ello pero hoy en dia no está listo
*   Que necesita un agregador para operar por Señor Pago
    *   certificacion de agregador en la CNBV
    *   certificaciones de PCI
    *   tecnología/infraestrcutura, etc
## Requerimiento de Tzune
*   Habilitar a los comerciantes con una cuenta N3 para que pueda cobrar con tarjeta de crédito
*   El usuario ya pasó por el KYC de Tzune
*   Solo hace falta darlo de alta en Señor Pago
*   Mover los recursos de la cuenta de Señor Pago a la ccuenta del Banco
    *   sin que el usuario participe
    *   el usuario no tiene idea que Sr Pago está haciendo el trabajo
*   Señor Pago sugiere el modelo Join en lugar del modelo Agregador
Todo el proceso desde que ocurre un cobro, se opera, se procesa, se ejecuta, se concilia a traves de la tubería de Señor Pago y las reglas de negocio de Señor Pago: puede no ser factible esde el punto de vista de Señor pago
Demasiada carga operativa interna, intentar fungir emular el modelo agregador usando la solución de ecommerce

*   **Agregador** \- liquidación directa a la cuenta master y el master se ocupa de la distribución
*   **Join** \- señor pago tiene que controlar el settlement de cada cuenta

**Dongles de Sr Pago**
*   como el dispositivo ejecuta la transaccion
    *   se comunica con la plataforma Tzune o ejecuta toda la transaccion en si mismo?

## Reunión de Aclaraciones 22-12
## **Agregador**
*   No está aun listo el servicio de agregador, no lo estará en Enero
    *   Se necesitan muchas certificaciones para ser agregador
    *   ABC Capital podria serlo?
        *   Institución Bancaria (circular unica de banco)
        *   Bancacao es otra institución, no está en ese alcance, para funcionar como agregador
        *   Se debe solictar a la CNBV
            *   Es como una excepción, el tipo de licencia necesaria
        *   Consejo de Francisco Fernandoez: Se debe validar como es la relación contractual entre ABC y Bancaco y el tipo de agregador se definirá en la CNBV en función de eso
        *   [Hector Candia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/5bec3273fbe1467261e65c69?ref=confluence) aclara que es una extensión del banco, un producto del Banco
        *   Tipo de PCI Compliance
            *   Francisco Fernandez: no cosidera que VGS + ABC sirva para tener el PCI
*   Mediados de Febrero podría estar la funcionalidad de Agregador
*   En CNBV se pueden tomar mas de 2 meses en otorgar la licencia, dependiendo de la documentación presentada.
### **Join**
*   Toda la operación es del lado de Sr Pago (chargeback, control de fraudes)
*   Condicion, es NO ser agregador
*   El join debe permitir a Sr Pago controlar la cuenta de cada afiliado (cada “sub-merchant”)
*   Cada afiliado es cliente de Sr. Pago
**Modelo que propone Tzune**
*   Una sola cuenta general, de Tzune
*   El sub-merchant no se da de alta en Sr Pago, y las transacciones serán solo de la cuenta general, y Tzunes se ocupa internhamente de diferenciarlas
*   Problemas que plantea Sr. Pago: lo errático de la transacciones, el control de fraude

# PLAID

Plaid is the provider that allows the bank account information to be used as a fundiong or delivery method, in USA implementations, to be handled securely (tokenized) and outside the Numi Platform
## General Information
The PLAID interaction provides a web view that integrates within the mobile app, to allow the users to enter their bank account information, by login into plaid directly, enter the bank, and the details, and retrieves a tokenized data object to be handled in the Platform.
## Technical Information
See the page three for specifics on the integrations and technical details for each implementation
*   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-26997](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-26997))
*   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27017](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27017))
*   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27057](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27057))
*   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27037](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27037))
*   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27077](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27077))
*   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-26977](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-26977))

## Documentation

# Getting Started

In order to work with plaid the first action is to get the environment for the implementation. Such environment will depend on the credentials that are used.
For Leap there was a legacy configuration for “Leap Factor” and a New configuration in 2022 for Leap Financial
After the configuration for the environment with the App configuratin and the Api Keys, the “Plaid Link” needs to be implemented for the new proyect (with the corresponding specific credentials associted to “plaid link” url). See [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/862847002/Getting+Started#Using-PLAID-Link](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/862847002/Getting+Started#Using-PLAID-Link)

# **Using PLAID Link**
Link is the web element that should be embeded within the process in the moment of the flow where the user banking information needs to be requested/accessed
Link is used in the mobile layer with the information configured from backend layer
For Plaid technical documentation on link review the following link
[https://plaid.com/docs/link/webview/#create](https://plaid.com/docs/link/webview/#create)
Review in the page three the information for the configuration of Link´s URL and Parameters for each implementation

# Adding Collection to Postman

Una vez hecho login en la plataforma de PLAID
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/4f5fbf32-3d24-49f9-ab82-998be5ec5e68/image-20210112-162355.png)

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/600543b6-c156-4d09-9bda-bac089092c66/image-20210112-162339.png)

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/ae5dc125-130e-424d-9a29-8af07704694f/image-20210112-163011.png)

Del contenido descargado se deben identificar 2 archivos:

*   Sandbox Environment.json
*   Plaid API Endpoints Collection.json
*   
Del primero Sandbox Environment.json se deben reemplazar 2 parametros por los valores respectivos obtenidos de la seccion de Keys del dashboard de PLAID
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/dd39e17a-de23-4444-8e4f-d364a04b4b19/image-20210112-163537.png)

Una vez cambiado los valores, se pueden importar a Postman de la siguiente manera:
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/022485c6-df91-4ee0-a0ac-fae27edcb7c4/image-20210112-164215.png)

Se deben importar los 2 archivos con el fin de dejar todo en su sitio,

{
"override\_accounts": \[
{
"type": "depository",
"subtype": "savings",
"starting\_balance": 1570
}
\]
}

# Plaid Integration using Oauth

Plaid and the bank exchange information using Oauth for secure reasons. Oauth is a necessary protocol to be used in integration with the most important financial / bank institutions.
In order to comply with the Oauth Integration certain steps must be taken in the configuration of the Plaid information for the implementation, there is a specific secttion in the project configuration in the Plaid Dashboard for this.
Here is a full Guide from PLAID on all the Oauth information [https://plaid.com/docs/link/oauth/](https://plaid.com/docs/link/oauth/)
## Oauth implementation changes
The technical implementation for oauth depends on how the Plaid Link is used, the following documentation from plaid describes the possible scenarios and its implementation:
[https://plaid.com/docs/link/oauth/#implementing-oauth](https://plaid.com/docs/link/oauth/#implementing-oauth)
## Redirect Link
As part of the Oauth mechanism a link must be provided as a parameter of the original Link call, to allow the financial institutions that leveraga the Oauth Authentication to “come back” to the original call made.
For hway implementation 2022 the redirect link is the following:
[http://oauthredirect.h-way.com/oauthredirect.html](http://oauthredirect.h-way.com/oauthredirect.html)

This iinformation is updated as part of the backend configuration in: Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27137](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27137))
## Logic in the Mobile layer
Once the redirect parameter is defined in the url for Link it is necesary to include logic in the Mobile layer
To see how this needs to be done in webviews, please refer directly to PLAID documentation:
[https://plaid.com/docs/link/oauth/#webview](https://plaid.com/docs/link/oauth/#webview)

## Other Oauth Considerations
There is another considerations that are advised by PLAID to review in the Oauth implementation
[https://plaid.com/docs/link/oauth/#institution-specific-behaviors](https://plaid.com/docs/link/oauth/#institution-specific-behaviors)
Implement insittuion-specific logic for Oauth [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-459)[HWAY-459](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-459) Backlog

| The URL for the WebView of “Plaid Link” to be userd in each case<br><br>Plaid Link is the client-side component that your users will interact with in order to link their accounts to Plaid and allow you to access their accounts via the Plaid API.<br>see documentation of the parameters and definitions in :[https://plaid.com/docs/link/webview/#overview](https://plaid.com/docs/link/webview/#overview) | Url of “Link” - element where the user connects to their banking information<br>[https://plaid.com/docs/link/webview/#create](https://plaid.com/docs/link/webview/#create) | URLWebView | [Claudia Bolanos](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/557058:4d2023d6-97c0-4108-ba2b-cb62c1b0531c?ref=confluence) to confirm |
| A URI indicating the destination where a user should be forwarded after completing the Link flow; used to support OAuth authentication flows when launching Link in the browser or via a webview | redirect\_uri | RedirectUri | [https://oauthredirect.h-way.com/index.html](https://oauthredirect.h-way.com/index.html) |

# Validations for Bank Accounts with PLAID

It is necessary to perform certain validations in the Bank Account payment methods taking advantage of the information provided by PLAID
Plaids allows to, using the token provided as identifier of the bank account information, retrieve the information and get the balance of the account, using certain of their endpoints.
See=
auth/get:
identity/get
account/balance/get,

Also, due to the fact that the user might change their credentials it is necessary to force the user to re-validate their credentials, this can be achieved as explained by PLAID team:
The ITEM\_LOGIN\_REQUIRED error will be returned for any Plaid product endpoint (inclusive of Balance) in the event the item is in an "unhealthy" state and the connection has been broken (i.e. user changed their password or MFA settings as an example for what would cause this).
The best way to test this ITEM\_LOGIN\_REQUIRED state in sandbox for Update Mode and calling balance would be as follows:
**"Healthy" Item ID testing:**
1. Call /sandbox/public\_token/create
    *   Returns a public\_token
2. Using the public\_token, call /item/public\_token/exchange
    *   Returns an access\_token
3. Call the /accounts/balance/get with the access token to return balance data.
**ITEM\_LOGIN\_REQUIRED testing with update mode:**
1. Call /sandbox/public\_token/create
    *   Returns a public\_token
2. Using the public\_token, call /item/public\_token/exchange
    *   Returns an access\_token
3. Using the access\_token, call /sandbox/item/reset\_login
4. Call the /accounts/balance/get with the access token.
    *   You should receive an ITEM\_LOGIN\_REQUIRED error on this Balance call attempt.
5. Using the access\_token, [create an Update Mode link token](https://plaid.com/docs/link/update-mode/#using-update-mode).
    *   Returns a link\_token
6. Using the link\_token, launch Plaid Link and restore Item health.
The "ITEM\_LOGIN\_REQUIRED" testing instructions in sandbox will allow you to simulate this Update Mode behavior and allow the team to plan/implement any messaging/action you'd like the user to take (i.e. "reconnect your account").

The following EPIC Contains all the work involved to perform this validations and the technical tasks required to be able to create thes validations mechanisms

[![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/epic.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1778)[HWAY-1778](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1778) - Enhacements to Handling of Bank Account Information provided by PLAID Blocked

Also, the functional stories that are included in this feature are the following=

Crear un nuevo servicio PaymentMethodAPI cuya finalidad es validar y registrar medios de pago del usuario.
Deberá escuchar los evento de UserCreated, UserUpdated [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1779)[HWAY-1779](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1779) - Escuchar los evento de UserCreated, UserUpdated CLOSED
Incluir migración de los usuarios actuales [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1780)[HWAY-1780](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1780) - Incluir migración de los usuarios actuales CLOSED
Persistir los datos (registro y actualización del access\_token) que permitan la validación del nombre del propietario de la cuenta y balance
Acordar cambios con mobile [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1782)[HWAY-1782](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1782) - Acordar cambios con mobile Cancelled para evitar el envió de datos del método de pago, bastaría con enviar el identificador del método de pago que ya estaría registrado en la plataforma.
Emitir eventos [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/rest/api/2/universal_avatar/view/type/issuetype/avatar/10318?size=medium)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1783)[HWAY-1783](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-1783) - Estructura para emitir eventos desde PaymentMethodApi CLOSED PaymentMethodCreated, PaymentMethodUpdated, PaymentMethodRemoved en el servicio PaymentMethodAPI

# TabaPay

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/a6ffdf37-8d04-42f3-ac25-7ef3c4286ee7/TabaPay%20Logo.png)
## General Details
In Order to be Able to allow the USA User to perform “Funding” to his wallet with external Credit or Debit Cards, or Bank accounts, it is necessary to incorporate a “Payment Gateway”, which allows the management of the money flow outside the platform and the provides the necessary mechanisms of security and legal coverage to be able to complete such flows.

# Address Verification Service

## General Details
The AVS Service by Tabapay is a service that allows to query the card with the information of the card owner’s Address to perform a validation of “second factor” to avoid card fraud.

### About
Address Verification Service (AVS) is a fraud prevention mechanism that reduces fraud and chargebacks. The service verifies if the card issuer recognizes the address provided by a cardholder. The results of the verification will help you determine whether to accept or decline a particular transaction or take further action. AVS is effective to both reduce fraud and reduce chargebacks.
Availability: It is available worldwide.
## Technical Details
In order to perform the AVS Validation, the following Query Card endpoint needs to be used, adding a specific parameter “AVS” to the url, as detailed in the documentation:
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/091d0123-c206-43b6-bac1-aecb5ed7c581/image-20210518-191236.png)
## **Provider Documentation**
The AVS general instructions and information from Tabapay in the “new” site can be reviewed in the link that follows: [https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/how-to-use-avs](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/how-to-use-avs)
The card Query API information can be found in the link that follows= [https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/cardquery](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/cardquery)
Please note the examples and descriptions in this site correspond to an implementation withouth digital wallets, with the card info in clear, which is not the scenario to be implemented in 2023)
## Implementation with Digital Wallets (2023)

# AVS with Digital Wallets

## General Details
this implementation of AVS is intentended to work with the Tabapay Integration leapfinancial has as per 2023, using Digital Wallets (Apple Pay and Google Pay) as gateway to gather the information of the cards wehre the funds will be pulled from into the user account
### Endpoint
The endpoint is similar for the different implementations, is a specific query to the cards endpoint, including the client id provided by Tabapay (see Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27237](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27237)) )
**Sandbox Endpoint**
[https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/v1/clients/{client-id-provided by tabapay}/cards?AVS](https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/v1/clients/zgoQDpQBwOtn81zd1NtX6g/cards?AVS)

## Examples and Data
### Example JSON (packed) for calling AVS with Digital Wallets information (Google Pay)

```css
{"card":{"device": {"id":"GooglePayMastercard|G600171","blob":""}},"owner":{"name":{"first": "Vanesa","last": "Vilas"},"address":{"line1": "2900 Southwest 28th Terrace","line2": "apt 2","city": "MIAMI","state": "FL","zipcode": "33133","Country": "840"},"phone": {"number": "3057536086","countryCode": "1"}}}
```

### Details for Google Pay

| **Element**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Data described by Tabapay**<br> | **Data in the Platform**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| **DEVICE OBJECT** |
| <br>id | string<br><br><br> | YES | Either a:<br><br>GooglePay|gatewayMerchantId<br> | Google pay merchant id provided by Tabapay, see = Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27357](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27357)) OR Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27337](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27337)) |
| blob | string<br><br> | YES | URL-safe, Base64 encoded card data | Blob provided by the mobile layer within the integration of GOOGLE PAY |
| **OWNER OBJECT** |
| **NAME OBJECT** |
| first | string | YES | First Name | First Name of the User |
| last<br> | string | YES | Last Name | Last Name of the User |
| **Address OBJECT**<br>All fields optional. But for AVS provide an accurate zipcode at the minimum. If available, provide an accurate line1 value as well. |
| line1 | string | YES if available | Address Line 1 | Addres 1 of the user |
| line2 | string | NOT | Address Line 2 | Addres 2 of the user |
| city | string | YES if available | City | City of the user |
| state | string | YES if available | State Code<br>State Code must be a valid 2-character code if country is 840 or 124. | State of the user |
| zipcode<br> | string | YES, MANDATORY | Zip Code<br>If country is 840, zipcode must be 5 or 9 digits.<br>If country is 124, zipcode must be in the A1A 1A1 format.<br>Zip codes from other countries will not be checked. | Zipcode of the user |
| country |  | YES if available | Country Code<br>Default is 840 | Not necessary |
| **Phone OBJECT** |
| countryCode<br><br> | string | NO | 1-3 digit Country Calling Code<br>Optional<br>_Default is_ `1`. | not necessary |
| number<br> | string | YES | 4-14 digit Phone Number. For example, 10 digits if US phone `code` is `1` (840) and 4-14 if foreign phone `code` is `101`(non 840). | Phone of the user |

### **Details for Apple Pay**

| **Element**<br> | **Type**<br> | **Required**<br> | **Data described by Tabapay**<br> | **Data in the Platform**<br> |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| **MOBILEPAY OBJECT** |
| accountNumber<br> | string<br><br><br> | YES | Pseudo Payment Card Account Number | field obtained after decription of the ApplePayPaymentToken provided by the mobile layer within the integration of APPLE PAY = `applicationPrimaryAccountNumber` |
| expirationDate | string<br><br> | YES | Expiration Date in YYYYMM.<br>Convert YYMMDD format to YYYYMM. | field obtained after decription of the ApplePayPaymentToken provided by the mobile layer within the integration of APPLE PAY = `applicationExpirationDate` |
| cryptogram | string<br><br> | YES | Payment Data Cryptogram | field obtained after decription of the ApplePayPaymentToken provided by the mobile layer within the integration of APPLE PAY = `onlinePaymentCryptogram` |
| transactionID | string<br><br> | YES | Transaction Identifier in Hex | field obtained after decription of the ApplePayPaymentToken provided by the mobile layer within the integration of APPLE PAY = `transactionID` |
| eciIndicator | string<br><br> | YES | 1-digit ECI Indicator.<br>For example if received ECI is 05 convert to 5 and populate this field<br>Usually only present when network is Visa | field obtained after decription of the ApplePayPaymentToken provided by the mobile layer within the integration of APPLE PAY = `eciIndicator` |
| network | string<br><br> | YES | Card Network.<br>Visa<br>MasterCard<br>Discover<br>Amex<br>etc... | review with backend where this field is obtained |
| type | string<br><br> | YES | Card Type. Either<br>Debit<br>Credit<br>PrePaid<br>etc... | review with backend where this field is obtained |
| **OWNER OBJECT** |
| **NAME OBJECT** |
| first | string | YES | First Name | First Name of the User |
| last | string | YES | Last Name | Last Name of the User |
| **Address OBJECT**<br>All fields optional. But for AVS provide an accurate zipcode at the minimum. If available, provide an accurate line1 value as well. |
| line1 | string | YES if available | Address Line 1 | Addres 1 of the user |
| line2 | string | NOT | Address Line 2 | Addres 2 of the user |
| city | string | YES if available | City | City of the user |
| state | string | YES if available | State Code<br>State Code must be a valid 2-character code if country is 840 or 124. | State of the user |
| zipcode<br> | string | YES, MANDATORY | Zip Code<br>If country is 840, zipcode must be 5 or 9 digits.<br>If country is 124, zipcode must be in the A1A 1A1 format.<br>Zip codes from other countries will not be checked. | Zipcode of the user |
| country |  | YES if available | Country Code<br>Default is 840 | Not necessary |
| **Phone OBJECT** |
| countryCode<br><br> | string | NO | 1-3 digit Country Calling Code<br>Optional<br>_Default is_ `1`. |  |
| number<br> | string | YES | 4-14 digit Phone Number. For example, 10 digits if US phone `code` is `1` (840) and 4-14 if foreign phone `code` is `101`(non 840). |  |

## Example Responses (Google Pay)
1. **Approved AVS**

```json
{"SC":200,"EC":"0","card":{"pull":{"enabled":true,"network":"Visa","type":"Debit","regulated":false,"currency":"840","country":"840"},"push":{"enabled":true,"network":"Visa","type":"Debit","availability":"Immediate","regulated":false,"currency":"840","country":"840"},"bin":"489537","last4":"3478"},"AVS":{"avsID":"wcEa_gYCgI-NH0geTaOHgg","networkRC":"85","networkID":"2023111516274312000","authorizeID":"162743","resultText":"NOT DECLINED","codeAVS":"Y"}}
```

1. **Denied AVS (Address matches, ZIP does not)**

```auto
{"SC":200,"EC":"0","card":{"pull":{"enabled":true,"network":"Visa","type":"Debit","regulated":false,"currency":"840","country":"840"},"push":{"enabled":true,"network":"Visa","type":"Debit","availability":"Immediate","regulated":false,"currency":"840","country":"840"},"bin":"489537","last4":"3478"},"AVS":{"avsID":"hWco8jQTiO0yE3XE3XxfQA","networkRC":"05","networkID":"2023120716482507000","authorizeID":"164825","resultText":"DECLINE","codeAVS":"A"}}


```

## Details of the Response
The important section for AVS of this Query Card response is the following:

```json
"AVS":{"avsID":"wcEa_gYCgI-NH0geTaOHgg","networkRC":"85","networkID":"2023111516274312000","authorizeID":"162743","resultText":"NOT DECLINED","codeAVS":"Y"}}
```

### The details of the fields as follows:
*   avsID = AVS Transaction Identifier
*   networkRC = [Network Response Code](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/iso-network-codes)
*   networkID = NetworkID
*   authorizeID = ID of the authorization
*   resultText = AVS Result Text. **_not always present_**
*   codeAVS = [AVS Response Code](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/avs-response-codes)
*   codeSecurityCode = [Security Code Response Code](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/cvv2-response-codes)
*   EC = [Internal Error Code](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/internal-error-codes). This is used to help TabaPay team members trace an error.

### Analysis of the Response and AVS Outcome Validation Steps
Please review the explanation provided by Tabapay in “How To Use” section here = [https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/how-to-use-avs#how-to-use-it](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/how-to-use-avs#how-to-use-it)
1. Check “networkRC” field.
2. If “networkRC” is “00” or “85” = this tells you card is live and can transact, **move to step 4**
3. If you get **a different code** you can use the following page to determine the reason: [Network Response Codes](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/iso-network-codes). = the card is not live and the transaction will NOT WORK, Hence the **Platform Transaction should not be executed. The process should be terminated.**
**Note**: the actual pull transaction may still get a decline after this check for other reasons including, but not limited to: amount limits, other checks by the issuer, etc.
1. If the Card is live, the Analysis of AVS results should take place: Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27397](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27397))
2. Depending on the Outcome of the Analysis of AVS, the Pull Transaction should be executed, or the Transaction will be failed
    1. **Note: pending to define if the user will be prompted to select another card or the transaction will be failed definitively**

# Sandbox Testing data for AVS

The following scenarios were indicated by Tabapay team to force the different outcomes/results of AVS in sandbox environment
Since in the platform this Data will be automatically setted internally using the user’s information, it is necessary to somehow mock this data into the process, to be able to force this scenarios.
Bearing in mind that the way the user gets its address into the process is by entering a real address using google autocomplete, the simulation of this scenarios must be done by handling the data trhough a mock service
The idea of the mock is to obtain a fixed address from newly created users, for each scenario, and then change that address into the harcoded one to make the actual call to Tabapay and simulate and force each scenario
The Platform team should work with the QA team to obtain one address for each scenario and perform this mock
Please note the following AVS results are the only ones provided for testing from the Tabapay Team (there are some missing AVS response codes, sadly not available in sandbox)

**AVS = "N":**
The request with the hardcoded address as requested by Tabapay is
{"card":{"device": {"id": "GooglePayMastercard|G600171","blob":"….."}},"owner":{"name":{"first": "Lionel","last": "Carrasco"},**"address":{"line1":"999 Bad.","zipcode":"**99992**"}**,"phone": {"number": "3057536086","countryCode": "1"}}}
The Response for this harcoded Address is
{"SC":200,"EC":"0","card":{"pull":{"enabled":true,"network":"Visa","type":"Debit","regulated":false,"currency":"840","country":"840"},"push":{"enabled":true,"network":"Visa","type":"Debit","availability":"Immediate","regulated":false,"currency":"840","country":"840"},"bin":"489537","last4":"3478"},"AVS":{"avsID":"xWcK4zQDgOdUX4v-LajPzg","networkRC":"05","networkID":"2023120716351889000","authorizeID":"163518","resultText":"DECLINE",**"codeAVS":"N**"}}

# AVS old implementation (not tokenized, not Digital Wallets)

**Note:**
this information pertains to the old implementation of AVS with Tabapay that was only set in production with SACBE project

Please Review the Full information of this endpoint in the Tabapay Developer Site, under the Section:
[https://developers.tabapay.net/default.html?v=21.0516](https://developers.tabapay.net/default.html?v=21.0516)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/2ea03897-3684-4a0d-9bca-837548216de7/image-20210518-191519.png)
## Fields to Be used in the Request
According to the Tabapay Documentation the fields to be used mandatory for this Valicatin Service are as follows
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/0ddf1c6d-7150-4c58-8bca-79ad391b1c30/image-20210518-194934.png)![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/93752744-8bbd-4585-8896-8cad7e8d167e/image-20210518-195058.png)
## Handling the Response
The field of the Response are as follows:
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/f78fbced-b6b1-41b6-896e-401c828dec54/image-20210518-232418.png)![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/de0f8189-67c7-4084-9c3e-24fe52742f18/image-20210518-232533.png)![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/e64bf66a-723d-4926-92d8-449a05e501c6/image-20210518-232618.png)
The following is an example of the Response of this Service
{"SC":200,"EC":"0","card":{"pull":{"enabled":true,"network":"Visa","type":"Debit","regulated":false,"currency":"840","country":"840"},"push":{"enabled":true,"network":"Visa","type":"Debit","availability":"Immediate","regulated":false,"currency":"840","country":"840"}},"AVS":{"networkRC":"85","authorizeID":"213435","resultText":"NOT DECLINED","codeAVS":"Y"}}

On the AVS object of the response the codeAVS field is to be noticed
{"SC":200,"EC":"0","card":{"pull":{"enabled":true,"network":"Visa","type":"Debit","regulated":false,"currency":"840","country":"840"},"push":{"enabled":true,"network":"Visa","type":"Debit","availability":"Immediate","regulated":false,"currency":"840","country":"840"}},"AVS":{"networkRC":"85","authorizeID":"213435","resultText":"NOT DECLINED","**codeAVS":"Y"**}}

The following Table Transaltes what is the Expected behaviour of the Platform based on the codeAVS field retrieved
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/a06d96a1-0c65-49e7-8ac7-6a65fa969410/image-20210518-213423.png)
## SACBE Implementation Example
The following CURL command contains an example on how the call to this endpoint needs to be performed with SACBE PAYMENTS account credentials
**Note: the State Parameter is the ISO two letter code for the States in USA -**
IMPORTANT **Pending: need to check how it works with international addresses**

```auto
curl --location --request POST 'https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/v1/clients/zgoQDpQBwOtn81zd1NtX6g/cards?AVS' --header 'Authorization: Bearer v925DarBkDMymU5UQeaCoefEwVuszvPpfBA' --header 'Content-Type: application/json' --data-raw '{
    "card": {
        "accountNumber": "4000056655665556",
        "expirationDate": "203001"
    },
    "owner": {
        "name": {
            "first": "John",
            "last": "Benson"
        },
        "address": {
            "line1": "465 Fairchild Drive",
            "line2": "Suite #222",
            "city": "Mountain View",
            "state": "CA",
            "zipcode": "94043"
        }
    }
}'
```

# Analysis of AVS Results in the Platform

| **Code**<br> | **Visa**<br> | **MasterCard**<br> | **Discover**<br> | **American Express**<br> | **Behaviour in the Platform**<br> | **Sandbox Data To Reproduce** |
| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| ---| --- |
| **Y** | Address & 5-digit or 9-digit ZIP match | Address & 5-digit ZIP match | Address only matches | Address & ZIP match | AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed<br>(Risk assessment: Ok) | any address<br> |
| **A** | Address matches, ZIP does not | Address matches, ZIP does not | Address & 5-digit ZIP match | Address only matches | TBD with Business if transaction should be executed or failed [Judith Tapia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/61e983f108c1f7006a3a64e1?ref=confluence)<br>(Risk assessment: Ok)<br>**Decision:**<br>**AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed**<br> | `"address":{"line1":"123 Main St.","zipcode":"99992"}`<br> |
| **S** | AVS not supported | AVS not supported | AVS not supported | AVS not supported | TBD with Business if transaction should be executed or failed [Judith Tapia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/61e983f108c1f7006a3a64e1?ref=confluence)<br>(Risk assessment: Ok)<br>**Decision:**<br>**AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed** |  |
| **R** | System unavailable, retry | System unavailable, retry | Not applicable | System unavailable, retry | TBD with Business if transaction should be executed or failed [Judith Tapia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/61e983f108c1f7006a3a64e1?ref=confluence)<br>(Riesgos: Bloquear, pero con analysis posterior para mejorar la configuration o generar sistema de alerta)<br>**Decision:**<br>**_AVS NOT PASSED TRANSACTION SHOULD FAIL_** |  |
| **U** | there is no address available for the issuer/issuer processor to match against,<br>or<br>the issuer does not participate in AVS<br>or<br>the card network tried to do a match, but there is no address available to do a match |  |  |  | TBD with Business if transaction should be executed or failed [Judith Tapia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/61e983f108c1f7006a3a64e1?ref=confluence)<br>(Risk assessment: NOT)<br>**Decision:**<br>**_AVS NOT PASSED TRANSACTION SHOULD FAIL_** | `"address":{"line1":"123 Main St.","zipcode":"99990"}`<br> |
| **Z** | Either 5-digit or 9-digit ZIP match, address does not | 5-digit ZIP matches, address does not | 5-digit ZIP matches, address does not | ZIP code only matches | AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed<br>(Risk assessment: ok) | `"address":{"line1":"999 Bad","zipcode":"94043"}`<br> |
| **N** | Neither ZIP nor address match | Neither ZIP nor address match | Neither ZIP nor address match | Neither ZIP nor address match | **_AVS NOT PASSED TRANSACTION SHOULD FAIL_**<br>(Risk assessment: NOT) | `"address":{"line1":"999 Bad.","zipcode":"99992"}`<br> |
| **W** | Not applicable | For U.S., 9-digit ZIP matches, address does not. For non-U.S., ZIP matches, address does not | Information not available | Not applicable | AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed<br>(Risk assessment: Ok) |  |
| **X** | Not applicable | For U.S., all digits match. For non-U.S., ZIP and address match. | Address & 9-digit ZIP match | Not applicable | AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed<br>(Risk assessment: Ok) |  |
| **B** | Address matches, ZIP not verified | Not applicable | Not applicable | Not applicable | TBD with Business if transaction should be executed<br>(Risk assessment: Ok)<br>**Decision:**<br>**AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed** |  |
| **T** | Not applicable | Not applicable | 9-digit ZIP matches, address does not | Not applicable | TBD with Business if transaction should be executed or failed [Judith Tapia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/61e983f108c1f7006a3a64e1?ref=confluence)<br>(Risk assessment: Ok)<br>**Decision:**<br>**AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed** |  |
| **P** | ZIP matches, address not verified | Not applicable | Not applicable | Not applicable | AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed<br>(Risk assessment: ok) |  |
| **C** | Address and ZIP not verified | Not applicable | Not applicable | Not applicable | TBD with Business if transaction should be executed or failed [Judith Tapia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/61e983f108c1f7006a3a64e1?ref=confluence)<br>(Risk assessment: NOT)<br>**Decision:**<br>**_AVS NOT PASSED TRANSACTION SHOULD FAIL_** |  |
| **D** | Address & ZIP match (International only) | Not applicable | Not applicable | Not applicable | AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed<br>(Risk assessment: ok) |  |
| **G** | Address not verified for International transaction (International only) | Not applicable | Not applicable | Not applicable | TBD with Business if transaction should be executed or failed [Judith Tapia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/61e983f108c1f7006a3a64e1?ref=confluence)<br>(Risk assessment: NOT)<br>**Decision:**<br>**_AVS NOT PASSED TRANSACTION SHOULD FAIL_** |  |
| **I** | Address not verified (International only) | Not applicable | Not applicable | Not applicable | TBD with Business if transaction should be executed or failed [Judith Tapia](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/61e983f108c1f7006a3a64e1?ref=confluence)<br>(Risk assessment: NOT)<br>**Decision:**<br>**_AVS NOT PASSED TRANSACTION SHOULD FAIL_** |  |
| **M** | Address & ZIP match (International only) | Not applicable | Not applicable | Not applicable | AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed<br>(Risk assessment: Ok) |  |
| **F** | Address & ZIP match (UK only) | Not applicable | Not applicable | Not applicable | AVS Passed - Transaction should be executed<br>(Risk assessment: Ok) |  |
| **Q** | AVS Not Available | Not applicable | Not applicable | Not applicable | **_AVS NOT PASSED TRANSACTION SHOULD FAIL_**<br>(Risk assessment: NOT) |  |

# Response and Error Codes

The error Status and Error codes should be considered as explained in the following image (blue, status code, pink, error code)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/154248c5-329f-45bf-b1a1-92e669963a16/image-20201104-214113.png)
**Success Example**
{"SC":200,"EC":"0","transactionID":"Rfg2TZZFOG8ZbxL0bIZLKA","network":"Visa","networkRC":"00","status":"COMPLETED","approvalCode":"131523","AVS":{"codeAVS":"Y"},"fees":{"interchange":"0.01","network":"0.00","tabapay":"0.00"}}
SC = Status Code = 200 = Ok - The Transaction is created and the processing is completed
EC = Error Code = 0 = OK
**Error Example**
{"SC":400,"EC":"38110000","EM":"reversal/void"}
Status Code = 400 error
EC = Error Code
EM = Error Message

In the following chart,
*   the GREEN is the only Status indicating the Transaction was completed, this is, it won’t need to be requesting the Transaction Status (call to the Retrieve Transaction API)
*   The PURPLE status require the transaction to be reviewed through the Retrieve Transaction Call.
    *   **important**: this will cause the Operation associated to the Transaction to be re-tried
*   The RED status are Transactions that have error and could not be executed
    *   **important**: this will cause the Operation associated to the Transaction to be canceled and the funds returned to the original cc/dc (using Delete endpoint from TapaPay)
[https://developers.tabapay.net/default.html?v=20.0830](https://developers.tabapay.net/default.html?v=20.0830)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/5f00de70-8c2b-4d2f-943a-a980bcf43031/image-20201109-132949.png)

# Transaction API to Load with Card

## Account Load with Credit / Debit Card
The following steps describe the basic flow that involves how Tabapay interacts our platform to perform a Load to the Wallet:
> a. the user wants to load his wallet with a debit card or a credit card  
> b. The Platforms capture the data of the funding card through the VGS mechanism (PCI) which is tokenized in our end, and stored in VGS vault  
> c. The Platforms create a pull Transaction with Tabapay and the card data is sent in the request through a specific VGS configured outbound route for Tabapay (VGS is the one who actually sends the data)  
> d. When the transaction is completed successfully from TabaPay, (view Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27317](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27317))), it will consider  
> e. The Operation in the Platform associated to the Transaction , which has the information of the user/wallet that originated the transaction in our Platform, Updates thee balance of the Account/Wallet  
> f. The Platform updates the balance to the user of the wallet in the Card Processor, with the information of the load transaction made with the debit credit card, for this the Plattform needs to use the corresponding activity Type for the Card Processor, see activity types page
### **Transaction API**
In order to achieve the completion of the flow to Load funds into the wallet a Pull Transaction needs to be performed with the data of the credit / debit card the user has entered as source of funding.
The API to be used to perform the Transaction is **Transaction API**
[https://developers.tabapay.net/default.html?v=20.0830](https://developers.tabapay.net/default.html?v=20.0830)
*   The basic use for this API are as follows
    *   A transaction is created
    *   Its status checked
    *   The transaction can be reversed/voided
*   The example of the flow provided by the Vendor is detailed as flow number 9 of the reference page: [https://developers.tabapay.net/default.html?v=20.0830](https://developers.tabapay.net/default.html?v=20.0830)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/22e41d57-c4c9-402f-895e-276ad422e5e4/image-20201027-230407.png)

*   
### Example URLS
The call for the Transaction Creation in Sandbox Environment should be :
https://[**api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443**](https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/)/v1/clients/**<ClientID****ISO****\>**/transactions/Create
The call for the Transaction Status should be
https://[**api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443**](https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/)/v1/clients/**<ClientID****ISO****\>**/transactions/Status
### Data for the Transaction
Data to be considered to perform the Create Pull Transaction
**Reference ID:** the Transaction ID of the Operation
**Type**: a fixed four letter code from Tabapay describing the type of transaction: “pull”
**Source Account:** the data of the credit or debit card entered by the user as source of the Load
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/c19b9512-86e9-45a6-ace4-b821a572c1c4/image-20201028-011923.png)
**Destination Account:** the data of the `Settlement AccountID` asociated to the Credentials given by TabaPay (see Credentials section)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/2c87c3a7-bb7d-4be0-9baa-13ca75fdce66/image-20201104-184125.png)
**Amount**: The amount of the Transaction
## Example Create Pull Transaction:
Note he Destination Account field does not corresponds with the type we need to use for the purpose of loading the wallet, but it serves as an overall example. See notes on the Data section

```css
{
  "referenceID": "1",
  "type": "pull",
  "accounts":
  {
    "sourceAccount":
    {
      "card":
      {
        "accountNumber": "9999999999999999",
        "expirationDate": "202012"
      },
      "owner":
      {
        "name":
        {
          "first": "John",
          "last": "Benson"
        },
        "address":
        {
          "line1": "465 Fairchild Drive",
          "line2": "Suite #222",
          "city": "Mountain View",
          "state": "CA",
          "zipcode": "94043"
        },
        "phone":
        {
          "number": "4159808222"
        }
      }
    },
    "destinationAccountID": "TabaPay_AccountID_22-c"
  },
  "amount": "0.10"
}
```

## Notes
The following are notes to the usage of the Transaction API present in the Vendor Site:
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/4751dc10-4525-4617-8dc6-907e6aa6484e/image-20201027-235236.png)

TabaPay advises to Query the source Card , an then perform the Transaction. The Query card has fees to it so this requirement will not be implemented until reviewed or required by Compliance
The Api to be use to Query the card is Card API
[https://developers.tabapay.net/default.html?v=20.0830](https://developers.tabapay.net/default.html?v=20.0830)

# Transaction Status Response Handling

## Context Details

When executing the transactions through the endpiont [transactions](https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/v1/clients/zgoQDpQBwOtn81zd1NtX6g/transactions)
Sandbox Example
[https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/v1/clients/zgoQDpQBwOtn81zd1NtX6g/transactions](https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/v1/clients/zgoQDpQBwOtn81zd1NtX6g/transactions)
**Request**

```css
{"clientID":"zgoQDpQBwOtn81zd1NtX6g","referenceID":"AAAA1234558002","type":"pull","accounts":{"sourceAccount":{"card":{"mobilePay":{},"device":{"id":"GooglePayMasterCard|G600171","blob":""}},"owner":{"name":{"first":"Vanesa","last":"Vilas"},"address":{"line1":"1 Sabre Dr","city":"Cedar Hill","state":"TX","zipcode":"75104"},"phone":{"number":"9166019000"}}},"destinationAccountID":"jkIjHhQESI7mKp0h2Tc9XA"},"amount":"15.00"}
```

**Response**

```json
{"SC":200,"EC":"0","transactionID":"yS0KesNVgctBRotPQzG7aw","status":"ERROR","networkRC":"82"}
```

The current imlementation is only taking in consideration the SC and EC fields of the response

```plain
SC = 200

EC = 0
```

Only these two fields where assumed as a successfull response
But It was identified that also the field Status needs to be handled
`"status":"ERROR",`
Because the current implementation caused some transactions that were actually in Error of Failed in Tabapay, to be completed and reported in the System of Record (Galileo).
Due to this inconsistencies are presented between the money movements and reports for the actual movement by the provider Tabapay, and the Balance of the Account. This discrepancy is called out by the sponsor bank and needs to be fixed
Furhter more, the Transaction Status needs to be handled moving forward to avoid this discrepancies that caused Leap Financial to loose funds.
Tabapay returns information that needs to be handling specifically to be able to determine if the flow continues the transaction, or the transaction should be failed
## What needs to be done
The work to be done is decribed in the following Jira Task = Handle status response field for Tabapay [![](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/images/icons/issuetypes/story.svg)](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2168)[HWAY-2168](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/HWAY-2168) Dev Complete
*   Handle the status field in the response that might have different values
    *   See documentation from Tabapay [https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/transaction-resource-status](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/transaction-resource-status) / [https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/resource-status](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/resource-status)
*   The Status “COMPLETED” is the only one to be considred a successfull transaction
    *   The Transaction Flow needs to be continued
    *   The funds WILL BE LOADED into the User’s Account
*   The Status “UNKNOWN” needs to be followed up by cheking the Transaction Reports from Tabapay,
    *   see response from Tabpaay’s Neera Verma (Technical Lead)
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/5763fd96-d6bc-47bc-9dc4-8afde5245984/image-20230809-155257.png)

*   *   _TBD = what is the implementation needed for this follow up_
*   The Status “ERROR” is a failure status, and should be treated as a Failed Transaction
    *   No further action in the transacion flow needs to be done
    *   The transaction is failed and the funds ARE NOT loaded
    *   **_TBD = specific message that needs to be displayed to the user - This is part of Tabapay suggestions_**
*   The status “FAILED” is a failure status, and should be treated as a Failed Transaction
    *   No further action in the transacion flow needs to be done
    *   The transaction is failed and the funds ARE NOT loaded
    *   **_TBD = specific message that needs to be displayed to the user - This is part of Tabapay suggestions_**

# Tabapay Apple and Google Wallet integration

## General Details
Since 2022 Tabapay enforces the usage of the Digital Wallets (Apple and Google) to allow the programs to be integrated with them as Payment Gateway
Due to this, the current existing integration with Tabapay is obsolete, and a new integration using the standard wallets needs to be set in place

## Technical Details
Integration with Google Pay Tabapay Documentation Site
[https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/googlepay](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/googlepay)
Integration with Apple Pay Tabapay Documentation Site
[https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/apple-pay-1](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/apple-pay-1)

See child pages for the implementation technical details performed for HWAY on January 2022

# Apple's certificates creation

Having created the Merchant account, with a Merchant Id, the following commands must be ran in the OpenSSL command-line:

`openssl ecparam -out private.key -name prime256v1 -genkey openssl req -new -sha256 -key private.key -nodes -out request.csr`
These lines create an ECC-256 bit key pair and the CertificateSigningRequest to upload into Apple’s Certificates section when requesting the Apple Pay Payment Processing Certificate.
Once generated, this certificate is downloaded as `apple_pay.cer`.

The `apple_pay.cer` and `private.key` files is passed to the backend sector.

# Apple's token decryption and processing

**Transformation of the Apple’s certificate and private.key files:**

With the files `apple_pay.cer` and `private.key` obtained from the steps indicated in [https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/1979744257/Apple+s+certificates+creation](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/NUMI/pages/1979744257/Apple+s+certificates+creation), the following commands must be ran to generate the .p12 file with the certificate and the private key in it.
`openssl x509 -inform DER -outform PEM -in apple_pay.cer -out temp.pem`
`openssl pkcs12 -export -out key.p12 -inkey private.key -in temp.pem`

No password is added to the `key.p12`.
The `key.p12` must be installed before the following step.
Next, the following commands must be ran to generate the .pem file with the private key.
`openssl pkcs12 -in key.p12 -out privatePem.pem -nocerts -nodes`

The `key.p12` and the `privatePem.pem` must be added in the “Certificates” folder in the Operation.Worker project.
**Apple’s token Decryption**

With the .p12 and .pem files added to the required folder and the names of those files added in the enviroment configurations, the private key is obtained from the .pem file and the public key is obtained from the “EphemeralPublicKey“ field from the Apple’s token, both as ECParameters.

The key pair is used to obtain the Shared Secret using Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman.
Once obtained the shared secret, it is used, with the merchantID, to restore the symmetric key using the key derivation function.
These two steps are described in the following Apple’s link:
[https://developer.apple.com/documentation/passkit/apple\_pay/payment\_token\_format\_reference/restoring\_the\_symmetric\_key?changes=l\_2](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/passkit/apple_pay/payment_token_format_reference/restoring_the_symmetric_key?changes=l_2)
Once restored the symmetric key, it is used to decrypt the Apple’s token Data field.

For more details about the decryption process, the corresponding Apple’s link is attached below:
[https://developer.apple.com/documentation/passkit/apple\_pay/payment\_token\_format\_reference](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/passkit/apple_pay/payment_token_format_reference)

**Apple’s token processing when integrating with Tabapay**

With the decrypted token, the required values can be obtained and mapped, inside the MobilePay object, as indicated in the following Tabapay’s table.
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/2af5ab99-ed71-42ea-88aa-626a26b60aca/image-20230116-175301.png)
Source: [https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/apple-pay-1](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/apple-pay-1)

In adition to the Apple’s token Data field, the PaymentMethod.TokenData also contains the “network” and the “type“ of the card. These two values also must be passed into Tabapay’s MobilePay object.

**Example of the Postman’s request**
curl --location --request POST '[https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/v1/clients/zgoQDpQBwOtn81zd1NtX6g/transactions'](https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/v1/clients/zgoQDpQBwOtn81zd1NtX6g/transactions') \\
\--header 'Authorization: Bearer v925DarBkDMymU5UQeaCoefEwVuszvPpfBA' \\
\--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \\
\--data-raw '{"referenceID":"aaac331b-1126","type":"pull","accounts":{"sourceAccount":{"card":{"mobilePay":{"accountNumber":"5372534899933821","expirationDate":"202602","cryptogram":"ABwEdoRTNLaKAAbSd4uwAoABFA==","transactionID":"8f2d10589f60392a6bd0b86d68fc3b5db912b287adac7c1ebd901accda36e793","network": "MasterCard","type": "Debit"}},"owner":{"name":{"first":"John","last":"Benson"},"address":{"line1":"465 Fairchild Drive","line2":"Suite #222","city":"Mountain View","state":"CA","zipcode":"94043"},"phone":{"number":"4159808222"}}},"destinationAccountID":"jkIjHhQESI7mKp0h2Tc9XA"},"amount":"20.00"}'

# Google's token encoding and processing

**Google’s token Enconding**

Google’s token Data field must be encoded in Base-64 Url-Safe.

**Google’s token processing when integrating with Tabapay**
With the encoded token, the required values can be obtained and mapped, inside the Device object, as indicated in the following Tabapay’s table.
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/5bf9c5bd-9d38-4b49-a747-d6ac9a5abc8a/image-20230116-181817.png)
Source: [https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/googlepay](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/googlepay)

In adition to the Google’s token Data field, the “Id” value is taken from the enviroment configurations

**_Note: At the moment, this request is not working properly. Waiting for a resolution from the Provider (2023-01-16)_**

**Example of the Postman’s request**

curl --location --request POST '[https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/v1/clients/zgoQDpQBwOtn81zd1NtX6g/transactions'](https://api.sandbox.tabapay.net:10443/v1/clients/zgoQDpQBwOtn81zd1NtX6g/transactions') \\
\--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \\
\--header 'Authorization: Bearer v925DarBkDMymU5UQeaCoefEwVuszvPpfBA' \\
\--data-raw '{"referenceID":"AAAA1234558560","type":"pull","accounts":{"sourceAccount":{"card":{"device":{"id":"GooglePay|G600171","blob":""}},"owner":{"name":{"first":"John","last":"Benson"},"address":{"line1":"465 Fairchild Drive","line2":"Suite #222","city":"Mountain View","state":"CA","zipcode":"94043"},"phone":{"number":"4159808222"}}},"destinationAccountID":"jkIjHhQESI7mKp0h2Tc9XA"},"amount":"11.05"}'

# Mobile Wallet - Apple Pay

Para integrar Apple Pay a h.way, se tuvo en cuenta los siguientes documentos:
[Tabapay Apple Pay](https://developers.tabapay.com/docs/apple-pay)
[Xamarin.IOS ApplePay](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/xamarin/ios/platform/apple-pay)
[Apple Pay](https://developer.apple.com/videos/play/wwdc2022/10041/)
[Apple Pay Support Configuration](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/xcode/configuring-apple-pay-support?changes=_7)
[Apple Pay Decrypter](https://github.com/fscopel/CSharpApplePayDecrypter)

Ademas otra lectura interesante para terminar de entender la integración:
[Apple Pay - Stripe - Xamarin](https://www.mindfiretechnology.com/blog/archive/apple-pay-stripe-xamarin/)

# Mobile Wallet - Google Pay

Para integrar Android Pay a h.way, se tuvo en cuenta los siguientes documentos:
[Tabapay (android pay)](https://developers.tabapay.com/reference/googlepay)
[Google Pay - Paso a paso](https://developers.google.com/pay/api/android/overview)
[Google pay - Tarjetas para test](https://developers.google.com/pay/api/android/guides/resources/test-card-suite)

Ademas otra lectura interesante para terminar de entender la integración:
GooglePay Stripe Xamarin (utiliza Stripe )

Para iniciar la integración de Google Pay en Android se tuvo que configurar el manifest.xml para darle permiso de poder usar google pay:

```haskell
<meta-data
  android:name="com.google.android.gms.wallet.api.enabled"
  android:value="true" />
```

Luego tuvimos que bajar el nuget correspondiente para Xamarin.Android e integrarlo a la parte de Android

```plain
Xamarin.GooglePlayServices.Wallet version(119.1.0)
```

Paso siguiente se construyo el nuevo componente **GooglePaymentButtonRenderer** que es el que se encarga de configurar y levantar **Google Pay** en la app.
Ademas se tuvo que solicitar MarchanInfo que es utilizado para generar el _GooglePaymentRequest_

```plain
MerchantInfo = new MerchantInfo { MerchantName = "XXXXXXXXX", MerchantId = "xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx" },
```

Nota: Para realizas las pruebas con **Google Pay** sin necesidad de ingresar tarjetas verdaderas, se tuvo que unir al grupo de test con el usuario de google que vamos a utilizar en las pruebas ([Join Card Test](https://groups.google.com/g/googlepay-test-mode-stub-data))

# VGS

## General Information
VGS is the Third Party that allows to provide the PCI Compliance handling of the data in the Platform
VGS provides a security vault where the Platform stores in a tokenized manner information such as the user’s accounts (e.g. debit cards, bank accounts) . This way the sensitive information is never stored in the Platform.
VGS provides the means to cofigure inboutnd an outbound “routes” to send the tokenized information to third parties
VGS provides a UI SDK to be able to handle the sensitive information of the debit cards, called VGS-Collect
## Technical Information
To configure VGS Vaults or Routes it is necessary to access the Dashboard
The VGS-Collect information

See also:
*   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27637](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27637))

# SACBE Implementation VGS



# VGS Collect

VGS collect is the component provided by VGS to allow the data capture of credit/debit cards and is inmediatly tokenized and stored in the VGS Vault, allowing to never being handled in clear by the Numi Platform.
The Provider provides different means to use this component, for the Numi Platform the component is used as an SDK for the Mobile Applications
## Documentation from the Provider
[https://www.verygoodsecurity.com/docs/vgs-collect](https://www.verygoodsecurity.com/docs/vgs-collect)

## SACBE Implementation
*   Sacbe implementation Details

*   [Alfredo Vina](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/wiki/people/557058:e0e8fe2e-3f6d-4338-a529-6c028c4a250a?ref=confluence) to provide details of the differences

# VGS Going Live



# Zendesk

Zendesk is one of the support ticketing providers that have available adapters in NUMI Platform

## General Details

Actualmente la conexion a Zendesk tiene dos vias:
1. **Backend -** esta conectado a zendesk directamente por medio de acceso directo con las credenciales provistas para cada implementación
    1. Hay varias entradas de configuracion en los cuales se usan en varios puntos de la plataforma, se debe analizar y actualizar lo necesario para cada implementación
    2. Estas entradas serán las que luego envien mensajes a zendesk for diferentes eventos: ej: errores / mensajes especificos de excepciones / mensajes por determinadas situaciones de usuarios/ETC
2. **Consola -** Ios htmls estáticos de la sección “support” en la APP están apuntando a un servicio expuesto por el IEP de la consola EP. De esta forma la capa de la app movil se conecta con este servicio que luego gestiona la conexión directa como se mencina en el punto 1.

## Implementation Details
Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-28077](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-28077))

Tzune
