# Visa

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/d8dafe50-3939-45b7-8eb0-f2a2aadfbcf6/image-20200730-165718.png)
## General Info
Visa has several APIs to be used eeither as services to obtain certain information (e.g. validate paument methods or provide exchange rates) as well as the execution of specific transactions (visa direct: to deposit money into a credit-debit card using oct payment)

Please see below the list of APIs of this provider currently in use from the Platform
*   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27917](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27917))
*   Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27937](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27937))

## Authentication Details
In order to authenticate there is certain steps that needs to be taken into consideration for each API, please review the following page to get the details
Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27837](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-27837))

## **Environment Information**
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/cdf98752-ce3a-4c87-8383-6b044d5f6481/image-20201124-164627.png)

# Visa APIS Details

**How to Try the APIs**
Visa APIs can be tried through their Sandbox environement by performing the following steps
1) creating a project in Visa Developer [https://developer.visa.com/](https://developer.visa.com/)
2) generating the necessary configuration to obtain the credentials within the project dashboard

**Note**: the access to the current Visa Dashboard for leap financial can be granted by the TA, or Infraestructure team

**How to Promote to PROD**
The APIs need to be promoted to prod through a process that involves filling certain forms and due diligence steps defined in the "going live" section [https://developer.visa.com/pages/going-live#section5](https://developer.visa.com/pages/going-live#section5)

**Base URLS**
*   Sandbox: [https://sandbox.api.visa.com](https://sandbox.api.visa.com) \- used in development / discovery process
*   Certification/test: [https://cert.api.visa.com](https://cert.api.visa.com) \- not needed if not enforced by Visa
*   Production: [https://api.visa.com](https://api.visa.com) \-used for prod

See child pages for each specific API that is integrated / in the process of being integrated

# Foreign Exchange Rate API

This API provides the daily foreing exchange for a given set of origin/destination currency
The configuration of the usage of this API needs to have a Visa Project reflected on Visa developers site, the information for this specific project is the following:
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/bbacbb8d-a843-4eb1-ab54-0e125c92e168/image-20201124-165658.png)
## Overview - Getting Started
[https://developer.visa.com/capabilities/foreign\_exchange/docs-getting-started#section0](https://developer.visa.com/capabilities/foreign_exchange/docs-getting-started#section0)
## API Specification
[https://developer.visa.com/capabilities/foreign\_exchange/reference#tag/Foreign-Exchange-Rates-API/operation/ForeignExchangeRates\_v2 - Latest](https://developer.visa.com/capabilities/foreign_exchange/reference#tag/Foreign-Exchange-Rates-API/operation/ForeignExchangeRates_v2%20-%20Latest)
**Currency Codes**
[https://developer.visa.com/guides/request\_response\_codes#currency\_codes](https://developer.visa.com/guides/request_response_codes#currency_codes)
## Authentication Method
## [https://developer.visa.com/capabilities/foreign\_exchange/docs-authentication](https://developer.visa.com/capabilities/foreign_exchange/docs-authentication)
## Using Two Way SSL
[https://developer.visa.com/pages/working-with-visa-apis/two-way-ssl](https://developer.visa.com/pages/working-with-visa-apis/two-way-ssl)

this keys belong to a configuration for a Project in Visa Development Center that can be accessed by the following users:
*   Claudia Bolaños
*   Vanesa Vilas

# Payment Account Validation API

## General Details
This API provided by VISA is intended to be used to validate the Cards the users input throughout the lifetime in the Platform
This API should be used within the “CardValidation” service within the Platform
QA url for the service:
[https://qa.sacbepayments.com:443/numi/api/pmv/CardValidation](https://qa.sacbepayments.com/numi/api/pmv/CardValidation)
Jira for completing this work : Integrate VISA Payment Account Validation API[https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1860](https://leapfinancial.atlassian.net/browse/SAC-1860)

## Technical Details
API Reference
[https://developer.visa.com/capabilities/pav/reference#pav\_\_pav](https://developer.visa.com/capabilities/pav/reference#pav__pav)

## Authentication Details
Information of the VISA Dashboard Project for this API
[https://developer.visa.com/portal/app/06ac554a-a0cf-4ffd-8001-128c5d01ec8f](https://developer.visa.com/portal/app/06ac554a-a0cf-4ffd-8001-128c5d01ec8f)
**Project Information in Visa Dashboard**
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/80f0ee4d-a038-423d-9bfb-d3cf2b3757e9/image-20201124-170018.png)

# Payment Attributes Inquiry API

This API is provided by Visa to obtain the Attributes of a Payment Method (a Card)

Currently (July 2025) in analysis phase in Sandbox Mode Only

### API Spec
[https://developer.visa.com/capabilities/paai/reference#tag/Funds-Transfer-Attributes-Inquiry-API/operation/Funds%20Transfer%20Inquiry\_v5%20-%20Latest](https://developer.visa.com/capabilities/paai/reference#tag/Funds-Transfer-Attributes-Inquiry-API/operation/Funds%20Transfer%20Inquiry_v5%20-%20Latest)

Name of the Method to be used **Funds Transfer Inquiry**

### URL Endpoint
[https://sandbox.api.visa.com/paai/fundstransferattinq/v5/cardattributes/fundstransferinquiry](https://sandbox.api.visa.com/paai/fundstransferattinq/v5/cardattributes/fundstransferinquiry)

### Authentication

#### Sandbox Credentials
The Authentication for This API is
Two Way SSL

[

developer.visa.com

https://developer.visa.com/pages/working-with-visa-apis/two-way-ssl

](https://developer.visa.com/pages/working-with-visa-apis/two-way-ssl)

The Credentials can be found in the Project in Visa Developer Dashboard (see image below)

See document with process to install the credentials locally
#### Example (from Visa Developer Site) - VISA CARD

Note
the example belongs to the latest version of the API - V5

**Request (body)**

```json
{
  "systemsTraceAuditNumber": "451018",
  "primaryAccountNumber": "4060320000000127",
  "retrievalReferenceNumber": "412770452025"
}
```

**Response**

```plain
{
  "ppgsNetworkInfo": [
    {
      "billingCurrencyMinorDigits": 0,
      "octDomParticipant": "string",
      "octXbParticipant": "string",
      "aftXbParticipant": "string",
      "networkId": 0,
      "aftDomParticipant": "string",
      "cardIssuerCountryCode": 0,
      "billingCurrencyCode": 0
    },
    {
      "billingCurrencyMinorDigits": 0,
      "octDomParticipant": "string",
      "octXbParticipant": "string",
      "aftXbParticipant": "string",
      "networkId": 0,
      "aftDomParticipant": "string",
      "cardIssuerCountryCode": 0,
      "billingCurrencyCode": 0
    }
  ],
  "visaNetworkInfo": [
    {
      "nonMoneyTransferFastFundsDomestic": "string",
      "moneyTransferPushFundsDomestic": "string",
      "nonMoneyTransferPushFundsDomestic": "string",
      "nonMoneyTransferPushFundsCrossBorder": "string",
      "issuerName": "string",
      "moneyTransferFastFundsDomestic": "string",
      "issuerCountryCode": 0,
      "comboCardRange": "string",
      "billingCurrencyCodeMinorDigits": "string",
      "onlineGamblingFastFundsDomestic": "string",
      "onlineGamblingPushFundsDomestic": "string",
      "onlineGamblingFastFundsCrossBorder": "string",
      "billingCurrency": 0,
      "moneyTransferFastFundsCrossBorder": "string",
      "cardPlatformCode": "string",
      "cardSubTypeCode": "string",
      "cardProductType": "string",
      "onlineGamblingPushFundsCrossBorder": "string",
      "moneyTransferPushFundsCrossBorder": "string",
      "nonMoneyTransferFastFundsCrossBorder": "string"
    }
  ]
}
```

# Create Production CSR

#OpenSSL URL [https://www.openssl.org/docs/man1.0.2/man1](https://www.openssl.org/docs/man1.0.2/man1)
## Generate Key Pair with password
openssl genrsa -passout file:pass.txt -out example-key.pem 2048
## Generate Key Pair (password less)
openssl genrsa -out example-key.pem 2048
## Create a new CSR
openssl req -new -sha256 -key example-key.pem -out hwayProd.csr -subj "/CN=[exchangerateservice.h-way.com/OU=HWAY/O=Leap](http://exchangerateservice.h-way.com/OU=HWAY/O=Leap) Financial INC/L=Ashburn/ST=Virginia/C=US/UID=2c173187-648d-4176-88a2-74f39551c25f-PROD"

See attachments for the files created for production

[

developer.visa.com

https://developer.visa.com/pages/working-with-visa-apis/x-pay-token

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/36587559-53c4-4605-a203-c32133ee211d/Paso%201.png)
2\. Import certificate .p12
De acuerdo a la implementación el certificado habrá quedado definido en Operation.Worker/\[DeployNumber\]/ProviderConfigs/VisaDirect.json, como se indican en las llaves `CertificateFile` y `CertificatePassword`.
Crear una copia del certificado la cual se ubica en Operation.Worker/Certificates y cuyo nombre de archivo sea el que se indica en el archivo .json
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/d42a5e7a-78b8-42e1-bb27-bf890594f8cb/Paso%202.png)

Al importar el archivo, le solicitará el password, use el que se indicada en el archivo .json
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/40fa4aba-ea3a-4ef6-8d5e-817359d8fddd/Paso%203.png)

# (Old) Authentication Steps

The Authentication Method is through credentials and a key obtained trhough the processing of a PEM File
When creating a new project into VISA Developer Dashboard, the private key is provided thorugh a PEM file that is offered **only at that moment**
This File is very important now we called key-{HASHTAB}**pem**
This file needs to be saved and used later for the creation of the certificate (see section below: creation of certificate). This is the only opportunity you have the
Within the Dashboard, once the proyect is created, you can review the credentials informations within the Credentials Section.
##   

##   

## Credentials Information in Dashboard
The following Credentials are for Exhcange Rate Feature
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/e283d28c-65db-4ba2-a5cc-c230e8b4589f/image-20201124-165207.png)
### **CREATION OF THE CERTIFICATE**
See the video at:
[https://developer.visa.com/pages/working-with-visa-apis/two-way-ssl](https://developer.visa.com/pages/working-with-visa-apis/two-way-ssl)

Download the certificate file that we called PRIVATE\_CERT.pem
*   Use **Openssl** to execute this command and get the key to connect with VISA's platform
*   Excecute the following command
    *   openssl pkcs12 -export -in cert.pem -inkey key\_a3432b07-48f9-4d4e-a55c-9c77f1a70a03.pem -certfile cert.pem -out myqpp\_keyAndCertBundle.p12
    *   cert.pem → is downloaded here
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/f4d8d533-eb8e-42ea-be46-e609869c39c2/image-20201125-194839.png)

*   *   key\_a3432b07-48f9-4d4e-a55c-9c77f1a70a03.pem → remember; it is downloaded when the project is created
*   When this command is excecuted the Openssl request the password; this password is important to complete the connection; this File ProjectConnectionKEY.p12 is the indicated to stablish a secure channel to VISA.
*   Create this word
*   USER\_ID:PASSWORD => 9YN8FDPNLKR1YY263ZPE21I1eRs3wfe4u5rNByPdVw7\_AKSA8:6Wv97Z4Zyep72Uy3f52Pt49t
*   Now this word needs to be encoded BASE64 and get this:
    *   OVlOOEZEUE5MS1IxWVkyNjNaUEUyMUkxZVJzM3dmZTR1NXJOQnlQZFZ3N19BS1NBODo2V3Y5N1o0WnllcDcyVXkzZjUyUHQ0OXQ=
*   This string to be complete with "Basic" word and be include than header called Authorization like so:

| **Authorization**<br> | **Basic OVlOOEZEUE5MS1IxWVkyNjNaUEUyMUkxZVJzM3dmZTR1NXJOQnlQZFZ3N19BS1NBODo2V3Y5N1o0WnllcDcyVXkzZjUyUHQ0OXQ=**<br> |
| ---| --- |

*   After all this, you can connect correctly with VISA's services

# Wolkvox - Tzune Integration API

### 1\. Using rest services

| **Environment**<br> | **Url**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| Develop | [https://dev.tzune.com](https://dev.tzune.com) |
| QA | [https://qa.tzune.com](https://qa.tzune.com) |
| Pre-producción | [https://preproduccion.tzune.com](https://preproduccion.tzune.com) |
| Production | [https://wolkvox.tzune.com](https://wolkvox.tzune.com) |

### 2\. Authentication
Use Bearer Token Authentication for all endpoints
**ApiKey value**

w4lKB0x#t$vkjd
Headers:

```auto
Authorization: Bearer w4lKB0x#t$vkjd
```

### 3\. Endpoint GetUserRequest
GET /wolkvox/users

| **Parameter** | **Description** | **Data Type** |
| ---| ---| --- |
| MobilePhone | +\[Country code\]\[International calling code\]\[Mobile phone\]<br>For example +5215513109669 | query parameter |

Example:

```auto
GET /wolkvox/users?MobilePhone=%2B5215513109669 HTTP/1.1
Host: dev.tzune.com
Authorization: Bearer w4lKB0x#t$vkjd
```

### 4\. Endpoint GetOperationRequest
GET /wolkvox/operations

| **Parameter** | **Description** | **Data Type** |
| ---| ---| --- |
| Code | Transaction code | query parameter |

Example:

```auto
GET /wolkvox/operations?code=Y4HQEE HTTP/1.1
Host: dev.tzune.com
Authorization: Bearer 
```

### 5\. Endpoint BulkCardRequest
POST /wolkvox/cards

| **Parameter** | **Description** |
| ---| --- |
| UserId | User mobile phone with format +\[Country code\]\[International calling code\]\[Mobile phone\]<br>For example +5215513109669 |
| AccountId | ID\_Cuenta value from Wolkvox ticket |
| CardSubtypeId | ID\_Tipo\_Tarjeta value from Wolkvox ticket |

Example:
Request

```auto
POST /wolkvox/cards/ HTTP/1.1
Host: dev.tzune.com
Authorization: Bearer w4lKB0x#t$vkjd
Content-Type: application/json
Content-Length: 153

{
    "UserId":"+5215515373804",
    "AccountId":"16084fff-c14a-4a04-b4b7-d8fab2310204",
    "CardSubtypeId":"55ceb430-1a49-441b-b263-f8f0a0a4a77b"
}
```

Response Success

```auto
{
    "BulkCardId": "c055f428-1db6-43c4-ab8e-0ec527eb022c"
}
```

Response Error

```auto
{
    "ErrorDescription": "REQUESTED_CARD_ALREADY_EXISTS",
    "ErrorCode": "105"
}
```

| ApiKey | ApiKey | Api-Key JiBrBBg6.Pq4FpxoasxCcU84vDGMgwwle3uDH6cdH |
| Token | Token | 7b6c656170666163746f722d707275656261737d2d7b32303232303131373132313435397d |

| Environment for develop | Environment | Desarrollo |
| Environment for QA | Environment | QA |
| Environment for production | Environment | produccion |

| Description for Open status | OpenStatusDescription | Open |
| Description for Closed status | ClosedStatusDescription | Close |

# Wolkvox modules
Wolkvox separates the tickets in two modules: “Tickets” and “Operaciones”.
The tickets generated during the CIP process will be reported in the “Operaciones” module
## Integration parameters of support tickets

| **Description**<br> | **Parameter name**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| Valid values: produccion, Preproduccion, QA, DEV | Ambiente |
| Mobile user, country code included without + | Mobile |
| User name | Name |
| Last name | Lastname |
| Email | Email |
| Internal description | Internal\_Description |
| Valid values: iOS, Android | Device\_OS |
| Codes according to the support tree | Error\_code |
| Valid values: Open, Closed | Status |
| Operation Code | Transaction\_ID |
| CorrelationId from Operation | Correlation\_ID |
| Title o subject of ticket | Subject |
| Way contact | preferred\_channel |
| Only for TZUNE | ID\_Cuenta |
| Only for TZUNE | ID\_Tipo\_Tarjeta |
| Valid values: Tickets, Cards, Incident, Automatic Report | Ticket\_Type |
| Set value according implementation: Hway, Nomas\_cash | module |

## Integration parameters of Operations

| **Description**<br> | **Parameter name**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| Valid values: produccion, Preproduccion, QA, DEV | Ambiente |
| Mobile user, country code included without + | Telefono |
| User first and middle name | Nombres |
| First last name | Apellido1 |
| Second last name | Apellido2 |
| Email | Correo |
| Userid+reason for the ticket | Titulo |
| Passport,<br>NationalId,<br>StateId,<br>ResidentCard,<br>DriverLicense,<br>SocialSecurityNumber,<br>INE\_IFE,<br>ConsularId,<br>ITIN, | ID |
| Valid value: Hway | Cuenta |
| Valid values: Abierto, Cerrado | Estado |
| Validacion de OFAC<br>Validacion de identidad<br>Validacion de Comprobante de Domicilio<br>Contracargo<br>Error in ID Document - Invalid or manipulated ID detected | Descripcion |
| Set value: Operaciones | module |
| SR<br>SR-W<br>SR-R<br>HardFail<br>OFAC<br>IDCheck<br>SoftFail | Tipo\_de\_ticket<br><br>\*SoftFail added for use case of reminders |

# ZOHO

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/7eb2e055-2fb5-427e-a4e0-5f8146a9ccd0/image-20210802-210843.png)
[https://www.zoho.com/es-xl/](https://www.zoho.com/es-xl/)
**Zoho** es el nombre de un conjunto de [aplicaciones web](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aplicaci%C3%B3n_Web) desarrolladas por la empresa india [Zoho Corporation](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zoho_Corporation), antes AdventNet. Para poder utilizar dichas herramientas es obligatorio registrarse y la gran mayoría cuentan con una versión gratuita. Fue creada en 1996. Las aplicaciones de Zoho se distribuyen como [software como servicio](https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software_como_servicio) (SaaS).
**Zoho Creator** es una plataforma de código bajo que le brinda la capacidad de hacer que la transformación sea rápida, segura y sin problemas, independientemente de la complejidad de sus requisitos y la experiencia en codificación.
[https://www.zoho.com/creator/?src=zoho-home&ireft=ohome&zredirect=f&zsrc=langdropdown&lb=es-xl](https://www.zoho.com/creator/?src=zoho-home&ireft=ohome&zredirect=f&zsrc=langdropdown&lb=es-xl)

# Zoho RESTful API

### 1\. Overview
Zoho Creator's v2 APIs are RESTful APIs that use OAuth 2.0 ⁠— an open standard authorization framework that enables client applications to gain limited access to a user's data without giving away the user's password.
[https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/](https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/)

**_USING THE API_**
**Private (**[**https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-28117**](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-28117)**)**
[https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/oauth-overview.html](https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/oauth-overview.html)

**Private (**[**https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-28157**](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-28157)**)**
*   Request Authorization Code
[https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/authorization-request.html](https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/authorization-request.html)
*   Generate Tokens
[https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/generate-token.html](https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/generate-token.html)
*   Refresh the Access Token
[https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/refresh-the-access-token.html](https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/refresh-the-access-token.html)

**Private (**[**https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-28137**](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-28137)**)**
*   Add Record
[https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/add-records.html](https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/add-records.html)
*   Get Record
[https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/get-records-detail-view.html](https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/get-records-detail-view.html)
*   Delete Record
[https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/delete-specific-record.html](https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/delete-specific-record.html)

# I. OAuth 2.0

**_How OAuth works_**

| Term | Description |
| ---| --- |
| Resource owner or user | An end user who can grant access to your Zoho Creator account's resources |
| Protected resource or scope | The Zoho Creator resource such as the data in your Creator application and its meta-information |
| Client application | A web, mobile or another type of application that requires access to your Creator resources |
| Client ID and client secret | Credentials accorded by Zoho to a client application to authenticate API requests from it |
| Authorization code | A very short-lived code that's accorded to the client application, which can be exchanged for an access token |
| Access token | A short-lived token that allows the client application to access the protected resource |
| Refresh token | A relatively longer-lived token that's used to fetch additional access tokens for the client application |

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/206b0337-a3e0-4721-ba63-e0ca3aa3f114/image-20210729-174731.png)
1. The client application requests authorization to access the protected resource owned by the resource owner.
2. The resource owner first authenticates the client and then authorizes it to access the requested resource by giving it an authorization code. (Zoho Creator uses the _authorization code_ grant type)
3. The client exchanges the authorization code for an access token and optionally a refresh token as well.
4. If its identity and authorization code are authenticated, the client receives the said tokens.
5. The client requests access to the protected resource by presenting its access token.
6. If the access token is valid, the client receives the requested resource.

# II. Authorization

### 1\. Request Authorization Code

This step is done once by the ZOHO Creator admin. Refer to this page for details on how is done:
[https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/authorization-request.html](https://www.zoho.com/creator/help/api/v2/authorization-request.html)

| **Type**<br> | **Self Client (sample)**<br> |
| ---| --- |
| Client ID | 1000.S6BTZ8T5AZHXEIQ9ZJ7XCPKFEKABCD |
| Client Secret | 73f7a8a5393af3f71d515b9292297d8de8b4f5llertr |

| **Code Returned (sample)** |
| --- |
| 1000.bf0e3723d718ac74660843a2128946b8.c6a7d9ce01ea879a22803f5f88bb0a6c |

### 2\. Generate Tokens
Once the client application receives an authorization code, it can exchange for an **access token** by making the following request:
#### Request URL
https://<base\_accounts\_url>/oauth/v2/token?grant\_type=authorization\_code&client\_id=<client\_id>&client\_secret=<client\_secret>&redirect\_uri=<redirect\_uri>&code=<authorization\_code>
#### Request Method
POST
where,

| **Parameter** | **Value** |
| ---| --- |
| base\_accounts\_url | [accounts.zoho.com](http://accounts.zoho.com) |
| client\_id & client\_secret | (refer to point 1) |
| redirect\_uri | [https://www.tzune.com](https://www.tzune.com) |
| code | 1000.bf0e3723d718ac74660843a2128946b8.c6a7d9ce01ea879a22803f5f88bb0a6c (sample) |

| **Sample Response** |
| --- |
| {<br>"access\_token": "1000.8cb99dxxxxxxxxxxxxx9be93.9b8xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxf",<br>"refresh\_token": "1000.3ph66exxxxxxxxxxxxx6ce34.3c4xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxf",<br>"api\_domain": "[https://www.zohoapis.com",](https://www.zohoapis.com%22,)<br>"token\_type": "Bearer",<br>"expires\_in": 3600<br>} |

### 3\. Refresh the Access Token
Access tokens expire in an hour. Client applications must make the following request and use their user's refresh token to generate another access token:
#### Request URL
<https://<base\_accounts\_url>/oauth/v2/token?refresh\_token=<refresh\_token>&client\_id=<client\_id>&client\_secret=<client\_secret>&grant\_type=refresh\_token>
#### Request Method
POST
where,

| **Parameter** | **Value** |
| ---| --- |
| base\_accounts\_url | [accounts.zoho.com](http://accounts.zoho.com) |
| refresh\_token | (refer to point 1) |
| client\_id & client\_secret | [https://www.tzune.com](https://www.tzune.com) |

| base\_url | [creator.zoho.com](http://creator.zoho.com) |
| ---| --- |

| app\_link\_name | tzune |
| report\_link\_name | Orden\_de\_Entrega |

[https://creator.zoho.com/api/v2/zohocreator.admin\_leapfactor/tzune/form/Orden\_de\_Entrega](https://creator.zoho.com/api/v2/zohocreator.admin_leapfactor/tzune/form/Orden_de_Entrega)
**COLLECT CARD IN A MARKET**
#### **Data JSON**
{
"data": {
"Cuenta": "+525523457890",
"Estatus": "No Asignada",
"Fecha\_de\_Entrega\_Programada": "10-Aug-2021",
"Entrega\_de": "4037017000001002011",
"Tipo\_de\_Tarjeta": "4037017000000866007",
"Lugar\_de\_Entrega": "Mercado",
"Mercado": "4037017000001032005",
"Notas": "Generada desde Wolkvox",
"Nombre": "Juan",
"Paterno": "Jimenez",
"Materno": "Solis",
“Ambiente”: “QA”,
“latitudeB”: 19.379,
“longitudB”: -99.219
},
"result": {
"fields": \[
"Cuenta"
\],
"message": true,
"tasks": false
}
}

| **Field**<br> | **Values**<br> | **Description**<br> |
| ---| ---| --- |
| Entrega\_de | 4037017000001002011 | Entrega de Tarjeta fuera de mercado |
|  | 4037017000001002003 | Entrega de Tarjeta en mercado |
|  | 4037017000001002015 | Entrega de contrato en mercado |
|  | 4037017000001002019 | Entrega de contrato fuera de mercado |
| Tipo\_de\_Tarjeta | 4037017000000866003 | Xocol Moderna |
|  | 4037017000000866007 | Xocol Tradicional |
|  | 4037017000000916025 | Poolil Moderna |
|  | 4037017000000873003 | Poolil Tradicional |
| Lugar\_de\_Entrega | Mercado | Para recolección de tarjeta en un mercado |
|  | Domicilio | Para entrega de tarjeta a domicilio |
| Mercado | 4037017000001032011 | Agrícola Oriental |
|  | 4037017000001032017 | Xotepingo |
|  | 4037017000001032005 | Olivar del Conde |

| Dirección\_Línea\_1 |  | Calle y número exterior |
| Dirección\_Línea\_2 |  | Segunda línea de la dirección. Normalmente contiene el Número Interior y la Colonia |
| Ciudad\_/\_Municipio |  | Delegación (Alcaldía), Ciudad, Municipio |
| Estado |  | Estado (entidad Federativa) |
| CP |  | Código Postal |

# Cross River Bank

As per 2024 Cross River Bank will replace MCB as the Sponsor Bank of the Prepaid Programs in Leap Finacial

## Documentation Drive
[https://drive.google.com/open?id=1j2xm-lBZtGf6q4wRrglXxpbTCV7prQkk&usp=drive\_fs](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1j2xm-lBZtGf6q4wRrglXxpbTCV7prQkk&usp=drive_fs)

# CRB Operating System (COS)

The CRB operating system is the bank's system that allows the handling of the program information, managing of the products (such as accounts and cards) creation of accounts, creation of account holder profiles, and all the managing of the information related to both business handled by this bank: Prepaid program and Domestic OCT (also called P2C)

## CRB COS Documentation
Starting up; [https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/CommonTopics/COSProductsAndProductID.htm](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/CommonTopics/COSProductsAndProductID.htm)

[https://docs.crossriver.com/](https://docs.crossriver.com/)

## CRB Explorer
CRB provides the access to a dashboard called Explorer that cointains all the information of account,s customers and transactions -within the main system(\*)-

Access to Explorer in Sandbox
[https://sandbox.crbcos.com/explorer2/core/cm/customers](https://sandbox.crbcos.com/explorer2/core/cm/customers)
The access to this dashboard is handled by the TA

Access to CRB Explorer in PROD
[https://api.crbcos.com/explorer2/auth](https://api.crbcos.com/explorer2/auth)

The Access to this dashborad is handled by Tech Ops lead / CIO

(\*) the transaction in the main system executed within the Explorer or with the Transaction API from COS, not to be mistaken with Transaction from PTPE System (AFT and OCT transactions)

# Authentication RAAS (Payments)

this page aims to have the information layed out ina an orderly fashion for the authentication information for the RAAS project. This project is also called "Payments Program" by the CRB team

## General Authentication Info

**Sandbox - HWAY - Prepaid Program**
*   The IP address whitelisted for your COS and PTPE access is [54.163.142.3/32](http://54.163.142.3/32)
*   Partner ID is
445e45fd-847d-4567-ac3c-b10200e79933
**PROD - HWAY - Prepaid Program**

## COS Credentials
The following credentials must be used for the connection with Cros River Bank Operating System (COS) which are the Core apis for example for Customer Onboarding

### Sandbox Info
**client ID**
MUBbGpBQgVxKsMZ5IgLuemKOh74ympwa
 **client secret**
UtQq1F3sjoezLhE42\_YnqG8fwEU0g6Y6tKJ\_GScZVigQKp2PuGSDp4-Bu5WH\_wCg
**webhook secret** mVuHzO9LVeSepJd5x4Md8ohR/+tNHgOKvZwLXUfJXMkzkxTtu6kYaWRGjJJ/0Metk616p9tkxJorSCD3B4RDzg==

**URL**
To obtain the Bearer Token the URL is =
[https://idptest.crbcos.com/connect/token](https://idptest.crbcos.com/connect/token)

**Example CURL**

```plain
curl --location "https://idptest.crbcos.com/connect/token" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "grant_type=client_credentials" ^
--data-urlencode "client_id=MUBbGpBQgVxKsMZ5IgLuemKOh74ympwa" ^
--data-urlencode "client_secret=UtQq1F3sjoezLhE42_YnqG8fwEU0g6Y6tKJ_GScZVigQKp2PuGSDp4-Bu5WH_wCg" ^
--data-urlencode "audience=https://api.crbcos.com/"
```

## PTPE Credentials
The following credentials must be used for the Push To Pay Express APIs which are the Pay To Card related APIs of the CRB system

### Sandbox Info
Push To Pay Express Staging environment account has been configured and activated.

**client ID**
PTPELEAPStgClient  

**client secret**
9f103c1fe8444248a962a2164af00a34

**Program ID** _(used in "sourceSenderId" field in certain APIS)_
5D6B977B-1BD6-4D82-B408-B10500A865E6

**URL**
The URL to obtain the bearer token is
[https://oauthtest.crbnj.net/connect/token](https://oauthtest.crbnj.net/connect/token)

**Example CURL**

```plain
curl --location "https://oauthtest.crbnj.net/connect/token" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" ^
--data-urlencode "grant_type=client_credentials" ^
--data-urlencode "client_id=PTPELEAPStgClient" ^
--data-urlencode "client_secret=9f103c1fe8444248a962a2164af00a34" ^
--data-urlencode "audience=https://api.crbcos.com/"
```

# Web Hooks

In order to obtain the statuses of the different processes ran within the CRB system it is necessary to implement mechanism to listen to the Events that CRB exposes after processing a request (e.g. after a reuest to onboard a customer, a series of events are triggered in a specific web hook)

The information of the Web Hooks available overall can be reviewed in the folowing links

**For COS**
[https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Banking/Webhooks/WebhookReference.htm](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Banking/Webhooks/WebhookReference.htm)

**For PTPE**
[https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Payments/CardPayments/P2C\_API/Webhooks/P2CWebhooksEventRegistration.htm](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Payments/CardPayments/P2C_API/Webhooks/P2CWebhooksEventRegistration.htm)

## How to register for a web hook for COS
see below the information provided by CRB with instructions for event registration
## **Event registration**
To keep informed of the status of relevant activity in our system, we offer many event notifications for each product. There are several ways to receive event notifications:
*   Push
*   Webhook events that are reported to a registered partner endpoint (a callback URL).
*   Poll
*   Events that are reported after a partner polls the events API.
**Note**
The `POST` calls for the registration methods are the same. Make sure to select the correct registration `type`, either `Push` or `Poll`. We recommend that you use Push registration.
### **Register for webhook event reports (Push)**
Use `POST /webhooks/v1/registrations` to register the callback URLs where event reports should be delivered.
In this example, your `partnerId` identifies you as the entity requesting webhook registration to the `eventName: Core.Account.Opened`, which is a `Push` registration. The `eventName` is being registered to the `callbackUrl:`[`https://cos.yourcompanysite.com/account-events`](https://cos.yourcompanysite.com/account-events).

**Sample webhook push registration**

```json
{
  "partnerId": "8gj76s99-jhso-89as-nsl8-119nss8ch7ab",
  "eventName": "Core.Account.Opened",
  "type": "Push", 
  "callbackUrl": "https://cos.yourcompanysite.com/account-events",
  "authUsername": "{userName}",
  "authPassword": "{password}",
  "format": "Basic"
}
```

**Important**
To confirm your callback URL is registered, call `PUT /v1/registrations{id}/ping`. It simulates a webhook event sent to your callback URL but doesn't include any resources.

## How to Register for a Web hook for PTPE
See the documentation in the following link [https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Payments/CardPayments/P2C\_API/Webhooks/RegisterForWebhookEvents.htm](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Payments/CardPayments/P2C_API/Webhooks/RegisterForWebhookEvents.htm)

# Web hooks for Customer Onboarding

In order to obtain the events after a request for customer onboarding the following needs to be done:
**1 ) Register de WebHooks**
The following webhooks are required for any program involving customer onboarding, such as BaaS and Card programs:

Core.Customer.Onboarded
Core.Customer.Ofac.Changed
Core.Customer.PepScan.Changed
Core.Customer.Address.Added
Core.Customer.Address.Changed
Core.Customer.Address.Removed
Core.Customer.BeneficialOwner.Added
Core.Customer.BeneficialOwner.Changed
Core.Customer.BeneficialOwner.Removed
Core.Customer.Email.Added
Core.Customer.Email.Changed
Core.Customer.Email.Removed
Core.Customer.Identification.Added
Core.Customer.Identification.Changed
Core.Customer.Identification.Removed
Core.Customer.Name.Changed
Core.Customer.Phone.Added
Core.Customer.Phone.Changed
Core.Customer.Phone.Removed
Core.Customer.Profile.Changed

# Registered Events QA

"CrossRiverApi.CrossRiverManager.WebhookEvent ->"

# Registered Events Certification DEV

Registered events for Sandbox using Dev Environment Callback as per 24th May 2024
**IMPORTANT Note:**
The highlited in RED have been deleted and added to the new URL
In order to add the rest to the new URL for the actual integration the ones highlited in YELLOW need to be deleted using the endpoint

# Onboard Customers on CRB system

As part of the integration with CRB the Customer Onboarding needs to be performed when a new user is created in the Numi Platform
When the user is created, before continuing with the creation of the account with the processor, Galileo, it is necesary to onboard the customer in CRB System and obtain the CLEAR status for that customer.
In order to onboard the customer the customer endpiont needs to be used from CRB System, see documentation below
[https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Banking/CustomerManagement/CustMgtAPIs.htm#OnboardCust](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Banking/CustomerManagement/CustMgtAPIs.htm#OnboardCust)

```json
{
```

To Consider the the Customer succesfully onboarded, the events need to be processed to obtain the result of the process, see Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-33317](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-33317)) and Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-33377](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-33377))

# OFAC and PEP status handling

When the Customer is Onboarded, the OFAC validations trigger in CRB systems
The WebHooks `Core.Customer.Ofac.Changed` and `Core.Customer.Pep.Changed`will then repor the status of the SCAN, within seconds

Examples of Events
**OFAC scan**

```plain
{\"Id\":\"3105eafd-c130-43e1-96af-b186014f7357\",\"EventName\":\"Core.Customer.Ofac.Changed\",\"Status\":\"Pending\",\"PartnerId\":\"445e45fd-847d-4567-ac3c-b10200e79933\",\"CreateAt\":\"\\/Date(-62135596800000-0000)\\/\",\"LastAttemptedAt\":\"\\/Date(-62135596800000-0000)\\/\",\"Resources\":[\"core/v1/cm/customers/09b59cac-168e-4746-b9c5-b186014f5248\"],\"Details\":[{\"customerId\":\"09b59cac-168e-4746-b9c5-b186014f5248\",\"ofacBefore\":\"Pending\",\"ofacAfter\":\"Failed\"}]}
```

**PEP scan**

```plain
\"Id\":\"d2628293-38bc-4639-9d4d-b186014f7357\",\"EventName\":\"Core.Customer.PepScan.Changed\",\"Status\":\"Pending\",\"PartnerId\":\"445e45fd-847d-4567-ac3c-b10200e79933\",\"CreateAt\":\"\\/Date(-62135596800000-0000)\\/\",\"LastAttemptedAt\":\"\\/Date(-62135596800000-0000)\\/\",\"Resources\":[\"core/v1/cm/customers/09b59cac-168e-4746-b9c5-b186014f5248\"],\"Details\":[{\"customerId\":\"09b59cac-168e-4746-b9c5-b186014f5248\",\"pepScanBefore\":\"Pending\",\"pepScanAfter\":\"Clear\"}]}
```

The platform needs to handle this status to be able to determine if the onboarding is succesfull:
The fields to analyze are =
`"pepScanAfter\":\"Clear\"`
`"ofacAfter\":\"Failed\`
**Clear** \= means the onboarding is successfull, the account creation process can continue
**Pending or Reviewing** = means the onboarding is being reviewed, the account creation is to be put on hold until we get a definitive status
**Postitive** \= means the onboarging is failed due to a hit in the lists, the account creation is DENIED to the user, so the account will not be created and the user should not be provided service
**Failed** \= means the onboardng is failed due to lack of data to analyze the information in the lists, as of August 2024 the action will be the account will not be created and the user should not be provided service, TBD with the bank if this status is definitve or may change

Below is the CRB documentation and detail on how to handle the results of the OFAC Scan inthe web hook

1. The webhook event `Core.Customer.Ofac.Changed` is triggered and returns an initial status of Clear or Failed.
2. If the status is Clear, you can approve and create the customer credit card facility on your side.
3. If the status is Failed, the CR AML team investigates the case. The webhook event is triggered again, sending a status of Reviewing or Pending, indicating that a person is reviewing the case. This can take up to 48 hours. Very rarely, the CR team might reach out to you to ask for additional supporting documents. You will need to provide an email address for this. The `Core.Customer.Ofac.Changed` webhook returns any change in status, not just Reviewing or Pending.
4. After reviewing, the CR team moves the status to either Clear or Positive. The webhook event fires again.
    *   If Clear, you can continue with approving and creating a credit card facility for your customer.
    *   If Positive, you must decline the application. This is quite rare.
5. To trigger an OFAC/PEP failure in the Sandbox use these values in your call:
    *   For PEP, in the `name` object use the name Vladimir Putin to get a hit.
    *   For OFAC, in the `name` object use the name Francisco Javier Arellano Félix; and in the `profile` object use the birthdate 1969-12-11 (December 11, 1969)
    *   For OFAC entity, in the `name` object use the `entityName` value Blue Star Diamond
6. Once the customer record is cleared, there is no longer a reason to subscribe to the specific customer webhook.
    *   Cross River scans customer records nightly for OFAC/PEP. If a customer hits OFAC during a nightly scan, our AML team will reach out via Zendesk to help clear.
7. You must keep the customer records you create up to date.
    *   If a customer changes their name, address, email, or other details, use the Customer Management APIs to update their record.
    *   You can make this part of a nightly process.
8. The scans take less than a second. Anything that needs manual review is taken care of within 3 business days.
9. Status Definition Changes (applies to OFAC, PEP, and Internal List). Webhook event responses from this point on mean:
    *   Positive: Must be rejected (no change)
    *   Clear: Everything is fine (no change)
    *   Pending: The case is being reviewed (no change)
    *   Reviewing: A potential match within a category is being reviewed by CR and is pending a disposition. Previously this was Failed.
    *   Failed: There isn’t enough information to know the status (for example, RFI not responded to).
See [How to onboard a customer](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Tutorials/CustomerManagementTutorials/HowToOnboardACustomer.htm).

# Update CRB Customer information

**General**
to comply with certain requirements from compliance from CRB it is necessary to implement the ability to update certain customer information in CRB Core system once the user has been onboarded succesfully
In the event the user wishes to change some of their data, the Platform will need to implement the means to invoke the corresponding CRB methods of their APIs to update or add information fo the CRB Customer Profile

**Note from TA:** as per 2026 it is required to have the ability to update dhe following fields
Email (already implemented)
Address (to be implemented in Beta)

## Technical Details
**Change Email**

**Change Address**

It is necessary to use the **addresses** method including the id of the customer provided in the CRB onboarding for the user

Example of the method call in sandbox
[https://sandbox.crbcos.com/core/v1/cm/customers/{CUSTOMER ID FROM CRB}/addresses](https://sandbox.crbcos.com/core/v1/cm/customers//502d4ff3-69d0-47a1-91ad-b1db01548470/addresses)

**Example CURL Request**

```rust
curl --location 'https://sandbox.crbcos.com/core/v1/cm/customers//502d4ff3-69d0-47a1-91ad-b1db01548470/addresses' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: ••••••' \
--data '{
  "addressType": "Work",
  "classification": "Business",
  "isPrimary": true,
  "street1": "400 Kelby St",
  "city": "Fort Lee",
  "state": "NJ",
  "postalCode": "07024",
  "countryCode": "US"
}'
```

**Example Response**

```json
{
    "id": "980ee55a-8dc2-43be-8c15-b3e801468622",
    "customerId": "502d4ff3-69d0-47a1-91ad-b1db01548470",
    "isPrimary": true,
    "status": "Active",
    "classification": "Business",
    "addressType": "Work",
    "street1": "400 Kelby St",
    "city": "Fort Lee",
    "state": "NJ",
    "postalCode": "07024",
    "countryCode": "US",
    "createdAt": "2026-02-05T14:48:50.2418964-05:00",
    "lastModifiedAt": "2026-02-05T14:48:50.2418964-05:00",
    "partnerId": "445e45fd-847d-4567-ac3c-b10200e79933"
}
```

# Create an Account for the users in CRB System

## General Details
The CRB provides a series of mechanisms to be able to create accounts to be used directly in integration with their systems.

The creation of accounts will be used with the programs that need to create a bank account for the users to handle a representation of a virtual account.

Conceptually, each user account is a portion (commonly named sub-ledger) of the whole program account (commonly named master account)

The master account (leap financial's account) will have an implicit sub-ledger to reflect the total balance, and then the each user will have a corresponding subledger with the balance individually handled

See the following graphic
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/7fc66e4c-3371-4f94-8b81-ff176ceea940/image.png)

## Integration
There is a specific set of steps that need to be taken in place to create an account for a customer
1. as a Pre-requisite the master account needs to be created with the Leap Financial details with the specific product ID provided by CRB
specifically for the program
1. The sub-ledgers will be crated for each customer **that have completed the onboarding process succesfully**. See= Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-33277](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-33277))
2. The account number provided in the cration of the sub-ledger, needs to be associated with the **Virutal Account created in the platform**
## CRB Documentation for Accounts

## CRB Documentation for Sub-Ledgers
[https://docs.crossriver.com/accounts/subledgers](https://docs.crossriver.com/accounts/subledgers)

## Technical Details
**Accounts API**
[https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Banking/AccountManagement/AccountAPIs/AccountManagementAPIs.htm#Open](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Banking/AccountManagement/AccountAPIs/AccountManagementAPIs.htm#Open)

**Subledger API**
[https://docs.crossriver.com/apis/subledgers#X\_Qgb](https://docs.crossriver.com/apis/subledgers#X_Qgb)
Old URL (Changed in August 2024)
[https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Banking/Subledgers/SubledgersAPIsBanking.htm](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Banking/Subledgers/SubledgersAPIsBanking.htm)

## Examples
User Sub-Account Creation
**Request**

```json
{
    "masterAccountNumber": "2791208180", //master account: leap financial deposit personal (in sandbox)
    "title": "Leap Financial Personal Checking Account - subdledger", // title in master account
    "customerId": "5545ec14-318a-4081-a527-b1db016f80f2", //customer id created in the onboarding
    "beneficiary": {
        "referenceId": "123456789-006", //reference id in our platform (user id in raas)
        "entityName": "Lionel Andres Messi Cuchitini" // full name of the sender
    }
}
```

Response

**Data to Consider**
**masterAccountNumber** and title will be fixed values (general configuration values) that correspond to the information of the Master Account created previously. All the costumers of the same program might have the same masterAccountNumber
the **title** is a fixed string value that will be displayed in the COS explorer, to be confirmed what is needed / desired in this field, for testing purposes use the one in the example
the **customerId** field is the CRB identifier provided in the onboarding of the user
the **beneficiary** object needs to have information about the user, it might be used to link the information with the information of th user in the platform

# Register Cards for Transactions with CRB

## General Details
The first step to execute a transaction with cards either
Push Funds to a Card= OCT Transaction
Pull Fundos from a card = AFT Transaction

Is register a card in CRB System
This method will allow to verify if the card is valid and ill provide a Token to execute the actual transaction.
The token allows to handle an identifier for the card already validated and registered in CRB system, in all the transactions executed with CRB using the card.

The following is an example request from Sandbox that will register the card using the VGS token(\*) information instead of the clear data
**Request**

```json
{
    "requestId": "d21daa6f-76a7-4422-b4c2-cbe306b81433",
    "firstName": "Vanesa Viviana",
    "lastName": "Vilas",
    "sourceSenderId": "5D6B977B-1BD6-4D82-B408-B10500A865E6",
    "creditCardNumber": "tok_sandbox_918vLyap8ZnLSVzZS8cUEa_9905",
    "expirationMonth": "02",
    "expirationYear": "25",
    "cvv": "tok_sandbox_cZT2PGbmnEPL4Se1gyJDZb"
}
```

**Response**

```plain
{
    "version": "1.0.0.0",
    "result": {
        "requestId": "d21daa6f-76a7-4422-b4c2-cbe306b81433",
        "cardToken": "TJQ8RRLI9C1VT0EF1X2CZXPKZDN71TGDJ298O1",
        "firstName": "Vanesa Viviana",
        "lastName": "Vilas",
        "ownerExternalId": null,
        "address1": null,
        "address2": null,
        "city": null,
        "zipCode": null,
        "isAuthorizationSucceeded": true,
        "state": null,
        "countryCode": null,
        "phoneNumber": null,
        "email": null,
        "expirationYear": "25",
        "expirationMonth": "02",
        "last4Digits": "9905",
        "cardCompany": "MasterCard",
        "isActive": true,
        "deactivatedAt": null,
        "addedOn": "2024-08-29T16:54:09.8355667Z",
        "authorizationJson": "{\r\n  \"RequestId\": \"d21daa6f-76a7-4422-b4c2-cbe306b81433\",\r\n  \"Authorized\": true,\r\n  \"ResponseReceived\": true,\r\n  \"ResponseCode\": \"OK\",\r\n  \"ResponseDescription\": \"OK\",\r\n  \"Timestamp\": \"2024-08-29T16:54:09.8315449Z\",\r\n  \"Error\": null,\r\n  \"AddressVerified\": true,\r\n  \"Rail\": \"mcSend\",\r\n  \"CardType\": \"debit\",\r\n  \"IsFastFundsSupported\": true,\r\n  \"PushEnabled\": true,\r\n  \"PullEnabled\": true,\r\n  \"IssuerCountryCode\": \"US\",\r\n  \"NameVerificationUsed\": \"Not Supported\",\r\n  \"NameVerificationResult\": \"Unknown\",\r\n  \"ProductType\": \"unknown\"\r\n}",
        "isFastFundsSupported": true,
        "rail": "McSend",
        "pushEnabled": true,
        "pullEnabled": true,
        "issuerCountryCode": "US",
        "binNumber": "510258",
        "productType": "Unknown"
    },
    "isSuccessfull": true,
    "isSuccessful": true
}
```

(\*)The VGS token provided by the UI that has captured the card data trough VGS Collect UI. This is part of the platform's VGS integration to guarantee the PCI complicance. For more information see Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-23677](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-23677))

## Data to Consider for Validations
The following information is to be considered in the card registration for the different validations, this information is based on the fields available in the response, explaining in each case how this will impact in the validation

"**isAuthorizationSucceeded**": true,
this field will indicate overall if the card is valid to execute transactions. **_When false,_** the card is not valid overal and no other execution of validation need to be considered.

The following fields are information to be considered **_to be stored_** for destition-making

```json
        "expirationYear": "25",
        "expirationMonth": "02",
```

TBD if the card is to **close to expire** if it should be considered valid

```json
       "last4Digits": "9905",
        "cardCompany": "MasterCard",

        "isActive": true,
```

The **card company** and **the last 4 digits** will be userd to identify the card in the system
**IsActive** \= TBD scenario if the card is not active = should **not be** considered valid
### authorizationJson Object
This object will contain the full information of the authorization provided by all the services that are run in the validation of the Card, in the networks and in CRB internally, the format of the object is the following

"**authorizationJson**":

```json
{
  "RequestId": "d21daa6f-76a7-4422-b4c2-cbe306b81433",
  "Authorized": true,
  "ResponseReceived": true,
  "ResponseCode": "OK",
  "ResponseDescription": "OK",
  "Timestamp": "2024-08-29T16:54:09.8315449Z",
  "Error": null,
  "AddressVerified": true,
  "Rail": "mcSend",
  "CardType": "debit",
  "IsFastFundsSupported": true,
  "PushEnabled": true,
  "PullEnabled": true,
  "IssuerCountryCode": "US",
  "NameVerificationUsed": "Not Supported",
  "NameVerificationResult": "Unknown",
  "ProductType": "unknown"
}
```

Below is the table with the specific information of this object to consider, including CRB Technical team advise in green

| **3)  The data of the authorizationJson object should be as follow to be considered a valid card?** |  |
| ---| --- |
| [Definitions of fields obained from =](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Payments/CardPayments/P2C_API/TheCardObject/CardManagementAPIs.htm#addACard) [https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Payments/CardPayments/P2C\_API/TheCardObject/CardManagementAPIs.htm#addACard](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Payments/CardPayments/P2C_API/TheCardObject/CardManagementAPIs.htm#addACard) |
| **authorized** boolean |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Definition = Determines whether a card is authorized for any financial activity by the Cross River/card network and issuing bank |  |
| Value = Should be True for a Card to be Valid |  |  |  |  | CRB: yes |
| **responseReceived** boolean |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Definition = True if P2C received a response from the external processor, otherwise false |  |
| Value=true | if  _authorized_ \= true, no need to consider this field |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| **responseCode** string |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Definition = A 2-digit code signifying the transaction result (approved or decline). For example, 00 means successful. |  |
| Should be 00 for a Card to be Valid |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| 00 | if  _authorized_ \= true, no need to consider this field |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| **ResponseDescription** string |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Definition = A string describing the transaction result. For example, Approved. |  |  |
| Should be Approved a Card to be Valid |  |  |  |  |  |
| Approved | if  _authorized_ \= true, no need to consider this field |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| **Error** string |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Definition = A code value returned if the network call fails |  |  |  |  |  |
| Error will trigger to call the API again |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | CRB: The recommendation is to call the API again, as the error represents problems calling the card networks, and not an indication of the card's validity state. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| **AddressVerified** boolean |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Definition = The result of the check by the address verification service (AVS). |  |  |
| This elements serves as a desition element but does not define if the card is valid or not. See: [https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Payments/CardPayments/P2CGuides/FraudDetectionServices/AVS.htm](https://docs.crossriver.com/Content/Payments/CardPayments/P2CGuides/FraudDetectionServices/AVS.htm) |  |  |  | Therefore, for AFTs, it is highly recommended. |
|  | CRB: This does not indicate if a card is valid, but is an additional security indication for the card verification. The merchant can decide whether to mandate AVS verification for triggering transactions. It is often mandated for AFTs (pulls) as it also has financial cost reduction and chargeback prevention. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| **pushEnabled =** boolean |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| True if push payment to card is enabled, otherwise false |  |  |  |
| Should be True in our scenario for a card to be valid to perform P2C |  |  |
|  | CRB: Correct, for push - OCT |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| **nameVerificationUsed** string |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Definition = Indicates if Cross River tried to contact the account name inquiry (ANI) service: |  |
| Used |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Not used |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Not supported |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| If the merchant isn't configured for the ANI service, this response lets you know. In that case, contact your Cross River relationship manager for information on connecting to ANI. |
| The status of the name verification service will be used to decide if the card is valid, this is dependent of a definition from Risk area in Leapfinancial<br> |
|  | CRB: This is good security information, but since not all issuers participate yet, there will be many cases where the result is not used or unknown, and the card will still be valid. |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| **nameVerificationResult** string |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Definition = This is the result of the account name inquiry (ANI) service check: |  |  |
| Full match |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Partial match |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
|  | match |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| No match |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| Unknown |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |
| The status of the name verification service will be used to decide if the card is valid, this is dependent of a definition from Risk area in Leapfinancial |  |

## Feature fields

```json
        "isFastFundsSupported": true,
        "rail": "McSend",
        "pushEnabled": true,
        "pullEnabled": true,
        "issuerCountryCode": "US",
        "binNumber": "510258",
```

The following needs to be taken in consideration for this fields
*   **BinNumber** = is the bin of the card, it should be stored in the platform to allow the desition making process / to validate if the bin is valid for certain transactions or not
*   **PushEnabled** = will indicate if the card is "good" to perform OCT Transactions
*   **issuerCountryCode** = when "different" than US, this, plus the pushEnabled = true, will indicate that the card is good for Cross Border OCT

*       *   This affirmation was provided by CRB technical team and is yet to be confirmed by business

*   **isFastFundsSupported** = this will indicate if the card is allowed for fast funds OCT Transactions
*   **PullEnabled** \= will indicate if the card is "good" to perform AFT Transactions

## How to Trigger ANI / AVS

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/e39ceafd-656d-4956-a9c6-9ac6465597d4/image.png)

# AVS and ANI handling

## Details

The CRB Add Card feature returns certain infromation related to AVS = Address Verification Service and ANI= Account Name Inquiry validations

AVS validates that the address provided as part of the card registration data in CRB system, matches with the registered as address associated to the card in the network's records.

This is a service provided by Visa

TBD if it works for mastercards cards

When the card has a succesful AVS validation, it will return true, false otherwise

ANI validates that the name provided as part of the card registration data in CRB system, matches the information registered as card holder name associated to the card in the network's records.

This is a service provided by Visa

TBD if it works for mastercards cards

Since not all issuers support ANI yet, the response of the Add Card feature will inform if the ani validation is supported and hence, was executed (used/ not used/ not supported ), and if executed, the type of match (full match / partial match / no match) or uknown if not supported or not used

See the actual definition of the result in =[](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/v/dc/15tww-59737/15tww-35317?block=block-27c6042a-592b-40ce-91d1-1c52c00441bb)
## Business Logic up to November 2024
The Address Verification Will Return true if it was executed or false if it was not executed
For the time being, with a **AVS=False** Result, the card needs to be considered "Not Valid". In a further release the user will be required to input another address (the billing addresss related to the card being used), but this feature is not yet defined in fully.
If the **AVS** returns **True**, then the logic for the ANI analysis can take place
The Account Naming Inquiry (ANI) will be handled by CRB in two fields, one indicating wheter or not it was supported and executed, and one returning the result
If the **nameVerificationUsed** is **Used** and the **nameVerificationResult** is **Full Match or Partial Match**, then the Card can be considered **Valid**
If the **nameVerificationUsed** is **Used** and the **nameVerificationResult is No Match** the card will **NOT BE Valid**
If the **nameVerificationUsed** is **Not Used or Not Supported**, then the **NameVerificationResult** will always be **Unknown**. If this is the situation, **a ticket** must be triggered to operations/customer supoprt, **and other mechanisms will need to be used** to determine the validity of the card.

For the full card validation process see = Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-39237](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-39237)) Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-39617](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-39617))

## Original Requirement:
Below table displays the business requirement for the logic to be implemented to analyze and trigger the different card validation results, depending on the AVS / ANI results provided in the Register Card CRB Featureprovided in the Register Card CRB Feature

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/c7c6f091-dec2-4ec4-8136-82496a718bce/image.png)

# Card Validation Process for AFT

The process of the card validation will be changed with the integration of CRB as the provider of the information of the card

After the **Add Card Process**, and after determining the card is **Accepted** (see Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-35317](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-35317)) )
the elements of the Response of CRB's Add Card API need to be handled to analyze all the elements to identify wheter or not a card is Valid **wheter or not a card is Valid for AFT**

**Elements provided by CRB Response**
*   Determination if the card is allowed for PULL
*   AddressVerification Result
*   ANI Result

Together with this elements, this process will include the in-house features for validations in certain scenarios

**Elements of In-house features**
*   Validate the **name of the card with Levenshtein algoritm**
*   Perform a micro **deposit and request the valid amount** to be entered by the user

If the Card undergoes the validation process succesfully, the Platform should mark the Card as "Validated for AFT"

*   The following graphic depicts the process to validate the card with the information retrieved by CRB Add Card API plus the in-house features **when the card is going to be used for AFT Transactions**

Note: in a future release, this process will be enriched with the handling of the Billing Address
**Implemented in Release 1.12**

**Enhacement for Process refining ANI result with Legal Name (October 2025)**
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/3e9746ed-5d01-43d8-8cba-8200fa90ad17/Leap%20Financial%20AFT%20and%20OCT%20Overall%20Process-CRB%20OCT%20Card%20Provisioning_updated.jpg)

**Process with Billing Address Handling**

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/34c4f46e-99ec-43b9-8b1c-42bea2af3ee0/image.png)

**Original Process withouth billing Address**

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/520d106f-f111-47de-9642-c8e22f253f2c/image.png)
Link to Diagram = [https://app.diagrams.net/#G1FbOa1i9O4\_qeb63Xg05VdleX34AiVnIX#%7B%22pageId%22%3A%22FdQLj6HTr0sgT2ZLEf-k%22%7D](https://app.diagrams.net/#G1FbOa1i9O4_qeb63Xg05VdleX34AiVnIX#%7B%22pageId%22%3A%22FdQLj6HTr0sgT2ZLEf-k%22%7D)

# Card Validation Process for OCT

The process of the card validation will be changed with the integration of CRB as the provider of the information of the card

After the **Add Card Process**, and after determining the card is Accepted (see Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-35317](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-35317)) )
the elements of the Response of CRB's Add Card API need to be handled to analyze all the elements to identify **wheter or not a card is Valid for OCTs**

**Elements provided by CRB Response**
*   Determination if the card is allowed for Push
*   AddressVerification Result
*   ANI Result

Together with this elements, this process will include the in-house features for validations in certain scenarios

**Elements of In-house features**
*   Get the card capabilites and validate the specifics for OCT ~~cross border~~ **~~using the Bin Lookup service from the legacy provider = Cybersource~~** **_this step was eliminated due to Cybersource not working properly in July 2025_**
*   Validate the **name of the card with Levenshtein algoritm**
*   ~~Perform a micro~~ **~~deposit~~** ~~and request the valid amount to be entered by the user~~ _thjis step was eliminated due to the inability of CRB to provide a syncrhonic respoinse ont he transaction API_

If the Card undergoes the validation process succesfully, the Platform should mark the Card as "Validated for OCT"

For Clarification Reasons, as per July 2025 the Validation Process roughly works as follows
*   The AVS response is IGNORED for the OCT Destination Cards
*   The Card has a Valid ANI response or a Valid In-house levenshtein verification

*   The following graphic depicts the process to validate the card with the information retrieved by CRB Add Card API plus the in-house features **when the card is going to be used for OCT Transactions**

**Enhacement for Process refining ANI result with Legal Name (October 2025)**
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/88f4ff76-20fd-4601-ab23-89aeb93974d0/Leap%20Financial%20AFT%20and%20OCT%20Overall%20Process-CRB%20OCT%20Card%20Provisioning_updated.jpg)
**Original Process**

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/b8d1a3b5-b449-4a8b-bf1a-3bebb1d47927/Leap%20Financial%20AFT%20and%20OCT%20Overall%20Process-CRB%20OCT%20Card%20Provisioning%20(2).jpg)

Original Diagram Source = [https://app.diagrams.net/#G1FbOa1i9O4\_qeb63Xg05VdleX34AiVnIX#%7B%22pageId%22%3A%22FdQLj6HTr0sgT2ZLEf-k%22%7D](https://app.diagrams.net/#G1FbOa1i9O4_qeb63Xg05VdleX34AiVnIX#%7B%22pageId%22%3A%22FdQLj6HTr0sgT2ZLEf-k%22%7D)

# Push to Card (P2C) Service

## General Details
Push to Card is a service provided by CRB to execute OCT Transactions using both Visa Direct and Mastercard Send rails.

CRB provides a set of APIs and Webhooks to execute the push to Card Service, that allow both to validate the payment method (the destination card) and execute the Transaction

As of Today June 2024 this service is available / was certified only for DOMESTIC transactions

## Technical Details

For leap financial implementation DOMESTIC push to funds, the apis and handling of the webhooks results need to be executed to validate the pauyment method to deposit the funds into, and to execute the actual transaction

**Important**
CRB provides an IFRAME feature to execute PCI compliant data capture of the card information. **This IFRAME feature and its related features, are not needed in Leapfinancial since the platform and the UI of our apps handles the PCI related data through VGS** vaults and its VGS-collect card capture.

## Steps to Execute
1. Add/Register a Card
    1. The execution of this api will provide the validation of the Card (like the Bin Lookup service, and other validations)
    2. The result of this api will provide if the card is valid and if it supports Push Funds
    3. this endopoint will also provide the TOKEN that CRB assigned to the card. This token is the identifier for the card and needs to be used when executing transactions using CRB Apis
2. Receive the status of the Card Validated to the corresponding callback / webhook (see if necessary, as it was required as part of the certification, but does not seems necessary to our implementation)
3. Execute the Push to Card Transaction with a validated card
    1. The execution of this api will create the actual OCT Transaction
4. Receive the status of the Transacion in the provided webhook
    1. The weebhook will provide if the Transaction was succesfull

# Push To Card Domestic with CRB

/The APIs provided by CRB allow to validate the payment method (the destination card) and execute the push funds transaction

# Pull From Card (AFT)

Cross river bank provides a feature to allow to pull funds from a card, this is, perform what is called an AFT Transaction

In order to integrate fully with AFT the Certification is pending as per October 2024
The documentation for this feature is based in Leap financial findings and is in process of being confirmed CRB

## Steps to Execute
1. Add/Register a Card
    1. The execution of this api will provide the validation of the Card (like the Bin Lookup service, and other validations)
    2. The result of this api will provide if the card is valid and if it supports Pull Funds
    3. this endopoint will also provide the TOKEN that CRB assigned to the card. This token is the identifier for the card and needs to be used when executing transactions using CRB Apis
2. Receive the status of the Card Validated to the corresponding callback / webhook (see if necessary, as it was required as part of the certification, but does not seems necessary to our implementation)
3. Execute the Pull Transaction with a validated card
    1. The execution of this api will create the actual AFT Transaction
4. Receive the status of the Transacion in the provided webhook
    1. The weebhook will provide if the Transaction was succesfull

# Pull Transaction API

See below documenation from CRB on the full process of pull Transaction
[https://docs.crossriver.com/card-payments/originate-a-pull-transaction](https://docs.crossriver.com/card-payments/originate-a-pull-transaction)

CRB Calls this process "Request funds from Payer"

Below also the original information of the API itself

# OCT Integration with Fiserv

## General Details
Fiserv provides a set of APIS they call "Digital Disbursement Payments", or DDP for short, aimed to solve many scenarios for payments, using the OCT rails.
For Leap Financial, the DDP solution is configured to provide the P2P (person to person) integration to provide the rails to execute OCT Transactions both domestic and Cross Border.

## Technical Details

The API specification for the Leap Financial Integration can be found in the following Developer Studio Hub Link

[

developer.fiserv.com

https://developer.fiserv.com/product/DigitalDisbursements/api/?type=post&path=/ddp/v1/payments&branch=main&version=1.0.0

](https://developer.fiserv.com/product/DigitalDisbursements/api/?type=post&path=/ddp/v1/payments&branch=main&version=1.0.0)

The scenario that needs to be selected to obtain the correct model of request is "p2p"
(see screenshot)

# Volcan

## Preface
Volcan is a partner that has two relations with leap financial
1) As a Partner of RAAS for their project for Remittances
2) As a Provider of the Virtual Network Account from Mastercard integration, acting as the processor of the VNA (a.k.a cardless account)

This page and its children is intended to cover the information for the 2nd relation described above
## General Details
Volcan will provide processor features to handle the Virtual Network Accounts
The Proyect that currently is set to implement this integration is **"Banco Popular de Dominicana**

The integration involves also a series of due diligence with Mastercard and Mastercard providing a BIN and an ICA after this process

drive.google.com

## Technical Details
Volcan provides different APIs to connect to them to create the VNA and other set of APIs to handle the VNA created
See this page children for the technical specification of each API to be used

# Authentication for APIs

The APIs are authenticated by accesing first a Login API with a user and password provided by Volcan.

The Login API then responds with a Token.

The **Token needs to be used in the Authorization header** of the rest of the APIs of Card Handling

Also, **all the APIs (including the login)** should carry a **header parameter called "Credenciales"**, that will cointain a value also provided by Volcan

For Sandbox below is the Example Request for Login

```bash
curl --location "http://10.23.106.33/wsParabiliumVolcan/api/login" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/json" ^
--header "Credenciales: OTlCRjlGMEY2RTMxNUY1NDdDNjIxMjZFOEEwQ0UxRTM6NTNFNEUxMjdDOTQ1NzkzMzExQUVmQkQxM0Y4Q0ZCRTQq" ^
--data "{
    \"NombreUsuario\":\"99BF9F0F6E315F547C62126E8A0CE1E3\",
    \"Password\": \"53E4E127C945793311AEfBD13F8CFBE4*\"
}"
```

Below is the Example Request with an API using the token obtained from Login

```swift
curl --location "http://10.23.106.33/wsParabiliumVolcan/api/AltaTarjetaOnb" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/json" ^
--header "Credenciales: OTlCRjlGMEY2RTMxNUY1NDdDNjIxMjZFOEEwQ0UxRTM6NjRBOEU0MTBENUFGNzJDRWEyQTMyOUUwQTc0QjkyQzQq" ^
--header "Authorization: " ^
--data-raw "{
  \"Nombre\": \"Test\",
  \"PrimerApellido\": \"Uno\",
  \"SegundoApellido\": \"Lopez\",
  \"Colonia\": \"Libertad\",
  \"TipoCliente\": \"\",
  \"NumInterior\": \"\",
  \"Latitud\": \"0.0\",
  \"GiroNegocio\": \"TI\",
  \"RFC\": \"\",
  \"Profesion\": \"Ingeniero\",

  \"Telefono\": \"55333999283\",

  \"NumeroIdentificacion\": \"897643111\",
  \"TipoActualizacion\": 1,
  \"PaisNacimiento\": \"1\",

  \"ClaveProducto\": \"PRE00055\",
  \"TipoManufactura\": \"F\",
  \"Bin\": \"11111111\",
  \"Delegacion\": \"Independencia\",
  \"MedioAcceso\": \"\",
  \"Longitud\": \"0.0\",

  \"CURP\": \"\",
```

# Login API

## Description
executes the login to obtain the credentials to execute the rest of the APIs to handle the VNA integration with Volcan

## URL
POST
[http://10.23.106.33/wsParabiliumVolcan/api/Login](http://10.23.106.33/wsParabiliumVolcan/api/Login)

## Original SPECS Documentation

[

drive.google.com

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lNgKYHMGcdE04OLWIP34ovO2VGucrNfT/view?usp=sharing

](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1lNgKYHMGcdE04OLWIP34ovO2VGucrNfT/view?usp=sharing)

## Example Request

```json
{
    "NombreUsuario":"",
    "Password": ""
}
```

### Fields Definition
NombreUsuario = user name provided by Volcan
Password = provided by Volcan

###  Example Response

```json
{
    "UserID": "f8afeb62-7225-4464-9b0d-1c14e1273454",
    "NombreUsuario": "WS Tar Int Volcán",
    "PrimerApellido": "WS Tar Int Volcán",
    "SegundoApellido": "WS Tar Int Volcán",
    "Token": "",
    "CodRespuesta": "0000",
    "DescRespuesta": "Aprobada"
}
```

# SEON

SEON is the platform / partner that Leap Financial uses for the Risk Management and Assesment

URL of full postman collection for SEON

[

god.gw.postman.com

https://god.gw.postman.com/run-collection/15016634-0374e41a-e09d-44d8-9dc5-b3481eb06cf0?action=collection%2Ffork&collection-url=entityId%3D15016634-0374e41a-e09d-44d8-9dc5-b3481eb06cf0%26entityType%3Dcollection%26workspaceId%3Dfa092807-59c6-477b-ad18-03c09ccfde3c

](https://god.gw.postman.com/run-collection/15016634-0374e41a-e09d-44d8-9dc5-b3481eb06cf0?action=collection%2Ffork&collection-url=entityId%3D15016634-0374e41a-e09d-44d8-9dc5-b3481eb06cf0%26entityType%3Dcollection%26workspaceId%3Dfa092807-59c6-477b-ad18-03c09ccfde3c)

# Authentication

**Authentication for APIs**
The Authentication for this API is handled through an API Key provided by SEON

The variable is called i SEON system as a "licence key" and the attribute is a header parameter named "X-API-KEY"

See below example in CURL of the header portion with this key parameter

```plain
curl --location "https://api.us-east-1-main.seon.io/SeonRestService/fraud-api/v2" ^
--header "X-API-KEY: [license_key]" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/json" ^
```

See attached for the current DEV / QA keys configured

[API KEYS SEON.txt](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/985ea298-f393-4b84-be96-9cac4990616d/API%20KEYS%20SEON.txt)

**Authentication for WebHooks**
Api key to decode the Webhooks - this is a generic API key provided by SEON under the name "Default SEON Sandbox API License Key"

**Note From TA - June 2025**
the API keys are only visible for Operations Teams, the current configuration of API Keys is as follows

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/5b5e4261-24f5-43ad-abea-de5923518b7b/image.png)

# Fraud API

The API that SEON provides contains the access to all the abilities that SEON has to receive an amount data feed and return a risk analysis

Depending on the parameterse selected, the FRAUD API will execute more or less analuysis, and will need more or less data

### API Reference
[https://docs.seon.io/api-reference/fraud-api#fraud-api-request](https://docs.seon.io/api-reference/fraud-api#fraud-api-request)

### URL for the Endpoint
[https://api.us-east-1-main.seon.io/SeonRestService/fraud-api/v](https://api.us-east-1-main.seon.io/SeonRestService/fraud-api/v)

See Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41237](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41237)) for the authentication details

### Field Specific Details
The current MVP for integrating this APP only requires a few fields, as detailed in the spreadsheet below, that also includes the mapping to the transaction elements in the platform
▶️ [https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-MO1M1LZkIK7BndPtO7ETnKvirESFUaE2aywur0wY1k/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1-MO1M1LZkIK7BndPtO7ETnKvirESFUaE2aywur0wY1k/edit?usp=sharing)

Below is the Request and Response **example for the current MVP integration**

**Note**:
it ws defined with Risk team that if any data of the required for this API call is not available at the moment of execution (e.g. because the receipient does not exist yet) the field will not be sent"

### Request Example CURL

```swift
curl --location "https://api.us-east-1-main.seon.io/SeonRestService/fraud-api/v2" ^
--header "Content-Type: application/json" ^
--header "X-API-KEY: ba2b5207-5829-4fee-8df7-80e5982a9235" ^
--data-raw "{
```
