# Fraud API Enhacement for AML



# Web hooks

URL for Webhooks specs

[

docs.seon.io

https://docs.seon.io/api-reference/webhooks

](https://docs.seon.io/api-reference/webhooks)

The Webhook that will be handled for the Transaction Processing will be the one triggered by the Fraud Api, that returns the transaction status

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/19f353b3-68af-436a-8c02-79bba28e4d55/image.png)
Example Payload of the Web hook

```plain
{"event": "transaction:status_update","date": "2017-08-30T13:47:42+00:00","transactions": [{"id": "e601f2dae8f9","state": "REVIEW","label": "Marked as review"}]}
```

Example of web hook obtained in our dev environment

[Seon event transaction-status\_update.txt](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/699da41a-220b-4ccd-8ba9-725dd60023fd/Seon%20event%20transaction-status_update.txt)

# Transaction Processing

# General
Seon Provides an API to allow to process the information of the transactions and rule over the risk of such transactions.

Then, the Leap Risk Management team can access the noumerous SEON tools to analyze patterns in the transactions and feed the engine of fraud detection so when each API call is made the potential risks can be identifyied accurately

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/c799fb30-288c-4bb0-afd0-f822069e2589/image.png)

# Technical Details
The execution of the Transaction Monitoring will occur rougly as follows
*   The Platform Initializes the transaction
*   The information of the transaction is sent to seon using the Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41257](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41257))
*   The Platforms listens for the event of the transaction status change through the Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41297](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41297))
*   The Event is processed with the transaction status returned, the logic needs to be set in place to accomplish the requirement Private ([https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4203](https://app.clickup.com/t/1238940/RAAS-4203))
*   If the Event returned a favorable outcome - according to the logic in the previous step- for the transaction, the Transaction will continue its lifecycle in the platform with no issues
*   If The Event returned a unfavorable outcome, the transaction will be stopped from their lifecycle and needs to be either set in on hold or set to the cancellation path
*   See the detail of each scenario in the child page Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41317](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41317))

# Transaction State Handling

The platform will need to handle the transaction state that is delivered from the Fraud API

The following scenarios describe the handling of this states at platform level

Depending on the value returned in the field "state" the Platform will either continue, hold or cancel the transaction, with the following Detail

→ If the state field value is **Decline**
the transaction needs to be **cancelled**
→ If the state field value is **Approve**
the transaction **can continue** to their next status
→ If the State field value is **Review**
the transaction needs to be **set on hold** with the specific "status Detail" "underRiskReview"
→When the Transaction is set on hold
The Transaction will remain in this status until Risk Team reviews the transaction in SEON system, they will Manually define if the transaction will be declined or approved, in SEON Console, this will trigger again the "status update" event, and the process will be executed one more time, with the corresponing outcome (either cancellation or continuation)
→When the transaction remains on hold
If the transaction remains on hold for certain period of time, and the time set expires, the transaction will be set to be cancelled as part of the cancellation Job for expired transactions in the platform

## **Testing Details:**

The Fraud APi Transaction Evaluation details can be reviewed with the following info in Kibana server logs

**URL**
[https://kibana-dev.leapfinancial.com/app/discover#/?\_g=(filters:!(),refreshInterval:(pause:!t,value:60000),time:(from:now-1d,to:now)](https://kibana-dev.leapfinancial.com/app/discover#/?_g=(filters:!(),refreshInterval:(pause:!t,value:60000),time:(from:now-1d,to:now)))&\_a=(columns:!(),filters:!(),hideChart:!t,index:%279f79577a-b3b5-4157-b4e5-27946a553e6e%27,interval:auto,query:(language:lucene,query:%27%22%2FSeonRestService%2Ffraud-api%2Fv2%22%20%26%26%20%22httpRequestResponse%22%27),sort:!(!(%27@timestamp%27,desc)))

**Search Syntax**
"/SeonRestService/fraud-api/v2" && "httpRequestResponse"

**SEON Mock in QA environment**
"FakePositiveResponseEnabled" en el SeonApi y deben ponerla en true

See Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41237](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41237)) for the authentication details

### Field Specific Details

# Testing in Sandbox

Sandbox Configuration allows to test the FRAUD API and the Events as follows

*   For the transaction to be Approved = any Amount under 475 and over 25 should be used
*   For the transaction to be set in Review = any amount between 475 and 500 should be used
*   For the transaction to be set in Cancelled/Declined = any amount above 500 or under 25 should be used
(amounts expressed in USD)

The cancellation of review can also be triggered by the velocity of the user, but the rules are Still pending confirmation from Risk Team

# Backend Dictionary Configuration

Development Environment Variables
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/fcb43329-4f84-4fe1-86eb-b9a3e4d0b36c/image.png)

**SeonUrl** \- base url for APis
**SeonApiKey** \- APi key for Authentication for APIs
This value goes in X-API-KEY Field - see = Private ([https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41237](https://app.clickup.com/1238940/docs/15tww-59737/15tww-41237))
**SeonSecretKey** \- Api key to decode the Webhooks - this is a generic API key provided by SEON under the name "Default SEON Sandbox API License Key"

Values for QA Environment
**SeonApiKey**

```plain
RAAS QA
ba2b5207-5829-4fee-8df7-80e5982a9235
```

**SeonSecretKey**

Values for PROD Environment

# Home Refreshment and Redesign

## General Details
The User Experience home page for NUMI was centered in the “remittances” or “send money to/from” experience
It was requested by the Business teams to re-center the experience in allowing the user to identify the different features the application has, as well as accesing all this features in a easy to find way

## UX Details
*   The home page now contains the main account information: its balance and the access to the specific acocunt information.
    *   Depending on the implementation this will be a carrousel like interaction to display the different accounts the user might have, or only one option
*   In the section that displays the account information only one action will be available to describe and access the “relation of the account with the outside world”, this is, how the account can be either loaded or unloaded. This action will lead to the section of transactions specifically related to the loads and unloads (transfer outs) of the account
*   The rest of the sections will display the different features that the application offers to the user within the scope of the account/accounts universe, this options displayed will depend on what the implementation offers in each case.
*   According to the implementation and the account type the user is viewing in the top section, the optins might be
    *   Domestinc Transfers from/ to other Users of the Program
    *   Crosobroder Transfers from/ to other Users of the Program
    *   Bill Payment
    *   Top Up
    *   Proffessional Charges
    *   Cash Out Features offered by users
*   Each section when selected, will display a corresponding “sub-home” with the details of the transactions performed for that specific feature
    *   When applies (e.g. transfers from/to users) the transaction list will keep the “chat metaphore” that was used in the Numi original implementation
    *   In other cases the Transactions will be displayed as a list
*   The Burger Menu will have reduced options only to information not related to the usage and details of User´s Program account.
    *   Depending on the Implementation this section might have:
        *   External Accounts details
        *   User Agreements
        *   Contracts / LEgal information
        *   Support Access

# Text Formats

In the following list you will find the detail of the specific formats for the texts given in the platform overall. This means, for every time a text from the ones detailed below is displayed in the Platform Apps, the format should be as detailed here:
**_Note: this page is ongoing changes_**
## **Payment Methods**
*   **Bank Account-PLAID**
    *   BOF-1111 = BANK ACRONYM +”-”+ LAST 4 DIGITS OF THE ACCOUNT
        *   if BANK ACRONYM DOES NOT EXIST
        *   BANK-1111 = WORD “BANK”+”-”+LAST 4 DIGITS OF THE ACCOUNT
*   **Bank Account - SPEI**
    *   If/When editing bank account details is available
        *   MAMA-1111 = FIRST FOUR CHARACTERS OF THE ACCOUNT ALIAS GIVEN BY THE USER AND LAST 4 DIGITS OF THE CLAVE
    *   BANCO-1111 = WORD “BANCO”+”-”+LAST 4 DIGITS OF THE ACCOUNT

# Branding Configuration Guide

## Purpose
This Guide is aimed to describe the specifications for configurable items in the application, to apply styles that allow the different Brands to give their desired look and feel for their instances of the application. 
In this document you will find some general branding configuration details and a section for each of the different visual components in the app, with a detail of the items that can be configured.
We also provide information in each section for better understanding of the Branding Styles scope.
## Configuration Details
The configuration consists mainly in the ability to choose colouring styles for backgrounds, texts or interaction elements such as buttons. It also allows to provide certain specific branding assets (e.g. images) . 
It´s important to point out that certain elements like font type and size, element placing and size, cannot be changed in the configuration.
Finally, the last element that cannot be changed is the background colour of the secondary areas, which is set as white (#FFFFFF) for all instances. 
Each section Below provides Detail of the Branding Styles
## Branding Styles:
### 1) Main Background Colour and its Text Colour
This two configurations will define the mail look and feel of the Brand.  It's suggested that the Defined Colour for this configuration is one that provided a proper contrast with the White areas of the application.
You can see the following image as an example to identify the areas where this configured style is used:
PrimaryColor = #000000
PrimaryTextColor= #FFFFFF
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/ef31d6ec-6b08-4f71-8ffc-3c81f1425119/SacbeGuide-PrimaryColor_BackgroundColor.jpg)
### 2) Secondary Colour and its Text Colour
The Secondary Colour will define mostly the areas of user gesture for the application (as opposite to the information display or information input areas). It also can be used to provide highlighted information within a main Background Colour screen.
In the following image example you will see defined:
SecondaryColor = #4383c4
SecondaryTextColor = #FFFFFF
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/ac6e19cb-93e0-434b-8a9c-fb661b7a3055/SacbeGuide-SecondaryColor.jpg)
### 3) Primary Button Colour and its Text Colour
The Primary Button  is aimed to identify the button element to be used for the main actions performed in the application, this is, actions that take place in the main flows of usage of the application,(should we detail here the main flows?) as opposite to the alternate flows or secondary actions.
In the image below you will see the example for this implementation:
PrimaryButtonColor = #FFFFFF
PrimaryButtonTextColor =  #4383c4
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/eff9e945-988a-4a1b-873b-86e68fc1f486/SacbeGuide-PrimaryButton.jpg)

### 4) Secondary button Colour and its Text Colour
The secondary button will be used in flows that are accessory to the main interactions of the application. 
In the example of this implementation:
SecondaryButtonColor = #edca03
SecondaryButtonTextColor = #ffffff

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/58507835-9514-492c-bba7-5381e7c148aa/SacbeGuide-SecondaryButton.jpg)

### 5) Specific Screens: Request Card
For specific Branding/Marketing sections, or to provide communications on special offers to the end user, such as Request Card, the application can be configured to display a specific background color in such pages.
In the image we can observe
RequestCardColor = #21a59d
RequestCardTextColor = #FFFFFF

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/6c732c5c-7336-4dd4-be44-8cce284334aa/SacbeGuide-RequestCard.jpg)
### 6) Specific Screens: Splash
The splash is the first section that will be loading every time the user loads the application. Its content is marketing related.
XXX OPlace Example XXXX
### 7) Spedific Sreens:  Onboardings
The Onboardings is a set of screens that are going to be displayed the first time the user gets into the application. They are meant to explain the usage and advantatges of the app. Its content is marketing related.
XXX OPlace Example XXXX
## Branding Assets
### Cards Images
The image assets related to the Card Images are also available to be configured within the application. A specific size/weight image needs to be provided for this images.
The configured assets will be used in the Request Card page or in the Card Info Page.
The image needs to comply with the following 
Request Card Asset
Height
Width
Maximum file size
Card Info Asset
Height
Width
Maximum file size

![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/8a24e80b-d76a-44e9-904c-69a079f1846f/SacbeGuide-Assets.jpg)
### Logos Images
There are certain areas in the application that will display different logos for the provided Branding and for the different card providers.
These logos needs to be provided to complete the branding strategy .
The images that appear as logos may be shown in the Main Menu, the Home Page, and the Card Features Page .
The images provided need to comply with the following
Home Page Logo
Height
Width
Maximum file size
Menu Logo
Height
Width
Maximum file size
Card Features Logo
Height
Width
Maximum file size
Card Features Card Provider Logo
Height
Width
Maximum file size
![](https://t1238940.p.clickup-attachments.com/t1238940/9c1a7a0a-79a6-4c20-9bbd-e3177582928a/SacbeGuide-Logos.jpg)

## Store Content
The content of each store to be placed (Google Play Store, Apple Store) needs to be provided to provide a unification of the marketing content
